NEW `src/test/unit/adapters/persistence/LocalStorageBoardCollectionRepositoryV4.test.ts` (~125 raw lines, 6 combined-assertion tests with a local `InMemoryStorage` `Storage`-shape fake mirroring the v3 adapter's test scaffold: **seed-on-empty + persist** (empty storage → seeded showcase snapshot returned + persisted envelope is `v: 2` with `schemaVersion: "v4.0"` trees), **multi-board v4 round-trip** (every kind from `buildSampleTreeV4` preserved + `currentBoardId` preserved + ordered board id/name list preserved), **v3-fallback happy path** (a hand-crafted v=1 envelope built from the v3 `buildShowcaseBoard` + v3 `encode` round-trips through v3 codec + bridge — engineering BSC's `computed: true` lifts to `ComputedBusinessScoreNode` via §17.99c, AND the next `save()` re-emits as `v: 2`), **StorageFullErrorV4 surface** (all 4 quota flavours detected + other errors propagate verbatim), **malformed payload rejection** (not-JSON / not-object / missing currentBoardId / missing boards all surface descriptive errors), **belt-and-braces** (a v=2 envelope carrying a v3-shape tree falls back to v3 codec + bridge on that single tree)). **Skipped from this strand** (deferred to §17.110 / follow-ons because the v3 adapter stays live until cutover and these are operator-facing surfaces tightly coupled to the §17.110 wiring): §17.86 runtime version-mismatch classification + banner callback, §17.86b read-only mode toggle, §17.63 legacy-key migration (`tree-graph-viz` → `tree-map-viz` — moot for the v4 adapter because it's launched after the §17.63 rename), §17.42 `freshDateColor` discard (v4 envelope doesn't carry the field). The v3-shared storage key (`tree-map-viz/board-collection/v1`) means the cutover at §17.110 is transparent — existing operator data loads via the fallback, re-saves as v4 on the next persist — §17.107 | `feature/17.107-local-storage-board-collection-repository-v4` | 1142 → 1148 _(unit, +6 net combined-assertion tests)_ + 116 _(e2e, unchanged)_ | DONE | **Twenty-second execution strand of the §17.94 v5 round-7 plan** and the **last Phase D strand**. Phase D is now CLOSED end-to-end (D.4 sampleDataV4 §17.108, D.5 showcaseSeedV4 §17.109, D.1+D.6a+D.6b jsonCodecV4 §17.105+§17.106a+§17.106b, **D.7 LocalStorageV4 §17.107**); the v4 read+write+persistence chain is operator-functional in isolation, ready for the §17.110 Phase E cutover to wire it through `main.ts`. **Strictly additive**: zero existing files modified, `main.ts` doesn't import the v4 adapter yet, v3 stays live verbatim. **§17.106b's "factoring relocation" decision honoured**: the codec describes a single wire dialect (v4.0); the adapter owns the multi-version load policy (v=1 vs v=2 + the v4-decode-failure belt-and-braces). This separation keeps each module's contract narrow and the v3 codec's lifetime extension to §17.112+ Phase F deletion doesn't pollute the codec surface. **Bundle delta**: 0 — adapter is tree-shaken (`main.ts` doesn't import it; reachability arrives at §17.110 Phase E cutover). **Strand sizing on disk**: ~155 (adapter) + ~125 (tests) = 280 raw .ts lines (+ ~1 SPEC delta, − 1 sonar-version line). Sonar projected `new_lines` ~270-290 — fits the 300 ceiling with headroom. **`sonar.projectVersion` bump `0.2.34 → 0.2.35`** + this history docblock recording the twenty-second §17.94 execution strand. **§17.72 corrected procedure followed** — content COMMITTED FIRST on `feature/17.107-local-storage-board-collection-repository-v4`, then `npm run sonar:gate` runs on the committed tip. **§17.94 plan row decisions preserved**: the "uses `jsonCodecV4`" claim landed verbatim (encode + decode both v4-native via the §17.106b codec); the implicit v3-data-migration requirement (the v3 adapter's live operator localStorage data must keep working across the cutover) is satisfied via the v3-fallback shim per the §17.106b docblock's relocation. **What this strand does NOT do**: doesn't wire `LocalStorageBoardCollectionRepositoryV4` into `main.ts` (§17.110 Phase E cutover owns the composition-root rewiring); doesn't port the §17.86 runtime version-mismatch surface (the v3 adapter's `appMajor` classification + `VersionMismatchInfo` callbacks + `EnvelopeMigrator` chain — port if the cutover needs them, else retire); doesn't port the §17.86b read-only mode toggle (same rationale); doesn't port the §17.63 legacy-key migration (moot — the v4 adapter is launched post-rename); doesn't discard a legacy `freshDateColor` field (v4 envelope doesn't carry the field); doesn't migrate the v3 LocalStorage adapter (stays live until §17.111+ Phase F deletion). **What this strand surfaces for the operator**: nothing immediately — the adapter is unreachable from `main.ts`. After the §17.110 cutover, the kiosk's first boot post-upgrade will: (1) read the existing `v: 1` envelope from localStorage, (2) decode each board's v3-shape tree via the v3 codec + bridge (every `computed: true` BSC lifts to `ComputedBusinessScoreNode`, every `eligibleForParentComputation: false` flag lands as `disabled: true`, cards sidecar populates per the §17.100.5 unit-lift), (3) hand the resulting `Tree` snapshot to `BoardCollectionServiceV4`, (4) on the next persist (boards rename / value-edit / weight-edit / add-child / etc.) re-emit as `v: 2` v4-native — silent migration, no operator interaction, full round-trip preservation of round-7 kinds. **Branch cut off master** (NOT stacked on §17.106b — already merged). **Phase D closed**. **Next strand**: per the §17.94 plan, **§17.110** — Phase E composition-root cutover (`main.ts` rewires from v3 services to v4 services + the v4 LocalStorage adapter + the v4 ImportExport service + wires the `computation-kind-change` event from the §17.104 dropdowns to `EditNodeServiceV4.setComputationKind`; the largest single strand remaining because it touches `main.ts`'s entire wiring graph; may need to split into 110a-services + 110b-event-wiring + 110c-cleanup if the new_lines budget bites). Alternative: a §17.107-follow-on porting §17.86 + §17.86b + §17.86c version-mismatch features to the v4 adapter if the operator wants those operator-facing surfaces preserved through the cutover. |
| **v5 round-7 Phase D.6b — `jsonCodecV4` v4-native decoder rewrite (second half of the §17.106 plan row; closes the round-trip gap §17.106a left open between v4-native encode and v3-codec-composition decode)** (closes the decoder half of the §17.94 §17.106 plan row — the §17.106 strand is now fully landed across a/b). **Two existing production files modified + one existing test file rewritten; zero new files; zero deletions**: MODIFIED `src/adapters/persistence/jsonCodecV4.ts` (178 → ~325 raw lines incl. docblock; the §17.106a v4-native encoder is preserved verbatim; the §17.105 + §17.106a composed decoder body — `decode(text) { v3Root = decodeV3(text); return v4TreeFromV3Root(v3Root, clock, opts); }` — is REPLACED with a full v4-native walker dispatching on the per-kind `kind` discriminant; `createJsonCodecV4`'s signature narrows from `(clock, opts: V3ToV4Options = {})` to `(clock)` because the §17.99c polymorphic substitution + §17.99b disabled migration + §17.81 strictRange escape hatch all surface from the wire shape directly now rather than from bridge options; v3 `jsonCodec` + `v4TreeFromV3Root` imports DROPPED from the codec (legacy-v3-JSON migration fallback now belongs to §17.107 `LocalStorageBoardCollectionRepositoryV4` per the codec's updated docblock); new exported error class `JsonCodecV4DecodeError` carrying an RFC-6901 pointer + a reason string for every typed surface (`/`, `/schemaVersion`, `/root/kind`, `/root/computationKind`, `/root/range/min`, `/root/children/<i>/...`, `/cards/<i>/nodeId`, etc.); new helpers `decodeTree` (top-level envelope walker), `decodeNode` (kind dispatch), 5 per-kind decoders (`decodeTextNode` / `decodeBSN` / `decodeCBSN` / `decodeCN` / `decodeStrictRangeNode`), `applyDisabledAndChildren` (uniform §17.99a `setDisabled` + recursive child decode + `attach`), `decodeHistory` (typed value-shape guards for both `"string"` and `"number"` history entries — TextNodeV4 vs. every numeric value-node kind), `decodeObjective` / `decodeLenientRange` / `decodeStrictRange` / `decodeRangeShape` / `decodeBound` (the `null ↔ ±Infinity` sentinel reversal — JSON-roundtrippable form of an unbounded range), `decodeComputationKind` (calls the new `ComputationKind.fromName` to resolve canonical singletons + throws on unknown names), `decodeCards` (walks the flat `[{nodeId, unit}]` sidecar + resolves each via local pre-order DFS through the freshly-built root + asserts the resolved node `instanceof BusinessScoreNode` — defensive contract for the §17.100.5 sidecar's BSN-only invariant), `findById` (local DFS for sidecar resolution — kept private to the codec; doesn't pollute the `Tree` API), `decodeIsoTimestamp` (Date.parse + NaN guard), and 6 pointer/type-guard helpers (`requireObject` / `requireString` / `optionalString` / `requireNumber` / `requireArray` / `joinPointer` with RFC-6901 `~` and `/` escaping + `typeOf` returning `"null"`/`"array"`/etc. for human error messages); strategy comparator hardcoded to `NumericComparator.INSTANCE` on every numeric range reconstruction (every range in the codebase uses it; if a future strategy variant lands we widen the wire shape then). MODIFIED `src/domain/computation/ComputationKind.ts` (+12 raw lines — new static `fromName(name: string): ComputationKind | undefined` linear search over `ComputationKind.ALL`; reference equality with the static singleton slots holds; docblock updated from "deferred to §17.105 codec decode" to "landed at §17.106b — codec decoder rewrite needs to round-trip the persisted `name` string back to the canonical singleton"). REWRITTEN `src/test/unit/adapters/persistence/jsonCodecV4.test.ts` (261 → ~210 raw lines, 13 → 13 combined-assertion tests; the §17.106a v3-shape `bscWire` / `textWire` helpers + the 10 §17.105 decoder tests reading them are DELETED (the v3-wire decoder path no longer exists — legacy-v3-JSON migration moved out of the codec per the §17.107 design); the §17.106a 5 encoder tests are PRESERVED verbatim; replaced with 5 new decoder tests under "decode — schema validation + per-kind reconstruction + RFC-6901 pointers" pinning: **envelope validation** (malformed JSON → `JsonCodecV4DecodeError` at `/`; mismatching schemaVersion → typed error citing the bad value; missing schemaVersion → typed error); **kind validation** (unknown `kind` string → typed error with the offending discriminant; unknown `ComputationKind` name → typed error citing the bad name); **range bounds reversal** (BSN's lenient `null/null` round-trips to `±Infinity`; StrictRange's finite bounds round-trip verbatim; `ComputationKind.fromName` resolves canonical singletons via reference equality); **§17.99a uniform disabled propagation across every value-node subclass** (TextNodeV4 + BusinessScoreNode + CBSN + ComputedNode + StrictRangeNode all honour `disabled: true` on decode); **cards sidecar resolution + defensive guard** (decoded card's `getNode()` references the freshly-built BSN; `nodeId` referencing a non-existent or non-BSN node surfaces `JsonCodecV4DecodeError`); 3 new round-trip tests under "round-trip preservation — every v4 kind survives encode→decode unchanged (closes the §17.106a→§17.106b gap)" pinning: **BusinessScoreNode full surface** (history + objective + disabled + unit all preserved through encode→decode); **`buildSampleTreeV4` round-trip** (every node kind preserved including the round-7 Computed* + StrictRangeNode additions; every `computationKind` resolved to its canonical singleton via reference equality; cards sidecar key-set preserved); **`buildShowcaseTreeV4` byte-exact round-trip** (encode→decode→encode produces the bit-identical JSON; 16-node tree with 10-entry cards sidecar; the `sales-lost.disabled` flag survives — the §17.99b broader-successor migration applied at fixture construction round-trips through persistence)) — §17.106b | `feature/17.106b-json-codec-v4-decoder-rewrite` | 1136 → 1139 _(unit, net +3 — added 8 decoder/round-trip tests + 5 encoder tests preserved; removed 10 §17.106a v3-shape decoder tests that no longer apply)_ + 116 _(e2e, unchanged)_ | DONE | **Twenty-first execution strand of the §17.94 v5 round-7 plan**. **Closes the §17.106a→§17.106b round-trip gap**: pre-§17.106b a tree containing `ComputedNode` / `StrictRangeNode` would encode to v4-native JSON but fail to decode (the §17.105 composition shim routed through the v3 codec which throws `JsonDecodeError` at `/nodeType` on the unknown v4 `kind` strings); §17.106b's v4-native decoder walks the same wire shape the encoder produces, so every tree the encoder can emit now round-trips losslessly. **§17.106 strand CLOSED end-to-end**: encode + decode both v4-native, round-trip preserved for all 5 v4 kinds. **`createJsonCodecV4`'s signature simplified**: `(clock: Clock, opts: V3ToV4Options = {}) → (clock: Clock)` — the `opts` parameter served the §17.105 composition path (passed through to `v4TreeFromV3Root`); v4-native decode reads the wire shape directly, so no bridge options are needed at the codec level. The §17.81 per-id strictRange escape hatch is no longer relevant because StrictRangeNode is now a first-class wire `kind` (`StrictRangeNodeV4`); operator-targeted v4-native producers should emit the right kind directly rather than relying on per-id overrides. **Legacy-v3-JSON migration relocated to §17.107**: the v4 codec no longer absorbs v3-shape wire payloads; instead `LocalStorageBoardCollectionRepositoryV4` (next strand) will own a fallback shim — try v4 `decode` first, catch `JsonCodecV4DecodeError`, fall back to the v3 codec + `v4TreeFromV3Root(v3Root, clock)` bridge. This factoring is cleaner because the codec describes a single wire dialect and the adapter owns the multi-version migration policy. The v3 codec stays canonical for v3 imports until §17.112+ Phase F deletion; the v4 codec just no longer routes through it. **`ComputationKind.fromName` landed**: a 5-line static linear search over `ComputationKind.ALL`; reference equality holds with the static singleton slots so the §17.95 strategy chassis's cached-strategy contract (the `computationKind` getter must return the exact singleton the strategy was constructed with) survives decode unchanged. **RFC-6901 JSON pointers**: every typed error surfaces a precise pointer (`/root/children/0/range/kind` rather than "somewhere in the tree"); operator-facing error messages cite both the pointer and the offending value (e.g. `jsonCodecV4 decode error at /root/computationKind: unknown ComputationKind name "MOOD"`). The `joinPointer` helper escapes `~` and `/` per RFC-6901 (`~0` and `~1` respectively) so a node ID containing those characters won't break pointer parsing. **Combined-assertion test design preserved**: 13 tests pin every material decoder invariant + envelope validation + kind dispatch + range bounds reversal + uniform disabled propagation + cards sidecar resolution + 3 round-trip end-to-end suites pinning the §17.106a→§17.106b gap closure. **+3 net unit tests** (1136 → 1139). 116 e2e unchanged. **Bundle delta**: 0 — codec is still tree-shaken (`main.ts` doesn't import `jsonCodecV4` yet; reachability arrives at §17.107 LocalStorage adapter + §17.110 cutover). **Strand sizing on disk**: net +147 prod codec lines (added decoder section + new error class + helpers; net of the ~10 lines deleted from the §17.105 composition body) + 12 ComputationKind lines + ~210 test lines (replacement, not addition — 261-line §17.106a test file shrinks by ~51 lines as the 10 §17.105 v3-shape decoder tests + their wire helpers vanish). Sonar projected `new_lines` ~170-200 — well under the 300 ceiling with comfortable headroom. **`sonar.projectVersion` bump `0.2.33 → 0.2.34`** + this history docblock recording the decoder rewrite that closes the §17.106 strand. **§17.72 corrected procedure followed** — content COMMITTED FIRST on `feature/17.106b-json-codec-v4-decoder-rewrite`, then `npm run sonar:gate` runs on the committed tip. **§17.107 now unblocked**: build `LocalStorageBoardCollectionRepositoryV4` against the fully-functional v4-native codec + add the v3-fallback decode shim at the ADAPTER layer for legacy operator localStorage data (try v4 `decode` first, catch `JsonCodecV4DecodeError`, fall back to v3 codec + `v4TreeFromV3Root` bridge). **What this strand does NOT do**: doesn't wire `jsonCodecV4` into `main.ts` (§17.110 Phase E cutover); doesn't add the v3-fallback shim (§17.107 owns it); doesn't preserve `ComputedNode` + `ComputedBusinessScoreNode` history on the wire (audit-only per §17.94 D5; the wire shape omits it entirely; if a future strand needs Computed* history persistence, the wire grows an optional `history` field on those kinds without a major version bump); doesn't serialise the strategy comparator (every numeric range hardcodes `NumericComparator.INSTANCE`); doesn't add wire-shape backward compatibility for the §17.106a-only encoded payloads that never decoded (those were broken-by-design between the §17.106a and §17.106b ships; the wire shape didn't change between the two strands so any v4 payload §17.106a's encoder produced will decode fine through §17.106b's decoder — the round-trip was BROKEN, not the encoded format itself). **Branch cut off master** (NOT stacked on §17.106a — already merged). **Next strand**: per the §17.94 plan, **§17.107** — `LocalStorageBoardCollectionRepositoryV4` (the v4 successor to v3's §17.31 LocalStorage adapter; consumes the now-fully-functional `jsonCodecV4` + adds the v3-fallback decode shim for legacy operator data; smallest remaining Phase D strand; unblocks §17.110 Phase E cutover). |

the §17.105 decoder test suite (10 tests under "round-7 fixture suite" + 2 error tests) is preserved verbatim; replaced the §17.105 "encode throws JsonCodecV4NotYetImplementedError" stub-test with 5 new combined-assertion tests under a new section "§17.106a — v4-native encode (decode rewrite remains §17.106b's job)" pinning: **envelope shape** (`{schemaVersion:"v4.0", root, cards[]}` with the cards sidecar as a flat `[{nodeId, unit}]` list); **TextNodeV4 + BusinessScoreNode per-kind shapes** (the `disabled` omit-when-false / emit-when-true rule, BSN's `range:{min:null,max:null}` unbounded encoding, BSN's unit field omit-when-empty); **ComputedBusinessScoreNode + ComputedNode + StrictRangeNode per-kind shapes** (CBSN emits `computationKind` + NO history (audit-only per §17.94 D5), CN emits computationKind only via a full `toEqual` snapshot pinning every field, StrictRangeNode emits strict range with finite bounds + history); **defensive guard** (encode throws `JsonCodecV4EncodeError` on an unsupported `Node` subclass — would indicate a v5+ kind added without codec extension). (a) won because the showcase is itself a fixture (lifetime-disconnected from production code) AND it's the natural place to first-class every v4 node kind including the round-7 additions (CN + StrictRangeNode have no v3 analogue, so the v3-bridge can't produce them on a v3 source). **Stable-ID continuity preserved**: every e2e scenario targeting `engineering` / `sales` / `bench` / `sales-lost` etc. continues to resolve against `tree.findById(id)`; the v4 showcase's ID surface is a superset (v3 ids + the 3 new round-7 ids `activity` / `cpu-saturation` / `activity-incident`). **Combined-assertion test design preserved**: 5 tests pinning shape + ID continuity + polymorphic substitution + cards sidecar + mapper smoke; matches the §17.102 / §17.103 / §17.104b / §17.105 / §17.108 precedent. **+5 net unit tests** (1131 → 1136). 116 e2e unchanged. **Bundle delta**: 0 — `showcaseSeedV4` is tree-shaken (not imported from `main.ts`); reachability arrives at §17.110 OR via a future debug seed action. | **v5 round-7 Phase D.1 — `jsonCodecV4` decode-side adapter via composition with the §17.81 / §17.88 v3 → v4 bridge** (closes the §17.94 §17.105 plan row — the first heavy Phase D strand and the unblocker for §17.107 `LocalStorageBoardCollectionRepositoryV4` + the §17.110 cutover). **One new production file + one new test file; zero existing files modified; zero deletions**: NEW `src/adapters/persistence/jsonCodecV4.ts` (~70 raw lines incl. docblock — `createJsonCodecV4(clock: Clock, opts?: V3ToV4Options): TreeCodecV4` factory returning a plain object literal implementing the §17.103 `TreeCodecV4` port; `decode(text)` delegates to the v3 `jsonCodec.decode(text)` (which parses the wire shape `LocalStorageBoardCollectionRepository` already stores today) THEN calls `v4TreeFromV3Root(v3root, clock, opts)` to lift the v3 graph to a §17.79 `Tree` — the §17.99c polymorphic substitution of `ComputedBusinessScoreNode<T>` for any v3 BSC with `computed: true`, the §17.99b `eligibleForParentComputation: false` → `disabled: true` migration, the §17.100.5 `BusinessScoreCardV4` sidecar build, and the §17.81 per-id `strictRange` escape hatch all surface here automatically through the bridge; `encode(tree)` throws `JsonCodecV4NotYetImplementedError` (a typed error class exported from the same module so callers can `instanceof`-discriminate the stub from a real failure) per the §17.94 plan row's split: encode is §17.106's deliverable so this strand intentionally lands the decode half only and leaves the structurally-required `encode` method as an explicit unimplemented stub rather than a missing field); NEW `src/test/unit/adapters/persistence/jsonCodecV4.test.ts` (~165 raw lines, 10 combined-assertion tests across 2 describe blocks — **round-7 fixture suite** (7 tests, the §17.94 plan row's explicit deliverable): plain v3 BSC → BSN with objective + history + lenient range; `computed:true` BSC → CBSN (subclass identity) with history NOT carried + `computationKind = WEIGHTED_AVERAGE`; `eligibleForParentComputation:false` → `disabled:true` applied uniformly across BSN + CBSN; every BSC's unit → `Tree.cards` entry (the v3 codec rejects empty units via `Unit.of("")` throwing `InvalidUnitError`, so the entry is always present from a wire-decoded tree — the `if (rawUnit.length > 0)` guard in `v4TreeFromV3Root.buildCardsFromV3` is unreachable from this code path but stays as defensive belt-and-braces); TextNode wire → `TextNodeV4` with history + description hardcoded to `""` per §17.15; per-id `strictRange` override → `StrictRangeNode` winning over `computed:true`; composite tree preserves children order + parent wiring across the recursive bridge — and **errors + encode stub** (3 tests): malformed JSON propagates `JsonDecodeError` from the v3 codec; unknown nodeType propagates `JsonDecodeError` with the offending pointer; `encode` throws `JsonCodecV4NotYetImplementedError` until §17.106) — §17.105 | `feature/17.105-json-codec-v4` | 1124 → 1131 _(unit, +7 net combined-assertion tests — would have been +10 but 3 collapsed under combined-assertion compression)_ + 116 _(e2e, unchanged)_ | DONE | **Eighteenth execution strand of the §17.94 v5 round-7 plan** and the **first heavy Phase D strand**. **Composition rather than re-implementation** was the operator-chosen path (decision 2026-05-16, ranked the three implementation strategies offered: compose, standalone, compose-plus-heavy-fixtures; operator picked the latter): the §17.81 bridge already encodes every v3 → v4 translation rule the §17.94 plan codified, so re-implementing them inside a standalone v4-native parser would duplicate ~250 lines of tested logic and create two seams to keep in sync across future migrations. Composition keeps the codec under 100 lines AND inherits every behavioural fix the bridge ships in subsequent strands automatically — the "first-load translation" claim from the §17.94 plan row is exactly the bridge's contract. **Why a factory function (not a class)**: the codec needs `clock: Clock` + optional `V3ToV4Options.overrides` injected at construction; a factory returning a plain object satisfies the `TreeCodecV4` port shape with no class overhead and matches the §17.102 / §17.103 factory convention. **Lifetime caveat**: the v3 `jsonCodec` retires at §17.113 (Phase F deletions). At that point this composition will need to either inline the v3 wire parser (the wire shape itself doesn't change — only the v3 NODE construction layer goes away) or be retired alongside the v3 codec depending on whether v4-native wire shapes have landed by then. Both paths are acceptable; the choice belongs to the Phase F strand. Documented in the codec's docblock so the Phase F strand has the full context. **Encode is explicitly deferred to §17.106**: the structurally-required `encode` method throws `JsonCodecV4NotYetImplementedError` (a typed error class exported alongside the factory) rather than being omitted from the returned object — this keeps the `TreeCodecV4` port satisfied at the type level AND surfaces a clean operator-facing failure message if any §17.106-pre code path calls `encode` accidentally. **+7 net unit tests** (1124 → 1131 — 10 new tests, 3 absorbed by combined-assertion compression). 116 e2e unchanged. **Bundle delta**: 0 — `jsonCodecV4` is tree-shaken (`main.ts` doesn't import it; reachability arrives at §17.107 LocalStorage adapter + §17.110 cutover). Bundle stays 265.06 KB / 71.92 KB gzip. **Strand sizing on disk**: 70 (codec) + 165 (tests) = 235 raw .ts lines (+ ~30 SPEC delta, − 1 sonar-version line). After the §17.110 cutover, every localStorage load that today flows v3 codec → v3 nodes → §17.81 bridge → v4 nodes will instead flow `jsonCodecV4.decode` end-to-end (same call sequence under the hood, just collapsed behind the v4 port). **Branch cut off master** (NOT stacked on §17.108 — already merged). **Next strand**: per the §17.94 plan, **§17.106** — `jsonCodecV4` encode path (the round-trip half; ~250 lines projected, may compose v4 → v3-shape encoder OR introduce a v4-native wire shape — design decision pending at strand start). Alternative: **§17.107** — `LocalStorageBoardCollectionRepositoryV4` (the smaller adjacent strand; can consume `jsonCodecV4.decode` immediately, encode-side persistence stays on v3 until §17.106). |

seeds history via `addValue` on each value node so the §17.95 strategy chassis has data to dispatch through; populates a `CardRegistry` with one `BusinessScoreCardV4` entry on the `sales` BSN so the §17.100.5 card-based unit precedence is exercised (the BSN's `unit` slot is set to a sentinel `"BSN-fallback"` string that the card's `Unit.percent()` overrides at read time); deterministic timestamps frozen via `Timestamp.of(new Date("2026-04-..."))` so the fixture round-trips identically across runs); NEW `src/test/unit/adapters/sampleDataV4.test.ts` (~50 raw lines, 3 combined-assertion tests: **kind shape** (all 5 kinds reachable via `tree.findById` + card sidecar present); `ComputedNode` `CountComputation` returns `2` over its enabled children); **mapper round-trip** (the produced tree maps cleanly through `viewModelMapperV4` — `mapFocusedToViewModelV4(root, ...)` produces a `TextNode` center and `[ComputedBusinessScoreNode, ComputedNode]` children; drilling into `health` produces a `BusinessScoreCardNode` slot whose `value.unit` reads `"%"` from the card sidecar, NOT the BSN's `"BSN-fallback"` slot — the §17.100.5 precedence end-to-end)) — §17.108 | `feature/17.108-sample-data-v4` | 1121 → 1124 _(unit, +3 net combined-assertion tests)_ + 116 _(e2e, unchanged)_ | DONE | **Seventeenth execution strand of the §17.94 v5 round-7 plan** and the **first Phase D strand**. | **v5 round-7 Phase C.6 follow-on — `viewModelMapperV4` extension for `ComputedNode<T>` + `ComputedBusinessScoreNode<T>`** (closes the §17.104 deferred sub-task that landed the visual layer without the producer side; the §17.104 docblock explicitly flagged "mapper updates DEFERRED" + "`ComputedNode<T>` would hit the `unsupported v4 Node subclass` throw branch today"). **One existing production file modified + one existing test file modified; zero new files; zero deletions**: MODIFIED `src/adapters/ui/views/viewModelMapperV4.ts` (+91 raw lines net — extends the §17.91 type-switch from 2 to 4 inhabitants (`TextNodeV4` / **`ComputedBusinessScoreNode`** / **`ComputedNode`** / `RangedValueNode` / throw, in that exact order — CBSN MUST precede `RangedValueNode` because it's a grandchild subclass, otherwise the §17.91 BSC branch would shadow the new CBSC branch; ComputedNode's order is free since it inherits from `HistorizableValueNode` not `RangedValueNode`); adds `mapComputedNode` + `mapComputedBusinessScoreNode` helpers + `computedValueVM` value-resolver helper that dispatches through `node.getValue()` (the §17.97 cached-strategy entry point) wrapped in a try/catch on `EmptyChildrenError` (the §17.95 strategy chassis's structural-empty signal); the catch surfaces a structured `{ kind: "empty", reason }` VM with the error's human message so the view never paints a meaningless glyph; non-finite numeric results (NaN / ±Infinity from `AverageComputation` overflow) ALSO surface as empty for the same reason; adds `AVAILABLE_KIND_NAMES` frozen constant materialising `ComputationKind.ALL.map((k) => k.name)` as the canonical `ComputationKindName[]` array every Computed* VM advertises to the §17.104 kind-switch dropdown (single source of truth, no per-call allocation); refactors `mapBusinessScoreObjectiveV4`'s signature from `(node, value: BusinessScoreCardValueViewModel, unit, options)` to `(node, currentNumber: number \| null, isRecordedValue: boolean, unit, options)` so the §17.40 objective math is polymorphic across the v3-shape BSC value VM AND the §17.104 `ComputedValueViewModel` — the BSC site computes `numericValueOf(value)` + `value.kind === "recordedValue"` at call time and forwards, the CBSN site forwards its strategy-applied number + `false` because computed values never qualify as recorded per §17.94 D5 — same trend-arrow + warning-glyph suppression v3 carried for non-recorded BSC value shapes); MODIFIED `src/test/unit/adapters/ui/views/viewModelMapperV4.test.ts` (+70 raw lines — adds 4 combined-assertion tests under a new `§17.104b` describe block: **ComputedNode happy path** (strategy-applied numeric value + computationKind name + availableKinds list); **ComputedNode empty + setComputationKind round-trip** (empty VM carrying the EmptyChildrenError reason + flipping the strategy via `setComputationKind` reflects on the next map call); **CBSN happy path** (strategy value + BSC-style objective row with valueColor gradient + corner timestamp + dateColor + trend arrow stays null per the §17.94 D5 audit-only contract); **CBSN unit via §17.100.5 card sidecar** (precedence preserved across the new VM shape — card-supplied unit wins over the BSN slot for both `value.unit` and `objective.unit`)) — §17.104b | `feature/17.104b-mapper-extension` | 1117 → 1121 _(unit, +4 net combined-assertion tests)_ + 116 _(e2e, unchanged)_ | DONE | **Sixteenth execution strand of the §17.94 v5 round-7 plan** and the **first §17.104 follow-on**. **Read-path UI surface now end-to-end reachable**: pre-§17.104b the dispatcher had `<computed-card>` + `<computed-business-score-card>` registered (§17.104), but no producer path emitted `ComputedNodeViewModel` / `ComputedBusinessScoreNodeViewModel`; §17.104b closes that gap so any `ComputedNode<T>` / `ComputedBusinessScoreNode<T>` in the tree — including every v3 BSC with `computed: true` substituted by the §17.99c v3-bridge — now resolves to the §17.104 visual layer instead of throwing (ComputedNode) or being silently downcast to a v3-shape BSC VM (CBSN, which today lands in the `RangedValueNode` branch and renders as a `BusinessScoreCardNode` VM). **`node.getValue()` rather than `computedValueV4`**: the existing CBSN fall-through ran `computedValueV4` which executes a structural weighted-mean regardless of the CBSN's computation strategy — so a `MinComputation`-configured CBSN would still render the weighted mean of its eligible children, ignoring the operator's chosen strategy. §17.104b's `computedValueVM` calls the node's `getValue()` directly so the cached strategy (§17.95 `ComputationCache<T>`) drives the rendered number. For the default `WeightedAverageComputation` the two paths produce identical numbers on every kiosk data shape (the eligible-children weighted mean IS the structural rule), so legacy boards see no change; for every other strategy the rendered number now reflects the operator's choice. **Trend-arrow + warning suppression preserved**: the §17.41 trend arrow + §17.44 warning glyph were always gated on `value.kind === "recordedValue"` because a computed value has no per-point history to project a rate from; §17.104b extends that gate to the new CBSC VM by passing `isRecordedValue: false` to the refactored objective mapper — same observable behaviour the BSC computed-mean branch already had, just polymorphic across the new VM shape. **Unit precedence via §17.100.5 cards preserved**: `resolveUnit(node, options)` reads the card sidecar first then falls back to `BusinessScoreNode.unit`; the new CBSN mapper threads that through identically so the §17.100.5 card-based unit-lift works for CBSC tiles without any further wiring. **Combined-assertion test design** (4 tests vs ~10 granular tests): each test pins multiple invariants per setup per the §17.95 + §17.102 + §17.103 + §17.104 precedent. **+4 net unit tests** (1117 → 1121). 116 e2e unchanged. **Bundle delta**: 264.68 → 265.06 KB raw (+0.38 KB), 71.84 → 71.92 KB gzip (+0.08 KB) — minimal because the mapper module was already production-reachable; the new helpers add inline functions but the registry side stays unchanged. **Strand sizing on disk**: ~91 (mapper) + ~70 (tests) = 161 raw .ts lines (+ ~25 SPEC delta, − 1 sonar-version line). Sonar `new_lines` projected ~170 — comfortable headroom under the 300 ceiling, no trim cycle anticipated. **`sonar.projectVersion` bump `0.2.28 → 0.2.29`** + this ~50-line history docblock recording the sixteenth §17.94 execution strand. **§17.72 corrected procedure followed** — content COMMITTED FIRST on `feature/17.104b-mapper-extension`, then `npm run sonar:gate` runs on the committed tip. **What this strand does NOT do**: doesn't wire the `computation-kind-change` event from §17.104's dropdowns to `EditNodeServiceV4.setComputationKind` (the §17.110 Phase E cutover owns the event → service binding; today the dropdowns dispatch with no listener); doesn't add the `ComputationKindName → ComputationKind` singleton bridge helper (also §17.110 — sits at the shell-event-handler boundary, not the mapper); doesn't touch the §17.110 `main.ts` integration (services + UI wiring stay v3-routed until cutover); doesn't extend the existing 2 Lit components (§17.104's tags are unchanged); doesn't migrate operator-facing kind names from `WEIGHTED_AVERAGE` to `Weighted average` (display-name layer is a future polish strand); doesn't change the v3 `viewModelMapper` (stays live for v3 boards traversing the §17.88 adapter). **What this strand surfaces for the operator**: a v3 BSC with `computed: true` (lifted to `ComputedBusinessScoreNode<T>` by the §17.99c bridge) now renders as a §17.104 `<computed-business-score-card>` tile (Σ aggregation badge + numeric value + kind-switch dropdown + BSC-style objective row + corner timestamp) — same visual surface the §17.104 strand designed, now actually reachable. The dropdown still doesn't propagate the operator's selection (the `computation-kind-change` event has no listener at §17.110-pre time), but the visual surface is now end-to-end producible. **Branch cut off master** (NOT stacked on §17.104 — already merged). **Phase C UI surface now end-to-end producible** for both round-7 Computed* kinds. **Next strand**: per the §17.94 plan, **Phase D §17.105** — `jsonCodecV4` decode path (highest-leverage unblock for the §17.107 LocalStorage adapter and §17.110 cutover). |
| **v5 round-7 Phase C.6 — `ComputedCard<T>` + `ComputedBusinessScoreCard<T>` Lit visual layer** (round-7 UI surface for the §17.97 `ComputedNode<T>` + §17.98 `ComputedBusinessScoreNode<T>` kinds; closes the §17.94 §17.104 plan row). **One new production module + one new test file; two existing files modified; zero deletions**: NEW `src/adapters/ui/views/ComputedNode/ComputedCards.ts` (~140 raw lines incl. docblock — two `@customElement`s in one module: `<computed-card>` for ComputedNode tiles (title + Σ aggregation badge + value + kind-switching `<select>`) and `<computed-business-score-card>` for ComputedBusinessScoreNode tiles (CBSC variant adds BSC-style objective row + corner timestamp inherited from `BusinessScoreNode`'s objective surface); shared `sharedStyles` CSS module + 4 inline render helpers (`renderKindDropdown`, `renderComputedValue`, `renderObjectiveRow`, `renderTrend`, `formatDateIso`); both components dispatch a `bubbles+composed` `computation-kind-change` custom event on dropdown change with `{ nodeId, newKind }` payload — the operator-facing surface that lands the §17.94 plan's "kind-switching dropdown that hits `setComputationKind`" deliverable, ready to be wired to `EditNodeServiceV4.editFields(node, { kind: "Computed"|"ComputedBusinessScore", computationKind })` at the §17.110 Phase E cutover); NEW `src/test/unit/adapters/ui/views/ComputedNode/ComputedCards.test.ts` (~165 raw lines, 6 combined-assertion tests across 2 describe blocks pinning: title + Σ badge + numeric value + dropdown preselect, empty-reason rendering + unit-slot suppression, event-dispatch round-trip for `<computed-card>`; full surface (title + Σ + value + objective + timestamp + dropdown) + trend-arrow rendering + event-dispatch for `<computed-business-score-card>`); MODIFIED `src/adapters/ui/views/NodeViewModel.ts` (+60 raw lines — extends `NodeKind` union from 2 to 4 inhabitants (`+"ComputedNode"|"ComputedBusinessScoreNode"`), adds `ComputationKindName` string union (Lit-friendly mirror of the §17.95 `ComputationKind` enum names — six inhabitants per §17.94 D2), adds `ComputedValueViewModel` discriminated union (`numeric|empty` — `numeric` carries the strategy-applied number + unit, `empty` carries the EmptyChildrenError / "no contributing children" reason string baked by the mapper), adds `ComputedNodeViewModel` (id + title + value + computationKind + availableKinds) + `ComputedBusinessScoreNodeViewModel` (CBSN extends with description + dateIso + dateColor + objective — same BSC-style surface as `BusinessScoreCardNodeViewModel` modulo the value-shape swap), extends the top-level `NodeViewModel` union accordingly); MODIFIED `src/adapters/ui/views/index.ts` (+18 raw lines — side-effect imports the new module, registers two new `(kind, role) → tag` entries in `nodeViewRegistry`: `ComputedNode → computed-card` and `ComputedBusinessScoreNode → computed-business-score-card`; both asParent + asChild collapse to the same tag because the role distinction is irrelevant for computed tiles (the kind-switching dropdown + Σ badge are the operator-facing surface, and the value-area is identical in both); barrel re-exports the new types + the `COMPUTATION_KIND_CHANGE_EVENT` constant + `ComputationKindChangeDetail` payload type) — §17.104 | `feature/17.104-computed-cards` | 1111 → 1117 _(unit, +6 net combined-assertion tests)_ + 116 _(e2e, unchanged)_ | DONE | **Fifteenth execution strand of the §17.94 v5 round-7 plan** and the **first Phase C UI strand** (Phase C.1a / C.1b / C.5 / C.2a / C.2b / C.3 / C.4 / **C.6** — Phase C is now closed end-to-end including the deferred UI deliverable). First strand to publish operator-facing surface for the round-7 Computed* node kinds. **Inlined renderers rather than reusing §17.40's `valueTemplate`**: considered reusing `renderTargetRow` / `renderTrendArrow` from `BusinessScoreCardNode/valueTemplate.ts` but they're typed against `BusinessScoreCardNodeViewModel` (whose `value` is the v3 `computedMean | recordedValue | childrenCount` union, not the §17.104 `numeric | empty` `ComputedValueViewModel`); re-typing the §17.40 helpers polymorphically would touch v3 code paths beyond §17.104's scope, so 2 small inline renderers (~10 lines each) kept the strand additive. The trend-arrow glyph table (`↑↗→↘↓`) duplicates §17.40's `TREND_ARROW_GLYPHS` (5 entries × 1 line each — below Sonar's CPD threshold per the §17.98 precedent's `sonar.cpd.exclusions` rationale, and inlining keeps the UI module self-contained). **Mapper updates DEFERRED**: the §17.91 `viewModelMapperV4.ts` doesn't produce the new VM kinds yet — `ComputedNode<T>` would hit the `unsupported v4 Node subclass` throw branch today (no production path constructs ComputedNodes; the §17.99c v3-bridge produces CBSNs via type-substitution but the mapper currently lands them in the `RangedValueNode` branch as `BusinessScoreCardNode` VMs). The mapper extension is a follow-on strand (provisional §17.104b or rolled into §17.110 Phase E cutover); §17.104 lands the visual surface so the wiring strand can produce VMs against a ready-to-receive view layer. **Both tiles use the same tag for both roles**: pre-§17.104 every kind had separate `asParent` + `asChild` tags because the role distinction carried density/typography differences (parent-strip tiles render bigger than treemap-tile children). Computed tiles' value-area is identical in both roles (Σ badge + value + dropdown — the role distinction would only add visual noise without semantic benefit), so the registry entry collapses both roles onto the same tag. This is the first kind to register the same tag for both roles. **Combined-assertion test design**: 6 tests pin all material invariants — render shape (title + Σ + value + dropdown for both kinds), value-branch dispatch (numeric vs empty), event-dispatch contract (`bubbles+composed`, `{ nodeId, newKind }` detail), variant-specific surface (objective row + timestamp + trend arrow for CBSC); coverage equivalence preserved at the assertion level. **+6 net unit tests** (1111 → 1117). 116 e2e unchanged. **Bundle delta**: 261.06 → 264.68 KB raw (+3.62 KB), 71.13 → 71.84 KB gzip (+0.71 KB), 117 → 118 modules (+1 — `ComputedCards.ts` becomes production-reachable via the side-effect import in the views barrel; ComputationKind / ComputedNode / ComputedBusinessScoreNode classes themselves remain tree-shaken until a write-side strand wires them through `main.ts`). First round-7 strand to ship a NEW production module (vs. extending existing modules; §17.99c was deletion-balanced, §17.100.5 added 2 modules via the cards walk but lift was the focus). **Strand sizing on disk**: 140 (component) + 60 (VM types) + 18 (barrel) + 165 (tests) = 383 raw .ts lines + ~50 SPEC delta − 1 sonar-version line. Sonar `new_lines` projected ~265 (the test file's combined-assertion shape kept lines tight; component module compressed via inlined helpers + shared CSS module) — fits the 300 ceiling with comfortable headroom. **`sonar.projectVersion` bump `0.2.27 → 0.2.28`** + this ~70-line history docblock recording the fifteenth §17.94 execution strand. **§17.72 corrected procedure followed** — content COMMITTED FIRST on `feature/17.104-computed-cards`, then `npm run sonar:gate` runs on the committed tip. **§17.94 plan row decisions preserved**: the "kind-switching dropdown that hits `setComputationKind`" deliverable is now wire-ready — the `computation-kind-change` event carries the new `ComputationKindName` payload that `EditNodeServiceV4.editFields(node, { kind: "Computed", computationKind: ComputationKind.<NAME> })` accepts (modulo the string → ComputationKind singleton lift, a 6-line switch the shell wiring at §17.110 will own). The Σ aggregation badge mirrors the §17.40 BSC "computedMean" branch's Σ pin (now present on every computed value branch — the previous BSC-only convention extends to the new node kinds). **§17.94 §17.104 row CLOSED**: the visual layer for Computed* kinds is on disk, registered with the dispatcher, and tested. Phase C of the §17.94 plan is now COMPLETE end-to-end across all six plan rows: §17.99 BSCv4 wrapper (a/b/c), §17.100 AddChildServiceV4 (a/b), §17.100.5 unit-lift, §17.101 EditNodeServiceV4 (a/b), §17.102 BoardCollectionServiceV4, §17.103 ImportExportServiceV4, **§17.104 Computed* visual layer**. **What this strand does NOT do**: doesn't extend `viewModelMapperV4` to produce `ComputedNodeViewModel` / `ComputedBusinessScoreNodeViewModel` (the mapper still throws on `ComputedNode<T>` and lands `ComputedBusinessScoreNode<T>` in the BSC branch — both behaviours are unchanged from pre-§17.104; mapper extension is a follow-on strand); doesn't wire the `computation-kind-change` event to any service (the event dispatches with no listener today — wiring lands at §17.110 cutover or a dedicated wiring strand); doesn't add a `ComputationKindName` ↔ `ComputationKind` singleton bridge helper (the §17.110 wiring strand owns that translation since it sits at the shell-event-handler boundary); doesn't touch the existing 2 Lit components (`<text-node-*>`, `<business-score-card-*>` — strictly additive); doesn't add operator-facing kind-naming labels (the dropdown shows raw enum names like `WEIGHTED_AVERAGE` rather than `Weighted average`; localisation + display-name layer is a future polish strand). **What this strand surfaces for the operator**: nothing immediately user-visible — the mapper doesn't produce the new VM kinds yet, so the `<computed-card>` / `<computed-business-score-card>` tags are unreachable through the dispatcher today. After the follow-on mapper extension strand, an operator will see a Σ-badged tile with the auto-derived number + a kind dropdown for any `ComputedNode<T>` / `ComputedBusinessScoreNode<T>` in their tree; changing the dropdown will (at §17.110 cutover) update the computation strategy and re-roll the displayed value through the §17.95 strategy chassis. **Branch cut off master** (NOT stacked on §17.103 — already merged). **Next strand**: **Phase D §17.105** — `jsonCodecV4` decode path (highest-leverage unblock for the §17.107 LocalStorage adapter and §17.110 cutover; the v3 codec doesn't speak round-7 kinds, so v4 nodes constructed via §17.100b survive in-memory but won't round-trip through persistence without §17.105 lifting decode to recognise the new kind discriminants). Alternative: the §17.104 mapper extension (the natural sibling strand closing the read-path UI surface) — but mapper extension without persistence is read-only forever, so codec-first ordering preserves the §17.110 cutover sequence integrity. |
| **v5 round-7 Phase C.4 — `ImportExportServiceV4` + `TreeCodecV4` port** (v4 successor to v3's §17.32 / §13.2 `ImportExportService` — same validate-before-replace contract, same callable-decoupling from the board collection, just typed against the §17.79 `Tree` container instead of v3's `TreeNode<unknown>` aggregate). **Two new production files + one new test file; zero existing files modified**: NEW `src/application/ports/TreeCodecV4.ts` (~22 raw lines incl. docblock — `TreeCodecV4` interface with `encode(tree: Tree): string` + `decode(text: string): Tree`; same throw/no-throw contract as v3 so the service's validate-first ordering survives byte-for-byte); NEW `src/application/ImportExportServiceV4.ts` (~41 raw lines, 2 public methods + 1 private static `errorReason` helper; constructor takes a `TreeCodecV4` + `getCurrentTree` + `replaceCurrentTree` callables; `exportCurrentTree` returns the codec's encoding of the current tree; `importIntoCurrentBoard` decodes-then-replaces with a typed throw catching pattern surfacing as `{ ok: false, reason }`; same as v3's body modulo the type substitution); NEW `src/test/unit/application/ImportExportServiceV4.test.ts` (~107 raw lines, 4 combined-assertion tests compressing v3's 7-test surface: **exportCurrentTree** covers happy path + no-caching (re-reads on every call) in one test; **importIntoCurrentBoard happy path + decode-before-replace ordering** in one test (uses a vi.fn that records `["decode", "replace"]` event ordering); **importIntoCurrentBoard failure modes** combining malformed JSON + unknown id (decoder throws) + replace-throw all into one test pinning each `{ ok: false, reason }` surface and verifying the current tree stays untouched in every case; **round-trip** pin (export then import preserves the tree reference) in one test) — §17.103 | `feature/17.103-import-export-service-v4` | 1107 → 1111 _(unit, +4 net combined-assertion tests)_ + 116 _(e2e, unchanged)_ | DONE | **Fourteenth execution strand of the §17.94 v5 round-7 plan** and the **seventh Phase C strand** (Phase C.1a / C.1b / C.5 / C.2a / C.2b / C.3 / **C.4**). **Closes Phase C entirely** — every v3 application service now has a parallel-additive v4 successor (`AddChildServiceV4` §17.100a/§17.100b; `EditNodeServiceV4` §17.101a/§17.101b; `BoardCollectionServiceV4` §17.102; `ImportExportServiceV4` §17.103); the `BSCv4` wrapper unit-lift (§17.99 / §17.100.5) and the §17.99a `disabled` foundation round out Phase C's cleanup deliverables. The remaining Phase C plan-row entry (`§17.104 ComputedCard<T>` visual layer) is deferred to a follow-on strand alongside the §17.110 cutover. **Behavioural clone verbatim**: the v4 service body is structurally identical to v3 modulo the `Tree` substitution; the validate-before-replace contract preserves byte-for-byte (decode FIRST, throw on malformed/structurally-invalid input, only on a successful decode does the service call `replaceCurrentTree`; a failing decode leaves the current tree untouched); the callable-decoupling pattern stays (the service knows nothing about boards / ids / persistence layout — it consumes `getCurrentTree` + `replaceCurrentTree` callables wired by the composition root). **Combined-assertion test design** compressed v3's 7-test surface (3 describes × 2-3 tests each) to 4 tests pinning the same invariants per setup; coverage equivalence preserved at the assertion level. **+4 net unit tests** (1107 → 1111). 116 e2e unchanged. **Bundle delta**: **0** — `ImportExportServiceV4` + `TreeCodecV4` are both tree-shaken (`main.ts` doesn't import them; reachability arrives at §17.110 when the composition root cuts over). Bundle stays 261.06 KB / 71.13 KB gzip / 117 modules — identical to §17.102 / §17.101b / §17.101a / §17.100.5. **Strand sizing on disk**: 41 (service) + 22 (port) + 107 (tests) = 170 raw .ts lines (+ ~30 SPEC delta, − 1 sonar-version line). Sonar `new_lines` projected ~165 — comfortable headroom under the 300 ceiling on a single pass (no trim cycle anticipated, in contrast to §17.101a/§17.102 which both hit the boundary). **`sonar.projectVersion` bump `0.2.26 → 0.2.27`** + this ~50-line history docblock recording the fourteenth §17.94 execution strand. **§17.72 corrected procedure followed** — content COMMITTED FIRST on `feature/17.103-import-export-service-v4`, then `npm run sonar:gate` runs on the committed tip. **§17.94 plan row decisions preserved**: the "also mostly type-only" claim landed verbatim — zero v4-semantic-aware code paths in the new service or port, just the parameterised `Tree` substitution. **What this strand does NOT do**: doesn't wire `ImportExportServiceV4` into `main.ts` (§17.110 Phase E cutover); doesn't add a concrete `jsonCodecV4` adapter implementing `TreeCodecV4` (§17.105 decode + §17.106 encode; the v4 codec is Phase D's first deliverable); doesn't migrate the v3 codec adapter (stays bound to the v3 `TreeCodec` port); doesn't touch the v3 `ImportExportService` (stays live verbatim; retires at the §17.111 Phase F deletion strand); doesn't bridge v3 ↔ v4 wire format (each codec speaks its own dialect; the §17.81 / §17.88 v3-bridge runs at the codec-load boundary, not at the import-flow boundary, so a v3 wire payload imported through the v4 service would fail decode at §17.105 unless the v4 codec is taught to absorb the v3 shape — that's a §17.105 question, not §17.103's). **What this strand surfaces for the operator**: nothing immediately — the service is unreachable from `main.ts`. After the §17.110 cutover, the Export and Import buttons (today wired through the v3 service) will dispatch through `ImportExportServiceV4` against the v4 codec adapter; behaviour will be observationally identical because the v4 service is a behavioural clone. **Branch cut off master** (NOT stacked on §17.102 — already merged). **Phase C closed**. **Next strand**: per the §17.94 plan, **§17.104** — `ComputedCard<T>` + `ComputedBusinessScoreCard<T>` visual-layer Lit components (Phase C's remaining UI deliverable); OR jump to **Phase D §17.105** — `jsonCodecV4` decode path which unblocks the §17.107 LocalStorage adapter and the §17.110 cutover. The latter has higher leverage for the cutover sequence (codec migration is the largest remaining blocker); the former delivers user-visible UI surface for the round-7 kinds. Operator decision deferred to the next session. |
| **v5 round-7 Phase C.3 — `BoardCollectionServiceV4` + `BoardCollectionRepositoryV4` port** (the v4 successor to v3's §17.31 `BoardCollectionService` — same behaviour, different tree type). Treats trees as opaque per the §17.102 plan row: the service never reads the v4 node graph, just orchestrates identity + persistence on a `Tree` (the §17.79 container holding root + the §17.100.5 `cards` sidecar) instead of v3's `TreeNode<unknown>` aggregate. **Two new production files + one new test file; zero existing files modified**: NEW `src/application/ports/BoardCollectionRepositoryV4.ts` (~40 raw lines incl. docblocks — `BoardV4 { id, name, tree: Tree }` + `BoardCollectionSnapshotV4 { boards, currentBoardId }` + `BoardCollectionRepositoryV4` interface; parallel-additive to v3's §17.31 port — v3 stays live in `main.ts` until §17.110 Phase E cutover); NEW `src/application/BoardCollectionServiceV4.ts` (~128 raw lines, 7 methods + private persist + Outcome type alias; all 7 v3 methods clone verbatim with `Tree` substituted for `TreeNode<unknown>` — `create` / `list` / `getCurrentBoardId` / `getCurrentBoard` / `switchTo` / `rename` / `updateSettings` / `createBoard` / `replaceCurrentTree` / `deleteBoard`); NEW `src/test/unit/application/BoardCollectionServiceV4.test.ts` (~121 raw lines, 5 combined-assertion tests aggressively merging the v3's 25-test surface: **create + load + opaque-tree exposure**; **switchTo (happy + same-id no-op + unknown-id)**; **rename + updateSettings (happy + trim + empty-name + unknown-id)**; **createBoard (happy + trim + empty-name + auto-current + idGen)**; **replaceCurrentTree + deleteBoard (opaque-replace + last-remaining-guard + current-fallback)**) — §17.102 | `feature/17.102-board-collection-service-v4` | 1102 → 1107 _(unit, +5 net combined-assertion tests)_ + 116 _(e2e, unchanged)_ | DONE | **Thirteenth execution strand of the §17.94 v5 round-7 plan** and the **sixth Phase C strand** (Phase C.1a / C.1b / C.5 / C.2a / C.2b / **C.3**). First Phase C strand to introduce a *fully cloned* successor service rather than extending an existing v4 file — the v3 service is type-locked to `TreeNode<unknown>`, can't be made polymorphic without breaking its existing consumers (the §17.93 cutover-time `main.ts` still imports the v3 service end-to-end). **Service shape preserved byte-for-byte**: same constructor invariants (private constructor + async `create` factory loads from the port), same method signatures (modulo the `Tree` substitution), same `Outcome` shape (`{ ok: true, board?: BoardV4 } | { ok: false, reason: string }` — represented as a conditional type alias to avoid the verbose dual-shape union duplication across 7 method signatures), same private `persist()` helper round-tripping the entire snapshot through the repo per call (atomic). **The `Tree` type is genuinely opaque to the service**: zero reads on `tree.root` / `tree.findById` / `tree.nodes()` / `tree.cards` — the service only ever passes the reference through (`createBoard(name, tree)` stores it; `replaceCurrentTree(tree)` swaps it; `list()` / `getCurrentBoard()` return it). This is the §17.102 plan row's "mostly type-only changes" claim concretised: no v4-semantic-aware code paths anywhere in the new service, just the parameterised tree type. **Combined-assertion test design**: v3's 25-test granular suite (5 describes × ~5 tests each) compresses to 5 combined-assertion tests pinning **the same** invariants (every method's happy path + every method's failure paths + the persistence call count where it matters); coverage equivalence preserved at the assertion level even though test count drops by 20. This is the §17.95 pattern reused — pin multiple invariants per setup, scaled aggressively because the service has minimal branching (mostly look-up + early-return + mutate-and-persist). +5 net unit tests (1102 → 1107). 116 e2e unchanged. **Bundle delta**: **0** — `BoardCollectionServiceV4` + `BoardCollectionRepositoryV4` are both tree-shaken (`main.ts` doesn't import them; reachability arrives at §17.110 when the composition root cuts over). Bundle stays 261.06 KB / 71.13 KB gzip / 117 modules — identical to §17.101b / §17.101a / §17.100.5. **Strand sizing on disk**: 128 (service) + 40 (port) + 121 (tests) = 289 raw .ts lines (+ ~30 SPEC delta, − 1 sonar-version line). Sonar `new_lines` projected ~275 — fits the 300 ceiling with comfortable headroom (the test file's combined-assertion shape kept lines tight). **`sonar.projectVersion` bump `0.2.25 → 0.2.26`** + this ~50-line history docblock recording the thirteenth §17.94 execution strand. **§17.72 corrected procedure followed** — content COMMITTED FIRST on `feature/17.102-board-collection-service-v4`, then `npm run sonar:gate` runs on the committed tip. **§17.94 plan row decisions preserved**: the "mostly type-only changes since the service treats trees as opaque" claim landed verbatim — zero v4-semantic-aware code paths in the new service, just the parameterised `Tree` substitution. The service's 7-method surface is the same as v3's; future write-path strands can subclass or compose it without re-implementation. **What this strand does NOT do**: doesn't wire `BoardCollectionServiceV4` into `main.ts` (§17.110 Phase E cutover); doesn't add a v4 LocalStorage adapter implementing `BoardCollectionRepositoryV4` (§17.107 — depends on the codec migration §17.105 + §17.106 first); doesn't migrate the v3 LocalStorage adapter (stays bound to the v3 port); doesn't migrate the `ImportExportService` (§17.103 — next strand, also type-only); doesn't touch the v3 `BoardCollectionService` (stays live verbatim; retires at the §17.111 Phase F deletion strand); doesn't bridge v3 ↔ v4 boards (the v3-bridge at §17.81 / §17.88 builds v4 nodes from v3 nodes, but a v3 `Board` → v4 `BoardV4` lift would mean adapting the entire tree — `v4TreeFromV3Root` already does that and the §17.110 cutover will use it at the snapshot-load boundary). **What this strand surfaces for the operator**: nothing immediately — the service is unreachable from `main.ts`. After the §17.110 cutover, the operator's board-management UI (boards panel modal + settings modal + import flow) will be backed by `BoardCollectionServiceV4` instead of `BoardCollectionService`; behaviour will be observationally identical because the v4 service is a behavioural clone. **Branch cut off master** (NOT stacked on §17.101b — already merged). **Next strand**: per the §17.94 plan, **§17.103** — `ImportExportServiceV4` (same "mostly type-only" shape as §17.102; the v3 `ImportExportService.replaceCurrentTree` callable gains a v4 codec wire-up at §17.105+, but §17.103 keeps the service body type-substituted and depends-on the v4 board service from §17.102). ~80 lines provisional. |
| **v5 round-7 Phase C.2b — `EditNodeServiceV4` extended with the three round-7 leaf kinds** (`StrictRange` + `Computed` + `ComputedBusinessScore`) + `setComputationKind(ComputationKind)` setter for the two Computed* kinds + `appendValue` support for `StrictRangeNode` (with `OutOfRangeError` surfacing as `{ ok: false }` for out-of-range values) + audit-only refusal of `appendValue` on Computed* kinds (their `addValue` throws `ComputationOverrideError` per §17.94 D5, which the service catches and surfaces as `{ ok: false, reason }`). Second half of the §17.101 strand, picking up after §17.101a landed the skeleton + v3-compat 2 kinds (TextNode + BusinessScore) + `appendValue`. First write-side strand to mutate `ComputedNode<T>` / `ComputedBusinessScoreNode<T>`'s `computationKind` from operator-facing payloads. Adds three new payload union members: **`StrictRange`** (common edits only — title + weight + description + disabled; no objective; no unit; range is structural and not edit-time mutable per the §17.94 plan); **`Computed`** (common + `computationKind?: ComputationKind`); **`ComputedBusinessScore`** (common + BSEdit (unit + objective) + ComputedEdit (computationKind)). **Description moves from BusinessScore-specific to CommonEdit** because every v4 value node extends `ValueNode<T>` which carries `setDescription` — TextNode payloads now also accept a `description?` field (additive change at §17.101b). **Two extracted helper structs** — `BSEdit` and `ComputedEdit` — let multiple payload variants reuse the same field-shape definitions cleanly (`ComputedBusinessScore` intersects `CommonEdit & BSEdit & ComputedEdit`). **`kindMatches` flips to exact-class equality** (`node.constructor === expectedClass` via a new `classFor(kind)` switch) — this is critical because `ComputedBusinessScoreNode` extends `BusinessScoreNode`, so a pure `instanceof` check would let a "BusinessScore" payload land on a CBSN node (which would morph between kinds, violating the v3-inherited invariant). The exact-class check enforces "kind matches runtime class **exactly**, not transitively". Test pins this contract directly: a "BusinessScore" payload against a CBSN node surfaces `{ ok: false }`. **`applyAppendValue` extended** with a `StrictRangeNode` branch (`addValue` + `removeValue` are inherited from `HistorizableValueNode`; the `StrictRange.requireValue` gate fires inside `addValue`'s super chain, so an out-of-range value throws `OutOfRangeError` which the outer try/catch surfaces as `{ ok: false }`); Computed* kinds intentionally NOT added (their `addValue` overrides throw `ComputationOverrideError` per §17.97 / §17.98 — appendValue on a CBSN node would hit the BusinessScoreNode `instanceof` branch and the inherited addValue throws, naturally surfacing `{ ok: false }`; appendValue on a ComputedNode hits the `unsupported` fallthrough because `ComputedNode<T>` extends `HistorizableValueNode<T>` directly, not `BusinessScoreNode`). + tests — §17.101b | `feature/17.101b-strictrange-computed-cbsn-edit` | 1099 → 1102 _(unit, +3 net combined-assertion tests)_ + 116 _(e2e, unchanged)_ | DONE | **Twelfth execution strand of the §17.94 v5 round-7 plan** and the **fifth Phase C strand** (Phase C.1a / C.1b / C.5 / C.2a / **C.2b**). Closes the §17.94 §17.101 plan row — `EditNodeServiceV4` now covers the full 5-kind v4 edit surface, mirroring §17.100b's closure of the AddChild service. **One production file modified; one test file extended; zero new files**: MODIFIED `src/application/EditNodeServiceV4.ts` (+91 raw lines incl. 8 new imports — `Computed` interface + `ComputationKind` enum + `ComputedBusinessScoreNode` / `ComputedNode` / `StrictRangeNode` concrete classes; 3 new payload union members; 2 extracted intersection-helper types `BSEdit` + `ComputedEdit`; description hoisted to `CommonEdit`; `applyCommonEdits` extended with a description branch reading via `ValueNode<T>.getDescription`; new `applyComputedEdits` helper (8 lines); `kindMatches` switches to exact-class via the new `classFor` switch (8 lines); `applyAppendValue` extended with a StrictRangeNode branch via a 2-class disjunction); MODIFIED `src/test/unit/application/EditNodeServiceV4.test.ts` (+77 raw lines — 3 new combined-assertion tests: **StrictRange edit + appendValue** covering common-edit propagation (title + weight + description + disabled) + in-range appendValue + out-of-range rejection with rollback in one test; **Computed edit + setComputationKind + audit-only history** covering setComputationKind flipping the cached strategy (kind getter reads back the new singleton) + appendValue rejection on a Computed node (ComputationOverrideError surfacing as `{ ok: false }`) in one test; **ComputedBusinessScore edit** covering the full 6-field surface (title + description + objective + unit + computationKind + disabled atomicity) + the exact-class kindMatches contract (a "BusinessScore" payload against a CBSN node surfaces `{ ok: false }`) in one test). **+3 net unit tests** (1099 → 1102). 116 e2e unchanged. **Bundle delta**: **0** — `EditNodeServiceV4` stays tree-shaken (`main.ts` doesn't import it yet); the §17.101b additions to the service module don't affect the bundle. Bundle stays 261.06 KB / 71.13 KB gzip / 117 modules — identical to §17.101a / §17.100.5. **Strand sizing on disk**: 91 prod + 77 test = 168 raw .ts lines (+ ~50 SPEC delta, − 1 sonar-version line). Sonar `new_lines` projected ~170 — comfortable headroom under the 300 ceiling on a single pass (no trim cycle anticipated, in contrast to §17.101a which landed at the boundary). **`sonar.projectVersion` bump `0.2.24 → 0.2.25`** + this ~80-line history docblock recording the twelfth §17.94 execution strand. **§17.72 corrected procedure followed** — content COMMITTED FIRST on `feature/17.101b-strictrange-computed-cbsn-edit`, then `npm run sonar:gate` runs on the committed tip. **§17.94 plan row decisions preserved**: the `setComputationKind(kind)` setter for Computed* nodes lands as designed; the §17.99a `setDisabled` already covered Computed* via `ValueNode<T>` inheritance; the §17.100.5 unit-lift to BSCV4 informs the `BSEdit` shape verbatim. The "modal exposes a `computationKind` dropdown for the Computed* kinds" surface is now ready end-to-end (both AddChild via §17.100b and Edit via §17.101b accept `computationKind: ComputationKind`); the modal-side UI lands at a Phase D+ strand. **§17.94 §17.101 row CLOSED**: the 5-kind dispatch is complete on both AddChild (§17.100a + §17.100b) and Edit (§17.101a + §17.101b); Phase C of the §17.94 plan is now write-side complete pending the §17.100.5 unit-card surface migration to §17.101+ for write-side card creation (deferred to a Phase D+ strand alongside the modal extension). **What this strand does NOT do**: doesn't wire `EditNodeServiceV4` into `main.ts` (§17.110 Phase E cutover); doesn't add a `setRange` operator-facing setter (range is structural — the v4 diagram treats range as construction-time-only; if operator-facing range mutation is ever needed, that's a new strand); doesn't add a `setEligibility` setter (eligibility was the §17.99b retired v3 flag, now subsumed by `disabled`); doesn't ship a card-creation path on the edit flow (operators can't introduce a unit on a BSN that didn't have one; the registry lookup either finds an existing card or surfaces `{ ok: false }`); doesn't touch the v3 `EditNodeService` (stays live verbatim; retires at the §17.111 Phase F deletion strand); doesn't add the modal-side `computationKind` dropdown UI (deferred). **What this strand surfaces for the operator**: an operator can now (via a hypothetical future modal extension) edit any of the 5 v4 leaf kinds — StrictRange's edit surface is intentionally minimal (no goals or units, just identity + weight + description + disabled), Computed* nodes can have their aggregation strategy flipped through the §17.95 enum singletons (`ComputationKind.SUM/AVERAGE/MIN/MAX/WEIGHTED_AVERAGE/COUNT`), ComputedBusinessScore can edit objective + unit + computationKind in one atomic transaction. An out-of-range value on a StrictRangeNode's inline value-edit surfaces as `{ ok: false, reason: "..." }` (StrictRange.requireValue's OutOfRangeError); an appendValue attempt on a Computed* node is similarly rejected (audit-only history). **Branch cut off master** (NOT stacked on §17.101a — already merged). **Next strand**: per the §17.94 plan, **§17.102** — first Phase D persistence strand (codec v4 read-side support for the round-7 kinds). The v3 codec doesn't know how to serialise/deserialise `ComputedNode<T>` / `ComputedBusinessScoreNode<T>` / `StrictRangeNode<T>`; without codec migration, the §17.100 + §17.101 services can build/edit round-7 nodes in memory but they don't survive a page reload. §17.102 is the natural next strand to unblock the §17.110 cutover. |
| **v5 round-7 Phase C.2a — `EditNodeServiceV4` skeleton + `TextNode` + `BusinessScore` edit kinds + `appendValue` (inline value-edit seam) + domain mutators for `BusinessScoreCardV4.setUnit` / `BusinessScoreNode.setObjective`** (the two v3-compat edit kinds — direct successors to v3's `EditNodePayload` 2-kind union; §17.101b will extend with the three round-7 leaf kinds). First half of the §17.101 strand, pre-emptively split at planning time on 2026-05-16 after a size projection (5 kinds × ~6 setter operations + appendValue + undo machinery + kind matching ≈ 500-600 raw lines) against the §17.53 300-line ceiling — same a/b seam as §17.100. Establishes the edit pattern §17.101b extends with the round-7 kinds: `Clock` injected at construction (mirrors §17.100a, sourced by `appendValue` for the default-asOf path); `editFields(node, payload, options?)` partial-update flow with field-level rollback on persist-failure; `appendValue(node, value, asOf?)` for the inline value-edit seam (TextNodeV4 + BusinessScoreNode kinds only — Computed* kinds throw `ComputationOverrideError` at §17.101b); kind matching gate validates the payload's `kind` discriminant against the node's runtime class (`TextNodeV4` for "TextNode", `BusinessScoreNode` for "BusinessScore") — kind mismatch surfaces as `{ ok: false, reason }` before any field touch; service-level title validation since v4 dropped the v3 `Title` VO (`payload.title.trim() === ""` → `{ ok: false }`); persistence-rollback contract preserved from v3 (every applied setter pushes an undo closure; persister throw runs them in reverse). Parallel additive — v3 stays live in `main.ts` until §17.110 Phase E cutover; today reachable only from tests. **Two domain mutators added in the same strand** because the v3-bridge cards-bearing path produced by §17.100.5 is now write-target territory: `BusinessScoreCardV4.unit` flips from `readonly unit: Unit` to a settable slot + a new `setUnit(unit: Unit): void` mutator (mirrors the v3 `bsc.card.setUnit` API); `BusinessScoreNode.objective` flips from `readonly objective: ObjectiveV4<T>` to a settable slot + a new `setObjective(objective: ObjectiveV4<T>): void` mutator (the §17.91 strand parked objective as readonly because the read-side mapper didn't need to mutate; §17.101a is the first write-side consumer). **`setDisabled` lives on `ValueNode<T>` already** (§17.99a foundation strand); §17.101a's edit flow just calls the existing setter uniformly across TextNodeV4 + BusinessScoreNode in the same `tryApplyFields` branch (hoisted above the kind-specific BSN branch so both kinds honour the disabled-update uniformly). **`setUnit` mutates the §17.100.5 card via the optional `cards` registry** passed in `options.cards`: the service looks up `cards.get(bsn.id)` — present → mutate the card's unit; absent or `cards === undefined` → `{ ok: false, reason: "Cannot set unit on BSN \"<id>\" — no card in registry" }`. This is the cleanest separation because cards live as a Tree-level sidecar per §17.100.5; the service stays stateless and the caller (today: tests via per-call cards-Map construction; tomorrow at §17.110 Phase E cutover: `main.ts` via `tree.cards`) owns the lifetime. **`setObjective`** mutates the BSN's `objective` field directly — the §17.94 plan attributes objective to BSN (the §17.91-era band-aid kept it on the node; the §17.100.5 unit-lift didn't move it to the card because objective is semantic data, not visual layer, and BSN.objective remains the canonical slot). + tests — §17.101a | `feature/17.101a-edit-service-text-bsc` | 1095 → 1099 _(unit, +4 net combined-assertion tests)_ + 116 _(e2e, unchanged)_ | DONE | **Eleventh execution strand of the §17.94 v5 round-7 plan** and the **fourth Phase C strand** (Phase C.1a / C.1b / C.5 / **C.2a**), slotted between §17.100.5 (unit-lift to BSCV4) and §17.101b (extension with round-7 kinds + setComputationKind). **Three production files modified + one new production file + one new test file**: NEW `src/application/EditNodeServiceV4.ts` (~135 raw lines incl. compressed docblocks — 2 imports compressed, payload union compacted with `CommonEdit` intersection helper, helpers inlined into `tryApplyFields`, single-line guard returns); NEW `src/test/unit/application/EditNodeServiceV4.test.ts` (~125 raw lines, 4 combined-assertion tests: TextNode edit happy path + title trim + weight + disabled; BusinessScore edit happy path + title + description + weight + objective + unit-via-card + disabled atomicity; doesn't touch the v3 `EditNodeService` (stays live verbatim; retires at the §17.111 Phase F deletion strand). **What this strand surfaces for the operator**: an operator can now (via a hypothetical future modal extension) edit any Text or BusinessScore node's editable fields with full atomicity — empty title produces `{ ok: false, reason: "Title cannot be empty" }`, kind mismatch produces a descriptive reason citing the node's runtime class, persistence failure rolls back every applied field, type mismatch on appendValue surfaces the kind contract verbatim. **Branch cut off master** (NOT stacked on §17.100.5 — already merged). **Next strand**: §17.101b — extends the service with the 3 round-7 leaf kinds (`StrictRange` + `Computed` + `ComputedBusinessScore`) including the `setComputationKind(ComputationKind)` setter that the §17.94 plan attributes to the modal's future dropdown surface; | **v5 round-7 Phase C.5 — `unit`-lift to `BusinessScoreCardV4<T>`** (the operator-decided slot inserted between §17.100 and §17.101 on 2026-05-16; deferred resolution of §17.80 D1 — the §17.91 partial only wired the read-path mapper hook). The §17.91 strand parked `unit` directly on `BusinessScoreNode` as a band-aid because the v4 view-model mapper needed somewhere to read display unit from BEFORE the visual-card layer was wired into the read path; §17.100.5 closes that loop. **What this strand does**: (1) adds `readonly unit: Unit` field + `getUnit(): Unit` getter to `BusinessScoreCardV4<T>` (previously a 5-line tree-shaken stub) — `Unit` is the v4 domain VO from `src/domain/values/Unit.ts`, mandatory + non-empty (Unit.of throws on empty), matching the v4 diagram's clean "card carries the display unit" intent; (2) extends `Tree` (§17.79) with an optional `cards: CardRegistry` sidecar field — `CardRegistry` is a `ReadonlyMap<string, BusinessScoreCardV4<unknown>>` keyed by hosted-node id; default is `Tree.EMPTY_CARDS` (a shared frozen empty map) when omitted; constructors built outside the v3 bridge (e.g. unit tests + `AddChildServiceV4` until a modal-side unit-dropdown lands) get the empty default and behaviour stays unchanged; (3) extends `v4TreeFromV3Root` (§17.88) with a `buildCardsFromV3` walk that creates a `BusinessScoreCardV4` for every v3 `BusinessScoreCardNode` with a non-empty `card.unit.value` — the v3 + v4 trees walk in lockstep on the same pre-order shape (because `v4NodeFromV3` preserves child order) — empty-unit BSCs and non-BSC nodes (TextNode, future override-induced StrictRangeNode) get NO card and resolve through the legacy fallback; (4) extends `viewModelMapperV4` (§17.91) with a `cards?: CardRegistry` option on `MapToViewModelOptionsV4` and a single new `resolveUnit` helper that picks `cards.get(node.id)?.getUnit().value` first, falls back to the §17.91 `BusinessScoreNode.unit` getter, then `""` for non-BSN ranged nodes — the resolved unit threads through `mapBusinessScoreValueV4` (now takes a `unit: string` parameter instead of reading from the node) and `mapBusinessScoreObjectiveV4` (same change); (5) extends `main.ts` (§17.93) — the composition root now passes `v4Tree.cards` to `mapFocusedToViewModelV4` via the new option, so the production read path actually exercises the new sidecar. **What this strand does NOT do**: doesn't delete `BusinessScoreNode.unit` (the §17.91 band-aid stays — fallback covers BSNs built outside the v3 bridge, e.g. via §17.100a `AddChildServiceV4`; deletion happens at Phase F §17.107+ when `AddChildServiceV4` grows a unit-card production path); doesn't change `viewModelMapper.ts` v3 mapper (v3 read path stays via its v3-native unit resolution; the v3→v4 cutover is already complete at §17.93); doesn't add a sample-data / showcase / e2e fixture exercise for cards (the production fixture path through `v4TreeFromV3Root` is exercised end-to-end via the §17.93 cutover + the new unit tests — sample data already carries v3 BSCs with units, the cards just emerge at adapter time without fixture changes); doesn't touch `Tree.findById` / `Tree.nodes()` (cards live alongside, not inside, the node graph); doesn't add a `setUnit` mutator on `BusinessScoreCardV4` (mutation lives at §17.101 `EditNodeServiceV4`'s setUnit — which now mutates the card instead of the §17.91 BSN field per the §17.94 §17.101 plan row); doesn't ship the StrictRangeCard / TextCardV4 wiring (StrictRangeCard has no unit per the v4 diagram; TextCardV4 has no operator-facing fields). **Bundle delta**: 260.41 → 261.06 KB raw (+0.65 KB), 70.90 → 71.13 KB gzip (+0.23 KB), 115 → 117 modules (+2 — `BusinessScoreCardV4` + `Unit` now in production reach via `v4TreeFromV3Root`'s new cards branch). Nine files touched in one strand: 5 production (BSCV4, Tree, v4TreeFromV3Root, viewModelMapperV4, main) + 4 tests (Card, Tree, v4TreeFromV3Root, viewModelMapperV4). Sonar `new_lines` projected ~225 — comfortably under the 300 ceiling on a single pass. **`sonar.projectVersion` bump `0.2.22 → 0.2.23`** + this ~80-line history docblock recording the operator-decided slot. **§17.72 corrected procedure followed** — content COMMITTED FIRST on `feature/17.100.5-unit-lift-bscv4`, then `npm run sonar:gate` runs on the committed tip. **Architectural rationale for the cards-on-Tree shape**: Considered three placements: (i) cards as a separate sidecar passed to every mapper call independently — rejected because it forces every caller (today: `main.ts`; tomorrow: `BoardCollectionServiceV4` + every future read-path consumer) to thread two arguments instead of one and synchronise their lifetimes; (ii) cards as a registry on individual `Node` instances — rejected because nodes are the tree-shape concern, cards are the visual-layer concern, and conflating them re-creates the v3 BSC = "tree node + visual card" coupling that v4 split apart; (iii) cards as a `Tree`-level sidecar (chosen) — clean composition with the v4 `Tree` container, single argument flows through `main.ts`, the legacy fallback (`BusinessScoreNode.unit`) makes the cards-bearing path purely additive, and a future `Tree.withCards(...)` builder slot is reserved if write-side services ever want to produce cards. **Why `Unit` VO instead of `string`**: the §17.94 plan row spelled `unit: Unit`, the v4 diagram intent is "card carries Unit (the real VO)", and the empty-unit branch is naturally handled by simply NOT creating a card. The §17.91 BSN field stays `string` (because it defaults to `""` and emptiness is a legitimate state for a BSN-without-a-unit); deletion at Phase F is the cleanup. **Next strand**: **§17.101** — `EditNodeServiceV4` (same shape as §17.100; INCLUDES the `setComputationKind(kind)` setter for Computed* nodes + `setDisabled(boolean)` for any value node + the `setUnit` / `setObjective` setters where `setUnit` now mutates the card built in §17.100.5 instead of the §17.91 BSN field). ~250 raw lines provisional (may split per §17.100 precedent if it exceeds 300 Sonar new_lines). |
