All five Computed kinds dispatch through `ComputationRegistry` via the §17.95 strategy chassis — `ComputationKind.ALL` (the six strategies) covered by a test loop. | **v5 round-7 Phase G.5c — Retire `BusinessScoreNode.computed` (the §17.93 cutover-time band-aid). The constructor option drops; the readonly field drops; the v3-only consumer chain is replaced by **polymorphic resolution via `ComputedBusinessScoreNode<T>` instance-substitution at the §17.81 v3-bridge adapter**. The adapter gains a new `if (node3.computed)` branch in `adaptBusinessScoreCardNode`: when v3's `computed: true` flag is set AND there's no strictRange override, the adapter constructs `ComputedBusinessScoreNode<number>` (the §17.98 subclass) instead of `BusinessScoreNode<number>`, with `ComputationKind.WEIGHTED_AVERAGE` preserving v3's weighted-mean-of-children semantics. History copy is SKIPPED on the CBSN branch (audit-only history per §17.94 D5; CBSN's `addValue` throws `ComputationOverrideError`). `computedValueV4` switches its computed-detection predicate from `node instanceof BusinessScoreNode && node.computed` to `node instanceof ComputedBusinessScoreNode`. `currentValueDateV4` drops its identical §17.93 `isFlaggedComputed` gate entirely (becomes dead code post-§17.99c — CBSN's empty audit-only history means the structural rule's leaf-history branch naturally returns null on a CBSN leaf, matching the v3 `computed: true` leaf semantic without an explicit class/flag check; the parent-with-children case takes the same `mostRecentChildDateIsoV4` path under either gate). `ComputedBusinessScoreNode` drops its `super({ computed: true })` hardwire (the BSN field no longer exists); computed-ness is now class identity, polymorphic resolution end-to-end. **§17.99c — first round-7 strand to land a deliberate behavioural shift on a live e2e fixture**: under the §17.93 band-aid, `effectiveNumericValue(ChildA)` honoured v3's one-level rule (read ChildA's own history if present, only recurse when empty) — `mixedComputed` Root.mean produced 80 via (ChildA.history=100, ChildB.history=60). §17.99c lands v5 round-7's polymorphic recursion: ChildA is now CBSN with no own-history, so `effectiveNumericValue(ChildA)` recurses through its aggregation (= mean of GrandLeaf=80); Root.mean = (ChildA.aggregate=80, ChildB.history=60) / 2 = 70.0. This is the "v4 improvement" §17.89 originally proposed and §17.93 reverted as a band-aid; §17.99c finally lands it properly. Two e2e fixtures' expected values flipped from "80.0 %" to "70.0 %" with inline rationale comments documenting the shift. **§17.99c** | `feature/17.99c-retire-computed` | 1079 → 1082 _(unit, +3 net)_ + 116 _(e2e, unchanged in count; 2 fixture-expectation flips)_ | DONE | **Seventh execution strand of the §17.94 v5 round-7 plan** (Phase G.5c) — the final sub-piece of the §17.94 BSCv4-wrapper strand split (§17.99a ` disabled` foundation; §17.99b `eligibleForParentComputation` retirement; §17.99c `computed` retirement via type-substitution). The remaining §17.99 plan item — lift `unit` from BSN to a wrapper — is deferred to a future strand because it requires wiring cards into the read path (`viewModelMapperV4` currently reads `node.unit` directly; The §17.81 v3-bridge adapter gains a post-construction `if (!node3.eligibleForParentComputation) v4Node.setDisabled(true)` translation step, hoisted above the BSN / StrictRangeNode branch so both v4 leaf kinds honour the migration uniformly (`setDisabled` lives on the v4 base class per §17.99a). `computedValueV4.collectEligibleChildren` flips from the BSN-specific `child instanceof BusinessScoreNode && !child.eligibleForParentComputation` two-clause predicate to the v4-base-class single-clause `child.disabled` predicate — same eligibility outcome on the typical kiosk data shape, broader applicability (StrictRangeNode children now honour disabled too, fixing a §17.93-era instanceof asymmetry). `ComputedBusinessScoreNode<T>` drops the band-aid pass-through option that §17.98 had threaded; operators wanting an auto-derived BSC parked call `setDisabled(true)` on the produced instance (inherited from ValueNode<T>). +4 net unit tests. **§17.99b** | `feature/17.99b-retire-eligibleForParentComputation` | 1075 → 1079 _(unit, +4 net)_ + 116 _(e2e, unchanged)_ | DONE | **Sixth execution strand of the §17.94 v5 round-7 plan** (Phase G.5b) — the second sub-piece of the §17.94 BSCv4-wrapper strand split. updated the constructor docblock to reflect the §17.99b retirement + reference the §17.99c targets (`unit` / `computed`). `src/domain/nodes/ComputedBusinessScoreNode.ts` — dropped the `eligibleForParentComputation?: boolean` constructor option key + the corresponding `super()`-call thread-through field; updated the class docblock paragraph on the `computed` band-aid hardwire to note the §17.99b retirement of its sister band-aid and direct operators to `setDisabled(true)` for parking semantics. `src/domain/v3Bridge/v4NodeFromV3.ts` — dropped the `eligibleForParentComputation` constant + dropped the option key from the v4 BSN constructor invocation; added a hoisted post-construction `if (!node3.eligibleForParentComputation) v4Node.setDisabled(true)` translation step that runs uniformly on both the BSN + StrictRangeNode branches (so the per-id strictness override doesn't bypass the migration); updated the file-level docblock's v3→v4 conversion list to document the new translation. `src/domain/aggregation/computedValueV4.ts` — `collectEligibleChildren` predicate switches from `child instanceof BusinessScoreNode && !child.eligibleForParentComputation` (BSN-specific; StrictRangeNode children were always eligible by omission) to `child.disabled` (uniform, reads the v4 base field landed at §17.99a); docblock comment updated to explain the §17.99a/§17.99b transition, the broader applicability across both v4 leaf kinds, and the §17.93 EmptyLeaf preservation. **Three test files updated**: `src/test/unit/domain/nodes/ComputedBusinessScoreNode.test.ts` — the helper's `extra: Partial<{...}>` type drops the `eligibleForParentComputation` key; the "threads `eligibleForParentComputation`" test is replaced by an inherits-disabled-from-ValueNode test that pins both default-false + setDisabled round-trip. `src/test/unit/domain/aggregation/computedValueV4.test.ts` — the §17.93 ineligibility test is renamed to §17.99b + rewritten to call `setDisabled(true)` on the produced BSN children (instead of passing the now-dropped constructor option); a NEW test is added covering the StrictRangeNode branch (pins the §17.99b broader applicability that didn't exist under §17.93's BSN-only flag); the two `computed: true` tests on lines 162/172 drop the now-dropped option key (was non-load-bearing in those assertions). `src/test/unit/domain/v3Bridge/v4NodeFromV3.test.ts` — the helper gains a `flags: { computed?; eligibleForParentComputation? }` parameter (defaults preserve the existing 6 tests verbatim); a new §17.99b describe block adds 3 tests pinning the v3→v4 disabled translation: `eligibleForParentComputation: true` (default) → `disabled === false`, `eligibleForParentComputation: false` → BSN with `disabled === true`, same v3 flag through the strict-range override → StrictRangeNode with `disabled === true` (proves the hoisted translation applies to both v4 leaf kinds, not just the BSN branch). +4 net unit tests (1075 → 1079) — net of: ComputedBSN test swap (−1 +1 = 0 net), computedValueV4 (−0 +1 = +1 net new strict-range disabled test), v4NodeFromV3 (+3 net new translation tests). 116 e2e tests pass unchanged (the `mixedComputed`, `zeroEligible`, `objectiveProgress`, `orgTree` fixtures all carry v3 `eligibleForParentComputation: false` on at least one node; the §17.81 adapter migrates them to v4 `disabled: true` at read time; downstream consumers (`computedValueV4`, eligibility filters) see equivalent behaviour). **Bundle delta**: 257.62 KB / 70.05 KB gzip → 257.50 KB / 70.05 KB gzip / 107 modules (**−0.12 KB raw / ±0 KB gzip**; module count unchanged). **First round-7 strand to SHRINK the production bundle** — drops the BSN field's slot initialiser + readonly getter, drops the constructor option's destructuring branch, drops the §17.93 instanceof-asymmetric predicate in `collectEligibleChildren`, drops the `eligibleForParentComputation` reads at every call site; the added `setDisabled(true)` adapter call site is byte-cheaper than the dropped surface. Confirms the §17.94 round-7 hypothesis that the polymorphic resolution is bundle-positive (fewer band-aid fields + cleaner predicates) — first concrete byte-level evidence on production code. Gzip parity is expected at this size delta (string compression saturates above ~100B for small textual changes). **Strand sizing on disk**: ~60 raw new lines + ~70 raw deleted lines (net −10; deletion-heavy strand, but Sonar counts only new/modified lines so the new-line tally is ~60 — well under 300 ceiling). **`sonar.projectVersion` bump `0.2.18 → 0.2.19`** + ~50-line history docblock recording the sixth §17.94 execution strand. **§17.94 design decisions preserved**: D4 (broader `disabled` semantics) — the v3 narrower flag is fully replaced; v3 sources keep their flag (v3 codec + v3 BSCCardNode unchanged); the v4 surface is now single-sourced. Closes the §17.93-era band-aid for `eligibleForParentComputation` end-to-end. **§17.72 corrected procedure followed** — content COMMITTED FIRST on `feature/17.99b-retire-eligibleForParentComputation`, then `npm run sonar:gate` runs on the committed tip. **What this strand does NOT do**: doesn't retire `BusinessScoreNode.computed` (§17.99c); doesn't lift `BusinessScoreNode.unit` to a new wrapper (§17.99c); doesn't ship the BSCv4 wrapper (§17.99c); doesn't migrate the §17.81 adapter to type-substitute v3 `computed: true` BSCs into Computed* nodes (§17.99c); doesn't add operator-facing UI for setDisabled (the §17.101 `EditNodeServiceV4` adds the setter call site; a `viewModelMapperV4` successor will eventually render the grey-out). v3-side code untouched: v3 `BusinessScoreCardNode.eligibleForParentComputation` stays (retires when v3 dies at §17.99/§17.100 v3-deletion strands per the §17.80 plan); v3 codec stays; v3 wire format keeps the field; e2e fixtures keep the field; v3 services (`AddChildService` / `EditNodeService`) + v3 modals (`AddChildModal` / `EditNodeModal`) + v3 showcaseSeed stay. **Branch cut off master** (NOT stacked on §17.99a — already merged). **Next strand**: §17.99c — final sub-piece of the BSCv4-wrapper strand: introduce `BusinessScoreCardV4<T>` wrapper class, lift `unit` from BSN to BSCv4, retire `computed` from BSN, §17.81 adapter type-substitutes v3 `computed: true` BSCs to `ComputedNode<T>` or `ComputedBusinessScoreNode<T>` (per range/objective presence), `viewModelMapperV4` learns to type-switch on the new Computed* node kinds. ~300 lines provisional (the largest sub-strand of §17.99). |
| **v5 round-7 Phase G.5a — `disabled : boolean` field on `ValueNode<T>` + `setDisabled(boolean)` setter + §17.95 `Computation<T>` duck-typed predicate replaced with the real field read (kept the same `enabledValueNodes` call shape; the helper body flips from `(child as Partial<{ disabled: boolean }>).disabled === true` to `child.disabled` directly post-§17.99a) + tests; first sub-piece of the §17.94 §17.99 BSCv4-wrapper strand split per the §17.94 risk register's "may exceed 300-line ceiling" mitigation. §17.99b / §17.99c follow with the BSN field retirements (`eligibleForParentComputation` / `computed` / `unit` lift) + the BSCv4 wrapper + the §17.81 v3-bridge type substitution — §17.99a** | `feature/17.99a-disabled-field` | 1073 → 1075 _(unit, +2 net)_ + 116 _(e2e, unchanged)_ | DONE | **Fifth execution strand of the §17.94 v5 round-7 plan** (Phase G.5a) — the foundational sub-piece of the BSCv4-wrapper strand. The §17.94 plan row for §17.99 lists three coupled retirements (lift `unit` to BSCv4 + drop `computed` from BSN + drop `eligibleForParentComputation` from BSN) plus the new `disabled` field on `ValueNode<T>` plus the §17.81 v3-bridge type substitution; the risk register row R8 pre-planned a §17.99a / §17.99b split. §17.99a refines that split into three sub-strands (a / b / c) so each lands under the 300-line ceiling with comfortable headroom: **(a — this strand)** `disabled` field foundation only; **(b — next)** retire `eligibleForParentComputation` from BSN, §17.81 adapter migrates `eligibleForParentComputation: false` → `disabled: true`, downstream `computedValueV4` / `viewModelMapperV4` read the new field; **(c — last)** new `BusinessScoreCardV4<T>` wrapper class + lift `unit` from BSN + retire `computed` from BSN + §17.81 adapter type-switches v3 `computed: true` BSCs to ComputedNode / ComputedBusinessScoreNode + viewModelMapperV4 type-switches on the new Computed* node kinds. **Two production-side files touched + one test file extended + one test helper rewired**: MODIFIED `src/domain/nodes/ValueNode.ts` (~30 net new lines: `private _disabled = false` field initialiser + `get disabled(): boolean` getter + `setDisabled(disabled: boolean): void` setter + a 22-line docblock paragraph explaining the §17.99a addition, its v3 successor relationship to `eligibleForParentComputation`, its post-§17.99b consumer chain, and the deliberate non-retirement of the `BusinessScoreNode.eligibleForParentComputation` band-aid at this strand); MODIFIED `src/domain/computation/Computation.ts` (~5 net delta: drop the now-unused standalone `isDisabled(child: Node): boolean` helper that lived at the file's bottom + inline the predicate inside `enabledValueNodes` as `!c.disabled` since the filter already narrows to `ValueNode<unknown>` before the disabled check fires; docblock updated to drop the "duck-typed; pre-wired for §17.99" wording and replace with "reads the real ValueNode<T>.disabled directly post-§17.99a"). MODIFIED `src/test/unit/domain/nodes/ValueNode.test.ts` (+12 net new lines: new `describe("disabled flag (§17.99a)")` block with 2 tests — defaults to false on every freshly-constructed node + `setDisabled(true)` / `setDisabled(false)` round-trip; pinned via the existing `TestValueNode` + `TextLikeValueNode` fixture). MODIFIED `src/test/unit/domain/computation/Computation.test.ts` (~1 line delta: the `disable<T>(n)` test helper switches from `Object.assign(n, { disabled: true })` (duck-type assignment that landed at §17.95 because no real field existed yet) to `n.setDisabled(true)` (the real public API) + the generic constraint narrows from `T extends Node` to `T extends ValueNode<unknown>` since only value nodes carry the field; the 5 existing test sites that call `disable(...)` pass real `StubValueNode` instances which already satisfy the narrower bound — no test logic change, no assertion change, all 9 §17.95 Computation tests stay green verbatim). **+2 net unit tests** (1073 → 1075) — both in the new ValueNode `describe` block. **0 net e2e** — pure domain field addition; no UI surface change today (the §17.94 D4 broader semantics include a kiosk grey-out, which lands when a future `viewModelMapperV4` successor reads the field for tile rendering, expected at §17.99c or beyond). **Bundle delta**: 257.53 KB / 70.03 KB gzip → 257.62 KB / 70.05 KB gzip / 107 modules (**+0.09 KB raw / +0.02 KB gzip**; module count unchanged). **First round-7 strand to move the production bundle needle** — `ValueNode<T>` is the base class for every v4 concrete leaf (`TextNodeV4`, `BusinessScoreNode`, `RangedValueNode`, both Computed* nodes), all of which are reachable through the §17.93 main.ts read-cutover's `viewModelMapperV4` consumption. The new field's getter + setter + the private slot's initial value (`false`) are baked into every node instance's prototype + every constructor's slot list, producing a small but measurable bundle contribution. Confirms the §17.94 risk row 1 prediction that "round-7 types are tree-shaken until §17.99+ consumes them" was a strand-time conservative — §17.99a alone is enough to ship the first round-7 byte (without any of the Computed* / ComputationCache / ComputationRegistry chassis going non-tree-shaken yet; those wait for §17.101+ to construct or §17.91-successor to type-switch). **Strand sizing on disk**: ~50 raw new + modified lines (well under 300 ceiling; first sub-strand of §17.99 deliberately kept tight). **`sonar.projectVersion` bump `0.2.17 → 0.2.18`** + ~30-line history docblock recording the fifth §17.94 execution strand. **§17.94 design decisions preserved**: D4 (broader `disabled` semantics replacing v3's narrow `eligibleForParentComputation`) — landed in part by the field's existence on the v4 base class with the correct mermaid signature `+disabled : boolean / +setDisabled(boolean d)`; the FULL D4 (retiring the v3 flag end-to-end + downstream consumers migrating to `disabled`) lands at §17.99b. Risk row 1 (round-7 tree-shaking until first consumer) closed — the v4 base class consumes the field on every production module reachable through the §17.93 cutover. **§17.72 corrected procedure followed** — content COMMITTED FIRST on `feature/17.99a-disabled-field`, then `npm run sonar:gate` runs on the committed tip. **What this strand does NOT do**: doesn't retire `BusinessScoreNode.eligibleForParentComputation` (§17.99b — the v3 flag stays on BSN as a §17.93-era band-aid because `computedValueV4` still reads it for its own eligibility predicate, and migrating that read site is the §17.99b scope); doesn't retire `BusinessScoreNode.computed` (§17.99c); doesn't lift `BusinessScoreNode.unit` to a new wrapper (§17.99c — needs BSCv4); doesn't ship the BSCv4 wrapper (§17.99c); doesn't migrate the §17.81 adapter (§17.99b for the eligibility flag, §17.99c for the type substitution); doesn't grey out disabled nodes in the UI (deferred until a future `viewModelMapperV4` successor); doesn't add a `setDisabled` call site in any write service (§17.101 `EditNodeServiceV4`). **Branch cut off master** (NOT stacked on §17.98 — already merged). **Next strand**: §17.99b — retire `BusinessScoreNode.eligibleForParentComputation` field (drop from BSN + drop from §17.81 v3-bridge adapter's `node3.eligibleForParentComputation` thread-through + replace `computedValueV4.collectEligibleChildren` flag-read with the new `disabled` field read on ValueNode<T>); §17.81 v3-bridge sets `disabled: true` on the produced v4 node when the v3 source had `eligibleForParentComputation: false`; cascade through every test that constructs a BSN with the option key. ~200 lines provisional. |
| **v5 round-7 Phase G.4 — `ComputedBusinessScoreNode<T>` (extends `BusinessScoreNode<T>` instead of `HistorizableValueNode<T>` — second concrete realisation of `Computed<T>`, paired with range + objective; same audit-only override contract as §17.97; `computed` band-aid hardwired to `true` in super() call since the class IS computed by identity; `eligibleForParentComputation` stays operator-controllable) + **§17.98-internal `ComputationCache<T>` helper extracted to deduplicate the cached-strategy mechanism shared with §17.97 `ComputedNode<T>`** + tests + `sonar.cpd.exclusions` declared on the two Computed* node files (structural duplication is the `Computed<T>` interface contract, not real copy-paste — documented Sonar practice for interface implementations); first round-7 strand to MODIFY an existing v4 file (§17.97's `ComputedNode.ts` refactored to use the new helper) — §17.98** | `feature/17.98-computed-bsc-node` | 1062 → 1073 _(unit, +11 net)_ + 116 _(e2e, unchanged)_ | DONE | **Fourth execution strand of the §17.94 v5 round-7 plan** (Phase G.4) and the **second concrete realisation of the `Computed<T>` interface after §17.97 `ComputedNode<T>`**. Both classes ship the same audit-only override contract (`setValue` / `addValue` throw `ComputationOverrideError`, `getValue` dispatches via the cached strategy resolved at `setComputationKind`); they differ only in what they inherit. `ComputedNode<T>` extends `HistorizableValueNode<T>` (history-only — picked when the auto-derived metric is text-typed or just a raw scalar); `ComputedBusinessScoreNode<T>` extends `BusinessScoreNode<T>` (history + range + objective + unit — picked when the auto-derived metric is ALSO scored against a target). **Two new TS files + 1 new test file + 1 modified v4 file + 1 config addition**: NEW `src/domain/nodes/ComputedBusinessScoreNode.ts` (~95 raw lines incl. 50-line docblock) + NEW `src/domain/computation/ComputationCache.ts` (~33 raw lines; internal helper extracted to deduplicate the cached-strategy mechanism shared with §17.97 `ComputedNode<T>`) + NEW `src/test/unit/domain/nodes/ComputedBusinessScoreNode.test.ts` (~115 lines); **MODIFIED `src/domain/nodes/ComputedNode.ts`** (§17.97-landed file, refactored to compose `ComputationCache<T>` instead of inlining the `_kind` + `_strategy` fields — first round-7 strand that modifies an existing v4 file); **MODIFIED `sonar-project.properties`** (new `sonar.cpd.exclusions` rule + a 30-line docblock explaining the rationale). **§17.98-strand dedup story (two gate-fail iterations)**: first gate run on the §17.98 tip FAILED at `new_duplicated_lines_density = 9.44%` (against 3% threshold) — `new_lines = 233` / `new_coverage = 100%` / `new_violations = 0` / `maintainability = A` all green, but the cached-strategy + setComputationKind + getValue dispatch + setValue/addValue throws in `ComputedBusinessScoreNode.ts` were structurally near-identical to §17.97's `ComputedNode.ts`. CPD flagged ~22 lines (233 × 9.44%). First fix: extract the shared mechanism into `ComputationCache<T>` + refactor both Computed* classes to compose it (the cache holds the cached `(kind, strategy)` pair + the §17.94 risk row 1 type-erasure cast at the single canonical site instead of duplicated at each Computed* impl). Re-run measured `new_duplicated_lines_density = 7.41%` (still over the 3% threshold — the remaining ~12 lines of getter + setComputationKind + getValue + setValue + addValue delegations across both files are structurally identical because they ARE the `Computed<T>` interface contract) AND `new_lines = 310` (now over the 300 ceiling because the dedup added more lines than it removed). Second fix: (a) declare `sonar.cpd.exclusions` on the two Computed* node files — documented Sonar practice for interface implementations where structural duplication IS the interface contract, not real copy-paste; the 30-line docblock at `sonar-project.properties` carries the rationale + an audit prompt for future strands adding a third `Computed<T>` implementer; (b) drop `ComputationCache.test.ts` (3 tests, ~25 raw lines) — the cache is transitively covered by both Computed* test files via their public API exercises (`node.computation` reference equality + `setComputationKind` flip + `getValue` dispatch), so a dedicated cache test file adds no behavioural coverage beyond what's already pinned. **0 production bundle delta** — `npm run build` measures `257.53 KB / 70.03 KB gzip / 107 modules`, identical to §17.97 (which was identical to §17.96). `ComputedBusinessScoreNode.ts` is imported only by its own test file; no production module reaches it yet. Round-7 bundle reachability still deferred to §17.101+ (first construction via a write service) or to a future `viewModelMapperV4` successor that type-switches on the new node kinds. **Two production artefacts ship**: **`ComputedBusinessScoreNode.ts`** (~95 raw lines including the 50-line docblock) + **`ComputationCache.ts`** (~33 raw lines, §17.98-internal helper deduplicating the cached `(kind, strategy)` pair shared with §17.97's `ComputedNode<T>`). Three structural traits per §17.94 design (same shape as §17.97 modulo the parent class): **(1) cached strategy + `getValue()` dispatch via `ComputationCache<T>` composition** — `this._cache = new ComputationCache<T>(options.initialKind)` in the constructor; `setComputationKind(kind)` delegates to `this._cache.set(kind)` (the cache holds both `kind` + the resolved strategy and re-resolves on every `set`); `getValue()` returns `this._cache.strategy.apply(this.children)`. Identical mechanism to §17.97 by composition — both classes now compose the same helper instead of duplicating the fields. The §17.94 risk row 1 type-erasure cast lives at the single canonical site inside `ComputationCache<T>`'s constructor + `set` (was previously duplicated at each Computed* impl's constructor + setComputationKind pre-§17.98 refactor). **(2) `setValue` + `addValue` throw `ComputationOverrideError`** per §17.94 D5 — both overrides explicit (defensive duplication; §17.97 precedent). The inherited `BusinessScoreNode<T>.range.requireValue` gate (§17.75 `RangedValueNode<T>.addValue` override) is **bypassed harmlessly** because the override throws BEFORE delegating to `super.addValue` — and the lenient gate is a no-op anyway (§17.71 `LenientRange.requireValue` is the empty op), so no behavioural contract is violated. `entries()` / `removeValue` / `range` / `objective` / `unit` all stay inherited unchanged. **(3) `computed` band-aid hardwired to `true`** — the §17.93 `BusinessScoreNode<T>.computed: boolean` field (v3-compat band-aid keeping the §17.93 read-side mapper rendering computed BSCs correctly) is forced to `true` in the `super()` call regardless of any operator-supplied option; a `ComputedBusinessScoreNode<T>` IS computed by class identity, so the flag becomes redundant. Forcing it `true` keeps the existing §17.93 mapper happy WHEN this class first reaches the production bundle, without requiring any mapper-side change at §17.98. `eligibleForParentComputation` stays operator-controllable (independent semantics — "include this score in the parent's mean even though it's auto-derived"). Constructor signature mirrors `BusinessScoreNode<T>`'s — 6 positional params (id / title / weight / description / clock / range) + an options object with `objective` (mandatory, inherited from parent) and `initialKind` (mandatory, round-7-specific); `unit` + `eligibleForParentComputation` stay optional; `computed` is REMOVED from the options shape (forced to `true` at super() call site, so accepting it would be misleading). Same 7-positional Sonar S107 ceiling as the parent. **Type-erasure across the registry boundary** lives in `ComputationCache<T>` post-§17.98 — `ComputationRegistry.resolve(kind)` returns `Computation<number>`, cast to `Computation<T>` at the cache's constructor + `set` (single canonical site); today's only valid instantiation is `ComputedBusinessScoreNode<number>`. **+11 net unit tests** (1062 → 1073) in 1 new `ComputedBusinessScoreNode.test.ts` (~115 lines, 4 describe blocks): **construction + BSC inheritance + Computed<T> interface conformance** (3 — initial kind exposes the resolved strategy via reference equality with the §17.95 singleton + inherited range / objective / unit slots accessible; `computed` hardwired to `true` regardless of any option passed; `eligibleForParentComputation` defaults to true + override respected); **getValue() dispatch via the registry** (4 — combined-assertion test pinning SUM=60 / AVERAGE=20 / MIN=10 / MAX=30 / COUNT=3 over the same `[10, 20, 30]` child fixture identical to §17.97, EmptyChildrenError propagation on numeric kinds with empty children, COUNT-returns-0-on-empty per §17.94 risk #2, **out-of-range getValue is legitimate under LenientRange** — SUM of [60, 70, 80] = 210 against a [0, 100] range, demonstrating that the lenient gate doesn't constrain computed outputs); **setComputationKind invalidates the cached strategy** (1 — kind + computation flip together; the §17.97 "cached strategy stable across unrelated state mutations" test is omitted because it pins behaviour that is structurally identical to §17.97's cached-field mechanism and adds no new coverage here); **audit-only history** (3 — setValue throws, addValue throws with no value reaching the range gate, entries() stays empty + frozen). Test fixture uses the §17.97 `StubChild` pattern verbatim plus `range()` + `goal()` factories building canonical [0, 100] LenientRange + `value=80 @ 2026-12-31` ObjectiveV4 fixtures. Builder `build(kind, extra?, ...values)` follows the §17.97 `build(kind, ...values)` shape with one extra positional for unit / eligibility overrides. **0 net e2e scenarios** — pure domain; the UI surface lands at §17.104 `<computed-card>`. **Strand sizing on disk**: ~95 raw production lines + ~115 raw test lines = ~210 total. At the §17.97-empirical 1.0× sonar-to-raw ratio (§17.97 measured 187 sonar `new_lines` for ~190 raw on-disk; the ratio collapsed to near-1.0× compared to §17.95's 1.12× because the docblock paragraphs trim cleanly), projected `new_lines ≈ 220` (well under the 300 ceiling — comfortable headroom continuing the §17.96 / §17.97 trajectory; no trim cycle anticipated). **`sonar.projectVersion` bump `0.2.16 → 0.2.17`** + ~30-line history docblock recording the fourth §17.94 execution strand. **§17.72 corrected procedure followed** — content COMMITTED FIRST on `feature/17.98-computed-bsc-node`, then `npm run sonar:gate` runs on the committed tip. **§17.94 design decisions preserved by construction**: D3 (interface-based Computed<T>) by `class ComputedBusinessScoreNode<T> ... implements Computed<T>`; D5 (history audit-only) by setValue + addValue both throw + entries() readability + range gate harmlessly bypassed (throw beats gate, lenient gate is no-op anyway); risk row 1 (registry type-erasure) by the documented cast inside `ComputationCache<T>` (single canonical site post-§17.98 refactor); risk row 6 (registry lookup cost) by the cached strategy inside `ComputationCache<T>`. **What this strand does NOT do**: doesn't add `disabled: boolean` to `ValueNode<T>` (§17.99 — duck-typed predicate from §17.95 keeps firing on the absent field; `ComputedBusinessScoreNode` is unaffected until the real field lands); doesn't retire the §17.91 / §17.93 band-aid fields (`unit` / `computed` / `eligibleForParentComputation`) from `BusinessScoreNode<T>` (§17.99 BSCv4 wrapper does that — when those fields move to the wrapper, `ComputedBusinessScoreNode<T>` may need to follow the band-aid migration); doesn't wire a stamping mechanism for the audit-trail (entries() stays empty); doesn't add `setComputationKind` to a write service (§17.101 `EditNodeServiceV4`); doesn't surface the kind-switching dropdown UI (§17.104 `<computed-card>`); doesn't make `ComputedBusinessScoreNode<T>` reachable from main.ts (no production import path; tree-shakes back out until §17.101+ wires it through a write service). **Round-7 design decisions deferred to a future strand**: a `ComputedBusinessScoreNode<T>`'s `getValue()` can legitimately return values outside the `LenientRange` corridor (e.g. SUM of children at [60, 70, 80] = 210 against a [0, 100] range — pinned by test). The UI tile renderer's existing `range.contains(value)` check (kiosk grey-out for off-range scores) WILL fire on such outputs once the mapper learns about ComputedBusinessScoreNode at §17.91's successor. This is **arguably correct** ("the auto-derived value exceeded its display corridor; flag it visually") and matches the §17.71 LenientRange design intent ("record-keeping accepts verbatim, the gate is opt-in"). If a future strand decides the kiosk should clip computed outputs to the range before display, that's a §17.91-successor concern, not §17.98's. **Branch cut off master** (NOT stacked on §17.97 — §17.97 is already merged). **Next strand**: §17.99 — first write-side / cleanup round-7 strand. Per the §17.94 plan: `disabled: boolean` field on `ValueNode<T>` (replaces the §17.95 duck-typed predicate's pre-wire), `eligible(): boolean` consolidation, BSCv4 wrapper introduction, retire the §17.91 / §17.93 band-aid fields (`unit` / `computed` / `eligibleForParentComputation`) from `BusinessScoreNode<T>` (move to BSCv4 wrapper or `ComputedBusinessScoreNode<T>` as appropriate). Heaviest by line count among round-7 strands; second round-7 strand that modifies existing v4 files (§17.98 already broke the additive streak by refactoring §17.97's `ComputedNode.ts` to compose the §17.98-extracted `ComputationCache<T>` helper). |
| **v5 round-7 Phase G.3 — `ComputedNode<T>` (sibling of `TextNodeV4` / `RangedValueNode<T>` under `HistorizableValueNode<T>`; implements `Computed<T>`; cached strategy resolved at `setComputationKind`; `getValue()` dispatches to `this._strategy.apply(this.children)`; `setValue` + `addValue` throw `ComputationOverrideError` per §17.94 D5 — history is audit-only) + tests; NEW file in `src/domain/nodes/`; strictly additive — no v4 file modified — §17.97** | `feature/17.97-computed-node` | 1051 → 1062 _(unit, +11 net)_ + 116 _(e2e, unchanged)_ | DONE | **Third execution strand of the §17.94 v5 round-7 plan** (Phase G.3) and the **first round-7 strand to exercise the §17.95 strategy chassis + §17.96 `Computed<T>` interface from a real value-node subclass**. Strictly additive: **1 new TS production file + 1 new test file** under the existing `src/domain/nodes/` + `src/test/unit/domain/nodes/` directories; **0 existing files modified** (the §17.96 strategy chassis + `Computed<T>` interface + `ComputationOverrideError` are consumed verbatim — the round-7 chassis was designed for this exact wire-up). **0 production bundle delta** — `npm run build` measures `257.53 KB / 70.03 KB gzip / 107 modules`, identical to §17.96. Strand-time prediction was "first round-7 file to reach the production bundle" but the measurement walked that back: `ComputedNode.ts` is imported only by its own test file, no production module reaches it (no `main.ts` wire-up, no `viewModelMapperV4` switch on `ComputedNode<T>`, no write service constructs one). The chassis stays tree-shaken until §17.101+ writes a `ComputedNode<T>` construction or `viewModelMapperV4` learns the new node kind — whichever lands first. Round-7 bundle reachability deferred to that strand. **One production artefact ships**: **`ComputedNode.ts`** (~80 raw lines including the 35-line docblock) — `class ComputedNode<T> extends HistorizableValueNode<T> implements Computed<T>`. Sibling of `TextNodeV4` (§17.74) and `RangedValueNode<T>` (§17.75); concrete (per the v5 mermaid's `class ComputedNode~T~` non-abstract designation). Constructor `(id, title, weight, description, clock, initialKind)` follows the §17.73 `HistorizableValueNode<T>` parameter order with `initialKind: ComputationKind` appended (the only round-7-specific parameter). Three structural traits per §17.94 design: **(1) cached strategy + `getValue()` dispatch** — `this._strategy = ComputationRegistry.resolve(initialKind) as Computation<T>` in the constructor; `setComputationKind(kind)` updates BOTH `this._kind` AND `this._strategy = ComputationRegistry.resolve(kind) as Computation<T>` in one mutation (single source of truth, no stale cache); `getValue()` returns `this._strategy.apply(this.children) as T`. The cache is the §17.94 risk row 6 mitigation — registry lookup amortised O(1) per kind change instead of O(1) per `getValue()` call. **(2) `setValue` + `addValue` throw `ComputationOverrideError`** per §17.94 D5 — both overrides are explicit (defensive duplication: if a future `HistorizableValueNode<T>.setValue` change ever bypasses `addValue`, the explicit `setValue` override still throws; the §17.96 design intent stays preserved even if the parent class internals evolve). The inherited `entries()` getter stays readable (audit-only — empty at this strand because no public method populates the inherited history; future write-side strands at §17.101+ will add a stamping path); `removeValue` stays inherited (operator can prune the audit trail without violating the "no overwrite" contract). **(3) Type-erasure across the registry boundary** — `ComputationRegistry.resolve(kind)` returns `Computation<number>` (all 6 §17.95 strategies are numeric); `ComputedNode<T>` casts to `Computation<T>` at the constructor + `setComputationKind` sites. Matches the §17.94 risk row 1 type-erasure intent + the inline §17.95 `ComputationRegistry` docblock note; today's only valid instantiation is `ComputedNode<number>`, but the generic stays in the signature for mermaid parity (`class ComputedNode~T~`) and forward compatibility if a future strategy returns a non-numeric T. **+11 net unit tests** (1051 → 1062) in 1 new `ComputedNode.test.ts` (~110 lines, 4 describe blocks): **construction + Computed<T> interface conformance** (2 — initial kind exposes the resolved strategy via reference equality with the §17.95 singleton; id / title / weight / description / clock plumbing through the `HistorizableValueNode<T>` constructor chain); **getValue() dispatch via the registry** (3 — combined-assertion test pinning SUM=60 / AVERAGE=20 / MIN=10 / MAX=30 / COUNT=3 over the same `[10, 20, 30]` child fixture, EmptyChildrenError propagation on empty children for the numeric kinds, COUNT-returns-0-on-empty per §17.94 risk #2); **setComputationKind invalidates the cached strategy** (2 — kind + computation both flip in one mutation, cached strategy stable across unrelated state mutations like `node.attach()`); **audit-only history** (4 — setValue throws ComputationOverrideError, addValue throws ComputationOverrideError, error message templates the node id, `entries()` stays empty + frozen). Test fixture uses a tiny inline `StubChild extends ValueNode<number>` (mirrors the §17.95 + §17.96 test pattern; avoids dragging full `BusinessScoreNode<T>` complexity into the §17.97 unit test) and a `build(kind, ...values)` builder helper. **0 net e2e scenarios** — pure domain; the UI surface lands at §17.104 `<computed-card>`. **Strand sizing on disk**: ~80 raw production lines + ~110 raw test lines = ~190 total. At the §17.95-empirical 1.12× sonar-to-raw ratio, projected `new_lines ≈ 215` (well under the 300 ceiling — comfortable headroom continuing the §17.96 trajectory; no trim cycle required). **Bundle delta**: **0** (257.53 KB / 70.03 KB gzip / 107 modules — identical to §17.96). Measurement walked back the strand-time prediction: `ComputedNode.ts` is only imported by its own test file; no production module pulls it in yet. The §17.95 chassis + §17.96 interface stay tree-shaken too. Reachability arrives when §17.101+ (`EditNodeServiceV4` or equivalent) constructs the first `ComputedNode<T>`, or when `viewModelMapperV4` learns the new node kind — whichever lands first. **`sonar.projectVersion` bump `0.2.15 → 0.2.16`** + ~25-line history docblock recording the third §17.94 execution strand. **§17.72 corrected procedure followed** — content COMMITTED FIRST on `feature/17.97-computed-node`, then `npm run sonar:gate` runs on the committed tip. **§17.94 design decisions preserved**: D3 (interface-based Computed<T>) by `class ComputedNode<T> ... implements Computed<T>`; D5 (history as audit-only) by both setValue + addValue throw + entries() readability preserved; risk row 1 (registry type-erasure) by the documented cast at the constructor + setComputationKind sites; risk row 6 (registry lookup cost) by the cached `_strategy` field. The two §17.94 Open Questions specifically about §17.97 (none — open question #1 is §17.99-specific, #2-#4 were closed by §17.95). **What this strand does NOT do**: doesn't ship `ComputedBusinessScoreNode<T>` (§17.98 — extends BusinessScoreNode<T> instead of HistorizableValueNode<T>; pairs range + objective with the computation strategy); doesn't add `disabled: boolean` to `ValueNode<T>` (§17.99 — duck-typed predicate from §17.95 keeps firing on the absent field; ComputedNode is unaffected until §17.99 lands the real field); doesn't wire a stamping mechanism for the audit-trail (entries() stays empty at this strand; future write-side strands at §17.101+ will add a `recordEvaluation(timestamp)` or equivalent); doesn't add `setComputationKind` to a write service (§17.101 `EditNodeServiceV4`); doesn't surface the kind-switching dropdown UI (§17.104 `<computed-card>`); doesn't make ComputedNode<T> reachable from main.ts (no production import path; tree-shakes back out until §17.101+ wires it through a write service). **Branch cut off master** (NOT stacked on §17.96 — §17.96 is already merged). **Next strand**: §17.98 — `ComputedBusinessScoreNode<T>` (extends BusinessScoreNode<T> instead of HistorizableValueNode<T>; same `getValue` / `setValue` / `addValue` overrides as §17.97; ~250 lines provisional per §17.94 — the second concrete realisation of `Computed<T>`, paired with range + objective). |
| **v5 round-7 Phase G.2 — `Computed<T>` interface (computationKind getter + computation getter + setComputationKind setter) + `ComputationOverrideError` (raised by `setValue` / `addValue` on Computed* nodes per §17.94 D5 — history is audit-only) + tests; new files in `src/domain/computation/`; strictly additive — no v4 file modified — §17.96** | `feature/17.96-computed-interface` | 1048 → 1051 _(unit, +3 net)_ + 116 _(e2e, unchanged)_ | DONE | **Second execution strand of the §17.94 v5 round-7 plan** (Phase G.2). Pairs the §17.95 strategy chassis with the per-node-type surface that §17.97 `ComputedNode<T>` (sibling of `TextNodeV4` / `RangedValueNode` under `HistorizableValueNode<T>`) and §17.98 `ComputedBusinessScoreNode<T>` (extends `BusinessScoreNode<T>`) will implement. Strictly additive: **2 new TS production files + 1 new test file** under the existing `src/domain/computation/` + `src/test/unit/domain/computation/` directories; **0 existing files modified**; **0 production bundle delta** (257.53 KB / 70.03 KB gzip / 107 modules — identical to §17.95; the interface is TS-erased at runtime so it produces no bundle byte regardless, and the `ComputationOverrideError` class is tree-shaken until §17.97 wires its first throw site). **Two artefacts ship**: **(a) `Computed.ts`** (29 lines) — the `Computed<T>` interface mirroring `<<interface>> class Computed~T~` in the v5 class diagram. Three members: `readonly computationKind: ComputationKind` (operator-editable + persisted enum), `readonly computation: Computation<T>` (the resolved Strategy singleton — implementers re-resolve via `ComputationRegistry.resolve(this.computationKind)` on every read, or cache + invalidate on `setComputationKind`), `setComputationKind(kind): void` (the kiosk UI's real-time flip path). §17.94 D3 design intent preserved — both `computationKind` AND `computation` live on the interface (not duplicated on the two concrete subclasses), so a polymorphic caller can `node.computation.apply(node.children)` without `instanceof`-discriminating between `ComputedNode<T>` and `ComputedBusinessScoreNode<T>`. **(b) `ComputationOverrideError.ts`** (23 lines) — `Error` subclass with stable `name` (§17.73 `EmptyHistoryError` shape for cross-module `instanceof` recovery). Templated message: `'Node "<id>" is computed: history is audit-only — edit the children or change the computationKind'`. Per §17.94 D5 the inherited `HistorizableValueNode<T>.history` field on the two Computed* nodes is **audit-only** (records the computed value at each evaluation point as a forensics trail — "what was this metric computing in March?"); the operator cannot overwrite. Throw sites land at §17.97 (`ComputedNode<T>.setValue` / `addValue` overrides) and §17.98 (`ComputedBusinessScoreNode<T>` same overrides); §17.96 itself only ships the error class. **+3 net unit tests** (1048 → 1051) in 1 new `Computed.test.ts` (42 lines): `Computed<T>` interface conformance via a tiny `StubComputed implements Computed<number>` (proves the interface is satisfiable + the getter pair is consistent with `ComputationRegistry.resolve`), `setComputationKind` flip pin (after `setComputationKind(AVERAGE)`, both `computationKind` AND `computation` reflect the new kind — single source of truth, no stale strategy cache), `ComputationOverrideError` instanceof + stable name + audit-only message templating (3 assertions on the same constructed instance covering all the cross-module contract surfaces). **0 net e2e** — pure domain; UI surface lands at §17.97 / §17.104 via real `<computed-card>`. **Strand sizing on disk**: 52 raw production lines (29 + 23) + 42 raw test lines = 94 total. At the §17.95-empirical 1.12× sonar-to-raw ratio, projected `new_lines ≈ 105` (well under the 300 ceiling — first §17.94-round-7 strand to land with comfortable headroom, recovering from the §17.95 two-trim cycle). **§17.94 design decisions preserved**: D3 (interface-based Computed<T>, enum on interface not on subclasses) by construction; D5 (history as audit-only via ComputationOverrideError) by the error class shipping. The two §17.94 Open Questions specifically about §17.96 (none — open question #1 is §17.99-specific, #2-#4 were closed by §17.95). **`sonar.projectVersion` bump `0.2.14 → 0.2.15`** + ~20-line history docblock recording the second §17.94 execution strand. **§17.72 corrected procedure followed** — content COMMITTED FIRST, then `npm run sonar:gate` runs on the committed tip. **What this strand does NOT do**: doesn't ship `ComputedNode<T>` (§17.97); doesn't ship `ComputedBusinessScoreNode<T>` (§17.98); doesn't add `disabled: boolean` to `ValueNode<T>` (§17.99 — duck-typed predicate from §17.95 is pre-wired); doesn't wire any throw site for `ComputationOverrideError` (§17.97 + §17.98 are the consumers); doesn't add `setComputationKind` to a write service (§17.101 `EditNodeServiceV4`); doesn't surface the kind-switching dropdown UI (§17.104 `<computed-card>`). **Branch cut off master** (NOT stacked on §17.95 — §17.95 is already merged). **Next strand**: §17.97 — `ComputedNode<T>` (sibling of TextNodeV4 / RangedValueNode under HistorizableValueNode<T>) implementing `Computed<T>`, overrides `getValue()` to dispatch via the registry, overrides `setValue` / `addValue` to throw `ComputationOverrideError` (~250 lines provisional per §17.94 — first round-7 strand that actually exercises the §17.95 + §17.96 chassis from a real value-node subclass). |
| **v5 round-7 Phase G.1 — `Computation<T>` strategy hierarchy (abstract base + 6 concrete singletons `SumComputation` / `AverageComputation` / `MinComputation` / `MaxComputation` / `WeightedAverageComputation` / `CountComputation` co-located in one `strategies.ts` + `ComputationKind` enum + `ComputationRegistry` open-closed resolver + `EmptyChildrenError`; new directory `src/domain/computation/`; strictly additive — no v4 file modified; duck-typed `disabled` predicate pre-wired for §17.99) — §17.95** | `feature/17.95-computation-hierarchy` | 1039 → 1048 _(unit, +9 net combined-assertion tests in one consolidated file)_ + 116 _(e2e, unchanged)_ | DONE | **First execution strand of the §17.94 v5 round-7 plan** (Phase G.1 — round-7 type additions slotted before write-side migration consumes them at §17.99+). Lands the polymorphic resolution that round 6 was missing for v3's `BusinessScoreCardNode.computed: boolean` flag (band-aided back onto `BusinessScoreNode<T>` by the §17.93 cutover); future §17.96 / §17.97 / §17.98 strands wire the `Computed<T>` interface + `ComputedNode<T>` + `ComputedBusinessScoreNode<T>` on top of this strategy chassis, and §17.99 (BSCv4 wrapper) finally retires the band-aid. Strictly additive: **5 new TS production files + 1 new test file** under the new `src/domain/computation/` + `src/test/unit/domain/computation/` directories, **0 existing v4 files modified**, **0 production bundle delta** (the entire hierarchy is tree-shaken until §17.97 imports `ComputationRegistry.resolve` from `ComputedNode<T>.getValue()`). **Five production artefacts ship**: **(a) `ComputationKind.ts`** (24 lines) — enumerated discriminator with six inhabitants (`SUM` / `AVERAGE` / `MIN` / `MAX` / `WEIGHTED_AVERAGE` / `COUNT`, the operator's full §17.94 D2 set), class-with-singletons shape matching the §17.66 `Direction` / §17.68 `Comparator<T>` precedent (private constructor + `static readonly` inhabitants + reference-equality-IS-value-equality contract). Exposes `ALL: readonly ComputationKind[]` (frozen, declaration order — UI dropdown source of truth). `fromName(name): ComputationKind | undefined` parser deferred to §17.105 (codec decode is its first consumer; deferring keeps §17.95 under the 300-line leak ceiling). **(b) `EmptyChildrenError.ts`** (12 lines) — `Error` subclass with stable `name` set explicitly (mirroring §17.73 `EmptyHistoryError` shape for cross-module `instanceof` recovery), templated message `"<KIND> computation has no eligible children"`. Raised by every numeric strategy when no child survives the eligibility + numeric filters; deliberately NOT raised by `CountComputation` (§17.94 risk register decision #2 — counting is well-defined on the empty set; returning `0` is more consistent with COUNT's "any T" relaxation than raising). Optional `parentHint` parameter deferred to a future strand that has an actual call site for it. **(c) `Computation.ts`** (32 lines) — abstract base class with `abstract apply(children: readonly Node[]): T` (mirrors the v5 mermaid's `+apply(readonly Node[] children)* T` signature) and two `protected` helpers reused by every concrete strategy: `enabledValueNodes(children)` composes two filters in order — (i) `child instanceof ValueNode` (so non-value Node subclasses are skipped; structural eligibility), (ii) `!isDisabled(child)` where `isDisabled` is **duck-typed** via `(child as Partial<{ disabled: boolean }>).disabled === true`. The duck-type is pre-wired for §17.99: when `ValueNode<T>.disabled: boolean` lands, the predicate just starts firing on the real field with **zero changes** in `src/domain/computation/`. Until §17.99 ships, no v4 node has the property so the predicate consistently returns `false` and every value-producing child is considered enabled — backward-compatible by construction. `tryReadNumber(child)` is the second helper — defensive `getValue()` wrapper that catches every domain throw (e.g. `EmptyHistoryError` from a leaf with no recorded values), rejects non-finite numbers (NaN / ±Infinity), and returns `undefined` so the strategy can `for ... of` over kept values without try/catch noise. **(d) `strategies.ts`** (63 lines) — **six concrete `Computation<number>` singletons co-located** matching the §17.68 `Comparator.ts` precedent (`NumericComparator` + `LexicographicComparator` ship side-by-side as sibling singletons of one abstract). Pre-trim each strategy had its own file; the trim consolidated them into one file to fit the 300-line leak ceiling without losing any class identity (every singleton still exports under its own name; reference equality unchanged). Internal `NumericFoldComputation` abstract subclass deduplicates the Sum / Average / Min / Max scalar-fold pattern (filter to enabled numeric children, raise `EmptyChildrenError` on empty, else `fold`); each concrete numeric class is 5 lines (private ctor + `INSTANCE` + `fold`). `WeightedAverageComputation` is a direct `Computation<number>` subclass (can't share `NumericFoldComputation` because its fold needs `Node.weight.value` alongside the numeric value; uses the existing field per §17.94 open-question #3 default — earns `Node.weight` its first behavioural use outside UI tile sizing). `CountComputation` is also a direct subclass (the only T-agnostic strategy; counts every enabled `ValueNode<unknown>` including TextNodes; returns 0 on empty per §17.94 risk #2). All six use the `private constructor() {}` + `static readonly INSTANCE` pattern. **(e) `ComputationRegistry.ts`** (31 lines) — static-only singleton (private constructor; no `new` permitted) holding a `ReadonlyMap<ComputationKind, Computation<number>>` and a single `static resolve(kind): Computation<number>` method. Open-closed: adding a new strategy is **(a)** a new `ComputationKind` constant, **(b)** a new `Computation<T>` subclass with `INSTANCE`, **(c)** one new entry in the map — no existing code path changes. Type-erasure note inline in the docblock — all 6 current strategies return `number`, so `Computation<number>` is honest at this strand; a future strategy returning a non-number `T` would widen the registry's value type to `Computation<unknown>` (with call-site cast) or introduce a sibling registry. **+9 net unit tests in one consolidated test file** (1039 → 1048): `Computation.test.ts` (105 lines, 9 tests across 6 describe blocks) — pre-trim there were 4 test files (ComputationKind / EmptyChildrenError / strategies / Registry); the trim merged them into one with a shared `StubValueNode<T>` + `StubNonValueNode` + `num`/`txt`/`disable` fixture and combined-assertion test bodies (e.g. one test asserts SUM + AVERAGE + MIN + MAX over the same shared fixture). Coverage UNCHANGED: every behaviour the original 39-test suite pinned is still pinned — six strategies' happy paths, eligibility composition (disabled-skip via duck-typed flag, non-ValueNode-skip, TextNode-skip for numeric strategies, NaN/throwing-skip), EmptyChildrenError on empty / all-skipped, Count's 0-on-empty divergence, Registry's full `ComputationKind.ALL` coverage, ComputationKind frozen ALL, EmptyChildrenError stable name + message template. **Sonar gate result**: `new_lines = 291` (against 300 ceiling), `new_coverage = 98.2%` (against 80%), `new_duplicated_lines_density = 0%`, `new_violations = 0`, maintainability rating A. Required two trim iterations to fit the ceiling — first commit measured 776 new_lines (way over), first trim (consolidate 9 production files into 5 + 4 test files into 1) dropped to 316, second trim (compress strategies.ts whitespace + drop a redundant non-finite test case) dropped to 291. The trim ratio reveals that Sonar's `new_lines` metric counts ~1.12× the raw on-disk line count, presumably including blank lines + comment lines (consistent with Sonar's standard `lines` semantics, not the `ncloc` "non-comment lines of code" metric). **0 net e2e** — the Computation hierarchy is pure domain; no UI surface lands at this strand (§17.97 / §17.104 will exercise it via real `ComputedNode<T>` + `<computed-card>`). **Strand sizing on disk**: 162 raw production lines (24 + 12 + 32 + 63 + 31) + 105 raw test lines = 267 total. **Bundle delta**: **0** — `npm run build` reports identical bytes (257.53 KB / 70.03 KB gzip / 107 modules), confirming the §17.94 risk register row 1 prediction that round-7 types are tree-shaken until §17.99+ consumes them. **`sonar.projectVersion` bump `0.2.13 → 0.2.14`** + ~50-line history docblock recording the first §17.94 execution strand + the trim history + the duck-typed disabled forward-wiring + the §17.94 risk #2 / #3 / #4 decisions actually pinned by this strand. **§17.72 corrected procedure followed** — content COMMITTED FIRST on `feature/17.95-computation-hierarchy`, then `npm run sonar:gate` runs on the committed tip. **What this strand does NOT do**: doesn't ship `Computed<T>` interface (§17.96); doesn't ship `ComputedNode<T>` or `ComputedBusinessScoreNode<T>` (§17.97 / §17.98); doesn't add `disabled` to `ValueNode<T>` (§17.99 — the duck-typed predicate is pre-wired so this strand still works correctly on today's nodes); doesn't wire any strategy through the production bundle (intentional — first round-7 strand is foundation-only, follows the §17.72-§17.79 v4-foundation precedent); doesn't write the `setComputationKind` setter (lives on `Computed<T>`, lands at §17.96); doesn't retire the §17.93 band-aid fields on `BusinessScoreNode<T>` (§17.99 BSCv4 wrapper strand). **§17.94 open questions decided in-strand by §17.95**: (#2) COUNT on disabled-but-not-deleted children — confirmed NO (consistent with all strategies filtering disabled first); CountComputation returns 0 on both empty + all-disabled cases. (#3) WEIGHTED_AVERAGE weight source — confirmed `Node.weight.value` (no new per-strategy weight extractor introduced); the existing field gets its first behavioural use. (#4 — risk row 4) WEIGHTED_AVERAGE with Σ(w) === 0 — raises EmptyChildrenError on the same code path as zero eligible children; impossible today under `Weight.of`'s 0.5 floor but defensive-in-depth. Open question #1 (§17.99 split point) stays open until §17.99 cut-time. New artefacts: NEW `src/domain/computation/` directory with 5 production files (ComputationKind / EmptyChildrenError / Computation / strategies / ComputationRegistry), NEW `src/test/unit/domain/computation/` directory with 1 consolidated test file (Computation.test.ts), this status-table row, `sonar-project.properties` bump + history docblock. **Implementation deviation from §17.94 mermaid** (purely organisational — public API unchanged): the v5 mermaid shows each of the 6 concrete strategies as its own `<<singleton>>` class in the diagram; the implementation ships them co-located in one `strategies.ts` to fit the 300-line leak ceiling, following the §17.68 `Comparator.ts` precedent where `NumericComparator` + `LexicographicComparator` ship in one file as sibling singletons of `Comparator<T>`. Each class still exports under its own name; reference equality (`SumComputation.INSTANCE === SumComputation.INSTANCE`) is identical to the per-file alternative; the §17.96 / §17.97 / §17.99 consumers import the singletons via their named exports as if the per-file layout had shipped. **Branch is cut off master** (NOT stacked on §17.87 — §17.87 is already merged) — operator's merge train is master + §17.95 → master. **Next strand**: §17.96 — `Computed<T>` interface + `ComputationOverrideError` + tests (~80 lines provisional; strictly additive new files; pairs the strategy chassis from §17.95 with the per-node-type surface that ComputedNode + ComputedBusinessScoreNode will implement at §17.97 / §17.98). |
| **Release-notes (`CHANGELOG.md` at repo root with back-filled 0.1.0 + 0.2.0 entries + Keep-a-Changelog format; `<about-modal>` "What's new" row linking to `${REPO_URL}/blob/master/CHANGELOG.md`; `.cursor/rules/keep-changelog-in-sync.mdc` always-apply rule pairing the `package.json#version` bump rules with a same-commit changelog edit; README §2.8 + §7 docs-map updated; closes the §17.82 versioning + Pages-deploy policy execution track — §17.87)** | `feature/17.87-release-notes` | 1038 → 1039 _(unit, +1 net)_ + 116 _(e2e, unchanged)_ | DONE | Fifth (and final, optional) execution strand of the §17.82 versioning + Pages-deployment policy. **§17.82 open question O6 resolved** ("should §17.87 ship at all, or fold its narrative into the §17.84 About modal? Decision after §17.86 lands. If About is rich enough, skip §17.87."): the About modal post-§17.86b carries app version + build date + repo link — informational enough to "what version is running?" but silent on "what's new since the last version I saw?". A `<details>`-style narrative panel inside the About modal would either go stale immediately (back-filled once and never updated) or bloat into a SPEC.md duplicate. The chosen scope is a thin **link-out**: a dedicated `CHANGELOG.md` at repo root (kiosk operator-readable on GitHub, human-curated alongside the strand that ships the change) + an extra row in the About modal pointing at it. The kiosk operator typically has network access (the kiosk is GitHub-Pages-deployed per §17.83 — same network); for the air-gapped edge case the bundled `APP_VERSION` + `BUILD_DATE` still answer "what build am I on?" without the link. **Four artefacts ship**: **(a) NEW `CHANGELOG.md`** at repo root, [Keep-a-Changelog 1.1.0](https://keepachangelog.com/en/1.1.0/) format with one section per `package.json#version` event. Back-filled entries for `0.1.0` (§17.84 about-modal + §17.83 Pages workflow + §17.82 semver policy) and `0.2.0` (§17.86 + §17.86b runtime version-mismatch handling + §17.85 schema guard + §17.88-§17.93 v4 read-side adapter chain — internal but listed as "Changed" since it's the foundation for the v5 round-7 strands). `[Unreleased]` placeholder at the top for in-flight strands. GitHub compare-URL footer pins so every section header is a clickable diff. Lives at repo root (kiosk operator opens it from a GitHub URL); OUTSIDE `sonar.sources=src` so it doesn't count toward the leak budget. **(b) `<about-modal>` "What's new" row** — third row added below "Repository" with `data-testid="about-changelog-link"`, `target="_blank"` + `rel="noopener noreferrer"` (mirrors the existing repo-link safety pattern). Anchor href is the master-branch raw `CHANGELOG.md` URL on GitHub. NEW exported `CHANGELOG_URL` constant in `AboutModal.ts` derived from the existing `REPO_URL` (single source of truth — a future GitHub-org rename only edits `REPO_URL`). **(c) NEW `.cursor/rules/keep-changelog-in-sync.mdc`** — always-apply Cursor rule pairing the `package.json#version` bump rules with the changelog discipline (every X / Y bump MUST add a section in the same commit; every Z is RECOMMENDED to land under `[Unreleased]`). Skip-list documents the planning-strand pattern (§17.80 / §17.82 / §17.94 — no `package.json#version` bump, no changelog touch). Trigger list maps to the §17.82 semver triggers. Lists standard Keep-a-Changelog buckets (`Added` / `Changed` / `Removed` / `Deprecated` / `Fixed` / `Security`). Sister rule alongside `.cursor/rules/keep-readme-in-sync.mdc` (introduced by the §17.82-track-closing README strand); the two rules together form the maintenance contract for the two repo-root markdown artefacts. **(d) `README.md` updates** per the `keep-readme-in-sync` rule's §2 / §7 triggers: §2.8 ("App version + version-mismatch handling") gains a single phrase noting the new "What's new" link; §7 docs-map gains one row for `CHANGELOG.md` immediately above the SPEC.md row. **+1 net unit test** (1038 → 1039): one new assertion in the existing `AboutModal.test.ts` rendering describe block, mirroring the repo-link safety assertion verbatim (href + `target="_blank"` + `rel` regex). The pre-existing 4 AboutModal tests carry through unchanged. **0 net e2e scenarios** — the link-out is purely informational; the existing burger-menu → About flow covers the modal mechanics, the changelog content lives on GitHub and is verified by readers, not by Playwright. **Z-bump only** — `package.json#version` UNCHANGED at `0.2.0` per §17.82: adding a hyperlink and an external markdown file is invisible to the running app's wire shape; zero JSON wire-shape change, zero snapshot delta, no `appMajor` migration concern. **Bundle delta**: 258.x KB / ~70.x KB gzip → 259.x KB / ~70.x KB gzip (one new row in the About modal's template + one new `CHANGELOG_URL` constant + one new test assertion; module count unchanged). Actual measurement at gate-run time. **`sonar.projectVersion` bump `0.2.12 → 0.2.13`** + ~25-line history docblock recording the §17.82 track closeout, the O6 link-out decision, the four artefacts, and the new Cursor rule pairing with `keep-readme-in-sync.mdc`. **§17.72 corrected procedure followed** — content COMMITTED FIRST on `feature/17.87-release-notes`, then `npm run sonar:gate` runs on the committed tip. **§17.82 / §17.86 / §17.86b worked example fully closed**: the chain "X-bump trigger → CI guard fires → operator either bumps X or reverts → running build refuses to silently corrupt → operator sees an actionable banner → operator picks read-only or reset" landed at §17.86b; §17.87 adds the missing "what changed between versions?" surface so the operator can answer their own follow-up question ("why does this build behave differently?") from inside the About modal. **What this strand does NOT do**: doesn't auto-generate CHANGELOG.md from git log / PR titles / conventional commits (the §17.82 plan considered this; the human-curated approach is chosen because it matches the existing `keep-readme-in-sync.mdc` precedent — the kiosk codebase already ships rules that say "edit alongside the change", and the CHANGELOG joins that pattern instead of a separate automation surface); doesn't bundle the CHANGELOG content into the app (the kiosk has network access; the link-out is sufficient; a future air-gapped strand could `?raw`-import the file via Vite if needed); doesn't add a CI workflow checking that CHANGELOG.md is touched alongside `package.json#version` (deferred — the Cursor rule + agent discipline is the front-line enforcement; a stricter CI guard joins `check:version-bump` if discipline slips). New artefacts: NEW `CHANGELOG.md`, NEW `.cursor/rules/keep-changelog-in-sync.mdc`, edits to `src/adapters/ui/modal/AboutModal.ts` + `src/test/unit/adapters/ui/modal/AboutModal.test.ts` + `README.md`, this status-table row, `sonar-project.properties` bump + history docblock. **Track close**: §17.87 closes the §17.82-§17.87 versioning + Pages-deploy policy execution sequence end-to-end. The next strand on master is **§17.95** — first v5 round-7 implementation strand (`Computation<T>` abstract + 6 concrete singletons + `ComputationKind` enum + `ComputationRegistry` + `EmptyChildrenError`; ~280 lines provisional; strictly additive, all-new files in `src/domain/computation/`). |
| **Runtime version-mismatch handler — UI surface half (`<version-mismatch-banner>` Lit component + `<tree-map-screen>` injection + `main.ts` callback replacement + `isReadOnly` option on `LocalStorageBoardCollectionRepository.save()` — §17.86b)** | `feature/17.86b-version-mismatch-banner` | 1032 → 1038 _(unit, +6 net)_ + 116 _(e2e, unchanged)_ | DONE | UI half of the §17.86 runtime version-mismatch handler — completes the deferred banner-surface scope while §17.86 (the persistence half) keeps the §17.82 worked example's Y-bump intact. Pairs the §17.85 CI guard ("no breaking merge without an X-bump") and §17.86's `load()` classifier ("don't silently corrupt incompatible persisted data") with the operator-facing surface: when a future X-bumped build (or a forced rollback to an older build) hits a mismatch, the kiosk shows a non-blocking strip across the top of the focused panel with kind-specific copy + two action buttons. Five coupled artefacts ship: **(a) NEW `<version-mismatch-banner>` Lit element** at `src/adapters/ui/shell/VersionMismatchBanner.ts` — a non-modal, non-blocking inline strip. NOT placed under `adapters/ui/modal/` because it is **not** a modal (no backdrop, no focus trap, no Escape capture, no z-index stacking). Renders kind-specific copy (`future-data` vs `migration-failed`) interpolating `persistedMajor` + `runningMajor`, plus three buttons each dispatching its own bubbling+composed CustomEvent: `version-mismatch-continue-read-only`, `version-mismatch-reset`, and `version-mismatch-dismiss` (the close-X for "I acknowledge but didn't pick an action"). When the `info: VersionMismatchInfo | null` property is `null`, `render()` returns `nothing` so the banner element is in the DOM as a 0-height grid participant — keeps the host's 3-row grid template stable regardless of mismatch state. **(b) `<tree-map-screen>` injection** — three new methods (`surfaceMismatchBanner(info)`, `dismissMismatchBanner()`, `currentMismatchBanner` getter), one `@state` slot (`versionMismatch: VersionMismatchInfo \| null`), one private dismiss-collector handler (listens to all three banner events and clears the screen state in a single place — the operator-chosen side-effect lives in the composition root, not here). The screen's host CSS `grid-template-rows` flips from `auto 1fr` (top bar + layout) to `auto auto 1fr` (top bar + banner row + layout) so the banner gets its own track between the permanent top bar and the focused-panel layout. The banner element is always rendered (whether or not `versionMismatch` is non-null) so the grid keeps the 1fr layout track regardless. Render slot inserted between `${topBar}` and `<div class="layout">` in both the `view === null` (loading) and `view !== null` branches. **(c) `isReadOnly?: () => boolean` option** on `LocalStorageBoardCollectionRepositoryOptions` — when the getter returns `true`, `save()` short-circuits with a silent `return` before the `serialize()` + `setItem` call. The composition root maintains an external closure flag (`let readOnlyMode = false`) that the banner's "Continue read-only" handler flips; this design avoids mutating the repository instance (no setter method on the adapter), keeps the read-only-mode concern at the composition root, and lets every existing `repo.save()` call site become a no-op without any branching in the call sites themselves. **Why a getter, not a boolean field**: the closure flag is set AFTER the repo is constructed (the banner is shown AFTER `repo.load()` raises the mismatch); a snapshot boolean would freeze at construction time. The getter is called fresh on every save. **(d) `main.ts` callback replacement** — pre-§17.86b, the composition root's `onVersionMismatch` was a `console.warn`. Post-§17.86b: (i) declares `let pendingMismatch: VersionMismatchInfo | null = null` and `let readOnlyMode = false` BEFORE `repo` construction; (ii) `onVersionMismatch` writes to `pendingMismatch` instead of warning (the callback fires synchronously inside `await BoardCollectionService.create(repo, idGen)`, BEFORE the `<tree-map-screen>` lookup below); (iii) `isReadOnly: () => readOnlyMode` plumbed into the same constructor options; (iv) AFTER the screen lookup, if `pendingMismatch !== null`, `screen.surfaceMismatchBanner(pendingMismatch)` replays it; (v) two listeners on the screen: `version-mismatch-continue-read-only` flips `readOnlyMode = true` (banner already cleared its own state via the screen's `handleMismatchDismiss`), `version-mismatch-reset` runs `window.localStorage.removeItem(BOARD_COLLECTION_STORAGE_KEY)` + `window.location.reload()`. The "Reset" path uses the persistence module's exported `STORAGE_KEY` constant (re-exported in `main.ts`'s import as `BOARD_COLLECTION_STORAGE_KEY`) so the composition root clears the exact same key the adapter reads — no constant duplication. **(e) Unit tests** — `+6 net unit tests` (1032 → 1038, +1 test file): 3 in NEW `src/test/unit/adapters/ui/shell/VersionMismatchBanner.test.ts` (banner returns `nothing` when info is null; both kinds render kind-specific copy + `data-kind`; all three buttons dispatch their bubbling+composed CustomEvents), 2 in the existing `TreeMapScreen.test.ts` under a new `version-mismatch banner seam (§17.86b)` describe block (banner rendered empty by default with surface+dismiss round-trip; all three banner events clear the screen state via the dismiss-collector handler), 1 in the existing `LocalStorageBoardCollectionRepository.test.ts` under a new `§17.86b read-only mode` describe block (save is a no-op when isReadOnly returns true and resumes writing when the closure flag flips back to false). Test bodies kept compact (table-driven `for` loops over the three button events and the read-only flip case) to fit the §17.85 300-line ceiling — first §17.86b gate run measured `new_lines=455`, trim run measured under 300. **0 net e2e scenarios** — the version-mismatch flow is only observable after a forced major-version mismatch which no e2e fixture can produce without the Phase E codec migration (~§17.97) shipping the first concrete migrator; deferred until that lands. **Bundle delta**: 257.48 KB / 69.98 KB gzip → 258.x KB / ~70.x KB gzip (banner component + screen wiring; module count +1 for the new Lit element file). **Strand sizing**: ~230 net new src lines (banner component ~105; screen injection ~50; persistence option ~10; main.ts wire ~25; tests ~155); well under the 300 ceiling. **Z-bump only** — `package.json#version` UNCHANGED at `0.2.0` per §17.82: this strand surfaces an already-existing callback through a UI element, with zero JSON wire-shape change and zero snapshot delta (the §17.85 guard does not fire). **§17.86 Open Question — surface-mechanism choice consequence**: §17.86 picked the adapter-callback approach over expanding `BoardCollectionRepository.load()`'s return type or introducing a new `VersionMismatchPort`; §17.86b validates that choice — the entire UI half slots in without touching any port file (no `BoardCollectionRepository.ts`, no application service signatures). The only files touched outside the new banner component are the screen (a Lit shell element, not a port), `main.ts` (the composition root), and the persistence ADAPTER (the `isReadOnly` option — adapter-specific, not a port concern). **§17.82 / §17.86 worked example completed**: the full chain "X-bump trigger → CI guard fires → operator either bumps X or reverts → running build refuses to silently corrupt → operator sees an actionable banner → operator picks read-only or reset" is now implemented end-to-end. The remaining §17.87 release-notes strand is optional polish that doesn't affect this chain. **`sonar.projectVersion` bump `0.2.10 → 0.2.11`** + ~50-line history docblock recording the §17.86b artefact list + the §17.82 track close-out. **§17.72 corrected procedure followed** — content COMMITTED FIRST on `feature/17.86b-version-mismatch-banner`, then `npm run sonar:gate` runs on the committed tip. **What this strand does NOT do**: doesn't add a CI workflow step for `check:version-bump` (§17.85 deferred that as a manual operator-wire); doesn't register any concrete envelope migrator (Phase E codec migration ~§17.97 ships the first); doesn't generate release notes (§17.87 optional strand); doesn't add e2e coverage (deferred until a real codec migration produces an observable mismatch). New artefacts: NEW `src/adapters/ui/shell/VersionMismatchBanner.ts`, NEW `src/test/unit/adapters/ui/shell/VersionMismatchBanner.test.ts`, edits to `src/adapters/ui/shell/TreeMapScreen.ts` + `src/main.ts` + `src/adapters/persistence/LocalStorageBoardCollectionRepository.ts` + existing TreeMapScreen + LocalStorageBoardCollectionRepository test files, this status-table row, `sonar-project.properties` bump + ~50-line history docblock. **Track close**: §17.86b closes the §17.82-§17.86 versioning + Pages-deploy policy execution sequence except for the optional §17.87 release-notes strand. The v3-retirement track (§17.95+ on the renumbered v5 plan, starting with the `Computation<T>` hierarchy) is the next strand on master. |
| **Runtime version-mismatch handler — persistence half (`parseMajor` + `APP_MAJOR` in `src/version.ts`; `appMajor` field on the wire envelope; `LocalStorageBoardCollectionRepository.load()` gains a 4-branch classifier with `onVersionMismatch` callback + empty `migrators` registry stub; `console.warn` placeholder in `main.ts`; banner UI deferred to §17.86b; `package.json#version` `0.1.0 → 0.2.0` Y-bump per §17.82 worked example — §17.86)** | `feature/17.86-runtime-version-mismatch` | 956 → 965 _(unit, +9 net)_ + 116 _(e2e, unchanged)_ | DONE | Fourth execution strand of the §17.82 versioning + Pages-deployment policy and the **runtime safety-net half** that pairs with §17.85's CI guard. §17.85 makes it impossible to MERGE a breaking schema change without an X-bump; §17.86 makes the running kiosk REFUSE to load incompatible persisted data after a future X-bump (rather than silently corrupting it). Five coupled artefacts ship: **(a) `src/version.ts` gains `parseMajor(version)` + `APP_MAJOR`** — `parseMajor` extracts the leading integer of `MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH` via a strict regex and throws on malformed input (the source value flows from `package.json#version` which SemVer gates at build time; parse failure = build configuration bug, not a runtime fault to swallow). `APP_MAJOR` is the eagerly-computed major of `APP_VERSION`, consumed by the persistence adapter as the "running build's major" reference. **(b) `WireEnvelope` gains an optional `appMajor?: number` field** in `LocalStorageBoardCollectionRepository`. Optional on the type so envelopes from pre-§17.86 builds load cleanly via the new "legacy" branch in `load()`. `serialize` always stamps the field; `deserialize` reads it via the new classifier path. **(c) `LocalStorageBoardCollectionRepository.load()` gains a 4-branch classifier** (the heart of the strand) — a private `classifyAndMaybeMigrate(envelope)` method that routes: **equal** (`persistedMajor === appMajor`) → load normally (hot path; no callback); **lower** (`persistedMajor < appMajor`) → walk the injected `migrators` chain in order; first migrator that returns a non-`undefined` envelope wins; if the chain is empty or all return `undefined`, invoke `onVersionMismatch({ kind: "migration-failed", … })` and **load with default tolerance** (the §17.42 forward-compat pattern); **higher** (`persistedMajor > appMajor`) → "future data" the running build can't safely consume; invoke `onVersionMismatch({ kind: "future-data", … })` and **seed + persist** the seed (overwriting the future payload with the safe fallback; the §17.86b banner-caller may still surface a "Reset and lose data" / "Continue read-only" prompt on top of that safe default); **legacy** (`appMajor` field absent entirely) → silently load (matches §17.42 / §17.14 / §17.63 tolerance pattern; first boot after upgrade should "just work" — the next save stamps the running major and brings the envelope into the §17.86 regime). **(d) Three new constructor options** on `LocalStorageBoardCollectionRepositoryOptions`: `appMajor?: number` (defaults to `APP_MAJOR` from `src/version.ts`; tests inject a fixed value to simulate mismatch without rebuilding the bundle); `onVersionMismatch?: (info: VersionMismatchInfo) => void` (defaults to a `noop`; composition root replaces with a banner-surface callback in §17.86b); `migrators?: readonly EnvelopeMigrator[]` (defaults to `[]`; Phase E codec migration ~§17.97 registers the first concrete migrator). Two new public types exported alongside: `VersionMismatchInfo = { kind: "migration-failed" \| "future-data"; persistedMajor: number; runningMajor: number }` (discriminated union — exhaustive matching at the call site); `EnvelopeMigrator = (envelope, from, to) => WireEnvelope \| undefined` (chain-of-responsibility return type; `undefined` means "I can't handle this jump, next migrator please"). **(e) Composition root callback** in `main.ts` — a `console.warn` placeholder logging the mismatch kind + persisted/running majors. §17.86b replaces this with `screen.surfaceMismatchBanner(info)` (or similar) once the `<version-mismatch-banner>` Lit component lands. **§17.86 Open Question — surface mechanism**: the persistence adapter could have expanded `BoardCollectionRepository.load()`'s return type to include mismatch metadata (touching the port + every test that uses `BoardCollectionService.create(repo, idGen)` ≈ 30 sites), OR routed through a separate `VersionMismatchPort` (introducing a new port for a single-shot event). This strand picks **callback option on the adapter constructor** — port stays unchanged, no test churn, the only files touching the new types are the adapter + main.ts + the new tests. Architecturally: mismatch is an adapter-specific concern (only LocalStorage has an envelope shape with `v` + `appMajor`; a future IndexedDB or remote-API adapter would handle versioning differently), so surfacing it through an adapter-level constructor option is the right scope. The port stays narrow + abstract; the adapter owns its versioning concerns. **(f) Snapshot regeneration**: `src/test/snapshots/wire-envelope.snap.json` gains a single `"appMajor": 0` line — the canonical fixture's `LocalStorageBoardCollectionRepository` instance defaults to `APP_MAJOR` (currently 0 per `package.json#version = "0.2.0"`). The `wire-tree.snap.json` is **unchanged** — the per-tree wire shape (from `jsonCodec.encode`) is not envelope-versioned (only the collection envelope is). **§17.85 CI guard interaction**: the wire-envelope snapshot is MODIFIED (not added), so the §17.85 guard fires unless an X-bump is paired. Per §17.82's worked example "adding a new optional wire field is backward-compat (Y-bump, not X-bump)", this strand bumps `package.json#version` Y (`0.1.0 → 0.2.0`) — not X — and uses the **§17.85 escape hatch** `CHECK_BUMP_OVERRIDE=1` (introduced exactly for this case).
