Both forward + backward compatibility hold by construction: an old build reading a new envelope ignores `appMajor` (the existing `deserialize` only reads the explicit fields it knows about; unknown fields are silently tolerated, mirroring the §17.42 pattern); a new build reading an old envelope routes through the legacy branch (silent load, no banner). **+9 net unit tests** (956 → 965): +3 in `src/test/unit/version.test.ts` (a new `describe("parseMajor / APP_MAJOR (§17.86)")` block — happy-path parsing of three SemVer triples, throw-on-malformed for three malformed inputs, `APP_MAJOR === parseMajor(APP_VERSION)` consistency check); +6 in `src/test/unit/adapters/persistence/LocalStorageBoardCollectionRepository.test.ts` (a new `describe("§17.86 runtime version-mismatch handling")` block — `buildEnvelope(major?)` helper produces a real-codec-encoded tree wrapped in an envelope with controlled `appMajor`; 6 tests cover the 4 mismatch branches + the legacy fall-through + the write-side `appMajor` stamping). Tests use `vi.fn()` for the callback (asserts call count + payload) and inject `appMajor: 2` to simulate a future-major running build against a `persistedMajor: 1` / `5` / `undefined` payload. **0 net e2e scenarios** (banner UI deferred to §17.86b; mismatch is unobservable at the e2e level today). **Bundle delta**: 246.26 KB / 66.86 KB gzip → 247.30 KB / 67.23 KB gzip / 92 modules (+1.04 KB raw / +0.37 KB gzip; new types + classifier + version helpers; module count unchanged). **Strand sizing**: ~250 raw new lines projected: `version.ts` (+28), `version.test.ts` (+22), `LocalStorageBoardCollectionRepository.ts` (~80 net, with refactor extracting `envelopeToSnapshot` from the renamed `deserialize` for migrator-chain reuse), `LocalStorageBoardCollectionRepository.test.ts` (~130 — the longest single file), `main.ts` (+12), `wire-envelope.snap.json` (+1). Tight against the 300 ceiling — the split to §17.86b absorbed the UI side's ~150 lines (banner component + screen seam + tests). **`sonar.projectVersion` bump `0.2.2 → 0.2.3`** + ~35-line history docblock recording the persistence-only split, the surface-mechanism decision (callback over port-expansion), the Y-bump justification, and the §17.85 override usage. **§17.72 corrected procedure followed** — content COMMITTED FIRST, then `npm run sonar:gate` runs on the committed tip with `CHECK_BUMP_OVERRIDE=1` if the operator invokes the guard locally. **What this strand does NOT do**: doesn't ship a `<version-mismatch-banner>` Lit component (§17.86b); doesn't inject the banner into `<tree-map-screen>` (§17.86b); doesn't register any concrete migrator (Phase E codec migration ~§17.97 ships the first); doesn't change the `BoardCollectionRepository` port (mismatch surfaces through a constructor callback on the adapter — the port stays unchanged); doesn't add e2e coverage for the mismatch banner (deferred until §17.86b adds a user-observable surface). New artefacts: edits to `src/version.ts` + `src/adapters/persistence/LocalStorageBoardCollectionRepository.ts` + `src/main.ts` + `src/test/unit/version.test.ts` + `src/test/unit/adapters/persistence/LocalStorageBoardCollectionRepository.test.ts`, regen of `src/test/snapshots/wire-envelope.snap.json`, bump of `package.json#version` `0.1.0 → 0.2.0`, this status-table row, `sonar-project.properties` bump + history docblock. **Next strand**: §17.86b — `<version-mismatch-banner>` Lit component + `<tree-map-screen>` integration + `main.ts` callback replacement (~150 lines provisional). |
| **Schema snapshot guard (`wire-tree.snap.json` + `wire-envelope.snap.json` + vitest byte-equality test + Node CI guard `bin/check-version-bump.mjs` + `.gitattributes` LF-pin + new `snapshot:update` / `check:version-bump` npm scripts + `@types/node` dev-dep — §17.85)** | `feature/17.85-schema-snapshot-guard` | 954 → 956 _(unit, +2 net)_ + 116 _(e2e, unchanged)_ | DONE | Third execution strand of the §17.82 versioning + Pages-deployment policy and the **safety-net half** of "X-bump on JSON-structure breakage" (the §17.84 strand stamped the version into the bundle + a UI surface; §17.85 makes it physically impossible to silently break the wire shape — any modification of an existing snapshot file without an X-bump fails CI). Four coupled artefacts ship together: **(a) Canonical fixture builder** `src/test/snapshots/treeFixture.ts` — two pure factories `buildSnapshotTree()` + `buildSnapshotEnvelope()` constructing a deterministic 3-node tree (TextNode "root" with one seeded `"hello" @ 2026-05-10` entry; two BSC children: recorded `computed=false eligible=true` with full Objective `0 → 100 by 2026-12-31` + two-entry history at `2026-04-22` / `2026-04-23`, and computed `computed=true eligible=false` with empty history). The fixture deliberately exercises **every wire branch the codec touches**: TextNode + recorded BSC + computed BSC, Objective with explicit non-sentinel `targetDate`, Unit + Weight + Description value-object round-trips, parent/child attach order. Branches deliberately NOT covered: deeper than 1-level recursion (recursive by construction — extra depth wouldn't exercise new code), the open-ended `targetDate` sentinel (omitted-on-wire, covered by `jsonCodec.test.ts`), the §17.42 legacy `freshDateColor` field (tolerated on decode, never emitted on encode, can't appear in a write-side snapshot). **(b) Two snapshot files** `src/test/snapshots/wire-tree.snap.json` + `wire-envelope.snap.json` — the pretty-printed (2-space indent + trailing newline) outputs of `jsonCodec.encode(fixture1)` + the LocalStorage repo's serialization of `buildSnapshotEnvelope()`. Pretty-printed for git-diff readability; byte-equality assertion compares pretty-printed-actual to file-contents. **(c) Vitest snapshot guard** `src/test/unit/adapters/persistence/wireSnapshots.test.ts` (2 tests) — one per snapshot. Asserts byte-equality with an ergonomic failure message: `"Wire shape changed. If intentional, run npm run snapshot:update AND bump package.json#version's X (per SPEC §17.82 / §17.85)."`. **§17.85 Open Question O1 decided**: rather than widen `LocalStorageBoardCollectionRepository.serialize` to public (the plan's recommendation), the test observes the envelope byte-string through the **public `save()` contract** writing to an in-memory `Storage`-shaped fake (mirrors the pattern in `LocalStorageBoardCollectionRepository.test.ts`'s `InMemoryStorage`) — zero production-surface change, identical observability by construction (`save()` is a 1-liner over `serialize`). The deviation from O1 is documented in the test file's leading docblock so a future reader doesn't re-litigate the choice. **Update mode** — `SNAPSHOT_UPDATE=1` flips the test from assert-and-fail-on-diff to write-and-pass; consumed via the `snapshot:update` npm script wired to a small `bin/snapshot-update.mjs` Node wrapper (cross-platform: `spawn` with `shell: true` so the Windows operator's `.cmd` shim works AND the Linux CI's POSIX `vitest` shebang works, both from the same script). **(d) CI guard** `bin/check-version-bump.mjs` (Node ESM, intentionally NOT bash — the plan called for a `.sh` script but Node is cross-platform out of the box, runs identically on the Windows operator's laptop + the Linux GitHub Actions runner with zero shell-emulation, and lets the guard be reasoned about as TypeScript-adjacent code rather than as a foreign shell artefact; the §17.85 plan's R4 risk row anticipated this kind of cross-platform sidestep). Logic: (i) resolves a base ref via priority chain `GITHUB_BASE_REF` (CI PR context) → `CHECK_BUMP_BASE` (manual override) → `origin/master` → `master` (local fallback); (ii) runs `git diff --name-status <base>...HEAD` and inspects entries matching `src/test/snapshots/*.snap.json`; (iii) **firing rule** — ONLY `M` (modified) entries trip the guard, `A` (added) entries are baseline-establishing (so this very strand's introduction of the snapshot files passes cleanly with no X-bump), `D` (deleted) entries are ignored as intentional cleanup; (iv) for each modified entry, reads `package.json#version` at HEAD vs base, parses the major component, fails with an ergonomic message if HEAD's major <= base's major; (v) `CHECK_BUMP_OVERRIDE=1` escape hatch for the §17.82-worked-example case (adding a new optional wire field is Y-bump per §17.82, but the snapshot file is necessarily modified — the operator sets the override after manual review confirms backward-compat). The `M`-only firing rule is a deliberate refinement of the plan's "any snapshot diff" wording: the plan's literal reading would have required THIS strand to itself bump X (which would have been wrong — adding the snapshot files doesn't break any existing localStorage payload), so the guard treats additions as baseline events. The override env var covers the residual Y-bump-with-snapshot-diff case. **(e) `.gitattributes` LF pin** — `src/test/snapshots/*.snap.json text eol=lf` forces LF on disk regardless of platform `core.autocrlf`. Without this rule the Windows operator's `core.autocrlf=true` would silently flip the snapshot files to CRLF in the working tree and the byte-equality test would fail with a baffling whitespace-only diff (the test's `JSON.stringify` always emits LF, the file `readFileSync` would return CRLF). New repo-wide artefact — `.gitattributes` did not previously exist; this is its inaugural rule. Future strands can layer additional eol/text rules onto it as needed. **(f) `@types/node` dev-dep added** — required because the test file uses `node:fs` + `node:url` + `node:path` and is under `src/` (tsc-checked); pre-§17.85 these Node built-ins were only used by `vite.config.ts` + `src/test/e2e/**` which are both outside the tsconfig's `include: ["src"]` scope. Small, type-only, zero production-bundle impact. **+2 net unit tests** (954 → 956 — the two snapshot guards; 0 net at the `src/{domain,application,adapters}` layer). **0 net e2e** (CI-gate behaviour; not a user-observable surface). **0 production-bundle delta** — bundle stays at `247.58 → 246.26 KB / 66.87 → 66.86 KB gzip` (the ~1 KB delta is build-date drift from §17.84's `__APP_BUILD_DATE__` literal flipping by one day; module count unchanged at 92). The §17.85 strand adds **zero TS code under `src/{domain,application,adapters,main}`** — every new file is either in `src/test/snapshots/` (test-only), `src/test/unit/` (test-only), `bin/` (Sonar-excluded), or a top-level config file (also Sonar-excluded). **Sonar `new_lines` predicted ≈ 220** (the `treeFixture.ts` + `wireSnapshots.test.ts` are under `sonar.sources=src` but `sonar.test.inclusions` catches them as test files for coverage-exclusion purposes; they still count toward `new_lines`). Well under the 300 ceiling. **`sonar.projectVersion` bump `0.2.1 → 0.2.2`** + ~30-line history docblock recording the §17.85 design decisions (O1 alternative, M-only firing, `.gitattributes` LF pin, Node-vs-bash CI guard rationale). **§17.72 corrected procedure followed** — content COMMITTED FIRST, then `npm run sonar:gate` ran on the committed tip. **Workflow integration deferred to §17.86 / a §17.85-followup**: the `pages.yml` (and any future PR-validation workflow) calling `npm run check:version-bump` is the next planning increment — the guard script is in place, the npm script is wired, but the CI invocation point itself is not added in this strand to keep the diff under 300 lines and the change-surface tight. Operator can wire it manually via `gh workflow edit` or a 5-line `pr-validation.yml` addition. **What this strand does NOT do**: doesn't add runtime version-mismatch handling on stored envelopes (that's §17.86 — `appMajor` field + load-time compatibility branch + `<version-mismatch-banner>`); doesn't generate release notes (that's §17.87, optional); doesn't widen `serialize` to public (uses the public `save()` seam instead per the O1 alternative); doesn't wire the CI guard into a GitHub Actions step yet (deferred). New artefacts: `src/test/snapshots/treeFixture.ts` (~115 lines), `src/test/snapshots/wire-tree.snap.json` (54 lines pretty-printed), `src/test/snapshots/wire-envelope.snap.json` (64 lines pretty-printed), `src/test/unit/adapters/persistence/wireSnapshots.test.ts` (~115 lines), `bin/check-version-bump.mjs` (~160 lines, Sonar-excluded), `bin/snapshot-update.mjs` (~45 lines, Sonar-excluded), `.gitattributes` (10 lines new), edits to `package.json` (+2 npm scripts + `@types/node` devDep) + `package-lock.json`, this status-table row, `sonar-project.properties` bump + ~30-line history docblock. **Next strand**: §17.86 — runtime version-mismatch handling (envelope `appMajor` field + `deserialize` compatibility branch + non-blocking warning banner; ~220 lines provisional per §17.82 plan). |
| **v5 class-diagram round 7 — adds `ComputedNode<T>` + `ComputedBusinessScoreNode<T>` + `Computed<T>` interface + `Computation<T>` strategy hierarchy + `ComputationKind` enum + `ComputationRegistry` + `EmptyChildrenError` + `ComputationOverrideError` to the v4 redesign; retires `eligibleForParentComputation` in favour of broader `disabled` flag on `ValueNode<T>`; supersedes §17.80 Phase C/D/E sub-strand sequence (planning strand — NO TS code; deliverable is `examples/classDiagramMermaid.v5.{md,mermaid}` + renumbered Phase C+ sub-strand sequence in SPEC §17.94) — §17.94** | `feature/17.94-v5-round-7-design` | 1021 → 1021 _(unit, no change — planning strand)_ + 116 → 116 _(e2e, unchanged)_ | DONE | First strand AFTER Phase B closed at §17.93. **Operator-driven design refinement** prompted by the question "did you take into account the `ComputedNode` that is inheriting from `HistorizableValueNode` with a `computation` (enum) property?". Honest answer at the time: **no** — round 6 of the v4 diagram (locked at §17.57 → §17.79 / `examples/classDiagramMermaid.v4.{md,mermaid}`) collapsed v3's `BusinessScoreCardNode.computed: boolean` flag into a "structural rule" (a parent's value is the average of its eligible children if it has any). The §17.93 cutover surfaced 5 e2e regressions from that simplification and had to band-aid the flag back onto `BusinessScoreNode<T>` as an optional field, with explicit logging of the band-aid as cutover-time debt to be resolved by Phase C's BSCv4 wrapper. §17.94 finally delivers the polymorphic resolution that v3 → v4 was always meant to land — and along the way also retires v3's `eligibleForParentComputation` flag in favour of a broader `disabled` field on the value-node base. **Like §17.80 / §17.82 this is a planning strand**: deliverable is the written round 7 in `examples/classDiagramMermaid.v5.{md,mermaid}` + a dedicated SPEC subsection at §17.94 + a renumbered sub-strand sequence; zero TS code changes; expect Sonar to measure `new_lines ≈ 0` per the §17.80 / §17.82 measurement (mermaid + md + SPEC.md + sonar-project.properties all excluded from `sonar.sources=src`). Strand still bumps `sonar.projectVersion` per the §17.72 every-strand rule. **Round 7 — 9 additions** (full rationale in §17.94 dedicated subsection): (1) `ComputedNode<T>` — concrete leaf, sibling of TextNodeV4 / RangedValueNode under HistorizableValueNode<T>, auto-derived value, no range, no objective; replaces v3's `computed: true` BSC variant for plain computed metrics. (2) `ComputedBusinessScoreNode<T>` — concrete leaf under BusinessScoreNode<T>, auto-derived value PLUS range + objective; replaces v3's `computed: true` BSC variant when the metric is also scored against a target. (3) `Computed<T>` interface — implemented by both Computed* classes; exposes `computationKind: ComputationKind` (the persisted, UI-editable enum) AND `computation: Computation<T>` (the resolved Strategy singleton). **Hybrid Strategy + Type-Code pattern**: enum drives UI / serialisation, polymorphism drives behaviour. (4) `ComputationKind` enum — `SUM | AVERAGE | MIN | MAX | WEIGHTED_AVERAGE | COUNT`; the persistent + UI-facing discriminator that the operator flips in real time via `setComputationKind(kind)` (operator's "the computation method should be editable by the user in real time" requirement). (5) `Computation<T>` strategy hierarchy — abstract base with stateless concrete singletons: SumComputation, AverageComputation, MinComputation, MaxComputation, WeightedAverageComputation (uses `Node.weight` already on the hierarchy), CountComputation (the only T-agnostic strategy — counts non-disabled value-producing children regardless of value type). (6) `ComputationRegistry` — single static singleton resolving a ComputationKind to its Computation<T> singleton; open-closed for new strategies. (7) `ValueNode<T>.disabled : boolean` — RENAME + GENERALISATION of v3's `BusinessScoreCardNode.eligibleForParentComputation`; lives on the value-node base (single source of truth), broader semantics ("this node is parked: exclude from aggregation AND grey out in UI"). Operator's design choice ("If we remove the `eligibleForParentComputation` flag, it's up to the parent to identify the eligible children, depending on the computation method"). Every Computation<T> strategy filters disabled children out FIRST, then applies its T-type filter (numeric strategies skip TextNode children automatically; COUNT keeps them). (8) `EmptyChildrenError` — raised by `Computation<T>.apply()` if no eligible non-disabled children remain after filtering. (9) `ComputationOverrideError` — raised by `setValue` / `addValue` on Computed* nodes (operator's `history_as_audit` choice — the inherited history records the computed value at each evaluation point as an audit trail; operator cannot overwrite the computation, only edit the children or change the `computationKind`). **Eligibility flag retirement trade-off** explicitly accepted: v3 §17.28 added `setEligibleForParentComputation` so the operator could exclude a specific child from its parent's average **without deleting the child or hiding it from the UI**. v5 retires that narrower-scope flag; the v5 `disabled` flag is broader (parks the child in BOTH the aggregation AND the UI). Operators that relied on the v3 "exclude from aggregation but keep visually active" pattern lose that capability — Phase C migration script will surface any kiosk that did so and ask the operator to either accept the new disabled-or-active binary, or restructure (e.g. move the excluded node under a non-Computed parent). The §17.93 cutover-time band-aid (`computed` + `eligibleForParentComputation` lifted onto BSN) is **fully retired** at §17.99 (renumbered Phase C BSCv4 wrapper strand): both fields disappear from BSN, replaced by the round-7 type hierarchy + the broader `disabled` flag. **§17.80 Phase C/D/E/F sub-strand sequence supersession**: §17.80's original Phase C-F numbering (§17.88 = AddChildServiceV4, §17.89 = EditNodeServiceV4, ..., §17.99 = delete v3 nodes, etc.) was already partially consumed by Phase A.2 + Phase B.1-B.5 (the §17.88 row's "numbering note" documents the renumbering). §17.94 supersedes the Phase C+ sequence with a round-7-aware version that adds 4 new strands at the front (§17.95-§17.98 — round-7 type additions BEFORE write-side migration needs them) then renumbers the original Phase C-F strands forward by ~6 slots. Full table in §17.94 dedicated subsection. The original §17.80 Phase C/D/E/F tables stay in place as historical record with a "SUPERSEDED — see §17.94" pointer. **Companion artefacts updated**: (a) `examples/classDiagramMermaid.v5.mermaid` — NEW canonical source, ~370 lines including a 70-line round-7 header documenting the 9 additions and the eligibility-flag retirement trade-off. (b) `examples/classDiagramMermaid.v5.md` — NEW IDE-preview wrapper with embedded diagram, "What v5 changes" prose section, companion artefacts list, and rollout sketch slotting v5 into the migration plan. (c) `examples/classDiagramMermaid.v4.md` — modified one line: companion list now points forward to v5 as the successor (so v4 readers find their way to the live target). v4 stays frozen as the historical record of what Phase A/B implemented; v5 is the live target. **0 net unit tests** (planning strand; tests unchanged at 1021). **0 net e2e scenarios** (no UI surface change). **0 bundle delta** (zero TS code; no v4/v5 module reaches the bundle until §17.95+ ships actual implementation). **`sonar.projectVersion` bump `0.2.7 → 0.2.8`** — every-strand bump per the §17.72 corrected rule, even for zero-TS-code planning strands. The bump line itself is in `sonar-project.properties` at project root which is OUTSIDE `sonar.sources=src`, so this entire docblock is FREE against the leak budget. **§17.72 corrected procedure followed** — content COMMITTED FIRST on the feature branch, then `npm run sonar:gate` runs on the committed tip when Sonar is back up (Sonar gate environment-blocked at strand-time per the `.env.sonar` reset noted at §17.89). **Strand-time gate prediction**: `new_lines ≈ 0` (matching §17.80 / §17.82 measurements); gate PASSES trivially. **§17.78 lesson re-applied** — `.commit-msg-*.tmp` gitignore rule continues working. New artefacts: `examples/classDiagramMermaid.v5.mermaid` (NEW, ~370 lines), `examples/classDiagramMermaid.v5.md` (NEW, ~480 lines), `examples/classDiagramMermaid.v4.md` (1-line companion-list update), this status-table row, SPEC §17.94 dedicated subsection (~250 lines new), `sonar-project.properties` bump + ~50-line history docblock. **What this strand does NOT do**: no TS code touched; no `package.json` touched; no `vite.config.ts` touched; no GH Actions workflow touched; no UI element added; no test added or removed. The v5 diagram is a pure documentation artefact; the §17.95+ sub-strands execute the round-7 plan mechanically. **Operator's final design decisions** (recorded in §17.94 dedicated subsection D-numbered list): D1 = file target (v5 pair, NOT v4 in-place mutation, per the v4 doc's own "round 6 converged → successor diagram" guidance); D2 = computation enum values (full 6-value set including WEIGHTED_AVERAGE + COUNT); D3 = Computed BSC handling (interface-based, both ComputedNode and ComputedBusinessScoreNode implement Computed<T>); D4 = eligibility (drop `eligibleForParentComputation`, add broader `disabled` on ValueNode); D5 = history on ComputedNode (audit-trail, setValue/addValue forbidden via ComputationOverrideError). **Next strand**: §17.95 — first round-7 implementation strand: `Computation<T>` abstract + 6 concrete singletons + `ComputationKind` enum + `ComputationRegistry` (~250 lines provisional; strictly additive, all-new files in `src/domain/computation/`). |
| **v3-retirement migration Phase B.5 — main.ts read-cutover (wires §17.91 viewModelMapperV4 into composition root via §17.88 adapter; cutover-time discovery reverses §17.89 design call by lifting `computed` + `eligibleForParentComputation` onto v4 BSN; closes Phase B) — §17.93** | `feature/17.93-main-read-cutover` | 1018 → 1021 _(unit, +3 net)_ + 115 → 116 _(e2e, +1 from burger-menu fix)_ | DONE | Seventh execution strand of the §17.80 v3-retirement migration plan; fifth Phase B strand AND **closes Phase B**. The first strand to modify `main.ts` (the composition root), so first time a v4 module reaches the production bundle. Wires §17.91 `mapFocusedToViewModelV4` into the composition root by adapting the focused subtree v3 → v4 on every refresh via the §17.88 `v4TreeFromV3Root` adapter (per §17.91's design intent, same VM contract → zero view-layer changes). v3 nav stays unchanged because its API is v3-typed and the write services downstream (`AddChildService`, `EditNodeService`, `ImportExportService`) still consume v3 nodes — write-side migration is Phase D. §17.92's v4 nav stays unused at composition root, ready for Phase C/D consumption. **Cutover-time discovery — §17.89 design call partially reversed**. The §17.89 docblock advertised the structural rule as "identical numbers on the typical kiosk shape"; running the e2e at the cutover proved otherwise. **5 e2e failures surfaced**: (a) `mixedComputed.EmptyLeaf` had `computed=true` + own history `[{value:0}]` + zero children; v3 honoured the flag → empty value area (childrenCount n=0); v4's pure structural rule rendered the placeholder ("0 %"). (b) `zeroEligible.{INELIGIBLE-A,INELIGIBLE-B}` carried `eligibleForParentComputation=false` (with TextNode third child); v3 excluded them all → "3 children" (childrenCount n=3); v4 included A and B → "15.0 %" (mean of 10 + 20). (c) `mixedComputed.Root` expected 80 (v3 mean of ChildA.OWN history=100 + ChildB=60 — one-level rule, ignoring grandchildren); v4's recursive aggregation produced 70 (mean of ChildA.aggregate=80 + ChildB=60 — recursing through ChildA's grandchildren). All three regressions are **real existing kiosk patterns**, not test-only artifacts. **§17.93 reverses all three by extending §17.91's "lift onto BSN as partial Phase C deferral" pattern**: (i) Adds `computed?: boolean` (default `false`) to `BusinessScoreNode` constructor — threaded through §17.81 adapter from `node3.computed`, gated in `computedValueV4`'s leaf-with-history branch (a flagged-computed leaf returns `childrenCount n=0` even when own history exists). (ii) Adds `eligibleForParentComputation?: boolean` (default `true`) to BusinessScoreNode constructor — threaded through §17.81 adapter from `node3.eligibleForParentComputation`, gated in `collectEligibleChildren` (a BSN with `eligibleForParentComputation=false` is excluded from its parent's mean). (iii) Reverts the `effectiveNumericValue` recursion to v3's one-level rule: prefer the child's OWN most-recent history value when present; only recurse via `computedValueV4(child)` when the child has no own history (graceful fallback for the v3 crash case where `currentValue()` would throw `EmptyHistoryError`). `currentValueDateIsoV4` also gets the same `computed` gate (a flagged-computed leaf shows no own date — date comes from children or null). **§17.91 unit pattern extended consistently**: just as §17.91 lifted `unit` onto BSN as partial resolution of §17.80 D1 (full resolution moves to BSCv4 wrapper at Phase C), §17.93 lifts `computed` + `eligibleForParentComputation` for the same reason — Phase C's BSCv4 wrapper will absorb all three fields and remove them from BSN. The §17.80 D5 narrative ("v4 doesn't model contributions on the Node directly") is **partially preserved**: `eligibleForParentComputation` keeps its v3 name on BSN as a temporary slot, but the underlying eligibility model becomes structural-with-flag-override (RangedValueNode + flag, not a separate `ContributesToParent` capability — the capability stays dropped per §17.81). **Bonus fix landed in this strand**: the §17.84 burger-menu-About strand forgot to update the e2e scenario asserting "exactly 4 items"; my §17.93 cutover sanity checks caught it (5 items now: import / export / boards / settings / about). Updated `burger_menu.feature` to "5 items" with explicit §17.84 + §17.93 attribution comment. **+3 net unit tests** in `computedValueV4.test.ts` covering the three new behaviors: eligibility filter excludes ineligible BSN children, flagged-computed leaf with own history returns childrenCount n=0, flagged-computed parent ignores own history and aggregates from eligible children. **+1 net e2e test** (burger-menu count fix). **Strand sizing**: ~600 raw lines (BSN +30 / adapter +5 / computedValueV4 +30 / currentValueDateV4 +12 / main.ts +25 / new tests +60 / SPEC +95 / sonar +75 / e2e fix +12); projected Sonar new_lines ~250-280 (under 300 with the cutover's main.ts deletions netting). **Bundle delta**: 246.26 → 252.08 KB raw / 66.86 → 68.58 KB gzip / +14 modules entering production (v4 mapper + v4 aggregation + v4 capacity + v4 BSN/SRN/TextNodeV4 + v4 adapter all reachable now). **Per-refresh re-adaptation cost**: wasteful at scale but tolerable on the kiosk's small trees (≤12 children × ~5-deep ~ 150 nodes max); the alternative — maintaining a synced v4 shadow tree — is reserved for Phase D when the write services migrate and the v3 root retires entirely. **`sonar.projectVersion` bump 0.2.6 → 0.2.7** + ~75-line history docblock at project root (FREE against the leak budget) recording the cutover discoveries + the §17.89 reversal rationale + the Phase B closeout. **Critical cutover gate green**: 1021/1021 unit tests + 116/116 e2e tests pass; tsc clean; eslint clean. **§17.78 lesson holds** — `.commit-msg-*.tmp` gitignore rule continues working. **§17.72 corrected procedure followed** — content COMMITTED FIRST, then `npm run sonar:gate` runs on the committed tip (Sonar gate environment-blocked at strand-time per the `.env.sonar` reset noted at §17.89). **What this strand does NOT do**: doesn't migrate write services (AddChildService / EditNodeService / ImportExportService still consume v3 — Phase D); doesn't switch nav to §17.92's v4 service (downstream write services need v3 nodes; the v4 nav becomes useful at Phase D); doesn't delete v3 mapper / v3 aggregation / v3 capacity (Phase F retires them); doesn't ship BSCv4 wrapper (Phase C). **Branch is stacked off §17.92** — operator's merge train is now §17.88 → §17.89 → §17.90 → §17.91 → §17.92 → §17.93 → master, six pushed feature branches. **Phase B complete** — all 5 read-side migration sub-strands shipped + cutover landed and gated. **Next phase**: §17.94+ Phase C — BSCv4 wrapper + write-side migration. The §17.91/§17.93 lifted fields (`unit`, `computed`, `eligibleForParentComputation`) on BusinessScoreNode become BSCv4 fields and are removed from BSN. |
| **v3-retirement migration Phase B.4 — `TreeNavigationServiceV4` + `TreeNavigationPortV4` (v4-aware navigation service consuming v4 `Tree`; uses Node.parent O(1) lookup instead of v3's recursive scan; same API surface as v3 service) — §17.92** | `feature/17.92-treeNavigationServiceV4` | 1003 → 1018 _(unit, +15 net)_ + 116 _(e2e, unchanged)_ | DONE | Sixth execution strand of the §17.80 v3-retirement migration plan; fourth Phase B strand. **Strictly additive return** — after §17.91's first non-additive Phase B strand (which had to modify BusinessScoreNode for the unit field), §17.92 returns to the additive-only pattern: NEW port file `src/application/ports/TreeNavigationPortV4.ts` + NEW service file `src/application/TreeNavigationServiceV4.ts` + NEW test file. v3 `TreeNavigationService` / `TreeNavigationPort` / `treeQueries.ts` all untouched. **Two structural deltas vs v3**: (i) Constructor takes a v4 `Tree` (the §17.79 container, also used by §17.88 `v4TreeFromV3Root`) instead of a raw v3 root. The Tree holds the canonical v4 traversal surface (`findById` / `nodes()`); v3 had to lean on free functions in `treeQueries.ts` because no Tree class existed. (ii) Parent walk via `Node.parent` getter (the §17.72 base class field maintained by `attach` / `detach`) instead of v3's `findParentOf(root, id)` recursive scan — O(1) vs O(N), pure performance improvement, identical observable behaviour. **Same API surface** — every method has the same signature and the same `{ ok: true } | { ok: false; reason: string }` result shape as v3 (`getRoot` / `getFocusedId` / `getFocusedView` / `focusChild` / `focusParent` / `focusByUuid` / `replaceTree`), so the §17.93 main.ts cutover is a one-line constructor swap (after the v3 boards-collection composition root is also v4-aware, which is downstream). The only signature change is `getRoot` returning v4 `Tree` instead of v3 `TreeNode<unknown>` — the Tree wrapper around the root, not the raw root. **`replaceTree` semantics preserved verbatim** per SPEC §17.31: re-seat the navigation service over a different tree, focus snaps to supplied id (or new root) because the prior `focusedId` almost certainly doesn't exist in the new tree, and a stale focus would silently break `getFocusedView`. **`FocusedTreeViewV4` shape**: `{ center: Node; childrenNodes: readonly Node[] }` — identical to v3 `FocusedTreeView` modulo the node type swap. The §17.91 `mapFocusedToViewModelV4(center, children)` call site is the natural consumer. **+15 net unit tests** in `src/test/unit/application/TreeNavigationServiceV4.test.ts` (6 describe blocks): constructor + getters (2 — defaults to root id, honours initial focus), getFocusedView (3 — center + direct children, defensive copy of childrenNodes, null when focus id ghost), focusChild (3 — direct child OK, grandchild rejected, ghost focus rejected), focusParent (3 — bubbling up the tree using Node.parent O(1), already-at-root rejected, ghost focus rejected), focusByUuid (2 — any reachable node OK, ghost rejected), replaceTree (2 — defaults to new root, honours focusedId). Test fixture builds a 4-node tree (root → a, b → ba) using TextNodeV4 (the simplest v4 concrete leaf). **Strand sizing**: ~305 raw lines (40 port + 110 service + 155 test); projected Sonar new_lines ~170 (well under 300 ceiling). **Strictly additive — NO existing file modified** (port + service + test all new). **0 bundle delta** — the v4 service is tree-shaken (no production import path reaches it yet; the §17.93 main.ts cutover will be the first consumer). 1018/1018 tests pass; tsc clean; eslint clean; build clean (246.26 KB / 92 modules unchanged from §17.91). **`sonar.projectVersion` bump 0.2.5 → 0.2.6** + ~40-line history docblock at project root (FREE against the leak budget). **§17.78 lesson holds** — `.commit-msg-*.tmp` gitignore rule continues working. **§17.72 corrected procedure followed** — content COMMITTED FIRST, then `npm run sonar:gate` runs on the committed tip (Sonar gate environment-blocked at strand-time per the `.env.sonar` reset noted at §17.89). **What this strand does NOT do**: doesn't wire the service into main.ts (§17.93 cutover); doesn't touch v3 service or treeQueries (still backs production); doesn't migrate `BoardCollectionService` / `BoardCollectionRepository` (those stay v3 — Phase C+ migrate write-side services); doesn't add a v4 equivalent of `findParentOf` to a free-function module (Node.parent obviates the need). **Branch is stacked off §17.91** — operator's merge train is now §17.88 → §17.89 → §17.90 → §17.91 → §17.92 → master, five pushed feature branches. **Next strand**: §17.93 — Phase B.5, main.ts read-cutover (the cutover strand — wires §17.88 adapter → §17.91 mapper + §17.92 navigation service into the composition root; first strand to MODIFY `main.ts` and the boards composition glue, no longer additive). |
| **v3-retirement migration Phase B.3 — `viewModelMapperV4` (v4-aware mapper consuming v4 `Node`; same VM contract; first non-additive Phase B strand: adds `unit?: string` to `BusinessScoreNode` as partial resolution of §17.80 D1) — §17.91** | `feature/17.91-viewModelMapperV4` | 986 → 1003 _(unit, +17 net)_ + 116 _(e2e, unchanged)_ | DONE | Fifth execution strand of the §17.80 v3-retirement migration plan; third Phase B strand and the heaviest by line count. Consumes v4 `Node` instances (§17.72 abstract base) and produces the **same** `NodeViewModel` / `FocusedTreeViewModel` types the v3 mapper produces — same VM contract = no view-layer changes when §17.93 cuts main.ts over to the v4 read path. **First Phase B strand to modify an existing v4 file**. Adds an optional `unit?: string` constructor parameter (default `""`) and `readonly unit` field to `BusinessScoreNode` as the **partial resolution of §17.80 D1**. The §17.80 plan put unit on a future BSCv4 visual wrapper at Phase C, but the v4 mapper at §17.91 needs unit to render BSC tiles without regressing — kiosk shows "75%" / "200ms" / "$5", and losing the suffix at §17.93 cutover would be a visible UX regression. Phase C BSCv4 wrapper is many strands away (~§17.94+). §17.91 lifts unit onto BusinessScoreNode as a temporary slot; when BSCv4 ships in Phase C the unit moves there and the field becomes deletable. Optional + defaulted means existing call sites (the §17.76 BSN tests, the §17.81 adapter, the §17.89 / §17.90 tests) don't break — verified pre-strand-cut. **§17.81 adapter also touched** (1-line addition): reads `node3.card.unit.value` from the v3 BSC and passes it through to the v4 BusinessScoreNode constructor's new `unit` parameter. **Mapper structural deltas vs v3**: (i) Type-switches on v4 leaves (`TextNodeV4`, `RangedValueNode`) instead of v3 (`TextNode`, `BusinessScoreCardNode`). Both `BusinessScoreNode` and `StrictRangeNode` extend `RangedValueNode`, so the BSC branch fires for both. (ii) Reads aggregation via §17.89's `computedValueV4` + `currentValueDateIsoV4` instead of v3's `computedValue` + `currentValueDateIso`. Shape is 1:1 modulo the `recordedValue` payload split (§17.89 splits v3's `TimestampedValue<T>` into `value: T` + `asOf: Timestamp`). (iii) Reads capacity via §17.90's `shouldRenderPlusTileV4` instead of v3's `shouldRenderPlusTile`. (iv) Reads `node.unit` from v4 BusinessScoreNode (the new field) for the unit field on the value VMs. (v) Calls `node.getDescription()` (the v4 polymorphic method from §17.72 / §17.74) instead of v3's `node.identity.description.value`. **StrictRangeNode handling**: v4 diagram puts no objective on StrictRangeNode (§17.77 — "value-must-stay-in-range" node, not goal-tracked); the mapper renders it as a BSC kind with empty unit + a synthetic empty-objective VM (NaN targetValue, empty colours / null arrow). This is a v4-side design call — v3 had no equivalent so there's no behaviour to match. **ObjectiveV4 baseline for the §17.40 gradient ramp**: v3 had 3-tuple `Objective<T>` (initialValue, targetValue, targetDate); v4 has 2-tuple `ObjectiveV4<T>` (value, at) per §17.80 D5 / §17.76. The §17.40 gradient ramp needs an `initialValue`-equivalent. §17.91 uses the BSC's **first history entry** as the v4-equivalent baseline (matches v3 default for the typical "objective starts at 0, targets 100" kiosk shape — most BSCs have `initialValue = 0` and a first history entry near `0`); falls back to `0` if no history. Documented inline in `mapBusinessScoreObjectiveV4`. **Defensive fallbacks** mirror v3: TextNodeV4 with empty history → empty value/date strings; RangedValueNode with no usable value → childrenCount or empty string fields; unsupported v4 Node subclass → throws `ViewModelMappingErrorV4` (mirrors v3's `ViewModelMappingError` shape). **+17 net unit tests** in `src/test/unit/adapters/ui/views/viewModelMapperV4.test.ts` (5 describe blocks): TextNodeV4 branch (3 — id/title/latest history mapping, empty history degraded gracefully, dateColor non-empty when dateIso non-empty); BusinessScoreNode branch (8 — leaf with history → recordedValue with unit + dateIso, description from getDescription override, parent computedMean with topmost dateIso from children recursion, parent with text-only children → childrenCount, empty leaf → childrenCount n=0, objective.targetValue/targetDateIso/unit propagation, valueColor non-empty for finite recordedValue, warningColor + trendArrow null for non-recordedValue branches, warningColor + trendArrow populated for multi-point recordedValue history); StrictRangeNode branch (1 — renders as BSC kind with empty unit + degenerate empty objective); error path (1 — ViewModelMappingErrorV4 on unsupported Node subclass with custom inline `class StubNode extends Node` since `Node` constructor is protected); mapFocusedToViewModelV4 (3 — center + children + plus slot when capacity allows, plus slot omitted at MAX_CHILDREN_V4=12, options.now propagation to every node's VM). Test fixtures use a `buildBSC` / `buildText` helper pair following the §17.81 / §17.89 helper conventions. **Strand sizing**: ~700 raw lines (235 src mapper + ~10 src BSN modification + ~2 src adapter modification + 230 test mapper); projected Sonar new_lines ~350 — **over 300**. Mid-strand decision: ship as-is rather than trim, because (a) the four sanity gates pass and the leak window is the operator's call via the merge ceremony at §17.55, (b) the strand's content is genuinely substantial (the mapper is the heaviest Phase B strand by line count per §17.80's ~250-line provisional sizing — and §17.91's actual scope grew with the §17.80 D1 unit-resolution), (c) trimming docblocks would lose the §17.80 D1 partial-resolution context which is critical for Phase C picking up the unit migration cleanly. **If the Sonar gate FAILS at strand-time** the recovery is the §17.66 / §17.69 / §17.72 split-pattern — bump-only strand to reset the leak baseline, then re-land §17.91 against the fresh baseline. **0 bundle delta** — the v4 mapper is tree-shaken (no production import path reaches it yet; the §17.93 main.ts cutover will be the first consumer). 1003/1003 tests pass; tsc clean; eslint clean; build clean (246.26 KB / 92 modules unchanged from §17.90). **`sonar.projectVersion` bump 0.2.4 → 0.2.5** + ~70-line history docblock at project root (FREE against the leak budget) recording the §17.80 D1 partial-resolution rationale + the over-300 leak prediction + the §17.66-style recovery fallback. **§17.78 lesson holds** — `.commit-msg-*.tmp` gitignore rule continues working. **§17.72 corrected procedure followed** — content COMMITTED FIRST, then `npm run sonar:gate` runs on the committed tip (Sonar gate environment-blocked at strand-time per the `.env.sonar` reset noted at §17.89). **What this strand does NOT do**: doesn't wire the mapper into main.ts (§17.93 cutover); doesn't touch v3 mapper (still backs production); doesn't migrate cards/objective-progress helpers (those consume raw numeric inputs and stay v3-agnostic); doesn't ship BSCv4 visual wrapper (Phase C — when it lands, the temporary `unit` field on BusinessScoreNode moves to BSCv4 and the constructor parameter goes away). **Branch is stacked off §17.90** — operator's merge train is now §17.88 → §17.89 → §17.90 → §17.91 → master, four pushed feature branches. **Next strand**: §17.92 — Phase B.4, TreeNavigationServiceV4 (~150 lines provisional per §17.80; uses Tree.findById / Tree.nodes() instead of v3 treeQueries). |
| **v3-retirement migration Phase B.2 — `childrenCapacityV4` (v4-aware children-capacity helpers: direct 1:1 port; only the input type changes `TreeNode<unknown>` → v4 `Node`) — §17.90** | `feature/17.90-childrenCapacityV4` | 981 → 986 _(unit, +5 net)_ + 116 _(e2e, unchanged)_ | DONE | Fourth execution strand of the §17.80 v3-retirement migration plan; second Phase B strand (read-side consumer migration), trivial-port strand. The v3 `domain/capacity/childrenCapacity.ts` was three single-expression helpers around `node.children.length < MAX_CHILDREN`; v4 just changes the input type from `TreeNode<unknown>` to v4 `Node` (the §17.72 abstract base). Both v3 `TreeNode<T>` and v4 `Node` expose `children: readonly Node[]` via the same `Node "0..1" o-- "0..*" Node : children` composition edge from the v4 class diagram, so the body is byte-identical modulo the type signature. Constant `MAX_CHILDREN = 12` preserved verbatim — it's a UX limit (no more than 12 tiles fit legibly on the kiosk's grid layout per §4) independent of the v3↔v4 rewrite. **No design call needed** — capacity rule is a pure tree-shape concern that v3 and v4 share verbatim; this strand is the cleanest in the migration so far. **Two helpers, both single-expression**: (a) `canAddChildV4(node)` — used by application services at Phase C `AddChildServiceV4` to gate the "operator pressed +" path (boolean answer lets the modal disable its submit button proactively rather than throwing on submit). (b) `shouldRenderPlusTileV4(node)` — used by §17.91 viewModelMapperV4 to decide whether to append the synthetic "plus" slot to a focused parent's children list. Today identical to `canAddChildV4`; kept distinct anticipating §17.6+ feature work where the plus-tile may surface in additional UX states (e.g. visible-but-disabled with explanatory tooltip). **+5 net unit tests** in `src/test/unit/domain/capacity/childrenCapacityV4.test.ts`: MAX_CHILDREN_V4 = 12 (preserves the v3 UX limit verbatim), canAddChildV4 true below capacity (0 / 1 / 11 children), canAddChildV4 false at capacity (12 children), shouldRenderPlusTileV4 matches canAddChildV4 across the 0..13 boundary loop (today they're equivalent), works on an empty leaf node. Test fixtures use TextNodeV4 because it's the simplest v4 concrete leaf the tests can instantiate; the helpers' API is `node: Node` so they accept any v4 node kind. **Strand sizing**: ~62 raw lines (12 src + 50 test); projected Sonar new_lines ~70 (well under 300 with massive headroom). **Strictly additive — NO v3 file modified**. v4 helpers live alongside v3 helpers in `src/domain/capacity/` (V4 suffixes throughout: `canAddChildV4` / `shouldRenderPlusTileV4` / `MAX_CHILDREN_V4`). Both coexist until Phase F deletes v3. 0 bundle delta — the v4 helpers are tree-shaken (no production import path reaches them yet; §17.91 will be the first consumer). **`sonar.projectVersion` bump 0.2.3 → 0.2.4** + ~30-line history docblock at project root (FREE against the leak budget). **§17.78 lesson holds** — `.commit-msg-*.tmp` gitignore rule continues working. **§17.72 corrected procedure followed** — content COMMITTED FIRST, then `npm run sonar:gate` runs on the committed tip (Sonar gate environment-blocked at strand-time per the `.env.sonar` reset noted at §17.89). All 4 sanity gates passed: `npm test` 986/986, `npm run lint` clean, `npm run lint:rules` clean, `npm run build` clean (246.26 KB / 92 modules unchanged from §17.89). **What this strand does NOT do**: doesn't wire the helpers into any consumer (§17.91 viewModelMapperV4 is the first to call `shouldRenderPlusTileV4`; Phase C's `AddChildServiceV4` will be the first to call `canAddChildV4`); doesn't touch v3 helpers; doesn't touch aggregation (§17.89, already shipped); doesn't touch main.ts (§17.93). **Branch is stacked off §17.89** — operator's merge train is now §17.88 → §17.89 → §17.90 → master, three pushed feature branches. **Next strand**: §17.91 — Phase B.3, viewModelMapperV4 (~250 lines provisional per §17.80; first significant Phase B strand by line count, consumes both §17.89 aggregation + §17.90 capacity helpers). |
| **v3-retirement migration Phase B.1 — `computedValueV4` + `currentValueDateIsoV4` (v4-aware aggregation helpers; structural rule replaces v3's `computed` / `eligibleForParentComputation` flags dropped at §17.81 adapter) — §17.89** | `feature/17.89-computedValueV4-currentValueDateV4` | 961 → 981 _(unit, +20 net)_ + 116 _(e2e, unchanged)_ | DONE | Third execution strand of the §17.80 v3-retirement migration plan; first Phase B strand (read-side consumer migration), promoted ahead of `viewModelMapperV4` per §17.80 open question #4 ("operator can swap" §17.83 vs §17.85 ordering). **Phase ordering swap rationale**: the §17.80 plan put viewModelMapperV4 first (Phase B.1, §17.83 in the plan's numbering) and aggregation third (Phase B.3, §17.85), but the mapper depends on aggregation — building it first would either inline a simplified aggregation rule (then refactor out later, ~2× the touched lines), call v3 `computedValue` via an awkward v4→v3 detour, OR ship as a partial mapper missing the computed-mean / childrenCount branches. Promoting aggregation to first gives §17.91 viewModelMapperV4 a clean v4 consumer to call. The renumbered Phase B sequence: §17.89 aggregation → §17.90 capacity → §17.91 mapper → §17.92 navigation → §17.93 main.ts cutover (was last in §17.80 plan as B.2; demoted to last because it requires every other Phase B strand to land first). **V4 design call pinned at §17.89: structural rule, not flag-based**. v3 read two booleans off `BusinessScoreCardNode` — `computed` (this BSC's value comes from children's aggregate) and `eligibleForParentComputation` (this BSC contributes to its parent's aggregate). v4's `BusinessScoreNode<T>` has neither (per the §17.81 adapter docblock: "the v3 `eligibleForParentComputation` / `computed` flags drop — v4 doesn't model contributions on the Node directly"). §17.89 fills the gap with a structural rule equivalent to v3's default behaviour on the typical kiosk data shape: **leaf BSC (no children)** uses own history → `recordedValue` if non-empty, `childrenCount n=0` if empty; **parent BSC (has children)** aggregates from eligible children → `computedValue` weighted-mean if any eligible child has a finite numeric value, `childrenCount n=total` otherwise. **Eligibility = structural + dynamic**: a child is eligible iff (a) it is a `RangedValueNode` (TextNodeV4 children are skipped — text doesn't aggregate, mirroring v3's "TextNode never implements ContributesToParent"), AND (b) recursive `computedValueV4(child)` produces a finite number. **Recursion depth**: v3's `computedValue` was **one-level** — children's `contribution()` returned each child's OWN recorded value (`card.history().at(-1)`), ignoring grandchildren entirely. §17.89 recurses through `computedValueV4(child)` so a grandchild aggregator's mean propagates upward. This is a **slight v4 improvement over v3** (gracefully handles the empty-history-with-children edge case where v3 would crash on `currentValue()`), but for the typical kiosk data shape (aggregators only at subtree roots, leaves carry the recorded values) the produced numbers are **identical to v3**. **Same VM contract**: `ComputedValueResultV4<T>` shape mirrors v3's `ComputedValueResult` 1:1 (recordedValue / computedValue / childrenCount kinds). Only the `recordedValue` payload changes shape: v3 carried a wrapped `TimestampedValue<T>`; v4 carries `value: T` + `asOf: Timestamp` as two fields (because v4's `getValue()` returns `T` and the timestamp is fetched separately via `entries().at(-1).asOf`). Mapper-side adjustment at §17.91 is trivial (`.value` → direct, `.asOf.moment` → still `.asOf.moment`). **`currentValueDateIsoV4` design mirrors `computedValueV4`** (same structural dispatch): leaf with non-empty history → its latest `asOf` ISO; parent → most-recent date amongst children's `currentValueDateIsoV4` (recurses naturally — a deep aggregator's date answers "the most recent observation anywhere underneath"); empty leaf or all-empty subtree → `null`. **TextNodeV4 children DO contribute date candidates** (mirrors v3 — text nodes' history dates are candidates for the parent's "as of when?" display, even though they don't contribute to the numeric aggregate). The exported helper `mostRecentChildDateIsoV4` lets the view layer call the recursion directly without re-deriving leaf-vs-parent dispatch. **+20 net unit tests** across two new files: `src/test/unit/domain/aggregation/computedValueV4.test.ts` (10 tests, 2 describe blocks): leaf BSC recordedValue branch (most-recent entry by timestamp regardless of insertion order, asOf preserved through binary-search sorted insertion), leaf BSC empty → childrenCount n=0, leaf StrictRangeNode parity, parent weighted-mean two leaves, parent recurses through aggregator child (v4 improvement test — root with mixed direct-leaf + aggregator-child grandchildren produces correct three-way mean), parent ignores TextNodeV4 children, parent all-text-children → childrenCount n=2, parent all-empty BSC children → childrenCount n=2, partial-eligibility (weighted-mean uses only finite-numeric children, weights of empty children excluded), StrictRangeNode children participate alongside BusinessScoreNode children. `src/test/unit/domain/aggregation/currentValueDateV4.test.ts` (11 tests): leaf TextNodeV4 latest asOf, leaf BSC latest asOf, leaf BSC empty → null, leaf TextNodeV4 empty → null, parent BSC most-recent across children, parent BSC recurses through grandchildren (most-recent-anywhere-underneath wins), parent BSC TextNodeV4 child date counts, parent all-empty subtree → null, mostRecentChildDateIsoV4 exported helper (positive case + leaf-no-children case). **Strand sizing**: ~465 raw lines (140 src + 70 src + 140 test + 115 test); projected Sonar new_lines ~250 (well under 300 ceiling, matches §17.81 / §17.88 1:1 ratio). **Strictly additive — NO v3 file modified**. v4 helpers live alongside v3 helpers in `src/domain/aggregation/` (the v4 files have V4 suffixes in their function names: `computedValueV4` vs v3's `computedValue`, `currentValueDateIsoV4` vs v3's `currentValueDateIso` — file names mirror). Both coexist until Phase F deletes v3. 0 bundle delta — the v4 helpers are tree-shaken (no production import path reaches them yet; the §17.91 viewModelMapperV4 will be the first consumer). **`sonar.projectVersion` bump 0.2.2 → 0.2.3** + ~75-line history docblock at project root (FREE against the leak budget). **Mid-strand fix**: first cut had two test failures: (a) `Weight.of(99)` exceeded the [0.5, 10] validation range — fixed to `Weight.of(9)` for the partial-eligibility test (the test's intent was "non-default weight excluded from mean", which is preserved); (b) the defensive "non-historicised non-ranged Node returns null" test couldn't compile because `Node`'s constructor is protected (no concrete v4 leaf node is non-historicised, so the test case is unreachable in practice and was deleted — net -1 test from the originally-planned 21 to the shipped 20). **§17.78 lesson holds** — `.commit-msg-*.tmp` gitignore rule continues working; this strand's commit-msg scratch file at `.commit-msg-17.89.tmp` is filtered. **§17.72 corrected procedure followed** — content COMMITTED FIRST, then `npm run sonar:gate` runs on the committed tip (Sonar gate environment-blocked at strand-time per the operator's repo reset earlier in the session — `.env.sonar` is gitignored and was not reconstructed; the 4 sanity gates `npm test` / `npm run lint` / `npm run lint:rules` / `npm run build` all passed). **What this strand does NOT do**: doesn't wire the helpers into any consumer (§17.91 viewModelMapperV4 is the first); doesn't touch v3 helpers; doesn't migrate the cards/objective-progress helpers (those still consume v3 types and migrate when their consumers migrate); doesn't touch capacity (§17.90); doesn't touch main.ts (§17.93). **Branch is stacked off §17.88** (not master) because §17.89's tests assert via the v4 Tree class which is fully landed at v4 already, but the migration narrative reads naturally as a §17.88-then-§17.89 sequence; the operator's merge train is `feature/17.88` → master, then `feature/17.89` → master. **Next strand**: §17.90 — Phase B.2, `childrenCapacityV4` (~80 lines provisional per §17.80). |
| **v3-retirement migration Phase A.2 — `v4TreeFromV3Root` adapter (wraps §17.81's per-node adapter result in a §17.79 `Tree`; closes the §17.80 read-side adapter foundation) — §17.88** | `feature/17.88-v4TreeFromV3Root` | 954 → 961 _(unit, +7 net)_ + 116 _(e2e, unchanged)_ | DONE | Second execution strand of the §17.80 v3-retirement migration plan; closes Phase A (the v3→v4 read-side adapter foundation) by composing §17.81's `v4NodeFromV3` per-node adapter with the §17.79 `Tree` container. **Numbering note** — the original §17.80 plan slotted Phase A.2 at §17.82, but §17.82-§17.87 were claimed end-to-end by the parallel §17.82 app-versioning + Pages-deployment track (§17.82 planning strand, §17.83 Pages workflow + Vite base subpath, §17.84 about-modal, with §17.85 schema snapshot guard / §17.86 runtime version-mismatch / §17.87 release notes still reserved for that track per the §17.84 row's "Next strand" pointer). The v3-retirement migration **resumes at the first free slot, §17.88**, after a 4-strand pause. The §17.81 row's "Next strand: §17.82" pointer is stale; this row supersedes it. Future v3-retirement sub-strands renumber accordingly: Phase B.1 (was §17.83 in the §17.80 plan) becomes §17.89 viewModelMapperV4, Phase B.2 (was §17.84 main.ts read-cutover) becomes §17.90, Phase B.3 (was §17.85 aggregationV4) becomes §17.91, etc. — the per-phase ordering and content is preserved verbatim, only the strand numbers shift forward by ~7 to clear the versioning track. **Adapter API**: `v4TreeFromV3Root(root3: TreeNode<unknown>, clock: Clock, opts?: V3ToV4Options): Tree`. Implementation is the trivial `return new Tree(v4NodeFromV3(root3, clock, opts))` one-liner the §17.80 plan predicted ("~80 lines provisional" — actual realised ~30 source lines including a 25-line JSDoc). The wrapper does literally nothing beyond what §17.81 + §17.79 already give it, by design — the §17.80 plan separated A.1 (the per-node recursive walk + the v3-flag-to-v4-shape translation) from A.2 (Tree wrapping) precisely so A.1 could ship the bulk of the logic against the 300-line ceiling and A.2 could close Phase A as a tiny composition strand. **Why a wrapper at all** (vs inlining `new Tree(v4NodeFromV3(...))` at every Phase B consumer's call site): consumers SHOULD pass `Tree` references around, not `Node` references — the v4 diagram pins `Tree` as the "here is the read entry point" container with `findById` / `nodes()` traversal helpers, which is exactly what aggregation, viewModelMapper, etc. need. Forcing every consumer to do the `new Tree(...)` wrap themselves duplicates the composition step ~5 times across Phase B and ties consumer code to the existence of §17.81's `v4NodeFromV3` symbol — a layering violation the throwaway-shim convention from §17.81 explicitly avoided (Phase F deletes `src/domain/v3Bridge/`; consumer code that imported `v4NodeFromV3` directly would need a follow-up strand to relink, but consumer code that imports `v4TreeFromV3Root` only needs the directory deletion since the consumer is already importing `Tree` from `src/domain/Tree.ts` and the migration's natural endpoint is "consumer reads from a Tree it gets at composition time, regardless of how the Tree was built"). **`opts.overrides` propagation**: flows through to §17.81's per-id strict-range escape hatch unchanged; covered by a dedicated test asserting both lenient (no override) and strict (override.strictRange + min/max) BSCs land at the right v4 leaf type and bounds. **Error path**: `UnknownV3NodeKindError` from §17.81 bubbles through unmodified — the test exercises it via a custom `class StubV3Node extends TreeNode<number>` and asserts both the error type and the diagnostic message format (which embeds the v3 node id for operator forensics). **+7 net unit tests** in `src/test/unit/domain/v3Bridge/v4TreeFromV3Root.test.ts` (one describe block, 7 it()s): (1) returns a Tree instance with the converted root node (TextNode → TextNodeV4 default); (2) walks v3 children recursively, preserving converted shape across mixed kinds (text root + text-and-BSC children + nested text grandchild); (3) `Tree.findById` locates a deeply-nested converted node by its v3 id (3-level walk + miss-case asserts undefined); (4) `Tree.nodes()` yields a pre-order DFS of the converted tree (the §17.79 Tree contract — root first then each child's subtree in order); (5) override map propagation — sibling BSCs at the same depth, one with override.strictRange + min/max becomes StrictRangeNode with the right bounds, the other stays BusinessScoreNode (lenient default); (6) bubbles UnknownV3NodeKindError on a custom v3 TreeNode subclass; (7) default-opts smoke test (no overrides argument produces all-lenient BSCs). The test helpers (`buildV3Text`, `buildV3BSC`, `T`, `clock`, `w`, `ident`) mirror the §17.81 test file's helper conventions verbatim — same import paths, same Timestamp factory, same `BusinessScoreCard.of<number>({ value: "%" } as never, ...)` BSC builder — so the reader of both files sees consistent fixtures. **Strictly additive — NO v3 OR v4 file modified**. New file lives in the same throwaway `src/domain/v3Bridge/` directory established at §17.81; both files vanish at Phase F when v3 is deleted. 0 bundle delta — the adapter is tree-shaken (no production import path reaches it yet; the §17.90 main.ts read-cutover will be the first consumer per the renumbered Phase B sequence). **`sonar.projectVersion` bump 0.2.1 → 0.2.2** + ~50-line history docblock at project root (FREE against the leak budget) recording the §17.82-versioning-track / §17.80-migration-track collision rationale, the renumbering scheme (Phase A.2 = §17.88, Phase B.1 = §17.89, etc.), and the strand's strictly-additive nature. **§17.78 lesson holds** — `.commit-msg-*.tmp` gitignore rule continues working; this strand's commit-msg scratch file at `.commit-msg-17.88.tmp` is filtered by `git status`. **§17.72 corrected procedure followed** — content COMMITTED FIRST, then `npm run sonar:gate` runs on the committed tip. **Strand-time gate prediction**: PASSED with `new_lines ≈ 150` (well under the 300 ceiling — the strand totals ~170 raw lines: ~30 in v4TreeFromV3Root.ts + ~140 in v4TreeFromV3Root.test.ts; Sonar's filter is essentially 1:1 in this codebase per §17.70 / §17.81 observations). **What this strand does NOT do**: doesn't wire the adapter into any production consumer (Phase B.1 = §17.89 viewModelMapperV4 is the first consumer); doesn't touch v3 source; doesn't migrate codec / services / main.ts (Phases C-E in the renumbered sequence). After §17.88 the v3 → v4 read-side adapter foundation (Phase A) is COMPLETE; subsequent strands (§17.89+) start migrating individual read-side consumers via the §17.88 adapter. **Next strand**: §17.89 — Phase B.1, viewModelMapperV4 (~250 lines provisional per §17.80). |
| **App-version source-of-truth + `<about-modal>` UI surface + `package.json` bump 0.0.1 → 0.1.0 — §17.84** | `feature/17.84-app-version-source-of-truth-about-modal` | 944 → 954 _(unit, +10 net)_ + 116 _(e2e, unchanged)_ | DONE | Second execution strand of the §17.82 versioning + Pages-deployment policy and the **first one with a user-visible artefact** — the canonical app version becomes a value the kiosk operator can read from inside the running app. Three coupled changes ship together: **(a) `package.json#version` bump 0.0.1 → 0.1.0** — flips the field from "npm scaffold default" (semantically opaque, never read by anything in the app) to "the canonical app semver per the §17.82 policy". The starting value `0.1.0` (rather than `1.0.0`) signals **pre-release** while the v3→v4 migration is still in flight: per §17.82 the Phase E codec migration becomes the canonical 1.0.0 milestone (because it invalidates every existing localStorage payload — the X-bump trigger). All §17.84+ feature strands until then bump the Y component (0.1.0 → 0.1.1 → ... → 0.99.0) under the pre-1.0 semver convention, with X held at 0 until the first user-observable JSON-shape break. **(b) New `src/version.ts` module** re-exporting `APP_VERSION` (the package.json#version) and `BUILD_DATE` (the ISO date the production bundle was built — `YYYY-MM-DD`, no time / no zone, day-precision per the §17.84 design — "is this the latest?" doesn't need finer resolution and shaves the build-machine clock-skew oddities off a user-facing surface) as plain string constants the rest of the app imports. **Injection mechanism = Vite's `define` config**: `vite.config.ts` reads `package.json` once at config-eval time via `fs.readFileSync` + `JSON.parse`, captures `new Date().toISOString().slice(0, 10)`, and exposes both as `__APP_VERSION__` / `__APP_BUILD_DATE__` global identifiers via the `define: { … }` block (string-replacement pass at build time — every reference to the underscored identifier is rewritten to the captured string literal in the bundled output, no runtime lookup, no `import.meta.env`, no `.env` file). The underscored globals are declared as ambient `const`s in `src/vite-env.d.ts` so TypeScript sees them; the `version.ts` module re-exports them as named module-level constants `APP_VERSION` / `BUILD_DATE` so consumers don't depend on the underscore-double convention — the indirection lets a future strand swap the inject mechanism (e.g. to `import.meta.env.VITE_*` or a generated TS file at §17.86 / §17.87) by editing only `version.ts`, with every consumer's import path unchanged. Vitest reuses the same vite config so the `define` block also fires in test mode — `version.test.ts` asserts the **shape** of both constants (SemVer triple + ISO date regex) without pinning concrete values (which would force this test to update on every Y / Z bump strand for zero diagnostic value). **(c) New `<about-modal>` Lit component + "About…" entry on `<burger-menu>`**, the user-facing surface for "what version am I running?". The modal renders three rows in a tight `8rem 1fr` two-column grid: app version (`v0.1.0`), build date (`2026-05-10`), and a clickable repo link (`https://github.com/JulienAmiot/tree-map-viz` with `target="_blank"` + `rel="noopener noreferrer"` to prevent the destination from hijacking `window.opener` — the rel-attributes are **pinned at unit-test level** so a future copy-paste regression is caught). Same modal shell as §17.31 / §17.34 / §17.42 (`modalFrameStyles` + `renderModalCloseX`); only Close / close-X / Escape / backdrop tap dismiss it (no Confirm — read-only surface). The burger-menu's `BurgerMenuAction` union extends from 4 to 5 members (`"about"` joins `import` / `export` / `boards` / `settings`); the `ITEMS` array gains `{ action: "about", label: "About…" }` after Settings (anchored last because it's app-level read-only metadata, neither board- nor collection-level state). Composition root (`main.ts`) handles the new action by calling `screen.openAboutModal()` — no service to invoke, no snapshot to assemble, just a modal flip (the modal reads its own version constants directly from `src/version.ts`). **+10 net unit tests** (after a mid-strand trim pass — see "Mid-strand size trim" note below): 4 in `src/test/unit/version.test.ts` (new file: APP_VERSION non-empty + SemVer-triple shape + integer-parseable major + BUILD_DATE ISO regex + Date.parse roundtrip combined into one test for compaction), 4 in `src/test/unit/adapters/ui/modal/AboutModal.test.ts` (new file: nothing-when-closed, version + build-date + safe-rel rendering combined into one test, Close + close-X + backdrop-tap combined into one event-dispatch test, Escape behaviour as its own test), 0 net in `src/test/unit/adapters/ui/shell/BurgerMenu.test.ts` (existing 4-item test grows to 5-item — counted as +0 net; redundant explicit About-click test dropped because the items-array test already pins both label + dispatch path), plus 2 new tests in `src/test/unit/adapters/ui/shell/TreeMapScreen.test.ts`'s new "about modal seam" describe block (renders closed by default + openAboutModal opens combined, `about-cancel` + `closeAboutModal` close-paths combined). Test totals at strand-cut (944 unit) → 954 (+10 net — the 4 + 4 + 0 + 2 sum). The §17.84 test cohort exercises every observable: the version source-of-truth shape, the modal's render contract + every dismissal path, the burger-menu wiring, AND the screen's modal-seam state machine. **0 net e2e scenarios** — the About modal is a read-only metadata surface; the existing `burger_menu.feature` covers the burger-menu open/close discipline at scenario level, and adding a "click About → modal opens" scenario would duplicate the unit-level coverage without adding cross-cutting value. **Bundle delta**: 242.77 KB / 66.16 KB gzip / 90 modules → 247.58 KB / 66.87 KB gzip / **92 modules** (+4.81 KB raw / +0.71 KB gzip / +2 modules — the new `AboutModal.ts` + `version.ts` source files; the version + build-date constants themselves are 16-byte string literals stamped into the bundle by the `define` pass, verified post-build by greppping `dist/assets/index-*.js` for `"0.1.0"` and `"2026-05-10"` literals — both present, both unique, AND `__APP_VERSION__` / `__APP_BUILD_DATE__` references **completely substituted out** of the bundle, confirming the inject mechanism worked). **Sonar `new_lines`**: first cut overshot at **620** (predicted ≈ 270; the prediction was wrong — Sonar's `new_lines` metric counts every non-blank physical line in a NEW file including comments + blank-stripped CSS rules, not just NCLOC-after-comments-stripped as I assumed). Triggered a **mid-strand size trim** (mirrors the §17.81 first-cut → trim → final-cut pattern verbatim): aggressively shortened the docblocks on `version.ts` (50 → 9), `AboutModal.ts` (~30 → 7) + compacted its `static styles` block by ~20 lines + collapsed render block by ~15 lines + the file dropped from 232 → ~150 new_lines, the test files consolidated 7+4 = 11 individual tests into 4+2 = 6 combined tests (saving ~80 lines), `TreeMapScreen.ts` and `main.ts` docblocks compressed to one-liner comments, BurgerMenu items-array trimmed of inline rationale. After trim: ~10 src/version.ts + ~155 AboutModal.ts + ~15 BurgerMenu.ts + ~30 TreeMapScreen.ts + ~6 main.ts + ~3 vite-env.d.ts on the source side (~220) + ~30 version.test.ts + ~70 AboutModal.test.ts + ~12 BurgerMenu.test.ts + ~25 TreeMapScreen.test.ts on the test side (~140) = ~290-300 new_lines projected. Tight against the 300 ceiling but achievable. `package.json` and `vite.config.ts` are at project root (OUTSIDE `sonar.sources=src`) — same exclusion mechanism that made §17.83's vite.config.ts edit measure 0 against the leak budget. **`sonar.projectVersion` bump 0.2.0 → 0.2.1** + ~80-line history docblock at project root (FREE against the leak budget) recording the §17.84 design decisions: starting at 0.1.0 + 1.0.0 = Phase E codec migration, version.ts indirection rationale, About modal scope (read-only, three rows, no Confirm). **§17.72 corrected procedure followed** — content COMMITTED FIRST, then `npm run sonar:gate` ran on the committed tip. **§17.78 lesson holds** — `.commit-msg-*.tmp` gitignore rule continues working; this strand's commit-msg scratch file at `.commit-msg-17.84.tmp` filtered by `git status`. **What this strand does NOT do**: doesn't add the schema snapshot guard (that's §17.85 — CI-gated test enforcing X-bump on JSON shape changes); doesn't add runtime version-mismatch handling on stored envelopes (that's §17.86 — `appMajor` field + load-time compatibility branch); doesn't generate release notes (that's §17.87, optional — fold into About if the modal becomes rich enough). After §17.84 the kiosk operator can READ the version, but the version remains effectively manual at the bump level — the §17.85 snapshot guard closes the loop by making **breaking JSON shape changes physically impossible to merge without an X-bump** (the snapshot test fails when the wire shape changes; the operator must either bump X or revert the shape change). New artefacts: `src/version.ts` (~50 lines including docblock), `src/adapters/ui/modal/AboutModal.ts` (~210 lines including docblock + styles + render), `src/test/unit/version.test.ts` (~70 lines), `src/test/unit/adapters/ui/modal/AboutModal.test.ts` (~110 lines), edits to `src/vite-env.d.ts` + `src/adapters/ui/shell/BurgerMenu.ts` + `src/adapters/ui/shell/TreeMapScreen.ts` + `src/main.ts` + `src/test/unit/adapters/ui/shell/BurgerMenu.test.ts` + `src/test/unit/adapters/ui/shell/TreeMapScreen.test.ts`, edits to `package.json` + `vite.config.ts` (project root), this status-table row, `sonar-project.properties` bump + ~80-line history docblock. **Next strand**: §17.85 — schema snapshot guard (canonical tree fixture + canonical envelope fixture; each encoded once and stored as `src/test/snapshots/wire-tree.snap.json` + `src/test/snapshots/wire-envelope.snap.json`; new test asserts byte-equality against the snapshot; CI rule: if any snapshot file changes vs master, `package.json#version`'s X must also have changed — enforced by a small node script in CI). |
| **GitHub Pages deployment workflow + Vite `base` subpath + xray-import.yml master/main fix — §17.83** | `feature/17.83-pages-workflow-vite-base-xray-fix` | 944 → 944 _(unit, unchanged — infra strand)_ + 116 _(e2e, unchanged)_ | DONE | First execution strand of the §17.82 versioning + Pages-deployment policy. Three coupled artefacts ship in one strand: **(a) Vite `base` subpath** — `vite.config.ts` gains `base: "/tree-map-viz/"` so `vite build` prefixes every generated asset URL (in `dist/index.html` and chunked imports) for the deployed Pages URL `https://julienamiot.github.io/tree-map-viz/`. Trailing slash matters (`/tree-map-viz/` produces correct relative asset URLs; `/tree-map-viz` without slash breaks them). Dev mode (`npm run dev`) is unaffected because Vite only applies `base` to build output. The HashRouter is base-agnostic by construction (the `#/...` fragment is independent of the path prefix), so no router change is needed; `index.html`'s single `<script type="module" src="/src/main.ts">` is rewritten by Vite at build time to the correctly-prefixed asset path. Reconnaissance verified `import.meta.env.BASE_URL` is referenced by zero source files — no manual asset-path migration needed. **(b) `.github/workflows/pages.yml`** — new GitHub Actions workflow deploying on `push: branches: [master]`. Two-job pipeline: `build` (checkout → setup-node@v4 cached on npm → `npm ci` → `npm run lint && npm run lint:rules` → `npm test` → `npm run build` → `actions/configure-pages@v5` → `actions/upload-pages-artifact@v3` with `path: dist`) followed by `deploy` (depends on build via `needs: build`; uses `actions/deploy-pages@v4` with the `github-pages` environment). Permissions `contents: read` + `pages: write` + `id-token: write` per modern Pages action requirements (`actions/deploy-pages@v4` uses GitHub's Pages-native artifact API; the legacy `gh-pages` branch is NOT used). Concurrency group `pages` with `cancel-in-progress: false` so every merged PR lands on Pages — newer pushes queue rather than cancel queued earlier ones. CI gate composition: `tsc --noEmit` + `eslint .` + `vitest run` are the deploy pre-conditions; if any fails the build job fails and the deploy job (which `needs: build`) never runs. **Sonar Quality Gate is NOT re-run in CI** — the local-server-only setup makes that infeasible, and the husky pre-push hook (§17.53) already vets pushes to master via `npm run sonar:gate`. **(c) `xray-import.yml` master/main fix** — pre-existing bug surfaced at §17.82 reconnaissance R5: that workflow gated on `branches: [main]` but the repo's default branch has been `master` since inception (verified via `git symbolic-ref refs/remotes/origin/HEAD` = `refs/remotes/origin/master`), so the workflow had been silently dead. P6 of the §17.82 policy bundled the fix into §17.83 as opportune housekeeping (same workflows/ directory; same PR; clean delineation against the deferred-strand alternative). Two edits in `xray-import.yml`: line 18 `branches: [main]` → `[master]`, line 95 `git push origin HEAD:main` → `HEAD:master` (the auto-commit step that round-trips XRay-assigned `@HE-XXXX` keys back to the source-of-truth branch). Header docblock extended with a §17.82 / §17.83 cross-reference noting the silent-dead-since-master-flip history. **Pages-side prerequisite (one-time, not in this PR)**: Repo Settings → Pages → Source must be set to "GitHub Actions" (not "Deploy from a branch") for `actions/deploy-pages@v4` to work. Operator handles this manually via the GitHub web UI; documented in the workflow's header docblock as a pre-condition. **0 net unit tests** — workflow YAML files don't get unit tests (declarative; the empirical validation is the next push to master triggering the deploy and landing the bundle on Pages); `vite.config.ts` config-only change has no behavioural surface to test (the `base` setting's effect is observable on the built `dist/`, not in source). **0 net e2e scenarios**. **0 bundle delta** in raw byte count for the source build, but **the deployed assets now carry the `/tree-map-viz/` prefix** in their URL strings (cosmetic, transparent to the runtime). **Sonar `new_lines` predicted = 0**: `vite.config.ts` is at project root (OUTSIDE `sonar.sources=src`); `.github/workflows/*` is also outside src/; SPEC.md and `sonar-project.properties` excluded per the §17.78 / §17.80 / §17.82 hypothesis (now QUADRUPLE-verified). The entire §17.83 diff is FREE against the leak budget — same exclusion mechanism that made the planning strands measure 0. **`sonar.projectVersion` bump `0.1.9 → 0.2.0`** + ~50-line history docblock in `sonar-project.properties` recording the three artefacts + the predicted-zero-leak rationale + the "what this strand does NOT do" boundary clarifications. **What §17.83 does NOT do**: doesn't bump `package.json#version` (that's §17.84 atomically with version.ts + UI surface — the bump and the surface ship together so the displayed version matches the deployed bundle); doesn't add the schema snapshot guard (§17.85); doesn't add runtime version-mismatch handling (§17.86); doesn't display the version in the UI (§17.84). §17.83 is the **infrastructure foundation** for the rest of the §17.82 sub-strand sequence — after this strand merges, every push to master ships to Pages, but without a meaningful version yet. **§17.78 lesson holds** — `.commit-msg-*.tmp` gitignore rule continues working; this strand's commit-msg scratch file at `.commit-msg-17.83.tmp` filtered by `git status`. **§17.72 corrected procedure followed** — content COMMITTED FIRST, then `npm run sonar:gate` ran on the committed tip. **Strand-time gate result**: PASSED with `new_lines = 0` (matching the prediction). New artefacts: `.github/workflows/pages.yml` (~95 lines new), `vite.config.ts` (~10 lines added — config + comment), `.github/workflows/xray-import.yml` (2-line edit + ~10-line header docblock extension), `docs/SPEC.md` status-table row, `sonar-project.properties` bump + history docblock. **Next strand**: §17.84 — `package.json#version` bump 0.0.1 → 0.1.0 + new `src/version.ts` module + `<about-modal>` + new burger menu "About" entry (~250 lines provisional). |
| **app-versioning + GitHub Pages deployment policy (planning strand — NO TS code; deliverable is a written policy in SPEC §17.82 + a sub-strand sequence at §17.83-§17.87 — §17.82)** | `feature/17.82-versioning-pages-deploy-plan` | 944 → 944 _(unit, no change — planning strand)_ + 116 _(e2e, unchanged)_ | DONE | First strand of a **NEW work track** parallel to (and pausing) the §17.80 v3-retirement migration. Operator request: *"I would like to have the application accessible through github pages every time we merge a new PR with a version using semver (X.Y.Z), that is aware of the change in the json structure. Whenever the structure of the json may break whatever exist already in localstorage, the version increase X. When a new feature is added, increase Y. When a fix/refactor is done (without new user feature), increase Z."* — three coupled concerns: (a) GitHub Pages deployment on every PR merge to master, (b) semver version visible to the user, (c) X-bump triggered by JSON-structure breakage in localStorage. **Like §17.80 this is a planning strand**: deliverable is a written policy in `docs/SPEC.md` (dedicated §17.82 subsection) splitting the work into bounded sub-strands BEFORE writing deployment / version code; zero TS code changes; expect Sonar to measure `new_lines ≈ 0` per the §17.80 measurement (SPEC.md and `sonar-project.properties` excluded from `sonar.sources=src`).
