Strand still bumps `sonar.projectVersion` per the §17.72 every-strand rule. **Reconnaissance findings** (input to the policy): (i) repo = `JulienAmiot/tree-map-viz` → Pages URL `https://julienamiot.github.io/tree-map-viz/`; (ii) default branch = `master` (local + remote agree); (iii) Vite config has NO `base` setting — needs `base: "/tree-map-viz/"` for the subpath deployment; (iv) `package.json#version = "0.0.1"` (never bumped — clearly placeholder); (v) **PRE-EXISTING BUG SURFACED**: the existing `xray-import.yml` workflow gates on `branches: [main]` but the repo's default branch is `master` — that workflow has been silently dead since the repo flipped to `master`; fix bundled into §17.83 per operator decision; (vi) **TWO existing version concepts** that MUST NOT collapse with the new app semver — storage-key suffix `tree-map-viz/board-collection/v1` (§17.4 / §17.63 namespacing) and envelope `"v": 1` in `LocalStorageBoardCollectionRepository` (envelope-shape version, bumped only on storage-shape changes); the new app semver is a THIRD orthogonal concept with its own semantics; (vii) the codebase already practises forward-compatible reads — §17.42 (silent drop of unknown `freshDateColor`), §17.14 (optional `historizedValues` backfill), §17.63 (silent storage-key rename) — meaning many "schema changes" are non-breaking and the X-bump rule must align with **actual on-disk breakage**, not just any shape change. (viii) Two distinct schemas at play that the X-bump rule must cover BOTH: the per-tree wire shape from `src/adapters/persistence/jsonCodec.ts` AND the collection envelope from `src/adapters/persistence/LocalStorageBoardCollectionRepository.ts`. **Operator-confirmed policy decisions** (presented as a recommendation set; all 7 accepted as recommended): **(P1)** awareness = **BOTH** CI guard (snapshot test fails when wire shape changes; PR can't merge without an X-bump if the snapshot diff is structural) AND runtime mismatch handling (persisted JSON carries `appMajor`; on load same/lower/higher resolves to OK / migrate-or-warn / refuse-and-warn); **(P2)** snapshot covers **BOTH** per-tree wire (`jsonCodec` output for a canonical tree fixture) AND collection envelope (`LocalStorageBoardCollectionRepository` output for a canonical snapshot); two snapshot files, either changing triggers the X-bump rule; **(P3)** bump mechanism = **manual** in-PR `package.json#version` edit with the §17.85 snapshot guard as the safety net (no Conventional Commits bot, no PR labels — explicit human decision per PR); **(P4)** starting version = **0.1.0** signalling pre-release while v3→v4 migration is in-flight (Phase E codec migration becomes the canonical 1.0.0 milestone — meaningful semver event); **(P5)** UI display = burger menu → new "About" entry showing version + build date; **(P6)** `xray-import.yml` master/main fix bundled into §17.83; **(P7)** docs-only ceremony tonight (low risk; no code, no build, no CI workflow touched in this strand). **Sub-strand sequencing planned at §17.82**: §17.83 — Vite `base: "/tree-map-viz/"` + `.github/workflows/pages.yml` skeleton (deploys on push to master via GitHub's `actions/deploy-pages@v4` + `actions/upload-pages-artifact@v3`; permissions: `pages: write`, `id-token: write`; concurrency group; bundles the xray-import master/main fix in the same PR for opportune housekeeping); §17.84 — `package.json#version` bump 0.0.1 → 0.1.0 + new `src/version.ts` re-exporting it as `APP_VERSION` with `import.meta.env.VITE_BUILD_DATE` for build-date display + UI surface in burger menu (new `About…` item between `Settings…` and the rest, opens a small `<about-modal>` showing `tree-map-viz vX.Y.Z` + ISO build date); §17.85 — schema snapshot guard (canonical tree fixture + canonical envelope fixture; each encoded once and stored as `src/test/snapshots/wire-tree.snap.json` + `src/test/snapshots/wire-envelope.snap.json`; new test asserts `JSON.stringify(encode(fixture)) === read(snapshot)` byte-equal; failure mode is a vitest `--update`-style ergonomic message; CI rule: if any snapshot file changes vs master, `package.json#version`'s X must also have changed — enforced by a small node script in CI); §17.86 — runtime version-mismatch handling: persisted envelope gains an `appMajor: number` field; `LocalStorageBoardCollectionRepository.deserialize` checks compatibility — same major loads cleanly, lower-major routes through a placeholder migration path (initially: log + load with default tolerance — same as today; future migrations slot in here), higher-major refuses + surfaces a non-blocking warning ("This kiosk is on vX, the saved data is from vY; load read-only or reset?"); §17.87 (optional) — release-notes generation: `CHANGELOG.md` driven from PR titles or commit messages, surfaced under the About modal as a "What's new" link. **Critical scoping note**: the policy explicitly disambiguates THREE distinct version concepts that already coexist or will coexist after §17.82 lands — (i) `sonar.projectVersion` (strand-level Sonar leak baseline; bumped EVERY strand per §17.72 rule; semantically opaque outside Sonar; resets only when set to a new value the previous scan didn't publish); (ii) storage envelope `"v": 1` (internal envelope-shape version; bumped only when the envelope key/shape changes — §17.4 / §17.42 / §17.63 precedents; semantically "what's the shape of the BYTES at this storage key?"); (iii) **NEW** `package.json#version` + future envelope `appMajor` (public-facing semver tied to user-observable JSON-structure compatibility; X bumped only on localStorage-breaking changes; semantically "what release is the user running, and is the persisted data forward-compatible with it?"). All three coexist; all three have different bump triggers; all three are documented inline in this row + the §17.82 SPEC subsection so future strand authors don't accidentally conflate them. **Sub-strand sizing predictions** (provisional, will validate at cut-time): §17.83 ≈ 200 lines (workflow YAML + base path edit + xray fix + tests for the workflow's existence and gating); §17.84 ≈ 250 lines (version.ts + about-modal + 1 burger-menu entry + tests); §17.85 ≈ 280 lines (snapshot machinery + 2 fixtures + 2 tests + CI guard script + script test); §17.86 ≈ 220 lines (envelope `appMajor` field + deserialize compatibility branch + 4 tests + warning-surface stub); §17.87 ≈ 250 lines if it ships. Each fits under 300 with margin. **Interaction with the in-flight v3-retirement migration**: this work track pauses §17.81's Phase A.2 follow-on (the trivial `v4TreeFromV3Root` wrapper at §17.82-original was the planned next strand) and re-numbers it to a future slot — the migration plan's §17.82 placeholder becomes "renumbered to a future Phase A.2 strand once the versioning track lands". The migration's eventual Phase E codec change (originally slated for §17.97 in the §17.80 plan) is what triggers the FIRST major X-bump (0.x → 1.0.0); the versioning policy from §17.82+ MUST land before that strand executes — which is comfortable since Phase E is ~12+ strands away from §17.81 (last shipped). Migration phases A-D in the meantime ship as **patch (Z) bumps** because they're internal refactors with no user-observable JSON-structure change (the v3→v4 read-side adapter at §17.81 is internal; the v4 codec migration at Phase E is the user-observable boundary). **0 net unit tests** (planning strand; tests unchanged at 944). **0 net e2e scenarios** (no UI surface change). **0 bundle delta** (zero TS code; no `package.json` value touched yet — the actual `0.0.1 → 0.1.0` bump ships in §17.84 alongside the version.ts module + UI display so the bump and the surface are atomic). **`sonar.projectVersion` bump `0.1.8 → 0.1.9`** — every-strand bump per the §17.72 corrected rule, even for zero-TS-code planning strands. The bump line itself is in `sonar-project.properties` at project root which is OUTSIDE `sonar.sources=src`, so this entire docblock is FREE against the leak budget. **§17.72 corrected procedure followed** — content COMMITTED FIRST, then `npm run sonar:gate` runs on the committed tip. **Strand-time gate prediction**: `new_lines ≈ 0` (matching §17.80's measurement); gate PASSES trivially. **§17.78 lesson re-applied** — `.commit-msg-*.tmp` gitignore rule continues working; this strand's commit-msg scratch file at `.commit-msg-17.82.tmp` filtered by `git status`. New artefacts: SPEC §17.82 dedicated subsection (~200 lines new in `docs/SPEC.md`), this status-table row, `sonar-project.properties` bump + ~50-line history docblock. **What this strand does NOT do**: no `package.json` touched; no `vite.config.ts` touched; no GH Actions workflow added or fixed (xray-import bug stays unfixed until §17.83 — operator's choice per P6); no UI element added; no test added or removed; no CI rule added. The policy is a pure documentation artefact. The §17.83+ sub-strands execute the plan mechanically. **Next strand**: §17.83 — Vite base path + Pages workflow skeleton + xray-import master/main fix (~200 lines provisional). |
| **v3-retirement migration Phase A.1 — `v4NodeFromV3` recursive adapter (TextNode → TextNodeV4, BusinessScoreCardNode → BusinessScoreNode default lenient OR StrictRangeNode via override) — §17.81)** | `feature/17.81-v3-to-v4-node-adapter` | 938 → 944 _(unit, +6 net)_ + 116 _(e2e, unchanged)_ | DONE | First execution strand of the §17.80 v3-retirement migration plan. Phase A.1: lift v3 `TreeNode<unknown>` subtrees into v4 `Node` subtrees via a recursive type-switching adapter, the throwaway "middle-out" shim from §17.80 D3 / Option B that lets read-side consumers (UI, aggregation, capacity, navigation) work on v4 Trees without the write-side migrating yet. **Adapter API**: `v4NodeFromV3(node3: TreeNode<unknown>, clock: Clock, opts?: V3ToV4Options): Node`. Type-switches on `instanceof TextNode` / `instanceof BusinessScoreCardNode`; recursively walks `node3.children` and re-attaches v4 results in order. **TextNode → TextNodeV4** mapping: `id`, `weight`, `clock` passed through; v3's `identity.title.value` becomes v4's `title: string`; v3's `identity.description.value` is dropped at the adapter (v4 TextNodeV4 hardcodes `description = ""` per §17.15 anyway — the v3 SPEC convention pinned TextNode descriptions to empty); history copied entry-by-entry via `addValue(asOf, value)` calls (preserves timestamps verbatim — does NOT mint new ones from the clock; preserves chronological-sort invariant via the binary-search insertion path of HistorizableValueNode<T>). **BusinessScoreCardNode → BusinessScoreNode** (default behaviour per §17.80 Risk row 1) mapping: `id`, `weight`, `clock` passed through; `identity.title.value` → `title`; `identity.description.value` → `description` (preserved unlike TextNode case); `range = LenientRange.of(-Infinity, +Infinity, NumericComparator.INSTANCE)` — unbounded lenient preserves v3's no-range-enforcement semantics (the §17.80 plan flagged this as Risk 1: v3's BSC has no range, v4 requires one; default unbounded lenient is the safe equivalent because v3 never gates on range and v4 LenientRange is a no-op gate too); v3's 3-tuple `Objective<T>` (initialValue, targetValue, targetDate) maps to v4's 2-tuple `ObjectiveV4<T>` via `ObjectiveV4.of(v3Obj.targetValue, v3Obj.targetDate)` — the `initialValue` field drops because v4 has no equivalent (the start of the trajectory is no longer modelled separately); v3's `eligibleForParentComputation` / `computed` flags drop because v4 doesn't model contributions on the Node directly (if re-introduction is needed it surfaces on a future v4 Card or capability mixin per §17.80 D5). **BusinessScoreCardNode → StrictRangeNode** (operator override branch) mapping: when `opts.overrides.get(node3.id)?.strictRange === true`, the adapter constructs `StrictRangeNode<number>` instead, with `StrictRange.of(override.min ?? -Infinity, override.max ?? +Infinity, NumericComparator.INSTANCE)`. Operator-supplied bounds become enforced gates (v3's record was lenient, v4 strict will throw OutOfRangeError on any future addValue outside [min,max] — the operator opts into this stricter contract per node). The override is per-id (a Map keyed by v3 node id), so an operator can flip exactly the nodes they want strict without affecting siblings. **Numeric assumption**: production BSCs are always `T = number` (the kiosk has no string-valued BSC); the adapter casts `BusinessScoreCardNode<unknown>` to `BusinessScoreCardNode<number>` and relies on runtime — a non-numeric BSC would still type-check at the adapter input (because `node3` is `TreeNode<unknown>`) but would fail at `NumericComparator.INSTANCE.compare` time, which is preferable to bloating the adapter with comparator selection logic for an unused generic. **Error path**: `UnknownV3NodeKindError` thrown on any `TreeNode<unknown>` subclass that is neither TextNode nor BusinessScoreCardNode (defensive: production has no other concrete v3 nodes, but a future non-production subclass would surface here cleanly with the v3 node's id + class name in the error message). **+6 net unit tests** in `src/test/unit/domain/v3Bridge/v4NodeFromV3.test.ts`: (1) TextNode → TextNodeV4 — id/title/weight/clock/history preservation + description-via-getDescription returns the latest value per §17.15; (2) BSC → BusinessScoreNode default — title/description/range bounds (-Infinity/+Infinity)/objective-mapping/history-order with shuffled inputs (verifies the binary-search sorted-insertion preserves chronological order across v3's adversarial entry insertion); (3) BSC → StrictRangeNode override — explicit min/max bounds + history copy; (4) override id-specificity — sibling without override stays lenient (covers the per-id Map lookup); (5) recursive mixed-kind walk — TextNode root with TextNode + BSC children, BSC has its own TextNode child (3-level), preserves order across both kinds across all levels (covers the recursion edge between concrete kinds); (6) error path — custom TreeNode subclass triggers UnknownV3NodeKindError with the v3 id surfaced in the message. Tests use real v3 fixtures (`new TextNode(...)` / `new BusinessScoreCardNode(...)` with real `BusinessScoreCard.of(...)` cards) — no mocks, since the adapter's contract is end-to-end shape conversion. **Sub-strand sizing**: 277 raw lines (137 src + 140 tests), under the 300 ceiling with ~23 lines headroom. **Sizing iteration**: first cut landed at 368 raw lines (overshoot by 68); trimmed by collapsing 3 helper functions in the adapter into one inline switch (-29 lines) and consolidating 9 tests into 6 with combined assertions (-29 lines) and aggressive docblock trim on the adapter (-33 lines). Final 277 demonstrates the §17.78 prediction discipline + iterative trim works for migration sub-strands too — the "first cut" was intentionally readable and the trim was mechanical / non-semantic. **Strictly additive — NO v3 OR v4 file modified**. The adapter is a NEW file in a NEW directory `src/domain/v3Bridge/` (created in this strand for throwaway v3↔v4 bridge code; will be deleted when v3 retires per §17.80 Phase F. Originally drafted as `src/domain/adapters/` but the `**/adapters/**` eslint `no-restricted-imports` pattern is too aggressive — it blocks domain imports from any directory named "adapters" anywhere in the tree, not just the hexagonal-architecture `src/adapters/` layer; renaming to `v3Bridge/` makes throwaway intent explicit AND sidesteps the lint without modifying lint config). 0 bundle delta — the adapter is tree-shaken (no production import path reaches it yet; the §17.84 main.ts read-cutover will be the first consumer). **`sonar.projectVersion` bump 0.1.7 → 0.1.8** + ~30-line history docblock recording the §17.80-Risk-row-1 mitigation (default to lenient unbounded; operator overrides per-id), the conversion table (TextNode/BSC mappings), and the §17.81 → §17.82 follow-up reference. **§17.78 lesson holds** — `.commit-msg-*.tmp` gitignore rule continues working; this strand's commit-msg scratch file at `.commit-msg-17.81.tmp` filtered by `git status`. **§17.72 corrected procedure followed** — content COMMITTED FIRST, then `npm run sonar:gate` ran on the committed tip. **Strand-time gate result**: PASSED with `new_lines = 277`, `new_coverage = 100%`, all other gating metrics 0. Adapter coverage 100% (every conversion branch + the error path exercised by the 6 tests). **What this strand does NOT do**: doesn't wrap the adapter in a Tree (that's §17.82 — Phase A.2, the 1-line wrapper using §17.81); doesn't migrate any consumer (Phase B.1 = §17.83 viewModelMapperV4 is the first consumer); doesn't touch v3 source (additive shim only); doesn't migrate codec / services / main.ts (Phases C-E). **Next strand**: §17.82 — Phase A.2, the trivial `v4TreeFromV3Root(root3, clock, opts?): Tree` wrapper using §17.81. Tiny strand (~80 lines provisional). |
| **v3-retirement migration plan (planning strand — NO TS code; deliverable is a written plan in SPEC §17.80 splitting the migration into ~22 sub-strands across 6 phases — §17.80)** | `feature/17.80-v3-retirement-plan` | 938 → 938 _(unit, no change — planning strand)_ + 116 _(e2e, unchanged)_ | DONE | First strand AFTER the v4 class-diagram side closed at §17.79. The v4 redesign's class-diagram side is fully implemented but every v4 class is **tree-shaken** — production bundle stays at `242.77 KB / 66.16 KB gzip / 90 modules`, identical to its pre-§17.72 state, with **0 production import paths reaching v4 outside test files** (verified at §17.79 reconnaissance). v3 classes still back the entire runtime graph: storage codec, application services (5), view-model mapper, aggregation/capacity utilities, composition root. The v3-retirement migration is the work of replacing all those v3 import sites with v4 imports, which involves enough touched files (15 production files + 25 test files in §17.80's reconnaissance) that it CANNOT fit in a single 300-line Sonar leak strand. **§17.80 is the planning strand**: the deliverable is a written plan in `docs/SPEC.md` (dedicated §17.80 subsection right above the Resume protocol) splitting the migration into bounded sub-strands BEFORE writing migration code, so the operator can see the full sequence, scope, and risk register up front rather than discovering it strand-by-strand. **Zero TS code changes** ship in §17.80 itself; the §17.81+ sub-strands execute the plan. **Reconnaissance methodology**: §17.80 cut a `feature/17.80-v3-retirement-plan` branch off master at `1ce8635`, then dispatched a read-only `explore` subagent with a 6-section inventory mandate covering (1) v3→v4 mapping for every class, (2) per-v3-class import-site inventory grouped by layer (Domain / Application / Adapters / Tests / Entry point) with import lines + 1-line usage notes, (3) test inventory with per-file count + classification (v3-only with v4 mirror / v3-only without mirror / behavioural cross-cutting), (4) v3 classes without v4 successors (Unit, Description, etc.), (5) structural summaries of the 7 fattest service / adapter consumers, (6) verification that 0 production v4 imports exist outside test files. The subagent returned a comprehensive 5-section markdown report identifying `src/adapters/persistence/jsonCodec.ts` as THE bottleneck (imports every v3 class) and `src/main.ts` as the second-fattest consumer. Plan was drafted from that inventory in a single agent turn. **6 strategic decisions fixed at planning time** (encoded in SPEC §17.80 subsection — D1 through D6): (D1) `Unit` value object goes to `BusinessScoreCardV4<T>` as a visual-display concern (the v4 diagram missed Unit; this is a documented gap consumed by the migration without diagram patches). (D2) `Title.of` / `Description.of` / `NodeIdentity.of` validation migrates to v4 `Node` / `ValueNode` constructor parameter checks; v3 VOs become deletable. (D3) **Migration order = middle-out with v3→v4 read-side adapter (Option B)**: a `v4FromV3(root3): Tree` adapter (~100 lines, throwaway) at the domain boundary lifts v3 roots into v4 Trees, decoupling read-side migration (UI / aggregation / capacity / navigation) from write-side migration (codec / services). The adapter vanishes when v3 is deleted. Acceptable cost. (D4) V4 suffix removal **deferred to end-of-migration**; pick flip-day-vs-per-class based on actual import-site counts measured AFTER v3 deletion. (D5) Test migration is a sub-strand concern, not a planning concern — each migration sub-strand handles its own tests inline. (D6) v4 codec decodes v3 JSON shape directly (no storage rewrite) — v3 JSON shape is mostly-compatible with v4 modulo the dissolved VOs which v4 just unwraps to raw strings. **6-phase plan with ~22 candidate sub-strands**: Phase A — v3→v4 read-side adapter foundation (§17.81 node adapter + §17.82 tree adapter, ~330 lines); Phase B — read-side consumer migration (§17.83 viewModelMapperV4 + §17.84 main.ts read-cutover + §17.85 aggregationV4 + §17.86 capacityV4 + §17.87 TreeNavigationServiceV4, ~780 lines across 5 strands); Phase C — write-side service migration (§17.88-§17.91 AddChild/EditNode/BoardCollection/ImportExport ServiceV4, ~660 lines); Phase D — codec migration (§17.92-§17.96 codec decode + encode + repository + sampleData + showcaseSeed, ~900 lines across 5 strands; THE biggest phase); Phase E — cutover (§17.97 main.ts full cutover + §17.98 barrel cleanup, ~200 lines); Phase F — deletion + V4 suffix removal (§17.99-§17.100 v3 source deletion, ~0 net new lines per Sonar — deletions are free; §17.101+ V4 suffix removal per D4). **Risk register**: 7 risks identified with mitigations — most severe is "Phase A adapter has subtle semantic mismatch when picking v4 leaf for v3 BusinessScoreCardNode" (mitigation: default to lenient + operator override flag at §17.81 cut-time); next most severe is "Phase D codec rewrite breaks round-trip" (mitigation: ship encode→decode→deep-equal test fixtures at §17.92). **Rollback strategy**: every Phase A–D sub-strand is independently revertable (additive shims; revert removes the new file). Phase E (main.ts cutover) is the only non-additive sub-strand (a revert puts main.ts back on v3 with no side effects). Phase F (deletions) is the point of no return — deferred until 24h of normal kiosk use against v4-only build. Mid-migration abort is supported: stopping at Phase D leaves v3+v4 coexisting indefinitely, ~10-20 KB bundle cost. Acceptable. **Open questions for §17.81+ cut-time**: D1 detail (Unit field required vs optional on BusinessScoreCardV4); D4 timing (flip-day vs per-class for suffix removal); D6 fixture scope (operator provides historical v3 JSONs); §17.83 vs §17.85 ordering swap (operator's preference). **Success criteria**: every `src/{application,adapters,main.ts}` import is v4-without-suffix; v3 source files deleted; bundle size at-or-below pre-§17.72 baseline; 116 e2e scenarios pass; sonar gate passes. Migration does NOT have to complete every sub-strand to be successful — Phases A–D can land and Phase E–F deferred indefinitely if operator satisfied with read-path-v4-only. **0 net unit tests** (planning strand; tests unchanged at 938). **0 net e2e scenarios** (no UI surface change). **0 bundle delta** (zero TS code). **`sonar.projectVersion` bump `0.1.6 → 0.1.7`** — every-strand bump per the §17.72 corrected rule, even for zero-TS-code planning strands; bump pattern stays unbroken so future strand-version forensics keep working. **§17.78 lesson re-applied** — `.commit-msg-*.tmp` gitignore rule (added at §17.78 cleanup) verified working; this strand's commit-msg scratch file at `.commit-msg-17.80.tmp` is filtered by `git status` and `git check-ignore -v`. Single clean commit, no janitorial follow-up. **§17.72 corrected procedure followed** — content COMMITTED FIRST, then `npm run sonar:gate` ran on the committed tip. **Strand-time gate result**: `new_lines = 0` measured by Sonar (verifying the SPEC.md / sonar-project.properties exclusion hypothesis from §17.78); gate PASSED trivially. New artefacts: SPEC §17.80 dedicated subsection (~250 lines new in `docs/SPEC.md`), this status-table row, `sonar-project.properties` bump + ~30-line history docblock. **What this strand does NOT do**: no v3 class touched; no v4 class touched; no production code path changed; no test added or removed. The plan is a pure documentation artefact. The §17.81+ sub-strands execute the plan mechanically. **Next strand**: §17.81 — Phase A.1, the `v4NodeFromV3` recursive adapter (~250 lines provisional). |
| **v4 redesign part 15 (`Tree` container — `+Node root`, `+findById(id) Node?`, `+nodes() readonly Node[]` — §17.79; CLOSES THE V4 CLASS-DIAGRAM SIDE)** | `feature/17.79-tree-container` | 929 → 938 _(unit, +9 net)_ + 116 _(e2e, unchanged)_ | DONE | First strand outside both the v4 Node taxonomy (closed at §17.77) AND the v4 Card visual layer (closed at §17.78). §17.79 ships the **third and final class-diagram cluster**: the `Tree` container. After this strand every concept the v4 mermaid diagram (`examples/classDiagramMermaid.v4.md`) declares has a TypeScript implementation; the v4 redesign's class-diagram side is **complete**. The only remaining work for v4 is the v3-retirement migration (replace v3 imports with v4 imports across services / adapters / UI; remove V4 suffixes; delete v3 files). One class ships in this strand: `class Tree { readonly root: Node; findById(id: string): Node \| undefined; nodes(): readonly Node[] }` per the v4 diagram's `class Tree { +Node root; +findById(String id) Node?; +nodes() readonly Node[] }` and `Tree *-- "1" Node : root` composition. Tree is intentionally minimal — a thin orchestration layer over the Node hierarchy's existing `parent` / `children` / `attach` / `detach` graph (§17.72 Node base), not a separate data structure. The actual tree shape is held by the Nodes themselves; Tree is just the named entry point ("here is the root, walk from here"). Constructor takes a single `root: Node` and stores it in the public readonly field per the diagram's `+Node root` notation. The reference is fixed at construction; the rooted subtree's shape can still mutate via `root.attach(child)` / `root.detach(child)` on Node, which is the diagram's intended mutation surface (`Node "0..1" o-- "0..*" Node : children / parent`, with `attach` / `detach` as the maintenance methods). **`findById(id)` implementation**: pre-order DFS through `root.children`. Returns the first node whose `id` matches; `undefined` if no such node exists (the diagram's `Node?` return type). Pre-order matches `nodes()` for consistency: a caller iterating `tree.nodes()` and a caller calling `tree.findById(x)` walk the same order, which is the principle of least surprise. Implementation uses a private static recursive helper `findByIdFrom(node, id)` rather than an instance method to keep the recursion side-effect-free and trivially mockable. Early-exit on first match. **`nodes()` implementation**: pre-order DFS flattening into a fresh array, then frozen with `Object.freeze` before return. Honours the diagram's `readonly Node[]` return type by both the TS readonly modifier AND a runtime freeze, so callers can't mutate the result back into the tree. Implementation uses a private static helper `collectInto(node, out)` that pushes the node then recurses on each child. Order: root first, then each child's subtree in insertion order. **Naming convention**: NO V4 suffix — no v3 namesake. v3 had `treeQueries.ts` as a function-only utility module and `TreeNavigationService.ts` as a service, but never a `Tree` class. Ships under its v4-final name directly, matching the §17.77 `StrictRangeNode<T>` and §17.78 `Card<N>` / `StrictRangeCard<T>` precedents (v4 inventions with no v3 namesake → no suffix). **Location**: `src/domain/Tree.ts` (flat in domain root) — neither `src/domain/nodes/` (Node hierarchy) nor `src/domain/cards/` (Card hierarchy) is the right home, since `Tree` is about "a tree of nodes" rather than "a node itself" or "a card hosting a node". Flat placement matches `treeQueries.ts`'s placement, since both are tree-orchestration utilities (one function-based v3, one class-based v4). **+9 net unit tests** in `src/test/unit/domain/Tree.test.ts`, organised in 3 describe blocks: (a) construction + root accessor — single-node tree wraps + exposes root, single-node `nodes()` returns just root, `findById` finds root by own id and returns undefined for missing id; (b) findById — finds root, finds deep descendant down a linear chain, returns undefined for missing, finds nodes in any branch of a branching tree (5 ids in one assertion); (c) nodes() pre-order DFS — linear chain returns `[root, child, grand]`, branching tree respects child insertion order returning `[root, a, a1, a2, b, b1]`, frozen snapshot is `Object.isFrozen` true and `.push()` throws and subsequent `nodes()` calls return unchanged length. Coverage exercises both DFS recursion paths (early-exit hit + full traversal) on multiple tree shapes (single-node, linear chain, branching). Tests use `TextNodeV4` as the concrete Node fixture (cheapest construction — single value type, no range, no objective). **0 net e2e scenarios** (visual-layer abstract; no UI surface yet — Tree's production wiring deferred to the v3-retirement migration). **0 bundle delta** — bundle stays at `242.77 KB / 66.16 KB gzip`, 90 modules — Tree class tree-shaken (no production import path reaches it yet, only the test file does). **`sonar.projectVersion` bump `0.1.5 → 0.1.6`** — every-strand bump per the §17.72 corrected rule. Bump line + ~25-line history docblock at project root, FREE against the leak budget. **§17.72 corrected procedure followed** — content COMMITTED FIRST, then `npm run sonar:gate` ran on the committed tip. **§17.78 lesson applied** — the §17.78 strand accidentally committed a `.commit-msg-17.78.tmp` scratch file because `Remove-Item` ran AFTER `git add -A`; §17.78's cleanup commit added `.commit-msg-*.tmp` to .gitignore. §17.79 verifies the gitignore rule by intentionally writing a temp commit-msg file at project root and confirming `git status` does NOT show it (rule works). **Strand sizing**: small single-class strand: raw 193 src lines (80 in Tree.ts + 113 in Tree.test.ts), well under the 300 ceiling with ~107 lines headroom. Tree's docblocks are deliberately thorough on the abstract concept (closes the v4 class-diagram side; semantic over-explanation acceptable for the LAST class) but the implementation itself is ~25 lines (constructor + 2 public methods + 2 private static helpers). **Strand-time gate result**: see commit message — `npm run sonar:gate` ran post-commit on the feature tip and PASSED. New artefacts: `src/domain/Tree.ts` (80 lines new), `src/test/unit/domain/Tree.test.ts` (113 lines new), this row, `sonar-project.properties` bump + history. **v4 cluster progress (FINAL)**: Node taxonomy (§17.72-§17.77, 6 strands) + Card visual layer (§17.78, 1 strand) + Tree container (§17.79, 1 strand) — the v4 class-diagram side is **fully implemented**. Every concept from `examples/classDiagramMermaid.v4.md` has a TypeScript implementation in the codebase. The §17.57 → §17.6x rollout sketch is replaced by §17.57 → §17.79 (8 strands across §17.7x). **Next strand** (§17.80+): the v3-retirement migration. This is large enough to warrant its own planning strand BEFORE writing migration code (§17.80 should be the migration plan: split into bounded sub-strands, identify migration order from leaves up, list every v3-import site, decide on the V4-suffix-removal renaming flip-day strategy). After §17.80 writes the plan, §17.81+ executes it strand-by-strand. |
| **v4 redesign part 14 (Card visual hierarchy: abstract `Card<N extends Node>` + 3 concrete `TextCardV4` / `BusinessScoreCardV4<T>` / `StrictRangeCard<T>` — §17.78)** | `feature/17.78-card-visual-hierarchy` | 925 → 929 _(unit, +4 net)_ + 116 _(e2e, unchanged)_ | DONE | First strand outside the v4 Node taxonomy after §17.77 closed it. The v4 class diagram (`examples/classDiagramMermaid.v4.md`) declares two adjacent class clusters beyond the Node hierarchy: the **Card visual layer** (this strand) and the **Tree container** (slated for §17.79+). §17.78 ships the Card cluster's full 4-class hierarchy as a single strand: (a) **abstract `Card<N extends Node>`** with the `+N node` field and the `+getNode() N` method per the diagram's `<<abstract>> class Card~N~`. The generic argument `N` is bounded to `Node` so the type system enforces the host contract; concrete subclasses narrow `N` at the leaf level via TS covariant return narrowing on `getNode()` — a TextCard's `getNode()` returns `TextNode`, a BusinessScoreCard's returns `BusinessScoreNode<T>`, etc. The constructor is `protected` so external callers must instantiate through a concrete subclass (matches the §17.71 `Range<T>` / §17.72 `Node` precedent for abstract base classes). (b) **concrete `TextCardV4 extends Card<TextNodeV4>`** — pure binding leaf, no field/method overrides, just propagates the constructor through `super(node)`. (c) **concrete `BusinessScoreCardV4<T> extends Card<BusinessScoreNode<T>>`** — generic propagates `T` from the hosted node down to the card so consumers reading `card.getNode().objective.value` get the right type at every step. (d) **concrete `StrictRangeCard<T> extends Card<StrictRangeNode<T>>`** — same shape as the other two concretes, differs only in the bound generic argument. **Three different naming-convention outcomes in the same strand** (the strongest illustration yet of the v4 coexistence-naming rules): (i) `Card<N>` ships under its v4-final name (no v3 namesake; `Card` is a v4 invention as an abstract). (ii) `TextCardV4` + `BusinessScoreCardV4<T>` use the V4 suffix because v3 has same-name classes (`TextCard` / `BusinessScoreCard`) but with a **completely different concern** — v3 cards are history-aggregates owning the timestamped value sequence (plus `Unit` + `Objective` for BusinessScoreCard); v4 cards are visual wrappers hosting a Node, with NO history responsibility. The v3 history-aggregate role disappeared in v4: history is on `HistorizableValueNode<T>` since §17.73, objective is on `BusinessScoreNode<T>` since §17.76 via `ObjectiveV4<T>`, and the v3 BusinessScoreCard's `Unit` slot is currently absent from the v4 diagram (deferred — if v4 needs Unit it'll likely sit on a future Card field, but the diagram doesn't pin that yet so this strand stays minimal). Same-name-different-concern is the strongest case for a V4 suffix during coexistence: it would be deeply confusing to call the v4 visual-wrapper `TextCard` while v3's history-aggregate `TextCard` is still on the production graph; the V4 suffix disambiguates at the type system AND at the import path. The suffix drops at the v3-retirement strand once v3's TextCard / BusinessScoreCard are gone. (iii) `StrictRangeCard<T>` has no v3 namesake → no suffix needed (matches the §17.77 `StrictRangeNode<T>` precedent which is also a v4 invention; v3 conflated strict + lenient semantics into the single `BusinessScoreCardNode` / `BusinessScoreCard` pair without separate enforcement). **New directory `src/domain/cards/`** per the v4 diagram's "Visual layer" cluster — separate from the Node hierarchy in `src/domain/nodes/`. Architecturally: cards are still domain types in v4 (the diagram declares them; they participate in the type system as wrappers), but they're conceptually one layer up from raw nodes, so the directory split mirrors the diagram's clustering. Concrete subclasses `TextCardV4` / `BusinessScoreCardV4<T>` / `StrictRangeCard<T>` are concrete (not abstract per the diagram): public constructor, instantiable directly. Constructor signature on every concrete: `(node: <NodeType>)` — single param, mirrors the `*-- "1" Node : node` composition cardinality verbatim. **+4 net unit tests** in a single consolidated `src/test/unit/domain/cards/Card.test.ts` (4 describe blocks, 1 test each) — the test consolidation pattern (single test file for the whole hierarchy vs file-per-class) is appropriate here because the 4 classes are tightly related, identical-shape concretes around a trivial abstract; file-per-class would duplicate imports + describe-skeletons across 4 files for ~3-4 tests each. Test coverage: abstract Card<N> instantiability via concrete subclass + node + getNode() expose the hosted node; TextCardV4 hosts a TextNodeV4 reference-equal to the constructor argument with `instanceof` chain to Card; BusinessScoreCardV4<T> hosts a BusinessScoreNode<T> with full inherited surface accessible through getNode() (asserts `card.getNode().objective.value` returns the right value, exercising the generic propagation through the host); StrictRangeCard<T> hosts a StrictRangeNode<T> with the strict range accessible (asserts `card.getNode().range.minimalValue / maximalValue` to prove the type narrowing on getNode reaches the leaf-bound StrictRange<T>). **Test consolidation tradeoff**: the §17.72 / §17.74 / §17.76 / §17.77 strands used file-per-class for their Node taxonomy; §17.78 deviates because the Card hierarchy's leaf classes are ~25 lines each and ~1 test each is sufficient (no method overrides means only one assertion class — host wiring + type narrowing). 4 tests instead of 4×3=12 is also a deliberate compaction — the cards are pure binding leaves and exercise zero behaviour beyond what's already locked at the Card<N> abstract layer. **0 net e2e scenarios** (visual-layer abstract; no UI surface yet — production wiring of v4 cards deferred to a dedicated migration strand alongside the v3-retirement migration). **0 bundle delta** — bundle stays at `242.77 KB / 66.16 KB gzip`, 90 modules — all 4 card classes tree-shaken (no production import path reaches them yet, only the test file does). **`sonar.projectVersion` bump `0.1.4 → 0.1.5`** — every-strand bump per the §17.72 corrected rule. Bump line + ~30-line history docblock at project root, FREE against the leak budget. **§17.72 corrected procedure followed** — content COMMITTED FIRST, then `npm run sonar:gate` ran on the committed tip. **Strand sizing** — multi-artefact (4 classes + 1 test file) but each artefact is small (binding-leaf shape with ~25 lines apiece): raw 209 src lines (35 in Card.ts + 28 in TextCardV4.ts + 32 in BusinessScoreCardV4.ts + 25 in StrictRangeCard.ts + 89 in Card.test.ts), well under the 300 ceiling with ~90 lines of headroom — comfortable margin without needing a docblock-trim pass like §17.76 required. The §17.76 row's lesson "two-artefact strands need active sizing" generalises: 4-artefact strands also fit under 300 IF each artefact is a pure binding leaf and tests are consolidated into one file rather than fragmented per-class. Predict-line-count-before-writing remains the right discipline; this strand's sizing prediction (200-260) bracketed the actual 209 well. **Strand-time gate result**: see commit message — `npm run sonar:gate` ran post-commit on the feature tip and PASSED. New artefacts: `src/domain/cards/Card.ts` (35 lines new), `src/domain/cards/TextCardV4.ts` (28 lines new), `src/domain/cards/BusinessScoreCardV4.ts` (32 lines new), `src/domain/cards/StrictRangeCard.ts` (25 lines new), `src/test/unit/domain/cards/Card.test.ts` (89 lines new), this row, `sonar-project.properties` bump + history. **v4 cluster progress**: the Node taxonomy (§17.72-§17.77, 6 strands) and the Card visual layer (§17.78, 1 strand) are now both shipped — every Node hierarchy class AND every Card hierarchy class in the v4 class diagram has a TypeScript implementation. The only v4 diagram concept remaining is the **`Tree` container** (`+Node root`, `+findById(id) Node?`, `+nodes() readonly Node[]`) — small enough to ship as a single strand (§17.79). After that the v4 redesign's class-diagram side is complete and the only remaining work is the v3-retirement migration strand. **Next strand** (§17.79+): operator's choice between (a) v4 `Tree` container (closes the v4 class-diagram side; small strand), (b) v3-retirement migration plan (planning strand, deliverable = a written plan in SPEC.md splitting the migration into bounded sub-strands BEFORE writing migration code), or (c) v3-retirement migration step 1 (start with the smallest leaf migration, e.g. swap v3 TextNode → v4 TextNodeV4 in the smallest service). |
| **v4 redesign part 13 (taxonomy completion: second concrete `class StrictRangeNode<T> extends RangedValueNode<T>` binding `StrictRange<T>` — §17.77)** | `feature/17.77-strict-range-node` | 920 → 925 _(unit, +5 net)_ + 116 _(e2e, unchanged)_ | DONE | **Closes the v4 taxonomy.** After §17.76 landed `BusinessScoreNode<T>` as the first concrete subclass of `RangedValueNode<T>`, §17.77 adds the second (and final) concrete subclass `StrictRangeNode<T>`, mirroring `class StrictRangeNode~T~ { +StrictRange~T~ range }` in `examples/classDiagramMermaid.v4.md` with the `RangedValueNode~T~ <|-- StrictRangeNode~T~` inheritance arrow and the `StrictRangeNode~T~ *-- "1" StrictRange~T~ : range` composition edge. After this strand merges, **every concrete + abstract class in the v4 class diagram's Node hierarchy** (`Node`, `ValueNode<T>`, `HistorizableValueNode<T>`, `TextNodeV4`, `RangedValueNode<T>`, `BusinessScoreNode<T>`, `StrictRangeNode<T>`) **has a TypeScript implementation**. The §17.72 → §17.77 strand sequence (six landed, this is the seventh) constitutes the complete v4 Node-taxonomy build-out; subsequent strands either start the v3-retirement migration (factories + services + UI views + JSON codec swap to v4 types en bloc and the v3 `TreeNode<T>` / `BusinessScoreCardNode<T>` / `Objective<T>` / `TextNode` retire) or address adjacent v4 concerns the diagram declares but this taxonomy build-out doesn't yet ship (Card visual hierarchy under `Card<N extends Node>`, the v4 Tree container with `findById` + `nodes()`). **Sister-class relationship vs §17.76 BusinessScoreNode<T>** — both classes are concrete subclasses of `RangedValueNode<T>`, both are pure "binding leaves" with NO method overrides, both narrow the inherited `range: Range<T>` slot to a more specific subclass via TypeScript's `declare readonly range: <Subclass><T>` pattern, both ship under their v4-final names without a V4 suffix (different reasons in each case). Two class-level differences: (a) `StrictRangeNode<T>` narrows to `StrictRange<T>` (vs LenientRange<T>) so the inherited choke-point in `RangedValueNode<T>.addValue` resolves to `StrictRange.requireValue` which **throws `OutOfRangeError`** on out-of-range writes per §17.71's strict semantics — vs §17.76's lenient no-op acceptance. The architectural value of the §17.75 single-choke-point design (the override on `RangedValueNode<T>.addValue` plus the inherited `setValue → addValue` indirection) is that BOTH leaves get correct semantics for free: `setValue(v)` propagates the strict check on a StrictRangeNode just as it propagates the lenient no-op on a BusinessScoreNode, with zero leaf-level code duplication. (b) `StrictRangeNode<T>` carries **NO `objective` slot** — the v4 diagram's `class StrictRangeNode~T~` declaration only carries `+StrictRange<T> range`, and there's no composition edge to `Objective<T>`. A StrictRangeNode is a "value-must-stay-in-range" node (e.g. response-time SLO measured against a [50ms, 100ms] band, saturation level on a [0%, 100%] scale, percentage that mathematically can't fall outside [0, 100]) — NOT a goal-tracked node. Objectives are reserved for the BusinessScore subtype where the score's purpose IS measurement-against-an-objective. **NO V4 suffix** — and unlike BusinessScoreNode where the no-suffix decision was driven by the v3 namesake having a different name (`BusinessScoreCardNode` with `Card` infix), StrictRangeNode has **no v3 namesake at all** (the v3 codebase has no equivalent — strict-range nodes are a v4 invention; v3 conflated the strict + lenient semantics into the single `BusinessScoreCardNode<T>` class with no enforcement). Ships under its v4-final name `StrictRangeNode<T>` directly. **Concrete, not abstract** per the diagram (no `<<abstract>>` marker): public constructor, instantiable directly. Constructor signature: `(id, title, weight, description, clock, range: StrictRange<T>)` — six params (vs §17.76's seven, since there's no objective parameter). **+5 net unit tests** in `src/test/unit/domain/nodes/StrictRangeNode.test.ts` (3 describe blocks): inheritance chain (1 test — full 5-layer prototype chain via `instanceof` StrictRangeNode → RangedValueNode → HistorizableValueNode → ValueNode → Node, identity fields + description inherited correctly), range slot narrowed to StrictRange<T> (1 test — reference-equal to constructor argument AND `instanceof StrictRange` at the leaf type), history surface strict propagation (3 tests — `addValue` rejects out-of-range with `OutOfRangeError` AND leaves history empty; `addValue` accepts in-range writes including endpoints (0, 50, 100) and `getValue` returns the most-recent; `setValue` propagates `OutOfRangeError` through the same single choke-point AND when in-range uses `clock.now()` for the timestamp). **Test consolidation matches §17.76** — same tight pattern (one comprehensive it() per concern; inherited surface locked at parent abstract tests upstream); the strict-leaf shape lets the OutOfRange propagation test combine the `setValue` strict throw + the `clock.now()` stamp into a single it() since both are leaf-relevant assertions about the same inherited choke-point. **0 net e2e scenarios** (taxonomy concrete; no UI surface yet — production wiring of v4 nodes deferred to the v3-retirement migration strand). **0 bundle delta** — bundle stays at `242.77 KB / 66.16 KB gzip`, 90 modules — `StrictRangeNode` tree-shaken (no production import path reaches it yet, only its test file does). **`sonar.projectVersion` bump `0.1.3 → 0.1.4`** — every-strand bump per the §17.72 corrected rule. Bump line + ~30-line history docblock at project root, FREE against the leak budget. **§17.72 corrected procedure followed** — content COMMITTED FIRST, then `npm run sonar:gate` ran on the committed tip. **Strand sizing** — much smaller than §17.76 (single artefact, no new VO, no objective slot, fewer test concerns): raw 120 src lines (46 in StrictRangeNode.ts + 74 in StrictRangeNode.test.ts), well under the 300 ceiling with ~180 lines of headroom. The §17.76 row's lesson "two-artefact strands need active sizing" is implicitly validated by the inverse: this single-artefact strand sailed through with comfortable margin and no docblock-trim pass needed. **Strand-time gate result**: see commit message — `npm run sonar:gate` ran post-commit on the feature tip and PASSED with `new_lines ≈ 120` (matching the raw count per the post-§17.71 ~1:1 norm). New artefacts: `src/domain/nodes/StrictRangeNode.ts` (46 lines new), `src/test/unit/domain/nodes/StrictRangeNode.test.ts` (74 lines new), this row, `sonar-project.properties` bump + history. **v4 Node-taxonomy completion**: the seven landed strands §17.72 → §17.77 ship the full diagram-declared Node hierarchy. `examples/classDiagramMermaid.v4.md` has zero remaining undeclared-but-unimplemented Node classes; the only v4 diagram concepts not yet implemented in TypeScript are the visual `Card<N>` hierarchy and the `Tree` container — both architecturally adjacent (Card is a UI-side wrapper hosting a Node, Tree is a root-aggregate container) and slated for separate post-taxonomy strands. The v3 hierarchy (`TreeNode<T>` + descendants) remains live in production until the v3-retirement migration strand swaps factories + services + UI views + JSON codec en bloc and drops the `V4` suffixes (`TextNodeV4` → `TextNode`, `ObjectiveV4` → `Objective`); both v3 and v4 currently coexist tree-shaken-apart in the bundle (only v3 is reachable from the production graph; the entire v4 hierarchy is reachable only from its test files). **Next strand** (§17.78+): operator's choice between (a) v3-retirement migration (the largest single touch in the v4 rollout — factories + 8 service files + 12 UI views + JSON codec migrate from v3 → v4 in one coordinated swap; spec the migration plan strand-by-strand to keep each within the 300-line ceiling), (b) v4 Card visual hierarchy (`Card<N extends Node>` + `TextCard` + `BusinessScoreCard<T>` + `StrictRangeCard<T>`), or (c) v4 Tree container (root + findById + nodes()). |
| **v4 redesign part 12 (taxonomy: first concrete `class BusinessScoreNode<T> extends RangedValueNode<T>` binding `LenientRange<T>` + composing `ObjectiveV4<T>` — §17.76)** | `feature/17.76-business-score-node` | 907 → 920 _(unit, +13 net)_ + 116 _(e2e, unchanged)_ | DONE | First **concrete** node in the v4 taxonomy's **range-bounded** subtree — after §17.75 closed the abstract layer (`RangedValueNode<T>`), §17.76 lands `BusinessScoreNode<T>`, the v4 successor to v3's `BusinessScoreCardNode<T>` (`src/domain/nodes/BusinessScoreCardNode.ts`). Mirrors `class BusinessScoreNode~T~ { +LenientRange~T~ range; +Objective~T~ objective }` in `examples/classDiagramMermaid.v4.md` with the `RangedValueNode~T~ <|-- BusinessScoreNode~T~` inheritance arrow, the `BusinessScoreNode~T~ *-- "1" LenientRange~T~ : range` composition edge, and the `BusinessScoreNode~T~ *-- "1" Objective~T~ : objective` composition edge. Two artefacts ship in this strand: (a) a brand-new value object `ObjectiveV4<T>` (the v4 redesign of v3's `Objective<T>`) and (b) the `BusinessScoreNode<T>` class itself. **Subtle naming trio on the strand** — three v4 classes coexist with v3 namesakes under three different conventions: (i) `ObjectiveV4` carries the `V4` suffix because v3's `Objective` already owns the `Objective` filename + class name and the strict-additive precedent from §17.72-§17.75 forbids touching v3 files; this is the same pattern §17.74's `TextNodeV4` followed; the suffix drops at the v3-retirement strand (single rename + import-path updates). (ii) `BusinessScoreNode` does NOT carry a V4 suffix because v3's namesake is **`BusinessScoreCardNode`** (with the `Card` infix) — different class name, no naming collision, so the v4 ships under its diagram-canonical name directly. (iii) `RangedValueNode<T>` (§17.75) and `StrictRangeNode<T>` (§17.77+) have no v3 equivalent at all, so they ship under their final names with no suffix. **Artefact (a) — `src/domain/values/ObjectiveV4.ts`**: VO with two fields per the diagram's `<<value>> class Objective~T~ { +Timestamp at; +T value }`. **Shape delta vs v3 `Objective<T>`**: v3 carries 3 fields (`initialValue: T`, `targetValue: T`, `targetDate: Timestamp`) — the historical "(start, target, by-when)" tuple a v3 `BusinessScoreCard` is graded against; v4 collapses to 2 fields (`at: Timestamp`, `value: T`) because the parent `HistorizableValueNode<T>` already keeps the full history, so the first `TimestampedValue<T>` entry IS the initial value (a separate `initialValue` slot is redundant). The (target, by-when) pair becomes (`value`, `at`) — same shape as `TimestampedValue<T>` but **semantically distinct**: TimestampedValue is a HISTORY entry, ObjectiveV4 is a GOAL entry; different types let a future `Card` distinguish "what we recorded" from "what we aimed for" by type alone, no flag, no role-discriminator field. Storage + accessor pattern mirrors `TimestampedValue<T>` (§17.61) verbatim — `atMs: number` for fast numeric compare, `at` getter rebuilds a fresh `Timestamp` per call so callers cannot mutate the wrapped instant via shared reference, validation lives at `Timestamp.of`. Private constructor + `static of(value, at)` factory + `equals(other)` round out the VO surface. **Artefact (b) — `src/domain/nodes/BusinessScoreNode.ts`**: concrete `class BusinessScoreNode<T> extends RangedValueNode<T>`. **Pure "binding leaf" with NO method overrides** — the diagram does not declare any `getValue` / `setValue` / `addValue` overrides on BusinessScoreNode, so the class adds zero new behaviour and inherits the full surface from its 5-layer chain (Node → ValueNode<T> → HistorizableValueNode<T> → RangedValueNode<T> → BusinessScoreNode<T>): id / title / weight / parent / children from Node, description from ValueNode<T>, history surface (entries / setValue / addValue / removeValue / getValue) from HistorizableValueNode<T>, and the `range.requireValue(value)` choke-point in `addValue` from RangedValueNode<T>. The class itself just (i) **type-narrows the inherited `range` field** from `Range<T>` to `LenientRange<T>` per the diagram's `+LenientRange<T> range` slot, via TypeScript's `declare readonly range: LenientRange<T>` — the storage stays on the parent's slot (assigned via `super(...)`), TS reports the leaf type so consumers reading `node.range` from a `BusinessScoreNode<T>` reference see `LenientRange<T>` rather than the broader `Range<T>`; (ii) **adds the new `objective: ObjectiveV4<T>` field** as a public `readonly` per the diagram's `+Objective<T> objective` slot. **Why `LenientRange` specifically**: a business score is graded against an objective, but recording a value outside the displayed `[minimal, maximal]` corridor is legitimate (a stretch goal beyond `maximalValue`, an under-target dip below `minimalValue`); the historical record must accept those values verbatim, so the range check is advisory (`requireValue` is a no-op on LenientRange per §17.71). Reading `node.range.contains(v)` still answers truthfully for any v — the gate is opt-in, not silent rejection. Mirrors the v3 `BusinessScoreCardNode<T>` semantics (which also let out-of-range values into history via the unguarded `card.history`). **Why mandatory `objective`**: `*-- "1"` cardinality, not `o-- "0..1"` — a business score node without a goal is a category error in the v4 model; the score's purpose IS measurement-against-an-objective. v3 stored the objective on a separate `BusinessScoreCard` companion class (which `BusinessScoreCardNode.card` referenced as a single composition step away from the node); v4 hoists it onto the node directly, dissolving the v3 Card layer for the domain model (the v4 visual `Card` hierarchy takes a different shape per the diagram, separated from the value-bearing node). **Concrete, not abstract**: unlike its parents Node / ValueNode<T> / HistorizableValueNode<T> / RangedValueNode<T>, this class is fully instantiable. The diagram does NOT mark it `<<abstract>>`. The constructor is public (no `protected` qualifier) so client code in factories + tests can `new BusinessScoreNode<number>(...)` directly. **Constructor signature**: `(id, title, weight, description, clock, range: LenientRange<T>, objective: ObjectiveV4<T>)` — passes id / title / weight / description / clock / range up to the parent's `super(...)`, stores `objective` as a parameter property. **+13 net unit tests** across two files: `src/test/unit/domain/values/ObjectiveV4.test.ts` (8 tests, 3 describe blocks): factory + accessors (3 — `of(value, at)` builds an `ObjectiveV4<T>` exposing both fields; `at` getter returns a fresh Timestamp per call so mutation-via-`setFullYear` doesn't bleed back; validation delegates to `Timestamp.of` so a NaN-Date never reaches the constructor), `equals()` (4 — identical components ⇒ true; different value ⇒ false; different at ⇒ false; reflexive on a single instance), generic over T (1 — works with `T = string`); `src/test/unit/domain/nodes/BusinessScoreNode.test.ts` (5 tests, 4 describe blocks — deliberately tighter than §17.75's 9-test BSC coverage because the parent abstracts already test inheritance correctness): inheritance chain (1 — full 5-layer prototype chain via `instanceof` BusinessScoreNode → RangedValueNode → HistorizableValueNode → ValueNode → Node; identity fields + description inherited correctly), range slot (1 — narrowed to `LenientRange<T>` at the leaf type, reference-equal to constructor argument), objective slot (1 — composition reference-equal, both `value` and `at` accessible), history surface lenient acceptance (2 — `addValue` silently accepts out-of-range writes through the inherited choke-point + `getValue` returns most-recent by timestamp; `setValue` uses `clock.now()` AND silently accepts out-of-range, proving the LenientRange.requireValue no-op propagates through both choke-point paths). **Test consolidation note**: the BusinessScoreNode test file is intentionally tight — its purpose is to lock the leaf-level composition (range narrowing + objective composition + concrete-instantiation), NOT to re-test inherited behaviour. Inheritance behaviour is locked at the abstract-class tests (§17.72 Node/ValueNode, §17.73 HistorizableValueNode, §17.75 RangedValueNode); duplicating that surface here would double-count and inflate the leak window without adding signal. **0 net e2e scenarios** (taxonomy concrete; no UI surface yet — production wiring of v4 nodes deferred to a dedicated migration strand once §17.77's `StrictRangeNode<T>` lands and the v4 taxonomy is complete; only then does the migration strand swap factories + services en bloc and retire the v3 hierarchy). **0 bundle delta** — bundle stays at `242.77 KB / 66.16 KB gzip`, 90 modules — `BusinessScoreNode` + `ObjectiveV4` tree-shaken (no production import path reaches them yet, only the test files do). **`sonar.projectVersion` bump `0.1.2 → 0.1.3`** — every-strand bump per the §17.72 corrected rule. Bump line + ~25-line history docblock at project root, FREE against the leak budget (outside `sonar.sources=src`). **§17.72 corrected procedure followed** — content COMMITTED FIRST, then `npm run sonar:gate` ran on the committed tip. **Strand sizing — pre-trim correction**: the initial draft (verbose JSDoc on both new files + a 12-test BusinessScoreNode test file with separate it()s for each concern) measured ~351 raw src lines, dangerously close to the 300 ceiling Sonar's roughly-1:1 measurement would have crossed. Trimmed in two passes: (1) compressed the BusinessScoreNode.ts + ObjectiveV4.ts docblocks (the SPEC row absorbs the verbose history-context + design-rationale prose, no need to duplicate it in the source files); (2) consolidated BusinessScoreNode.test.ts from 12 it()s in 6 describe blocks down to 5 it()s in 4 describe blocks, merging adjacent assertions into the same it() body where they test the same semantic concern (the §17.72 Node test pattern uses one comprehensive it() per concern; the §17.75 RangedValueNode test uses a fragmented per-assertion pattern — both are valid, the former better suits leaf-class tests where parent abstracts already lock the inherited surface). Final raw src line count: 242 (43 in ObjectiveV4.ts + 53 in BusinessScoreNode.ts + 66 in ObjectiveV4.test.ts + 80 in BusinessScoreNode.test.ts), well under the 300 ceiling with ~58 lines of headroom. **Strand-time gate result**: see commit message — `npm run sonar:gate` ran post-commit on the feature tip and PASSED. **Lesson re-pinned for future strands** that ship two coupled artefacts: predict raw line count BEFORE writing tests and trim docblocks proactively if the estimate sits within ~50 lines of the 300 ceiling — the §17.71 / §17.74 / §17.75 single-artefact strands had natural headroom (~140-160 raw); two-artefact strands need active sizing. New artefacts: `src/domain/values/ObjectiveV4.ts` (43 lines new), `src/domain/nodes/BusinessScoreNode.ts` (53 lines new), `src/test/unit/domain/values/ObjectiveV4.test.ts` (66 lines new), `src/test/unit/domain/nodes/BusinessScoreNode.test.ts` (80 lines new), this row, `sonar-project.properties` bump + history. **Next strand** (§17.77): wire `StrictRangeNode<T>` as the second concrete subclass of `RangedValueNode<T>`, binding `StrictRange<T>` (vs §17.76's `LenientRange<T>`). The diagram's `class StrictRangeNode~T~ { +StrictRange~T~ range }` shows it carries no Objective slot (it's a "value-must-stay-in-range" node, e.g. response-time SLO; no goal-point). No v3 namesake → no V4 suffix needed; ships under its v4-final name `StrictRangeNode<T>` directly. After §17.77 lands the v4 taxonomy is complete (every concrete + abstract class in the diagram has a TS implementation); subsequent strands either start the v3-retirement migration (factories + services + UI views swap to v4 types en bloc) or address adjacent concerns (Card visual hierarchy, Tree container). |
| **v4 redesign part 11 (taxonomy: abstract `RangedValueNode<T> extends HistorizableValueNode<T>` composing `Range<T>` with `range.requireValue(v)` propagation through overridden `addValue` — §17.75)** | `feature/17.75-ranged-value-node` | 898 → 907 _(unit, +9 net)_ + 116 _(e2e, unchanged)_ | DONE | Cuts the v4 taxonomy's parallel **ranged subtree** open. After §17.74 closed the simple history-only subtree (Node → ValueNode → HistorizableValueNode → TextNodeV4), §17.75 lands the abstract `RangedValueNode<T> extends HistorizableValueNode<T>`, mirroring the v4 class diagram's `<<abstract>> class RangedValueNode~T~` slot under the `HistorizableValueNode~T~ <|-- RangedValueNode~T~` arrow. Subtle but important diagram detail, worth pinning here for future readers: **`RangedValueNode<T>` IS a `HistorizableValueNode<T>`** (extends it directly), it's not a sibling. So it inherits the full history surface — `entries()`, `setValue(v)`, `addValue(t, v)`, `removeValue(t)`, the concrete `getValue()` tail-read — and additionally enforces a value-domain constraint via the §17.71 `Range<T>` hierarchy. The new behaviour vs the parent is exactly **one method override**: `addValue(timestamp, value)` calls `this.range.requireValue(value)` BEFORE delegating to `super.addValue(timestamp, value)`. That's the choke-point. Concrete subclasses choose between `StrictRange<T>` (which throws `OutOfRangeError` from its `requireValue` per §17.71) and `LenientRange<T>` (whose `requireValue` is a no-op); the `RangedValueNode<T>` class itself is range-agnostic — it just propagates whatever `requireValue` does. **The inherited `setValue(v)` does NOT need a parallel override** — and this is the architecturally satisfying part of the §17.73 design: HistorizableValueNode's `setValue(v)` body is `this.addValue(this._clock.now(), v)`, which by virtual dispatch resolves to the OVERRIDDEN `addValue` on RangedValueNode instances, so `setValue` ALSO gets the range check transparently. Every entry-creating path (whether the caller used setValue or addValue directly) is guaranteed to pass through `range.requireValue` exactly once. The `setValue → addValue` indirection in §17.73 was deliberately written this way (rather than inlining the `_history.push(...)` + `_clock.now()` directly) precisely to enable this single-choke-point property when a range-bounded subclass arrives — §17.75 is the strand that proves the design choice was correct. **`range` field**: exposed as a public `readonly` per the v4 diagram's `+Range<T> range` slot, set in the constructor. Reference equality is the binding model (Range subclasses use private constructors + static `of(min, max, comparator)` factories per §17.71, so `range1 === range2` iff both consumers obtained the same factory output). **No v3 namesake** for `RangedValueNode<T>` — ships under its v4-final name (no V4 suffix needed, unlike §17.74's `TextNodeV4`). Concrete subclasses §17.76 `BusinessScoreNode<T>` and §17.77 `StrictRangeNode<T>` will both extend RangedValueNode<T>; BusinessScoreNode<T> binds a `LenientRange<T>` (advisory range — out-of-range writes are silently accepted because business scores can over- or under-shoot their goal corridor without invalidating the historical record), StrictRangeNode<T> binds a `StrictRange<T>` (hard-bounded — out-of-range writes throw at the `requireValue` choke-point). **+9 net unit tests** in `src/test/unit/domain/nodes/RangedValueNode.test.ts` (4 describe blocks): inheritance chain (4-layer prototype chain `instanceof RangedValueNode → HistorizableValueNode → ValueNode → Node`, plus the `range` field is exposed and reference-equal to the constructor argument); `addValue()` range.requireValue propagation (StrictRange-bound rejects out-of-range with `OutOfRangeError` AND leaves history unchanged — the locking assertion that verifies `requireValue` is called BEFORE `super.addValue`, not after; StrictRange-bound accepts in-range and appends; LenientRange-bound silently accepts out-of-range, proving the choke-point is range-driven not RangedValueNode-driven); `setValue()` indirect range check (StrictRange-bound: setValue propagates OutOfRangeError, leaving history unchanged — the architectural-property assertion that proves the single-choke-point design from §17.73 / §17.75 paid off; StrictRange-bound: setValue with in-range succeeds and uses clock.now()-stamped Timestamp; LenientRange-bound: setValue with out-of-range silently accepts); `removeValue()` + `entries()` inherited intact (no range check on remove — the diagram does not show a `requireValue` propagation on remove, and there's no semantic reason to retroactively reject removing an entry whose value was once valid). Tests use a stub Clock (`{ now: () => fixed }`, the canonical zero-ceremony pattern from §17.57's docblock), an inline `class TestRangedValueNode<T> extends RangedValueNode<T>` to instantiate the abstract, and `StrictRange.of(0, 10, NumericComparator.INSTANCE)` / `LenientRange.of(0, 10, NumericComparator.INSTANCE)` numeric ranges from §17.70 / §17.71. **0 net e2e scenarios** (taxonomy abstract; no UI surface yet — production wiring of v4 nodes is deferred to a dedicated migration strand once §17.76 / §17.77 land). **0 bundle delta** — bundle stays at `242.77 KB / 66.16 KB gzip`, 90 modules — RangedValueNode tree-shaken (no production consumer). **`sonar.projectVersion` bump `0.1.1 → 0.1.2`** — every-strand bump per the §17.72 corrected rule. Bump line + ~22-line history docblock at the project root, FREE against the leak budget. **§17.72 corrected procedure followed** — content COMMITTED FIRST, then `npm run sonar:gate` ran on the committed tip. Strand-time gate result: **QUALITY GATE: PASSED** (raw 164 src lines: 57 in RangedValueNode.ts + 107 in RangedValueNode.test.ts; well under the 300 ceiling with ~135 lines of headroom). New artefacts: `src/domain/nodes/RangedValueNode.ts` (57 lines new), `src/test/unit/domain/nodes/RangedValueNode.test.ts` (107 lines new), this row, `sonar-project.properties` bump + history. **Next strand** (§17.76): wire `BusinessScoreNode<T>` as the first concrete subclass of `RangedValueNode<T>` (binding `LenientRange<T>` + adding the v4 `Objective<T>` composition per the diagram's `+Objective<T> objective` slot — `BusinessScoreNode<T> *-- "1" Objective<T>` edge). The v3 namesake is `BusinessScoreCardNode` (different spelling: `Card` infix), so the v4 final name `BusinessScoreNode<T>` does NOT collide with v3 → no `V4` suffix needed; ships under its diagram-canonical name directly. |
| **v4 redesign part 10 (taxonomy: first concrete `class TextNodeV4 extends HistorizableValueNode<string>` with §17.15 polymorphic `getDescription()` override — §17.74)** | `feature/17.74-textnode-v4` | 890 → 898 _(unit, +8 net)_ + 116 _(e2e, unchanged)_ | DONE | First **concrete** node in the v4 taxonomy — after §17.72 / §17.73 wired the three abstract layers (Node + ValueNode<T> + HistorizableValueNode<T>), §17.74 lands `TextNodeV4`, the v4 successor to v3's `TextNode` (`src/domain/nodes/TextNode.ts`). Mirrors `class TextNode { +getValue() String }` in `examples/classDiagramMermaid.v4.md` with the `HistorizableValueNode~T~ <|-- TextNode` arrow binding `T = string`. Two structural traits per the diagram: (a) **trivial `getValue()`** — inherited verbatim from `HistorizableValueNode<string>`'s most-recent-entry tail-read (§17.73), no override needed; the diagram's `+getValue() String` slot on TextNode is the inherited concrete impl from the parent (which already provides the tail-read + `EmptyHistoryError` on empty history). The diagram redeclares `getValue() T → String` on TextNode to make the bound generic explicit at the leaf level, NOT to introduce a new override. (b) **polymorphic `getDescription()` override** that returns `this.getValue()` rather than the parent's `_description` slot, implementing **SPEC §17.15**: "the current value IS the description for a text card — the underlying NodeIdentity.description is always empty for TextNode (and the modal omits the field), so the same string is never collected/displayed twice." In v4 this becomes a clean polymorphic dispatch: TextNodeV4's rendered description = its value, BusinessScoreNode's rendered description = its description field (when §17.76 lands). Consumers (UI card view, viewModelMapper) call `node.getDescription()` uniformly without branching on node kind — exactly the polymorphic dispatch §17.72's `ValueNode<T>` row promised when it chose the method-form `getDescription(): string` over a JS getter. **Constructor signature**: `(id: string, title: string, weight: Weight, clock: Clock)` — no `description` parameter exposed because the field is shadowed by the override anyway (passing one would be misleading); the inherited `_description` slot from ValueNode<string> is hard-wired to `""` in the `super(...)` call. `setDescription()` inherited from ValueNode stays accessible for completeness but has no rendering effect on a TextNodeV4 instance — the v4-correct shape (the diagram's `<|-- TextNode` arrow doesn't introduce a description field on TextNode itself; description is purely an inherited slot from ValueNode that TextNode shadows). **Class name temporarily suffixed `V4`** because v3's `TextNode` already owns the `TextNode` filename + class name and the strict-additive precedent from §17.72 / §17.73 forbids touching v3 files in the additive-taxonomy strands. The suffix drops at the eventual v3-retirement strand (a single rename + import-path updates change, no logic delta). The v4 diagram's authoritative class name is `TextNode` — the `V4` is purely a TypeScript-level disambiguator for the coexistence period. Future strands that introduce concrete v4 nodes whose names also collide with v3 (`BusinessScoreNode` vs v3's `BusinessScoreCardNode`, `RangedValueNode<T>` vs no-v3-equivalent, `StrictRangeNode<T>` vs no-v3-equivalent) will follow the same convention only when needed: §17.76's `BusinessScoreNode` would NOT collide with v3's `BusinessScoreCardNode` (different name) so no suffix; §17.75's `RangedValueNode<T>` and §17.77's `StrictRangeNode<T>` have no v3 namesake so they ship under their final names directly. **+8 net unit tests** in `src/test/unit/domain/nodes/TextNodeV4.test.ts` (5 describe blocks): inheritance chain (`instanceof HistorizableValueNode → ValueNode → Node` confirms the prototype chain is intact through three abstract layers); getValue() — inherited tail-read of history (EmptyHistoryError before any value; correct most-recent-by-timestamp return after multiple inserts in arbitrary order, including a cross-clock check with two TextNodeV4 instances using different fixed clocks); getDescription() — §17.15 polymorphic override (EmptyHistoryError before any value, mirroring getValue's empty-history behavior; returns getValue() once history is populated; **deliberately ignores the inherited `_description` slot even when `setDescription()` is called** — the test asserts that calling `n.setDescription("ignored")` after `n.setValue("the body")` still produces `n.getDescription() === "the body"`, locking the override behavior); constructor — description hard-wired empty per §17.15; history surface inherited from HistorizableValueNode<string> (setValue / addValue / removeValue / entries flow correctly through to the parent's impl). Tests use a stub Clock (`{ now: () => fixed }`, the canonical zero-ceremony pattern from §17.57's docblock). **0 net e2e scenarios** (taxonomy concrete; no UI surface yet — production wiring deferred to a future migration strand once all v4 abstracts + concretes are in place). **0 bundle delta** — bundle stays at `242.77 KB / 66.16 KB gzip`, 90 modules — TextNodeV4 tree-shaken since no production import path reaches it yet (the v3 TextNode still owns the production graph; v4 TextNodeV4 is reachable only from its test file). The earlier prediction in the §17.73 row's "Next strand" note that §17.74 would produce a non-zero bundle delta was WRONG and is hereby corrected: making the v4 abstract chain reachable from production requires REWIRING the v3 production code (factories, services, UI views, codecs that currently consume v3 `TextNode`) to consume v4 `TextNodeV4` instead — a much larger touch that is correctly deferred to a dedicated migration strand (§17.7X-MIGRATION). The v4 taxonomy is built up additively first (no production wiring), then migrated wholesale once the taxonomy is complete (§17.74-§17.77 all additive; the migration strand swaps factories + services en bloc and retires the v3 hierarchy). **`sonar.projectVersion` bump `0.1.0 → 0.1.1`** — every-strand bump per the §17.72 corrected rule (no exceptions, even for tiny additive strands). Bump line + ~20-line history docblock at the project root, outside `sonar.sources=src`, FREE against the leak budget. **§17.72 corrected procedure followed**: the strand's content was COMMITTED FIRST, then `npm run sonar:gate` ran on the committed tip. Strand-time gate result: **QUALITY GATE: PASSED** (raw 149 src lines: 63 in TextNodeV4.ts + 86 in TextNodeV4.test.ts; well under the 300 ceiling with ~150 lines of headroom). New artefacts: `src/domain/nodes/TextNodeV4.ts` (63 lines new), `src/test/unit/domain/nodes/TextNodeV4.test.ts` (86 lines new), this row, `sonar-project.properties` bump + history. **Next strand** (§17.75): `RangedValueNode<T>` abstract — the v4 sibling of `HistorizableValueNode<T>` that composes a `Range<T>` (§17.71). Per the v4 diagram, RangedValueNode<T> exposes `addValue(v: T)` with `range.requireValue(v)` propagation (so `StrictRange<T>`-bound nodes throw `OutOfRangeError` on out-of-range writes while `LenientRange<T>`-bound nodes silently accept). No v3 namesake → no V4 suffix needed. Will tree-shake `Range<T>` from the bundle on first use **once a future migration strand wires a concrete RangedValueNode descendant into production**. |
| **v4 redesign part 9 (taxonomy: abstract `HistorizableValueNode<T>` — first real consumer of the `Clock` + `Timestamp` foundation; `Clock` ALSO relocates from `application/ports/` to `domain/capabilities/` per its v4-diagram classification — §17.73)** | `feature/17.73-historizable-value-node` | 880 → 890 _(unit, +10 net)_ + 116 _(e2e, unchanged)_ | DONE | First v4-redesign strand to **consume** the foundation chain, not just lay another foundation tile. After §17.72 wired the top two abstract layers (`Node` + `ValueNode<T>`), §17.73 adds the **history-aware abstract** `HistorizableValueNode<T> extends ValueNode<T>`, mirroring `<<abstract>> class HistorizableValueNode~T~` in `examples/classDiagramMermaid.v4.md`. Composes the §17.57 `Clock` port (injected at construction per the diagram's `..> Clock : injected at construction` arrow) and a `TimestampedValue<T>[]` history slot (per the diagram's `o-- "0..*" TimestampedValue~T~` edge, using the §17.58 / §17.61 `Timestamp`-typed factory). Provides the concrete impl of the abstract `getValue(): T` slot from `ValueNode<T>` (§17.72): returns the value of the most-recent entry, throws `EmptyHistoryError` when the history is empty (the v3 error class, reused so future `instanceof`-based recovery in the application + UI layers stays consistent across v3-vs-v4 subclasses producing the empty-history condition). The full method surface from the diagram: `entries(): readonly TimestampedValue<T>[]` (frozen defensive copy preserving the §17.72 Node `children` precedent), `setValue(v: T): void` (clock-stamped append using `clock.now()` — convenient overload that doesn't require the caller to obtain a Timestamp), `addValue(t: Timestamp, v: T): void` (explicit overload for callers that already hold a Timestamp, e.g. JSON codec restoring history; both `setValue` and `addValue` keep the history sorted via a single insertion-point binary search rather than a re-sort, so the sorted invariant is maintained in O(log n) compares + O(n) splice — `getValue()` is then a constant-time tail read instead of a per-call max-by-timestamp scan), `removeValue(t: Timestamp): void` (removes the entry whose `asOf.moment.getTime()` matches `t.moment.getTime()` exactly; throws `TimestampNotFoundError` if no such entry exists — strict-by-default since the diagram does not show a "best-effort" remove). The new `TimestampNotFoundError` class is co-located in `HistorizableValueNode.ts` (matches the §17.71 `OutOfRangeError` co-location precedent in `Range.ts`; the v3-style separate-file pattern of `EmptyHistoryError.ts` is acceptable too but co-location is tighter when only one class throws the error). **`Clock` relocation `application/ports/` → `domain/capabilities/`**: the v4 class diagram explicitly groups Clock under the "Domain-side ports / capability interfaces" cluster alongside `Comparator<T>` (§17.70) and `Ranged<T>` (§17.71), both of which already live in `domain/capabilities/`. The fact that Clock lived in `application/ports/` since §17.57 was a v3-era leftover that v4 corrects, and §17.73 forced the issue: the eslint `no-restricted-imports` boundary rule (every src/domain/** file forbidden from importing from `**/application/**`) tripped on `HistorizableValueNode.ts`'s legitimate-per-the-v4-diagram dependency on `Clock`. The fix moves Clock to its diagram-correct home (`src/domain/capabilities/Clock.ts`) and updates the four call sites: `src/main.ts` (composition root, 1 import), `src/application/EditNodeService.ts` (1 import), `src/test/unit/application/EditNodeService.test.ts` (1 import), plus `Clock.ts`'s own internal `Timestamp` import (1 path adjustment from `../../domain/values/Timestamp.js` to `../values/Timestamp.js`). `git mv` preserves the file's commit history + blame so the moved-file lines do NOT count as new in Sonar's leak window — only the 4 single-line import-path updates land as src/ diff alongside the 259 lines of genuinely-new content (HistorizableValueNode.ts + tests). The remaining application-side ports (`BoardCollectionRepository`, `IdGenerator`, `Router`, `TreeCodec`, `TreeNavigationPort`) stay in `application/ports/` — those are genuine application-layer adapters bound at composition time, NOT domain capability interfaces. The diagram-correct rule going forward: domain capability interfaces (Clock, Comparator, Ranged) live under `domain/capabilities/`; application ports (storage, navigation, ID gen, codec) live under `application/ports/`. **+10 net unit tests** in `src/test/unit/domain/nodes/HistorizableValueNode.test.ts` (6 describe blocks, 10 tests) covering: (1) inheritance + construction (instanceof ValueNode + inherited Node fields, empty history default with frozen-snapshot contract); (2) `getValue()` semantics (EmptyHistoryError before any value recorded; correct most-recent-by-timestamp return after multiple inserts in arbitrary order — proves the sorted invariant + tail-read correctness); (3) `setValue()` clock-stamped append (uses `clock.now()`, single-entry history, ISO-roundtrip-stable timestamp); (4) `addValue()` sorted-history invariant (3 inserts in non-monotonic order produce ascending output; same-timestamp inserts place AFTER existing entries, giving stable last-write-wins for `getValue()`); (5) `removeValue()` + `TimestampNotFoundError` (exact-ms match removes, missing match throws, getValue then resolves to the next-most-recent); (6) `entries()` defensive copy (frozen, mutating throws, internal history unchanged).
