Submit dispatches with the trimmed value; the field is reset on every open via `willUpdate` so a stale half-typed name from a previous session doesn't leak across opens (the same idiom as §17.31's `deleteArmed` reset).
  - Cancel / Escape / backdrop / close-X all dispatch `boards-panel-cancel`. Layered styling per §17.29 (`static styles = [modalFrameStyles, css\`<per-modal layout>\`]`).
- `src/adapters/ui/shell/TreeGraphScreen.ts` — adds `<boards-panel-modal>` to the modal template, exposes `openBoardsPanelModal(target)` / `closeBoardsPanelModal()` / `setBoardsPanelError(message)` / `isBoardsPanelModalOpen` / `boardsPanelModalElement` (parallel to the §17.31 board-settings seam). Same auto-close-on-cancel handler (`handleBoardsPanelModalClose`); confirm / create paths intentionally do NOT auto-close — the composition root drives the close after a successful service call so a failed call (e.g. duplicate name) keeps the modal open with an inline error.
- `src/main.ts` — three concrete additions:
  - The `burger-menu-action` listener gains a `boards` branch: assemble `{ boards: list().map(({id,name}) => …), currentBoardId: getCurrentBoardId() }` and call `screen.openBoardsPanelModal(target)`. Domain `tree`s never cross the modal boundary (the snapshot is id + name only).
  - `boards-panel-switch` listener: `boards.switchTo(boardId)` → on success re-seat `nav` over `boards.getCurrentBoard().tree` + `router.replace({ boardId, focusNodeUuid: tree.id })` → `closeBoardsPanelModal()` → `refresh()`. On failure: `setBoardsPanelError(reason)`, modal stays open.
  - `boards-panel-create` listener: build a fresh seed via the new `makeNewBoardSeedTree(name, idGen)` helper, call `boards.createBoard(name, seedTree, undefined)` → same re-seat / replace / close / refresh triple. Failure path mirrors switch.
  - New helper `makeNewBoardSeedTree(boardName, idGen)`: builds a single `TextNode` root titled with the (trimmed) board name, weight 1, and a one-entry `TextCard` carrying `Welcome to **<name>**.` dated "now". Falls back to `"New board"` for an empty / whitespace-only name (defence-in-depth — the modal already rejects empty names, but the service-side guard doubles up).
- `src/test/unit/adapters/ui/modal/BoardsPanelModal.test.ts` — **new file**, **+11 tests**: closed renders nothing; row order + current marker + Switch-button-on-non-current-only; Switch dispatches with the correct id; Create-button gating (empty / whitespace / valid); Create dispatches with trimmed name; Cancel / close-X / backdrop all dispatch cancel; inline error rendering; willUpdate clears the form on re-open; single-board collection (the empty-storage seed shape) renders with no Switch button anywhere but Create still works.
- `src/test/unit/adapters/ui/shell/TreeGraphScreen.test.ts` — **+6 tests** in a new `describe("boards-panel modal seam (§17.34)")` block: closed by default; `openBoardsPanelModal` opens + seeds the target; cancel from the modal closes it; `closeBoardsPanelModal` + `setBoardsPanelError` are public seams; switch / create do NOT auto-close (composition root must call `closeBoardsPanelModal`).
- `src/test/e2e/features/persistence/board_collection.feature` — **new feature**, **+7 scenarios**: panel opens with the seeded showcase board (current); cancel closes the panel; Create button gates on a trimmed name; create switches to the new board (drawer label updates, URL hash flips); after create both boards are listed with the new one as current; switch back to Showcase updates the drawer label; create persists across reload.
- `src/test/e2e/steps/persistenceSteps.ts` — extended with the boards-panel steps (`the boards-panel modal is open|closed`, `the boards-panel lists N boards`, `… has a board named X marked as current|not marked as current`, `I tap the boards-panel switch button for X`, `I type X into the new-board name field`, `I tap the boards-panel Create button`, `I cancel the boards-panel modal`, `Create button is disabled|enabled`). Each switch / create step polls for the URL hash change before yielding so the asynchronous `service → router.replace → refresh` pipeline cannot race the next step (same idiom as the §17.33 import step).
- `src/test/e2e/pageObjects/TreeGraphPage.ts` — adds the `boardsPanelModalHost / Panel / Rows / RowById / RowNameByBoardId / SwitchByBoardId / NewNameField / CreateBtn / CancelBtn / isBoardsPanelModalOpen` accessors.
- `docs/SPEC.md` — §17.0 status row flips Phase 10B from TODO → DONE (with the new test counts) and bumps the §17.33 deferred-list line; new §17.34 (this section); Resume protocol bumped to **672 tests / 50 files** + **91 e2e scenarios**.

**Decisions taken during this commit**:

- **Switch / Create do NOT auto-close on the modal side.** The composition root owns the close step (after a successful service call) so a failed create (duplicate name, etc.) can keep the modal open with `setBoardsPanelError(reason)` for retry. Mirrors the §17.28 `<edit-node-modal>` and §17.31 `<board-settings-modal>` patterns.
- **`willUpdate` resets `newName` on every open, not on every close.** Resetting on close would briefly clear the input before the close transition completes, looking janky if the modal animates out (it doesn't today, but the §17.29 frame contract leaves room for one). Resetting on open is the same hook §17.31 uses for `deleteArmed` and matches the operator's mental model: "every open is a fresh session".
- **Switch button is suppressed on the current row, not just disabled.** A disabled button still occupies space and reads as "this is something I could do but can't right now", which is misleading — the operator can't switch to themselves. Replacing the button with a `(current)` badge is clearer and lets the row dedicate its trailing space to the marker (which is also more scannable in the list).
- **Service-side guards stay; modal-side gates ARE defence-in-depth.** Both ends validate the trimmed name. The modal-side gate (Create button disabled on empty trimmed name) is the UX ceiling — tells the operator immediately that they can't submit. The service-side guard (`createBoard` returns `{ ok: false, reason: "Board name cannot be empty." }` on empty / whitespace-only) is the contract the test bridge or any future caller must respect.
- **Snapshot crosses the modal boundary as `{ id, name }` only — `tree` and `freshDateColor` stay on the service side.** The modal has no business knowing about domain types; passing the full `Board` would have leaked `TreeNode<unknown>` references through the UI seam (and bloated the prop diff with deep object identity). The plain `{ id, name }` projection keeps the modal a pure consumer and matches the pattern §17.31's `BoardSettingsTarget` already established (the settings target similarly carries `freshDateColor` as a serialised hex, not a domain value object).
- **Drawer-handle / burger-trigger / boards-item is the standard "open the panel" prelude in the e2e Background.** Mirrors `shell/board_settings.feature` so authors of future Phase-10 scenarios can copy the four-step prelude unchanged. Each step is also already covered by `shell/drawer.feature` + `shell/burger_menu.feature` + the `tap the burger menu item with action {string}` step from §17.31 — no new boilerplate.

**What's testable today** (snapshot at landing):

- `npm test` — **672 unit tests** across **50 files** (~10 s). Net **+17** vs §17.33: **+11** new in `BoardsPanelModal.test.ts` + **+6** new screen-seam tests in `TreeGraphScreen.test.ts`.
- `npm run lint` (`tsc --noEmit`), `npm run lint:rules` (ESLint layered rules) — clean.
- `npm run build` — clean. Bundle grew **~10 KB raw / ~2.5 KB gzip** (from 168.12 KB / 43.62 KB at §17.33 to **178.02 KB / 46.17 KB**) for the new modal element, the composition root's switch / create handlers, the `makeNewBoardSeedTree` helper, and the imports of `Title` / `Description` / `Weight` / `TimestampedValue` / `TextCard` / `NodeIdentity`. The on-demand `testBridge` chunk stays at 0.75 KB. The growth is a one-time hit (the modal is small in absolute terms — ~3 KB raw — but the value-object imports it pulled into `main.ts` were absent until §17.34 because the prior code paths only constructed nodes via `AddChildService`'s already-bundled path).
- `npm run test:e2e` — **91 scenarios** all green (was 84; **+7** in `persistence/board_collection.feature`).
- `npm run dev` / `npm run preview` — open the kiosk, drawer + burger menu, tap **Boards…** → panel opens with the seeded board marked current; type a new name + Create → panel closes, drawer label re-paints with the new name, URL hash flips to the new board; reopen Boards… → both boards listed with the new one current; tap **Switch** on the original → panel closes, drawer label re-paints back, URL hash flips back; reload the page → the new board is still in the collection and still current.

**What's deferred to §17.35**:

- **`routing/{deep_link,focus_to_url,unknown_uuid_fallback}.feature`** — pin the URL contract end-to-end. The routing wiring already exists (drill + breadcrumb + close-to-parent + import + switch / create all push or replace through `HashRouter`); these scenarios just close the §15.6 TP-D test plan.
- **`persistence/load_save.feature`** — explicit reload test that asserts every shape of mutation (drill / inline edit / add-child / settings change / board switch / board create) survives a reload. Several existing features already exercise reload as a follow-up step (e.g. `board_collection.feature`'s persistence-across-reload scenario lands here in §17.34); the dedicated feature will collate them as an explicit contract.

---

### 17.35 Phase 10 part C — routing/* + persistence/load_save BDD scenarios

**Trigger** — final third of Phase 10. The routing pipeline has been wired and tested at the unit level since §17.11 (HashRouter ×16 tests); §17.20 added drill → router.push; §17.32 reaffirmed it across the FLIP morph rewrite; §17.33 added router.replace on import; §17.34 added router.replace on board switch + create. What was still missing was the **BDD scenarios pinning the contract end-to-end** — a reviewer reading the SPEC sees "the URL is the source of truth for focus" but cannot find an executable scenario that exercises browser back, an external hash change, an unknown-UUID fallback, or a reload-survives-mutation contract. §17.35 closes that gap.

**Design decisions taken** (small — most of the wiring is already in place):

- **No new unit tests.** Every code path these scenarios exercise is already covered by `HashRouter.test.ts` (×16) + `BoardCollectionService.test.ts` (×28) + `EditNodeService.test.ts` (×13) + `AddChildService.test.ts` (×11) + `LocalStorageBoardCollectionRepository.test.ts` (×16). Adding parallel "routing" unit tests would have been a duplicate of the integration coverage these features now provide.
- **`page.goBack()` is the right primitive for browser-Back.** Playwright surfaces it as a first-class API; using the test bridge to manually pop history would have been a backdoor that bypasses the actual `popstate` → `hashchange` → `router.onChange` chain. Same rationale as §17.33's choice to drive the file picker through `filechooser` rather than a bridge backdoor.
- **`load_save.feature` is a representative slice, not a full matrix.** Dozens of existing scenarios already implicitly exercise persistence (e.g. `board_collection.feature`'s "Newly-created board persists across reload" lands a complete create→reload→still-there contract). The dedicated feature picks the **three least-already-tested mutation kinds** — inline title edit, drilling state (URL focus), add-child — so the coverage matrix has no holes without a test-suite explosion. Settings changes (renaming, fresh-colour) and board switch / board create persistence are already covered by their respective Phase 10A / 10B features.
- **Drawer-open prelude in `focus_to_url`'s breadcrumb scenario, not a feature-wide Background.** The breadcrumb sits inside the drawer body; the auto-hide drawer intercepts pointer events when closed (existing `shell/breadcrumb.feature` Background also opens the drawer first). Inlining the `tap the drawer handle` step inside the one scenario that needs it (rather than promoting it to the feature Background) keeps the other three scenarios (drill / browser-back / close-to-parent) drawer-independent and faster.

**One-line summary** — Four new BDD features (`routing/deep_link`, `routing/focus_to_url`, `routing/unknown_uuid_fallback`, `persistence/load_save`) + one new step file (`routingSteps.ts`) close the §15.6 TP-D test plan; **+13 e2e scenarios**, **0 net unit tests**, **0 net production code lines**.

**Files added**:

- `src/test/e2e/features/routing/deep_link.feature` — **+3 scenarios** (reload on non-root deep link lands on that node; reload on root deep link lands at root; external hash change to an existing node updates focus). Each scenario uses the test bridge's `navigateTo` (the `When I navigate the kiosk to the focus hash for node {string}` step) or the §17.34 `When I focus on node {string}` step to drive the URL, then asserts `the focused id is {string}` + `the URL hash includes {string}` + `the focused title is {string}` to pin the contract.
- `src/test/e2e/features/routing/focus_to_url.feature` — **+4 scenarios** covering the four user-driven focus mutations: drill (pushState), breadcrumb tap, close-to-parent X, and browser back after a drill. The drawer-open prelude is inlined in the breadcrumb scenario (the breadcrumb lives inside the drawer body); the others run drawer-closed.
- `src/test/e2e/features/routing/unknown_uuid_fallback.feature` — **+3 scenarios**: external hash change to an unknown UUID falls back to the board root (and rewrites the hash); reload on an unknown-UUID hash falls back at bootup time; hash with a non-current board id is ignored (the §17.34 `router.onChange` short-circuit when `state.boardId !== boards.getCurrentBoardId()`).
- `src/test/e2e/features/persistence/load_save.feature` — **+3 scenarios**: inline title edit survives reload (proves `EditNodeService` → `persistCurrent` → `LocalStorageBoardCollectionRepository.save` → reload-decode round-trip); drilling state survives reload (URL hash is the source of truth); add-child survives reload (proves `AddChildService` → persist → reload).
- `src/test/e2e/steps/routingSteps.ts` — **new file** (~70 LoC). Two new steps:
  - `When I navigate the kiosk to the literal hash {string}` — drives the bridge's `navigateTo` with an arbitrary literal (e.g. `#/b/test-board/n/does-not-exist`) so the unknown-uuid / wrong-board / malformed-hash branches can be exercised directly.
  - `When I press the browser back button` — `page.goBack()` + an `expect.poll(...)` for the post-`popstate` refresh to land. The kiosk's `router.onChange` runs synchronously inside the `popstate` listener but the resulting Lit render schedules to the next microtask; polling for `focusedId() !== null` is the cheapest sync point that doesn't require a fixed timeout.

**Decisions taken during this commit**:

- **Two redundant-looking "navigate to focus hash" steps coexist on purpose.** `When I focus on node {string}` (in `viewSteps.ts`, used since §17.6) reads as a focus-driven verb; `When I navigate the kiosk to the focus hash for node {string}` (the new alias in `routingSteps.ts`) reads as a URL-driven verb. They drive the exact same code path (`kiosk.focusNode(uuid)` → bridge's `navigateTo` → `location.hash` write → `hashchange` → `router.onChange` → focus + refresh), but the routing scenarios benefit from the more explicit verb because the **subject** of the test is the URL, not the focus. The redundancy is one line of step code and zero production lines; it pays for itself in feature readability.
- **Bridge's `navigateTo` is sufficient — no extra "raw URL paste" backdoor needed.** The bridge already accepts arbitrary hash literals (it just writes to `location.hash` and awaits a `requestAnimationFrame`); `routingSteps.ts` calls it with whatever literal the feature provides. The bridge surface stays at 5 methods (no growth at this commit).
- **`expect.poll()` instead of `waitForFunction`** for the post-back sync. `waitForFunction` requires running JS in the page context, which adds a ~5–20 ms round-trip; `expect.poll` runs the predicate in Node and uses Playwright's built-in retry. For a 1–2-microtask wait the latter is dramatically faster (the assertion almost always passes on the first try).
- **No new fixtures.** All four features re-use the existing `textTree.json` fixture (small, deterministic ids — `TXT-ROOT` / `TXT-A` / `TXT-B`). Adding a routing-specific fixture would have given marginal test isolation in exchange for an extra file to keep in sync with codec changes; the existing fixture's shape is exactly what the routing scenarios need.

**What's testable today** (snapshot at landing):

- `npm test` — **672 unit tests** across **50 files** (~10 s). **No change** vs §17.34 — Phase 10C is a pure e2e closure.
- `npm run lint` (`tsc --noEmit`), `npm run lint:rules` (ESLint layered rules) — clean.
- `npm run build` — clean. **No change** vs §17.34 (no production code added; the new step file is e2e-only and doesn't ship in the bundle).
- `npm run test:e2e` — **104 scenarios** all green (was 91; **+13** across 4 new features: deep_link ×3, focus_to_url ×4, unknown_uuid_fallback ×3, load_save ×3).
- `npm run dev` / `npm run preview` — open the kiosk, drill into a child tile, hit browser-Back: the kiosk re-focuses the parent (`router.onChange` path). Paste `#/b/showcase-board/n/does-not-exist` into the URL bar: the kiosk falls back to the showcase root and rewrites the URL. Reload after any inline edit / add-child / drill / board switch / board create / import / settings change: the post-reload state matches the pre-reload state.

**Phase 10 status**: **complete**. Every `burger-menu-action` is wired (Import / Export §17.33, Boards §17.34, Settings §17.31), the routing pipeline is BDD-covered end-to-end, and the load-save contract is explicit. Next strand is **Phase 11 — manual kiosk smoke** (§16 / §15.7), which is operator-driven (no automation).

---

### 17.36 Polish — shared panel surface for the parent strip and child tiles + drill morph bg/border-radius transition

**Trigger** — operator feedback at the post-Phase-10 demo, two tightly coupled requests:
  1. *"The top panel of parent node should have the same border look than the children."* — pre-§17.36 the `<parent-identity-strip>` carried a single `border-bottom: 1px solid color-mix(currentColor 18%, transparent)` and no background. The children's `.tile[data-slot="node"]` (since §17.17) already carries a full panel aesthetic — `1px` border, `8px` radius, `~7%` currentColor bg. The strip read as a flush band over a tiled card grid: two visual languages.
  2. *"During the animation that promote a child as a parent, the background color of the child should transition smoothly to the background color of the parent node panel."* — once the strip becomes a panel, the drill-into FLIP morph (§17.32) needs to transition the tapped tile's bg into the strip's bg as it flies up. The §17.32 transition list only carried `transform`, `width`, `height`; bg and border-radius would have snapped at commit time, breaking the "smooth promotion" cue the operator asked for.

**Design decisions taken**:

- **Single source of truth via four CSS custom properties on `<tree-graph-screen>` `:host`.** `--panel-tile-bg` (≈ `7%` currentColor — children only), `--panel-strip-bg` (≈ `12%` currentColor — strip only), `--panel-border-color` (`28%` currentColor — both), `--panel-border-radius` (`8px` — both). CSS custom properties cascade through shadow DOM, so `<children-grid>` and `<parent-identity-strip>` (each in its own shadow root) read the same values via `var(--panel-…, color-mix(…))` with the existing `color-mix` literal kept as a fallback for standalone unit-mounted rendering. A future tweak (e.g. tightening the contrast on a high-contrast theme) is a one-place edit in `TreeGraphScreen.ts`.
- **Same border, distinct bg.** "Same border look" means same colour + same width + same radius — and that's exactly what both surfaces resolve to. The bg tints differ on purpose: if both used `7%` the focused panel would visually blend with a single large child tile (when N=1 the child fills almost the whole grid), and the operator's eye would have nothing to anchor "this is the focused parent" against. The `5%` delta (`12% - 7%`) is the smallest visible difference on the kiosk theme and is exactly what the drill morph bridges.
- **Layout breathing room: `padding: 4px; gap: 4px;` on `.layout`.** Without it the strip's rounded corners would touch the viewport edges and the strip's bottom border would meet the children-grid's top edge with no separation, defeating the panel aesthetic. The `4px` matches `TILE_PADDING_PX` (the inter-tile gutter inside the squarified treemap), so the visual rhythm — `4px` outer margin / `4px` between strip and grid / `4px` between tiles — is consistent. Existing geometry-sensitive tests (orientation reflow, tile dimensions, parent-timestamp parity) still pass because they test relative offsets / containment, not absolute pixel positions.
- **Extend the drill morph's transition list, write the destination panel-bg / radius onto the morphing tile.** `runDrillTransition` now registers `background-color`, `border-color`, `border-radius` alongside `transform`, `width`, `height` — and writes `tile.style.backgroundColor = "var(--panel-strip-bg, …)"` (plus the corresponding border-color and border-radius writes) when the morph is staged. The browser resolves the start value from the children-grid's static styles and the end value from the inline `var()`, then interpolates over the full settle window. Border-color is identical on both surfaces today so its transition is a visual no-op, but it stays in the list so a future divergence (e.g. a focused-panel accent border) animates without a second helper edit.
- **Reduced-motion path stays a no-op.** The new bg / border / radius writes sit in the same `if (reduce) { commit(); return; }` short-circuit as the §17.32 transform / opacity writes. The existing reduce-motion unit test gains three new "must-not-write" assertions (`backgroundColor === ""`, `borderColor === ""`, `borderRadius === ""`) so the contract is explicit.
- **e2e parity check pinned in `tile_layout.feature`.** The new scenario *"Parent panel and child tiles share the same panel surface (§17.36)"* reads computed styles from both surfaces (the strip's inner `.strip` element and the first `<children-grid>` `.tile[data-slot="node"]`) and asserts: both have a ≥ 1 px border; both backgrounds are non-transparent (alpha strictly between 0 and 1); border-colour matches exactly; border-radius matches exactly; backgrounds **do not** match (proves the `~7% / ~12%` delta is actually being applied — the value the morph bridges).

**One-line summary** — Parent strip becomes a panel matching the children's aesthetic via four shared `--panel-*` CSS custom properties; drill morph extends its transition list with `background-color` / `border-color` / `border-radius` so the tile-to-strip morph is uniformly smooth across surfaces. **+1 unit test**, **+1 e2e scenario**, **+5 e2e steps**, no Phase advancement.

**Files touched**:

- `src/adapters/ui/shell/TreeGraphScreen.ts` — `:host` gains the four `--panel-*` custom properties; `.layout` gains `padding: 4px; gap: 4px;` for visual breathing room.
- `src/adapters/ui/shell/ParentIdentityStrip.ts` — `.strip`'s `border-bottom: 1px solid color-mix(currentColor 18%, transparent)` divider is replaced by `background: var(--panel-strip-bg, …); border: 1px solid var(--panel-border-color, …); border-radius: var(--panel-border-radius, 8px);` so the strip becomes a panel.
- `src/adapters/ui/shell/ChildrenGrid.ts` — `.tile[data-slot="node"]` switches from inline `color-mix(…)` literals to `var(--panel-tile-bg, color-mix(…))` / `var(--panel-border-color, color-mix(…))` / `var(--panel-border-radius, 8px)`, with the literals retained as fallbacks for unit tests that mount the grid outside `<tree-graph-screen>`.
- `src/adapters/ui/animations/drillTransitions.ts` — `tile.style.transition` adds `background-color ${settleMs}ms ease, border-color ${settleMs}ms ease, border-radius ${settleMs}ms ease`; `tile.style.willChange` extends to cover the same three; the morph stages `tile.style.backgroundColor = "var(--panel-strip-bg, color-mix(in srgb, currentColor 12%, transparent))"` (plus corresponding border-color / border-radius writes) so the registered transitions resolve to the strip's destination panel surface. Module docstring grows a new "§17.36 — panel surface continuity" section.
- `src/test/unit/adapters/ui/animations/drillTransitions.test.ts` — **+1 new test** *"transitions the tile's panel surface (background-color, border-color, border-radius) to the parent strip's panel vars (§17.36)"* (asserts the new properties are in `transition` + `willChange` and that the destination values reference the panel CSS vars). Reduced-motion test gains three new "must-not-write" assertions (`backgroundColor`, `borderColor`, `borderRadius` all `""` on the no-anim path).
- `src/test/e2e/features/views/tile_layout.feature` — **+1 new scenario** pinning the panel-surface parity contract (border colour and radius match strip↔tile; background tints differ; both have visible borders + non-transparent backgrounds).
- `src/test/e2e/steps/viewSteps.ts` — **+2 panel-style readers** (`readStripPanelStyle`, `readFirstChildTilePanelStyle`) that pierce both shadow boundaries (`<tree-graph-screen>` → `<parent-identity-strip>` → `.strip`; `<tree-graph-screen>` → `<children-grid>` → `.tile`) and return resolved `borderColor` / `borderWidth` / `borderRadius` / `backgroundColor`. **+5 new step definitions** corresponding to the new scenario's `Then` clauses. The `non-transparent background` step accepts both modern Chromium's `color(srgb r g b / a)` serialisation of `color-mix(…)` and the legacy `rgba(r, g, b, a)` shape.
- `docs/SPEC.md` — §17.36 (this section) + status table footnote update; the "Phase 10 status: complete" header above stays.

**Decisions taken during this commit**:

- **The literal mix percentages (`7%`, `12%`, `28%`) live ONLY in `TreeGraphScreen.ts`.** Every consumer (`ChildrenGrid`, `ParentIdentityStrip`, `runDrillTransition`) references the property names. This means a theme tweak (e.g. moving to `8% / 14%` for a higher-contrast variant) is a one-place edit and the morph automatically picks up the new endpoints.
- **`color-mix(…)` fallbacks stay in the consumer files.** `<children-grid>` is unit-tested standalone (without a `<tree-graph-screen>` ancestor); the fallback ensures the tile still renders with a visible border + tint in that context. Same logic for the strip. Removing the fallbacks would have shrunk the bundle by ~80 bytes but broken the existing unit fixtures.
- **`willChange` covers the new properties too.** Without that hint Chromium would not hoist `background-color` / `border-color` / `border-radius` onto the compositor's own layer; on a kiosk-class machine they'd still render fine, but the willChange addition makes the morph indistinguishable from the §17.32 baseline performance-wise.
- **e2e scenario asserts strict inequality on backgrounds.** `expect(stripBg).not.toBe(tileBg)` would silently pass if a future refactor accidentally collapsed both to the same property (e.g. `--panel-bg` shared by both surfaces) — and the morph would then have no visible bg delta to bridge, breaking the operator's stated requirement. Pinning the inequality at the e2e layer catches that regression at CI time.
- **Live demo verified.** The dev server hot-reloaded all four edits (`TreeGraphScreen.ts`, `ParentIdentityStrip.ts`, `ChildrenGrid.ts`, `drillTransitions.ts`) with no errors. The kiosk now renders a rounded, bordered, slightly-tinted parent panel above a rounded, bordered, lightly-tinted children grid, with `4px` of visual breathing room between them and around the layout. Drilling into a child tile shows the tile's bg drift from the lighter tint to the strip's tint as it flies up — exactly what the operator described.

**What's testable today** (snapshot at landing):

- `npm test` — **673 unit tests** across **50 files** (+1 vs §17.35's 672 — the new drill panel-surface contract).
- `npm run lint` (`tsc --noEmit`), `npm run lint:rules` (ESLint) — clean.
- `npm run build` — clean. Bundle: **182.05 KB / 47.41 KB gzip** (up from `178.02 KB / 46.17 KB` at §17.35; +4 KB raw / +1.2 KB gzip — the new CSS-var declarations + drill helper changes + commentary).
- `npm run test:e2e` — **105 scenarios** all green (+1 vs §17.35's 104 — the new `tile_layout` panel-surface scenario).

---

### 17.37 Polish — parent-strip ↔ child-tile title alignment + inline title-edit fits the title row

**Trigger** — operator feedback at the post-§17.36 demo, two adjacent papercuts that both follow from the §17.36 panel polish:
  1. *"There's a discrepancy between the top panel title position and the title position of a zoomed promoted-to-parent child node."* — pre-§17.37 the `<parent-identity-strip>` `.strip` carried `padding: clamp(0.5rem, 1.5vw, 1.25rem)` (a hold-over from when the strip was a divider, not a panel — the padding gave the per-view some inner breathing room). On a `1280px` viewport that resolves to `19.2px` of extra inset on every side. A child tile's `.tile[data-slot="node"]` has **no** outer padding; only the inner per-view's `:host { padding: 0.4rem 0.6rem }` (from `tileLayoutStyles`) provides the `6.4px / 9.6px` inset. So a child title sat at `1px (border) + 0 + 0.4rem ≈ 7.4px` from the tile's outer top; a parent title at `1px + 19.2px + 0.4rem ≈ 26.6px` from the strip's outer top — a ~`19px` jump. The §17.32 drill-into FLIP morph made the discrepancy **visible**: the morphing tile lerped its position into the strip's geometry, but at commit Lit re-rendered the strip with the per-view at the strip-padded inset, so the title visibly **jumped down-and-right** the moment the morph settled. Under §17.36 (now both surfaces are panels with matching border + radius), the alignment-shift was the last visible artifact.
  2. *"A title input should be a one-lined, with size that should fit totally in the viewport."* — the §17.28 inline title-edit (`<input type="text" class="title-edit">`) inherited the `.value-edit` declaration's `padding: 0.25rem 0.4rem` because the two share a base rule. Combined with the parent-role's `font-size: 2.4vh` (≈ `17.3px` on `720px` height) and the `1px` border, the input's outer height resolved to ~`27px` on `720p` — but the `.title { height: 3vh }` row is only `21.6px` tall, so the input visibly **bulged ~5–6px below the title row** every time the operator clicked the title to edit. The `<input type="text">` element is single-line by HTML spec; the operator's "one-lined" was about the **rendered** behaviour, not the element type. Fitting the input exactly inside the title row (and constraining its width to never exceed the strip's content area) is the polish.

**Design decisions taken**:

- **Drop the strip's outer padding entirely.** `<parent-identity-strip>` `.strip` becomes `padding: 0` (was `clamp(0.5rem, 1.5vw, 1.25rem)`). The inner per-view's `:host { padding: 0.4rem 0.6rem }` (from `tileLayoutStyles`) is now the **only** padding in play on either surface, so the title (and every inner element — description, value-area, timestamp) lands at exactly the same `1px (border) + 0.4rem top` / `1px + 0.6rem left` inset on the strip and on every child tile. The drill morph commits without any visible jump because the per-view's own geometry is identical pre-and-post-commit.
- **Right-side gutter for the close-X / pencil moves to a class modifier.** The buttons reserve space via `.strip.has-close, .strip.has-edit { padding-right: clamp(3rem, 4vw, 3.75rem) }` and `.strip.has-close.has-edit { padding-right: clamp(5.5rem, 8vw, 7.5rem) }`. `padding-right` is *additive* on top of the symmetric `padding: 0` default, so the gutter exists only when the buttons are rendered and only on the right side — the title's left-edge alignment with the child is preserved. Button positions (`top: clamp(0.4rem, 1vw, 0.75rem); right: clamp(0.4rem, 1vw, 0.75rem);`) are measured from the strip's *padding edge*, which CSS resolves at the *border-inner* edge regardless of `padding` value, so the buttons themselves do not move at all post-§17.37 — only the per-view's content moves up-and-left into the freed outer padding.
- **`.title-edit` fills the title row exactly.** Both `*AsParent` per-views (`TextNodeAsParent` / `BusinessScoreCardNodeAsParent`) gain a tighter rule for the inline title input: `height: 100%` (= the parent `.title h1`'s `3vh` row), `padding: 0 0.4rem` (horizontal only — no vertical padding so the input height matches the row height), `line-height: 1` (so the `2.4vh` font-size fits inside the `3vh` content area minus `2px` border), `min-width: 0` + `max-width: 100%` (defeat the `<input>`'s intrinsic `size=20` min-content so a narrow title row still constrains the input to the row width and the input never extends past the strip — and therefore never past the viewport). The static title's geometry is unchanged; only the editor variant is constrained.
- **Modal title inputs are already viewport-bound — verify, don't over-engineer.** Every modal panel (`<add-child-modal>`, `<edit-node-modal>`, `<board-settings-modal>`, `<boards-panel-modal>`) uses `<input type="text">` for its title-equivalent field, with `width: 100%; box-sizing: border-box` inside a panel capped at `min-width: min(24–32rem, calc(100vw - 4rem))` and `max-width: calc(100vw - 4rem)` (§17.29). The input therefore fills the field column and the panel cannot exceed `viewport - 4rem`, so the modal title inputs are already single-line and viewport-fitting by construction; §17.37 leaves them alone and records the contract here so a future refactor doesn't accidentally lift the `viewport - 4rem` cap.
- **e2e parity check pinned in `tile_layout.feature`.** The new scenario *"Parent panel title sits at the same offset from its outer edge as a child tile title (§17.37)"* is a direct mirror of the §17.30 timestamp-parity check: read the `<parent-identity-strip>` host bbox + the parent title bbox + the `[data-id="ChildB"]` tile bbox + the child title bbox; assert `|parentTopOffset - childTopOffset| ≤ 4px` AND `|parentLeftOffset - childLeftOffset| ≤ 4px`. The `4px` tolerance absorbs sub-pixel rounding + the `1px` border. Same `mixedComputed` fixture as §17.30 so both parities exercise the same kiosk state in adjacent scenarios.
- **Pre-existing flake on the very first `tile_layout` scenario fixed in passing.** *"Tile titles share a viewport-relative height across all tiles"* called `evaluateAll` on a child-grid title selector with no auto-wait — the kiosk's first render after `seed + reload` could race the read and return zero matches. Two steps now `await expect(kiosk.childTiles().first()).toBeVisible()` before the `evaluateAll`, piggy-backing on Playwright's built-in retry. The flake reproduced on `b3b46db` (immediately before §17.37), so it predates this work; harmlessly fixed alongside the new title-parity scenario.

**One-line summary** — Parent strip drops its outer padding so its title aligns with the child tile's title (vertical + horizontal); the inline title-edit input shrinks to fit the `3vh` title row exactly; the post-§17.36 visual language is now operator-shippable. **0 net unit tests**, **+1 e2e scenario**, **+1 e2e step**, no Phase advancement.

**Files touched**:

- `src/adapters/ui/shell/ParentIdentityStrip.ts` — `.strip { padding: 0 }` (was `clamp(0.5rem, 1.5vw, 1.25rem)`); `.strip.has-close / .has-edit / .has-close.has-edit { padding-right: … }` modifiers preserved (additive right-side gutter for the buttons). Long inline comment captures the **why** for future readers — the per-view's own host padding is the only padding in play, the strip is now a pure visual frame.
- `src/adapters/ui/views/TextNode/TextNodeAsParent.ts` — new `.title-edit { height: 100%; padding: 0 0.4rem; line-height: 1; min-width: 0; max-width: 100% }` rule (overrides the `padding: 0.25rem 0.4rem` inherited from the shared `.title-edit, .value-edit` base block). Long comment pins the contract: input fills the title row exactly, no vertical overflow, never exceeds the strip's content area.
- `src/adapters/ui/views/BusinessScoreCardNode/BusinessScoreCardNodeAsParent.ts` — same `.title-edit` rule as TextNodeAsParent; same comment. Two-place edit (vs a shared style file) is intentional because the surrounding `.value-edit` rules differ between the two views (TextNode uses a `<textarea>`; BSC uses a `<input type="number">`) and pulling only the title-edit out into a shared module would split a tightly-coupled five-rule block awkwardly.
- `src/test/e2e/features/views/tile_layout.feature` — **+1 scenario** *"Parent panel title sits at the same offset from its outer edge as a child tile title (§17.37)"*. Same setup as the §17.30 timestamp-parity scenario above it (open kiosk → seed `mixedComputed` → reload → assert).
- `src/test/e2e/steps/viewSteps.ts` — **+1 step** *"the focused title offset matches a child tile title offset within {int} px"* (parses the offsets, asserts both top and left within tolerance). The two existing flaky steps (*"every tile title has the same font-size"* and *"every tile title's font-size is approximately 2vh"*) now `await expect(kiosk.childTiles().first()).toBeVisible()` before reading computed styles.
- `docs/SPEC.md` — §17.37 (this section) + status table footnote update; Resume protocol counts bumped accordingly.

**Decisions taken during this commit**:

- **`padding: 0` over `padding-top: 0; padding-left: 0`.** Setting all four sides keeps the rule self-documenting — the strip is a pure visual frame. The right-side gutter is then added back via the existing class modifiers, which is the only place where the strip's content area shrinks (and it shrinks for a specific layout reason: keeping the title from running into the buttons). Keeping the modifiers as `padding-right` overrides instead of full `padding` shorthand prevents a later refactor from accidentally re-introducing the symmetric padding.
- **Why `height: 100%` on the inline input instead of an explicit `3vh`.** `height: 100%` resolves against the parent `.title h1`'s computed height, which is whatever `tileLayoutStyles` declares (currently `3vh`). If §17.x ever bumps the title row to `3.5vh` for a high-contrast theme, the inline editor follows for free. Hard-coding `3vh` here would silently drift out of sync.
- **Why not bump the modal title inputs too.** Operator's BOTH-question answer *was* "both", but the modal's title inputs are already `<input type="text">` (one-lined by HTML), already `width: 100%; box-sizing: border-box` inside a panel capped to `viewport - 4rem` (§17.29). They cannot exceed the viewport. Adding a redundant `max-width: 100%` would be visual noise; recording the contract in this section is the durable answer.
- **Live demo verified.** Dev server hot-reloaded all three CSS edits with no errors. Drilled `Root → ChildA` on the `mixedComputed` fixture: the tapped tile's title flies up and lands exactly where the strip's title now renders — no visible jump. Clicked the focused title to enter inline-edit: the input occupies exactly the `3vh` title row with no vertical overflow into the description / value area.

**What's testable today** (snapshot at landing):

- `npm test` — **673 unit tests** across **50 files**. **No change** vs §17.36 — §17.37 is a CSS-only polish (the inline-edit-fit and the strip-padding change have no logical branches that warrant a new unit test; the e2e parity check is the durable pin).
- `npm run lint` (`tsc --noEmit`), `npm run lint:rules` (ESLint layered rules) — clean.
- `npm run build` — clean. Bundle: **185.49 KB / 48.22 KB gzip** (up from `182.05 KB / 47.41 KB` at §17.36; +3 KB raw / +0.8 KB gzip — almost entirely the new long-form §17.37 inline comments in the three Lit files; the actual CSS rule changes are < 200 bytes).
- `npm run test:e2e` — **106 scenarios** all green (+1 vs §17.36's 105 — the new `tile_layout` title-parity scenario). The pre-existing first-scenario flake is fixed in passing.

---

### 17.38 Polish — drill morph also bridges the parent-role title font-size (no commit-time size pop)

**Trigger** — operator feedback at the post-§17.37 demo:
  *"There's a visual jump of the title when zoomed. The top-left position seems good but the size of the font itself seems to increase abruptly after end of animation."*

  Pre-§17.38 the §17.32 FLIP morph translated + grew the tapped tile's bounding box, transitioned its `background-color` / `border-color` / `border-radius` (§17.36), and recoloured **only** its title via the `--drill-title-color` custom property (§17.32) — but the title's **font-size** was tied to the viewport (`font-size: 2vh` on the child role), not to the tile's geometry, so it stayed at the child's `2vh` for the full settle and then **snapped to `2.4vh`** the moment Lit re-rendered the strip with the morphed tile's per-view in the parent role. On a `720 px` viewport that's a `14.4 px → 17.28 px` jump (≈ `20 %` size delta) plus a `font-weight: 600 → 700` step, both fired on the same frame. With §17.37 fixing the simultaneous top-left jump (the title's outer-edge offset is now identical pre-and-post-commit), the size-pop became the **only remaining commit-artifact** of the drill morph — and now visible on its own, hence the operator's report.

**Design decisions taken**:

- **Pipe the parent-role title font-size through a custom property the child-role title reads.** `tileLayoutStyles.ts`' base `.title` rule changes `font-size: 2vh` to `font-size: var(--drill-title-font-size, 2vh)`. The fallback (`2vh`) preserves the static-render behaviour for every non-drilling tile — the var is unset outside the morph, so the cascade resolves to the literal child-role size. The parent-role's `*AsParent.ts` `.title { font-size: 2.4vh }` override wins by source-order at equal specificity (Lit concatenates the per-view's `[tileLayoutStyles, css\`…override…\`]` array; later rules override earlier at equal specificity), so the **resting** parent-role title still resolves to the literal `2.4vh` regardless of the var — the var only affects the morphing tile during the FLIP.
- **Add `font-size` to the `.title` `transition` list.** The `.title` rule already had `transition: color 320ms ease` for the §17.32 colour recolour; it now reads `transition: color 320ms ease, font-size 320ms ease`. Same `320 ms` settle, same `ease` curve so the size growth and the colour recolour land on the same optical beat. Outside the morph the transition is dormant (font-size doesn't change on a resting tile); the only side-effect is that a viewport `vh` change (e.g. operator resizing the kiosk window during a debug session) animates over `320 ms` instead of snapping — an upgrade, not a regression, on a kiosk that doesn't normally resize.
- **Helper writes the parent-role literal onto the morphing tile via `--drill-title-font-size`.** `runDrillTransition` gains one line next to the existing `--drill-title-color` write: `tile.style.setProperty("--drill-title-font-size", DRILL_PARENT_TITLE_FONT_SIZE)`. The cascade carries the var two shadow boundaries deep (`children-grid → node-view → text-node-as-child / bsc-as-child`), the `.title` rule reads it, the registered `font-size` transition resolves the start (= the static-render fallback `2vh`) and end (= `2.4vh`) values, and the title smoothly grows over the settle. At commit Lit unmounts the morphing tile and re-mounts the strip with the parent role's per-view at the literal `2.4vh` — same value as the morphing tile's resolved end-state, so the visual continues without a pop.
- **The parent-role literal is mirrored in an exported constant, not duplicated as a string.** A new `DRILL_PARENT_TITLE_FONT_SIZE = "2.4vh"` is exported from `drillTransitions.ts` alongside `DRILL_SETTLE_MS` and `DRILL_CLASS`. The unit test asserts BOTH that the helper writes the constant onto the tile AND that the constant equals `"2.4vh"` (the literal in `*AsParent.ts`), so a future theme tweak that updates one site without the other fails fast at test-time. A follow-up could promote the value to a CSS custom property on `<tree-graph-screen>` :host (single source of truth read by both `*AsParent.ts` and the helper), but the two-place edit with the lock-step test is acceptable for the operator-shippable cut.
- **Title font-size morph applies ONLY to the title, not to the tile's other text nodes.** The helper does not write `tile.style.fontSize`; that would cascade to every text node in the tile (value / timestamp / unit) and break their independent size rules (the value's `cqmin` clamp, the timestamp's `1vh`, the unit's `0.85vh`). Same pattern as the §17.32 colour recolour: a custom property the title rule alone reads, leaving every sibling at its own size by construction.
- **Reduced-motion path stays untouched.** On `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce` (or the `test-no-anim` testBridge sentinel), the helper short-circuits to `commit()` BEFORE setting any inline style — `--drill-title-font-size` is NOT written, the `.title` rule resolves to its `2vh` fallback during the (instantaneous) commit, and the post-commit re-render hands off to the parent role's literal in a single frame. The user opted out of motion; the morph stages no transition target on the tile, including the new font-size one. Pinned in the existing reduced-motion unit test (gains one new "must-not-write" assertion alongside the §17.32 / §17.36 ones).
- **No e2e check pinned.** The deterministic contract is fully captured by the unit test: the helper writes the parent-role font-size onto the morphing tile when the morph stages, doesn't write it when reduced-motion short-circuits, and the literal value mirrors the `*AsParent.ts` rule. The "no visible pop" property is fundamentally a same-value-at-handoff argument that the unit test pins; an e2e check trying to sample mid-morph frames would be flaky for no extra coverage. The static-state title font-sizes (parent `2.4vh`, child `2vh`) are not yet pinned in e2e at all — a follow-up could add a single scenario asserting both, but that's a separate "static-style invariants" concern from the §17.38 morph fix.

**One-line summary** — Drill morph now bridges the title's `2 vh → 2.4 vh` font-size delta on the same `320 ms ease` curve as the colour recolour, eliminating the post-commit size pop. **+1 unit test**, **0 net e2e scenarios**, no Phase advancement.

**Files touched**:

- `src/adapters/ui/views/tileLayoutStyles.ts` — `.title { font-size: var(--drill-title-font-size, 2vh) }` (was the literal `2vh`); `.title { transition: color 320ms ease, font-size 320ms ease }` (was just `color`). Long inline comments capture the **why** for future readers — the var is the morph's hand-off mechanism, the fallback is the static-render value, the parent-role override wins by source-order at equal specificity.
- `src/adapters/ui/animations/drillTransitions.ts` — new exported constant `DRILL_PARENT_TITLE_FONT_SIZE = "2.4vh"` (mirrors the `*AsParent.ts` literal); new `tile.style.setProperty("--drill-title-font-size", DRILL_PARENT_TITLE_FONT_SIZE)` next to the existing `--drill-title-color` write. Module docstring grows a new "§17.38 — title font-size morph" section.
- `src/test/unit/adapters/ui/animations/drillTransitions.test.ts` — **+1 new test** *"morphs ONLY the title's font-size via the --drill-title-font-size custom property (§17.38 — no commit-time size pop)"* (asserts the helper writes the constant, the constant equals `"2.4vh"`, and `tile.style.fontSize` is NOT set so the rest of the tile keeps its own sizes). Reduced-motion test gains one new "must-not-write" assertion (`--drill-title-font-size` is `""` on the no-anim path).
- `docs/SPEC.md` — §17.38 (this section) + status table footnote update; Resume protocol counts bumped accordingly.

**Decisions taken during this commit**:

- **Why a custom property and not a `transform: scale()` counter-rotation on the title.** The §17.32 morph deliberately uses `width / height` transitions instead of `transform: scale()` (the operator's "content must NOT deform" requirement); reintroducing a per-element `scale()` on the title alone — even an inverse one to compensate for an outer scale — would be both wrong (there is no outer scale to compensate) and structurally fragile (would require knowing the tile's current width/height every frame). The cascade-of-a-custom-property pattern is the same shape as the §17.32 colour fix; it's already proven to work two shadow boundaries deep, and it composes cleanly with the existing `transition` list.
- **Why `font-size 320ms ease` instead of `font-size 320ms ease-in-out` or a custom curve.** The colour recolour and the panel-surface drift (§17.36) both use plain `ease`; matching that on the size growth keeps the optical beat unified. A second curve would risk one part of the morph leading or trailing the others, which would itself read as a jump — a different one than the pre-§17.38 size pop, but still a jump.
- **Why mirror the literal in a constant instead of using a CSS custom property at `<tree-graph-screen>` :host level.** Single-source-of-truth via `:host { --drill-title-font-size-target: 2.4vh }` would be a cleaner architecture, but the `*AsParent.ts` `.title { font-size: 2.4vh }` override and the helper's write are physically a two-place edit today (in two different files, both shipped under the same commit). Adding a third file (the `:host` declaration) that both read from is a refactor, not a polish. The exported constant + the lock-step unit test gives 95 % of the safety with zero structural change. The follow-up note is recorded in this section so a future architecturally-driven cleanup pass picks it up.
- **Why not also smooth `font-weight: 600 → 700`.** Variable fonts support smooth `font-weight` transitions, but the kiosk's font stack is the OS-default sans-serif (no `@font-face` declared), and most regular fonts step at integer hundreds. The browser would either snap the weight instantly mid-morph (a smaller jump than the pre-fix size pop, but still a jump) or render at an interpolated synthesized weight (browser-dependent, often visually identical to one of the integer steps). Both outcomes are worse than the current "size grows smoothly, weight steps once at commit" — at the optical beat where the size growth lands, a sub-frame weight step is essentially invisible behind the size animation. Recording the call here so a future "load a variable font" decision picks up the loose end.
- **Live demo verified.** Dev server hot-reloaded both edits with no errors. Drilled `Root → ChildA` on the `mixedComputed` fixture: the tapped tile's title now grows smoothly from the child's `2 vh` to the parent's `2.4 vh` over the `320 ms` settle, lands at the same size + position the strip's parent-role title renders at, no visible jump in either dimension. Tested with `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce` via Chrome DevTools' rendering panel: the navigation commits instantly with no morph staging, exactly as before.

**What's testable today** (snapshot at landing):

- `npm test` — **674 unit tests** across **50 files** (was **673** at §17.37; +1 — the new font-size morph test).
- `npm run lint` (`tsc --noEmit`), `npm run lint:rules` (ESLint layered rules) — clean.
- `npm run build` — clean. Bundle: **186.51 KB / 48.55 KB gzip** (up from `185.49 KB / 48.22 KB` at §17.37; +1.0 KB raw / +0.33 KB gzip — almost entirely the new long-form §17.38 inline comments in `tileLayoutStyles.ts` + `drillTransitions.ts`; the actual logical change is one custom property, one transition entry, and one inline write).
- `npm run test:e2e` — **106 scenarios** all green (no change vs §17.37 — §17.38 is a unit-tested invariant; no e2e regression staged).

### 17.39 Polish — drill morph also bridges the title font-weight + parent BSC value re-centers to the strip's full width

**Trigger** — operator feedback at the post-§17.38 demo:
  *"There's still a jump in the end, probably because of the weight of the font. Transition the weight of the font more smoothly. Also, the centered value looks jumping to the left in the end, fix it so that it's still centered to the tile in the end when it's a parent node."*

  Two distinct artifacts surfaced together once §17.38 fixed the title font-size pop:

  1. **Font-weight pop on the title.** The child role's `.title` is `font-weight: 600`, the parent role overrides to `700` (baked into both `*AsParent.ts` overrides). §17.38's writeup deliberately *skipped* weight smoothing on the rationale "browser font-weight transitions are inconsistent across non-variable fonts" — recording the call as a loose end. The operator's feedback "still a jump in the end, probably because of the weight" confirms even the weight step is visually distinct from the (now-smooth) size growth.
  2. **Centered BSC value snaps left at commit.** When the focused node is a `BusinessScoreCardNode` with a `recordedValue` (e.g. `ChildB` in `mixedComputed`), the `.value` span is centered horizontally by the shared `.value-area { display: flex; justify-content: center }`. Pre-§17.39, after the drill morph commits, the centered value sits visibly left of where it sat at the morph's end-state. On a `1280 px` viewport the offset is ~`60 px` — half the strip's `padding-right` gutter (`clamp(5.5rem, 8vw, 7.5rem) ≈ 120 px` when both close-X and pencil are rendered, §17.37).

**Design decisions taken**:

- **Font-weight: same plumbing as §17.38's font-size morph.** `tileLayoutStyles.ts`' base `.title` rule changes `font-weight: 600` to `font-weight: var(--drill-title-font-weight, 600)`. The fallback (`600`) preserves the static-render behaviour; the parent-role override `font-weight: 700` literal in `*AsParent.ts` still wins by source-order at equal specificity for the resting parent strip. The transition list grows from `color 320 ms ease, font-size 320 ms ease` to `color 320 ms ease, font-size 320 ms ease, font-weight 320 ms ease` — same `ease` curve so all three properties land on the same optical beat. The helper `runDrillTransition` writes `tile.style.setProperty("--drill-title-font-weight", DRILL_PARENT_TITLE_FONT_WEIGHT)` next to the existing `--drill-title-font-size` write; the new exported constant `DRILL_PARENT_TITLE_FONT_WEIGHT = "700"` mirrors the `*AsParent.ts` literal with a lock-step unit test (asserts both that the helper writes the constant AND that the constant equals `"700"` so a future theme tweak that updates one site without the other fails fast at test-time).

- **Why the §17.38 doc-decision is reversed.** §17.38 noted "regular fonts step at integer hundreds, browser would either snap mid-morph or render at an interpolated synthesized weight". Two facts make that worry less load-bearing than it looked: (a) the kiosk's font stack starts with `system-ui`, which on every modern desktop / mobile OS resolves to a **variable** system font — Segoe UI Variable on Windows 10+, SF Pro on macOS / iOS, Roboto on Android — and Chromium interpolates the `wght` axis smoothly; (b) even the worst-case non-variable fallback (Linux-without-fontconfig-roboto) steps at the `50 %`-progress midpoint, which is still better than the post-commit step at `100 %` that was the pre-§17.39 behaviour. The footnote in §17.38 explicitly listed "load a variable font" as a follow-up; modern OSes have already done that for us via `system-ui`.

- **BSC value recentering: a negative `margin-right` that cancels the strip's `padding-right`.** The parent-strip's `.strip` wrapper carries `padding-right: clamp(5.5rem, 8vw, 7.5rem)` (when both close-X and pencil are rendered) so the title and description don't run into the absolutely-positioned buttons in the top-right gutter. The per-view's content area inherits the shrunken width; the `.value-area` flex centers its child within that shrunken box. The morphing tile, by contrast, is positioned in the children-grid's light DOM (with a `transform: translate(...)` that lands its bounding rect on the strip's outer rect), and so does NOT inherit the strip's padding — the centered value sits at the strip's full-width center while in flight. At commit Lit re-mounts the strip with the per-view in the parent role, the `padding-right` re-asserts itself, and the centered value snaps left. The fix lifts the gutter literal into a CSS custom property `--strip-gutter-right` (set on `.strip` alongside the existing `padding-right` rule, mirroring its clamp value), and the BSC parent's `.value-area` reads it via `margin-right: calc(0px - var(--strip-gutter-right, 0px))`. The negative margin extends the value-area back to the strip's full inner width; the centered `.value` lands at the strip's full-width horizontal center — same as the morph end-state. The fallback `0px` makes the rule a no-op when the strip carries no buttons (root focus or `vm: null`).

- **TextNode parent role does NOT need this fix.** The `.md-body` is `text-align: left`, so the rendered text always sits at the value-area's left padding-edge — extending the value-area's right edge via a negative margin would just leave more empty space on the right, not shift the visible text. The pre-§17.39 TextNode parent rendering is already jump-free for the value, and is left untouched. (If a future theme decision re-centers TextNode markdown on the parent role — e.g. for a "single-paragraph note" layout — the same `--strip-gutter-right` cascade would let it follow the same recipe; the var is published from the strip regardless of the per-view that consumes it, so adding a second consumer is a one-line per-view edit.)

- **Title and description stay constrained to the content area.** The fix is scoped to the `.value-area` rule on `BusinessScoreCardNodeAsParent`. The title and description rules (which sit in the strip's top zone, sharing Y-coordinates with the absolute-positioned buttons) still inherit the per-view's content width = `strip-width - padding-right`, so they continue to keep clear of the buttons. The buttons are `2.25 rem` tall (≈ `36 px`) anchored at the strip's top; the value-area sits below them in BSC's `display: flex; flex-direction: column` layout (title → description → value-area), so the value-area extending into the gutter zone does NOT visually overlap with the buttons.

- **Why publish `--strip-gutter-right` from the strip rather than hard-coding the clamp expression in the per-view.** Single source of truth for the gutter literal: the strip declares it once (alongside the matching `padding-right`), and any per-view inside the strip can consume it. The per-view doesn't need to know the strip's button-presence rules (one button vs two vs none — all three modifier branches set the var to the right value automatically). A future strip-side change to the gutter (e.g. wider on a 4K display) propagates to every consumer without a per-view edit.

- **e2e parity check pinned in `tile_layout.feature`.** The new scenario *"Parent BSC value is horizontally centered to the strip's full width (§17.39)"* focuses on `ChildB` (recordedValue BSC, always present in `mixedComputed`), reads the bounding boxes of the strip and the value, and asserts `|valueCenterX - stripCenterX| ≤ 2 px`. Tolerance `2 px` absorbs sub-pixel rounding from the negative-margin / clamp arithmetic without admitting a `60 px`-class regression (the pre-fix offset was approximately half the gutter, ~`60 px` on `1280 px`).

- **Reduced-motion path stays untouched.** On `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce` (or the `test-no-anim` testBridge sentinel) the helper short-circuits to `commit()` BEFORE setting any inline style — the new `--drill-title-font-weight` is NOT written, `.title { font-weight: var(--drill-title-font-weight, 600) }` resolves to the fallback, and the post-commit re-render hands off to the parent role's `font-weight: 700` literal in a single frame. Pinned in the existing reduced-motion unit test (gains one new "must-not-write" assertion).

**One-line summary** — Drill morph now bridges the title's `600 → 700` font-weight on the same `320 ms ease` curve as the size + colour, eliminating the post-commit weight pop; BSC parent's `.value-area` "escapes" the strip's right-side gutter via a negative margin so the centered value lands at the strip's full-width center instead of snapping left at commit. **+1 unit test** (font-weight morph), **+1 e2e scenario** (BSC value horizontal centering), no Phase advancement.

**Files touched**:

- `src/adapters/ui/views/tileLayoutStyles.ts` — `.title { font-weight: var(--drill-title-font-weight, 600) }` (was the literal `600`); transition list adds `font-weight 320ms ease`. Long inline comment captures the **why** for future readers.
- `src/adapters/ui/animations/drillTransitions.ts` — new exported constant `DRILL_PARENT_TITLE_FONT_WEIGHT = "700"`; new `tile.style.setProperty("--drill-title-font-weight", DRILL_PARENT_TITLE_FONT_WEIGHT)` next to the existing `--drill-title-font-size` write. Module docstring grows a new "§17.39 — title font-weight morph + BSC value re-centering" section.
- `src/adapters/ui/shell/ParentIdentityStrip.ts` — the existing `.strip.has-close, .strip.has-edit` and `.strip.has-close.has-edit` selectors gain a matching `--strip-gutter-right` declaration alongside their `padding-right` clamp value (single source of truth for the gutter literal). Comment block above the rules documents the var's purpose and consumers.
- `src/adapters/ui/views/BusinessScoreCardNode/BusinessScoreCardNodeAsParent.ts` — `.value-area` rule gains `margin-right: calc(0px - var(--strip-gutter-right, 0px))` so the centered `.value` span lands at the strip's full-width center. Long inline comment pins the contract: this rule is what cancels the strip's `padding-right` for the value-area only; title and description stay constrained.
- `src/test/unit/adapters/ui/animations/drillTransitions.test.ts` — **+1 new test** *"morphs ONLY the title's font-weight via the --drill-title-font-weight custom property (§17.39 — no commit-time weight pop)"*. Reduced-motion test gains one new "must-not-write" assertion (`--drill-title-font-weight` is `""` on the no-anim path).
- `src/test/e2e/features/views/tile_layout.feature` — **+1 scenario** *"Parent BSC value is horizontally centered to the strip's full width (§17.39)"*.
- `src/test/e2e/steps/viewSteps.ts` — **+1 step** *"the focused BSC value is horizontally centered to the parent strip within {int} px"* (parses the tolerance, asserts value-center-x within tolerance of strip-center-x).
- `docs/SPEC.md` — §17.39 (this section) + status table footnote update; Resume protocol counts bumped accordingly.

**Decisions taken during this commit**:

- **Why a negative `margin-right` instead of resizing the per-view's host.** A `width: calc(100% + var(--strip-gutter-right, 0px))` on the `:host` would have the same visual effect, but it would also widen the title and description (which we explicitly want kept constrained to the content area to avoid the absolute-positioned buttons). Scoping the escape to the `.value-area` is the surgical option.
- **Why the var is set on `.strip` (not on `:host` of `<parent-identity-strip>`).** The `:host` is the custom element's outer boundary; `.strip` is the inner wrapper that carries the `padding-right`. Co-locating the var with the padding-right rule keeps the two values mechanically inseparable — a future change to the padding-right will naturally update the var (and break loudly via the e2e scenario if forgotten) rather than silently drifting out of sync if they lived on different selectors.
- **Why write font-weight as a string `"700"` and not as a number `700`.** Two reasons: (a) symmetry with the existing `DRILL_PARENT_TITLE_FONT_SIZE = "2.4vh"` constant, which is unavoidably a string; (b) `tile.style.setProperty(name, value)` takes a string for the value, so `"700"` is the natural encoding. The CSS `font-weight: 700` and `font-weight: "700"` resolve identically.
- **Why a 2 px tolerance on the e2e centering check (vs the 4 px tolerance the §17.30 / §17.37 offsets use).** The §17.30 / §17.37 checks measure offsets from a tile's corner to a child element's corner — both ends of the offset are subject to sub-pixel rounding (a 1 px border + 0.4 rem padding chain), so a 4 px tolerance comfortably absorbs the worst-case rounding. The §17.39 check measures a single CENTER-TO-CENTER delta after a clamp(...) expression resolves and a negative margin cancels it — only one rounding step on either side. 2 px is a tight enough budget to fail loudly on a 60 px-class regression while still absorbing fractional clamp arithmetic.
- **Live demo verified.** Dev server hot-reloaded all four edits with no errors. Drilled `Root → ChildA` (recordedValue BSC) on the `mixedComputed` fixture: the title now grows smoothly from `600` to `700` weight on Windows Segoe UI Variable (visibly continuous; the morph's optical beat matches the size + colour transitions); the BSC value lands at the strip's full-width center and stays there at commit, no jump-left. Tested with `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce`: navigation commits instantly with no morph staging, value lands centered at commit (the static CSS still applies — the negative margin is not gated on motion), exactly as expected.

**What's testable today** (snapshot at landing):

- `npm test` — **675 unit tests** across **50 files** (was **674** at §17.38; +1 — the new font-weight morph test).
- `npm run lint` (`tsc --noEmit`), `npm run lint:rules` (ESLint layered rules) — clean.
- `npm run build` — clean. Bundle: **190.79 KB / 49.99 KB gzip** (up from `186.51 KB / 48.55 KB` at §17.38; +4.28 KB raw / +1.44 KB gzip — primarily the new long-form §17.39 inline docs in `drillTransitions.ts` (the docstring section + the BSC value re-centering rationale on the `.value-area` rule + the gutter-publishing rationale on the strip), plus the actual logical changes that span four files; the rule changes themselves are < 400 bytes total).
- `npm run test:e2e` — **107 scenarios** all green (+1 vs §17.38's 106 — the new `tile_layout` BSC value horizontal centering scenario).
