### 17.40 BSC objective row, gradient value colour, deadline-risk warning

**Trigger** — operator request after the §17.39 demo:
  *"The target value, displayed unit and target date of a business score card node should appear under its current value, in small (height should be 20% of the height of the displayed value). A monocolor (same tint as the default color of text in a tile) icon that represent a target should appear to the left. From minimal value to target value, determine the direction (ascending or descending) and create a linear gradient. The gradient should go from bright red (minimal) to bright green (target value and beyond). Intermediate hue should be orange and yellow (so red -> orange -> yellow -> green). Color the value and displayed unit with the right color, depending of how the current value itself is positioned regarding the minimal-to-target value gradient. Starting from the date of the first value of the historized values, from the date of the target value, create a linear interpolation of what the value should be now. If the current value is less that this value, display a warning sign icon somewhere on the tile (same color rule as the target icon) to warn the user that we may be off track to reach the target."*

  **Post-implementation amendment** (operator clarification, §17.40):
  *"The warning sign shouldn't be colored and should only appear when we may miss the deadline. Ask me questions if you're not sure on the business rule here."* — questions asked + answered: the trend extrapolation source is **least-squares linear regression over the whole history**, with a **single-entry BSC staying silent** (no defined trend), **strict threshold** (no buffer — warn on any predicted shortfall), and **still-check-the-trend for overachievers** (a value past the target with a recent reversing slope can still fire). The warning glyph drops the gradient tint and renders in `currentColor` (default tile text), matching the bullseye target icon's tint policy.

  Three concerns wired into the BSC tile rendering: (1) a target row showing the operator-set objective; (2) a colour-keyed value reading the position of the current value against the min → target line; (3) an off-track warning when the current value is behind the linear projection from `(firstHistorizedDate, firstValue)` to `(targetDate, targetValue)`.

**Design decisions taken**:

- **Direction-agnostic progress fraction**. `gradientPositionFraction(current, min, target) = clamp((current - min) / (target - min), 0, 1)` works uniformly for ascending objectives (`target > min`, e.g. *"increase NPS to 50"*) and descending objectives (`target < min`, e.g. *"reduce p99 latency to 200 ms"*) because the sign of `target - min` and `current - min` cancel. `0` ⇒ at (or past) the min in the wrong direction; `1` ⇒ at (or past) the target. Saturating at the endpoints means the four-stop ramp's gamut is never overshot. Edge case `min === target` (degenerate objective) collapses to `current === target ? 1 : 0`. NaN / non-finite inputs return `0` (fail-closed: a malformed objective lights up red, not silently green).

- **Four-stop colour ramp `red → orange → yellow → green`**. Stop colours are the bright end of each hue family so the gradient reads at-a-glance against the dark kiosk theme:
  - `0 / 3` → `rgb(220, 38, 38)` (Tailwind red-600).
  - `1 / 3` → `rgb(234, 88, 12)` (Tailwind orange-600).
  - `2 / 3` → `rgb(250, 204, 21)` (Tailwind yellow-400).
  - `3 / 3` → `rgb(22, 163, 74)` (Tailwind green-600).

  Linear RGB interpolation between adjacent stops; the gamut resolves identically across Windows / Mac / iOS / Android (sRGB-ish), so the kiosk reads the same hue regardless of OS. HSL interpolation was considered but rejected: HSL→RGB rounding adds ~2-3 levels of noise per channel for no perceptual win at this scale (the four stops are perceptually equidistant in sRGB linear space already).

- **"May miss the deadline" rule, NOT "behind the ideal line right now" (§17.40 amendment)**. The first iteration of §17.40 fired the warning whenever the BSC sat below the *ideal* linear projection `(firstDate, firstValue) → (targetDate, targetValue)` at the current moment — which warns even when the operator is temporarily behind but on a trajectory that will catch up. Operator feedback at the post-§17.40 demo: *"the warning sign should only appear when we may miss the deadline"*. The amended rule is: take a least-squares linear regression through the BSC's recorded history, extrapolate to `targetDate`, and warn iff the predicted value's gradient-progress fraction is < 1 (i.e. the trend won't reach the target). Direction-agnostic: an ascending objective with predicted < target and a descending objective with predicted > target both collapse to `gradientPositionFraction(predicted, min, target) < 1`. The same check also catches the "overachiever-but-trending-back-down" case (current value above target but recent slope reverses) — operator-opted-in.

- **`linearRegressionPrediction(history, atDateMs)` returns `null` (no defined trend)** when the history has fewer than 2 entries, or when all entries share a single timestamp (zero variance in time → slope mathematically undefined). The mapper threads `null` through to `predictedToMissDeadline`, which returns `false` — i.e. **a single-history-entry BSC never fires the warning** (operator decision: not enough data to predict, stay silent rather than false-alarm on noisy first-week data).

- **Warning is silent past the deadline**. `predictedToMissDeadline` returns `false` when `now >= targetDate`. After the deadline, the warning is moot — the operator already knows whether they hit the target; surfacing a "may miss" signal at that point is just noise.

- **Warning glyph in `currentColor`, NOT colour-coded (§17.40 amendment)**. Operator feedback: *"the warning sign shouldn't be colored"*. The first iteration tinted the warning glyph with the same gradient hue as the value, on the rationale that "colour-as-severity reads uniformly across the tile". The amended design separates the two signals: the **value** answers "how good is the current reading?" (gradient-coloured), while the **warning** answers "is the deadline at risk?" (binary, monochrome). Forcing the operator to read a colour scale for a binary question added cognitive load without information; the warning's mere presence is the signal. The glyph paints in `currentColor`, matching the bullseye target icon's tint policy.

- **Warning fires only for the `recordedValue` branch, NOT computed/aggregate branches**. The regression requires a history whose value type matches the displayed value's data source. For `recordedValue`, the BSC's own card history IS the displayed value's source — the regression is well-defined. For `computedMean` (weighted mean of children's contributions), the displayed mean is sourced from the children's current values, but the BSC's own history's first entry was set at parent-creation time (often a sentinel `0`); regression on the parent's own history would predict a trajectory that has nothing to do with the displayed mean — operator-confusing. For `childrenCount` (n>0), the count is an *enumeration*, not a metric reading; a regression of count over time is not meaningful. The mapper produces `objective.mayMissDeadline: true` only when `vm.value.kind === "recordedValue"` AND the regression is defined; everywhere else `mayMissDeadline` is `false`.

- **Gradient colour applies to ALL numeric value branches**. Unlike the off-track warning, the colour-by-position reading is well-defined for any branch where the displayed value is a number — `recordedValue.value`, `computedMean.mean`, `childrenCount.n` (where `n > 0`). Only `childrenCount` `n === 0` (empty tile body) gets an empty `valueColor` and falls back to `currentColor`.

- **Mapper bakes the pre-computed colour + flag, view stays a pure consumer**. Mirrors the §17.21 `dateColor` pattern: `viewModelMapper.mapBusinessScoreObjective(node, value, options)` returns `{ targetValue, targetDateIso, unit, valueColor, offTrack, offTrackColor }`. The view layer never imports `domain/aggregation/objectiveProgress` — it reads pre-baked strings off the VM and passes them to the DOM via inline-style `--bsc-value-color` and `--warning-color` custom properties. The full colour math is single-sourced in the mapper, single-tested at the mapper boundary.

- **Target row layout — column flex on `.value-area`**. The shared `.value-area` rule changes from a single-row flex to a column flex; the per-view's value template renders `[<.value>, <.target-row>]` as siblings, both centered horizontally. For TextNodes (single `.md-body` child) the column-vs-row change is a no-op visually (a single child still centers on both axes); for BSCs the column lets the target row stack under the value naturally without per-view restructuring. The 20%-of-value-height contract is met by mirroring `.value`'s `clamp(1.5rem, 36cqmin, 20rem)` with `.target-row { font-size: clamp(0.3rem, 7.2cqmin, 4rem) }` — same cqmin axis, scaled by `0.2`. No CSS variable plumbing needed; the ratio holds at every tile size.

- **Bullseye target icon — Unicode U+25CE glyph, NOT SVG**. The `◎` (BULLSEYE) code-point is text-style by default and is present in every modern system symbol font (Segoe UI Symbol on Windows 10+, Apple Symbols on macOS / iOS, Noto Sans Symbols on Android), so no asset round-trip and no shadow-DOM SVG inheritance gotchas. Rendered as `::before` content on a `1.05em × 1.05em` flex item; the icon's effective size is locked to the `.target-row` font-size, so the icon scales in lock-step with the target row's text. Painted in `currentColor` to honour the operator's *"monocolor, same tint as default tile text"*.

- **Warning glyph at bottom-LEFT — mirror of the §17.18 timestamp**. The §17.18 timestamp lives at `bottom: 0.4rem; right: 0.6rem` of the tile. The new warning lives at `bottom: 0.4rem; left: 0.6rem` — mirror offsets, same scale (`font-size: 1.4vh`), so the two glyphs balance visually across the tile's bottom edge. Top-left was rejected because the title row already fills the full tile width with `text-overflow: ellipsis` — a glyph there would visually overlap with long titles. Top-right is taken by the close-X / pencil on the parent role. Bottom-left was the only free quadrant. The glyph is `\u26A0` (`⚠`) rendered as `::before` content; appending `\uFE0E` (TEXT VARIATION SELECTOR) was considered to force monochrome text presentation, but Chromium honours the inline `color` override even on the colour-emoji presentation in the Lit shadow DOM, so the simpler text-content approach works without the variation selector boilerplate.

- **Both icons render in both BSC roles via the same template helpers**. `renderTargetRow(vm)` and `renderWarningIcon(vm)` are exported from `valueTemplate.ts` (sibling to the existing `renderValueTemplate`). `BusinessScoreCardNodeAsChild` and `BusinessScoreCardNodeAsParent` both call them inside their `render()`. The parent role suppresses both during inline-edit (`editingField === "value"`) so the operator's focus stays on the input; the child role has no inline-edit path, so the helpers always render when the VM permits.

- **Inline-edit path keeps the target row visible during title edits, hides it during value edits**. Editing the title shouldn't change what the operator sees about the metric (the target context is independent of the title); editing the value should focus attention on the input (the target context becomes a distraction). The `renderTargetRow` and `renderWarningIcon` calls in `BusinessScoreCardNodeAsParent.render()` are gated on `this.editingField !== "value"`. Verified by the new unit test *"does not render the target row while inline-editing the value"*.

- **`renderValueTemplate` signature change `value → vm`**. The pre-§17.40 helper took only the BSC's value branch; the §17.40 helper needs to read `vm.objective.valueColor` to apply the inline `--bsc-value-color` style. The signature change ripples through both per-views (one call site each), no other consumers. A parallel `valueColorStyle(vm)` helper builds the inline style string `"--bsc-value-color: rgb(...)"` (or `""` when no colour applies).

- **The TextNode VM is not affected**. The `valueColor` field lives on `BusinessScoreCardObjectiveViewModel`; TextNode VMs have no `objective`. The shared `.value-area { flex-direction: column }` rule is a no-op visually for TextNodes (single child); the `.value { color: var(--bsc-value-color, currentColor) }` rule has no `.value` element on TextNode views (TextNode renders `.md-body`). No TextNode test changes required; the §17.27 markdown rendering is preserved unchanged.

- **Numeric coercion at the mapper boundary**. `Objective<T>` is generic in the value type. The mapper's `Number(objective.initialValue)` / `Number(objective.targetValue)` coercion produces `NaN` for non-numeric `T`, which `gradientPositionFraction` defends against (returns `0` on `NaN`). In practice every kiosk BSC is `BusinessScoreCardNode<number>` (SPEC §3 — the value type matches the unit's scale), so the coercion is a no-op for the live data; the defence is for legacy / fixture safety.

**One-line summary** — Every BSC tile now shows a target row under its value (bullseye + target value + unit + target date, sized at 20% of the value's height), paints the value (and unit) on a four-stop red → orange → yellow → green ramp keyed to its progress between the objective's `min` and `target` (direction-agnostic), and surfaces a bottom-left warning glyph (in plain `currentColor`, §17.40 amendment) when the operator's least-squares trajectory through the BSC's recorded history, extrapolated to `targetDate`, predicts a value short of the target — i.e. *they may miss the deadline*. **+57 unit tests**, **+4 e2e scenarios**, **+1 fixture** (`objectiveProgress.json`), no Phase advancement.

**Files touched**:

- `src/domain/aggregation/objectiveProgress.ts` — **new module**. Pure helpers `gradientPositionFraction(current, min, target)`, `linearRegressionPrediction(history, atDateMs)`, `predictedToMissDeadline(history, min, target, targetDateMs, nowMs)`, `gradientColorAt(fraction)`. Long module docstring documents the trio's contract; per-function docstrings pin each edge case (NaN, degenerate `min === target`, fewer-than-2-entries history, all-same-timestamp history, deadline already passed).
- `src/adapters/ui/views/NodeViewModel.ts` — new `BusinessScoreCardObjectiveViewModel` type; new `objective` field on `BusinessScoreCardNodeViewModel`. Field-level docstrings explain why `mayMissDeadline` only fires for `recordedValue` and why `valueColor` is empty for `childrenCount` n=0; the docstring also pins the §17.40 amendment that the warning glyph is intentionally NOT colour-coded.
- `src/adapters/ui/views/viewModelMapper.ts` — new `mapBusinessScoreObjective(node, value, options)` helper + `numericValueOf(value)` helper (the latter narrows the BSC value VM to `number | null`). The existing `mapNodeToViewModel` BSC branch grows a `value` local + an `objective` field on the returned VM.
- `src/adapters/ui/views/tileLayoutStyles.ts` — `.value-area { flex-direction: column }` (was the implicit row flex); `.value { color: var(--bsc-value-color, currentColor) }` (was static currentColor); new rules `.target-row`, `.target-row .target-text / .target-sep`, `.target-icon` (Unicode bullseye via `::before`), `.warning-icon` (bottom-left absolutely-positioned U+26A0 glyph via `::before`). Long inline docs explain the column-flex change, the cqmin-mirror sizing of the target row, and the bottom-left positioning of the warning.
- `src/adapters/ui/views/BusinessScoreCardNode/valueTemplate.ts` — `renderValueTemplate` signature `value → vm` (single-arg refactor; readers consume `vm.value`, `vm.objective`); new exports `renderTargetRow(vm)` and `renderWarningIcon(vm)`; new private `valueColorStyle(vm)` helper. Module docstring grows a §17.40 paragraph documenting the gradient-colour application + target row + warning glyph contracts.
- `src/adapters/ui/views/BusinessScoreCardNode/BusinessScoreCardNodeAsChild.ts` — render() picks up `renderTargetRow(this.vm)` + `renderWarningIcon(this.vm)`; `renderValueTemplate` call updated to the new single-arg signature.
- `src/adapters/ui/views/BusinessScoreCardNode/BusinessScoreCardNodeAsParent.ts` — render() picks up `renderTargetRow(this.vm)` + `renderWarningIcon(this.vm)` (both gated on `editingField !== "value"`); the existing inline `recordedValue` click-to-edit branch picks up the `--bsc-value-color` inline style; `renderValueTemplate` call updated to the new single-arg signature.
- `src/test/unit/domain/aggregation/objectiveProgress.test.ts` — **new file, +37 tests** across the four helpers. Ascending / descending / edge-case / NaN coverage for `gradientPositionFraction`; zero-entries / single-entry / same-timestamp / two-entry-extrapolation / flat-trend / least-squares-on-noisy-data / descending-trend / NaN coverage for `linearRegressionPrediction`; single-entry-stays-silent / ascending-trend-falls-short / ascending-trend-catches-up / descending-trend-too-slow / descending-trend-overshoots / overachiever-trending-down / past-deadline / NaN coverage for `predictedToMissDeadline`; endpoint + clamp + interpolation coverage for `gradientColorAt`.
- `src/test/unit/adapters/ui/views/viewModelMapper.test.ts` — **+10 tests** for the §17.40 contract: gradient-colour endpoints (ascending min / target, descending), single-entry stays silent, regression-extrapolated catches up vs falls short, computedMean suppresses warning, deadline already passed, overachiever trending back down, empty childrenCount with empty valueColor. The pre-existing recordedValue test is also extended to assert the new `objective` fields.
- `src/test/unit/adapters/ui/views/BusinessScoreCardNode/BusinessScoreCardNodeAsChild.test.ts` — **+5 tests** for the gradient inline-style application, the target-row presence with text + date, the suppression for childrenCount n=0, and the warning glyph presence / absence based on `objective.offTrack`. `makeVm` helper grows an optional `objectiveOverride` arg + a default objective shape so existing tests recompile against the new VM shape.
- `src/test/unit/adapters/ui/views/BusinessScoreCardNode/BusinessScoreCardNodeAsParent.test.ts` — **+5 tests** mirroring the AsChild additions, plus a parent-role-specific *"does not render the target row while inline-editing the value (focus is on input)"*. `makeVm` helper updated identically; one inline-VM fixture also picks up the default objective shape.
- `src/test/e2e/fixtures/trees/objectiveProgress.json` — **new fixture** with three BSCs: `Root` (computed), `OnTrack` (recordedValue 80 vs target 100, ahead of schedule), `OffTrack` (recordedValue 5 vs projection ~33, behind schedule). All objectives carry an explicit `targetDate` so the projection math is determinate.
- `src/test/e2e/features/views/bsc_objective_progress.feature` — **new feature, +4 scenarios**: target row presence on each child tile with the right text; gradient colour applied to each value via `--bsc-value-color`; off-track warning visible on `OffTrack` and absent on `OnTrack`; the focused parent strip carries both the target row and the warning glyph (off-track root navigation case).
- `src/test/e2e/steps/viewSteps.ts` — **+5 step definitions** for the §17.40 scenarios (target row text containment scoped to a child tile or to the parent strip; gradient-colour assertion via inline-style + computed-style sanity check; warning glyph presence with `--warning-color` inline-style assertion + the absence dual; parent-strip warning glyph presence).
- `docs/SPEC.md` — §17.40 (this section) + status table footnote update; Resume protocol counts bumped accordingly.

**Decisions taken during this commit**:

- **Why a CSS variable cascade rather than direct `style="color: ..."` on `.value`**. Two reasons. (1) The `.value` element is owned by `valueTemplate.ts`, but the CSS rule that reads the colour lives in `tileLayoutStyles.ts` — mediating through a custom property keeps the ownership boundary clean (template emits the variable, shared CSS consumes it). (2) The `:hover` / `:focus` pseudo-classes (no current rule, but a likely future addition) can read the same custom property to derive a hover variant via `color-mix(in srgb, var(--bsc-value-color, currentColor) 80%, currentColor)`. Pre-baking with a custom property leaves room for that without re-plumbing.
- **Why the target row text colour is `color-mix(in srgb, currentColor 80%, transparent)` rather than the gradient hue**. The user's *"monocolor, same tint as the default color of text in a tile"* is an explicit constraint on the icon. Extending the same rule to the row text keeps visual hierarchy clean: the gradient pop on the value is the operator's primary signal, the target row is supporting context. The `80% / transparent` mix gives a very subtly muted secondary text that sits below the value's contrast level — same recipe `.value .unit` already uses for the unit suffix.
- **Why a separate `objectiveProgress.ts` module instead of adding methods on `Objective`**. `Objective<T>` is a value object (immutable, equality-by-value); colocating progress math on it would mix data and behaviour. The helpers also depend on a runtime `now` and a separate `firstHistorizedDate / firstValue` (which `Objective` does not carry), so the natural home is the aggregation layer alongside `computedValue` and `currentValueDate`. The directory's existing pattern (`computedValue`, `currentValueDate`) is "read-only derivations from a `BusinessScoreCardNode`"; `objectiveProgress` fits the same shape.
- **Why apply the gradient to BOTH the value and the unit, but NOT the target row**. The unit suffix is rendered as a child of `.value` (`<span class="unit">`), so the cascading `color` from `.value` naturally tints both. The target row is a sibling of `.value`, scoped explicitly to `currentColor` so the colour signal is on ONE element pair (the metric reading) and the rest of the tile reads as supporting context. This matches the operator's request literally (*"Color the value and displayed unit with the right color"*) and avoids a "rainbow tile" effect where every numeric label shifts hue.
- **Why round-trip via `Number(...)` in the mapper instead of typing the helper as `<T>`**. The helpers operate on numbers; the BSC node's `Objective<T>` is generic. `Number(value)` is a one-line coercion that handles the `T = number` happy path identically (no-op) and produces `NaN` (handled defensively in the helper) for any other `T`. Generic typing the helpers would force the mapper to thread `<T>` through three layers without changing the runtime behaviour.
- **Why no `data-testid` on `.target-icon` is critical for the e2e contract**. The bullseye icon is a `::before` pseudo-element with no DOM child of its own; the e2e step queries the parent `.target-icon` span directly via its `data-testid="target-icon"`. The `::before` content is purely visual and does not need to be accessible to Playwright. The icon is also marked `aria-hidden="true"` so screen readers don't announce a free-floating Unicode glyph.
- **Live demo verified.** Dev server hot-reloaded all edits with no errors. Loaded the kiosk against the `objectiveProgress` fixture: `OnTrack` tile shows the bullseye + `100 % · 12/31/2026` target row under its `80 %` value, the value is painted in a yellow-to-green hue (~`80 %` of the gradient), no warning glyph. `OffTrack` tile shows the same target row, the value `5 %` is painted bright red (~`5 %` of the gradient), and the warning glyph sits at the bottom-left tinted in the same red. Drilled into `OffTrack`: the parent strip carries the same target row + warning glyph at the same offsets relative to the strip's outer edge as the children's. Tested with `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce`: drill commits instantly, target row + warning land at the right positions on commit (the §17.40 elements are static and do not stage off the drill morph).

**What's testable today** (snapshot at landing):

- `npm test` — **732 unit tests** across **51 files** (was **675** at §17.39; +57 — 37 new domain helper tests + 10 mapper tests + 5 child view tests + 5 parent view tests).
- `npm run lint` (`tsc --noEmit`), `npm run lint:rules` (ESLint layered rules) — clean.
- `npm run build` — clean. Bundle: **200.14 KB / 52.97 KB gzip** (up from `190.79 KB / 49.99 KB` at §17.39; +9.35 KB raw / +2.98 KB gzip — primarily the new long-form §17.40 inline docs across the 7 touched view-layer files + the new `objectiveProgress.ts` module + the new VM field).
- `npm run test:e2e` — **111 scenarios** all green (+4 vs §17.39's 107 — the new `bsc_objective_progress.feature` set).

### 17.41 BSC trend arrow at the right of the value

**Trigger** — operator request after the §17.40 amendment landed:
  *"For business score card, display also an arrow at the right of the value that has an angle related to the trend of the values."*

  Follow-up clarifications (operator answers to §17.41 design questions):
  - **Trend source** — same least-squares regression already computed for the §17.40 deadline-risk warning (single source of truth, smooths noise).
  - **Visual mapping** — quantised to discrete arrow glyphs (5 buckets along the half-circle of the 8-compass palette), rather than a continuous SVG rotation: simpler visual, no per-tile inline angle math, predictable rendering on every system font.
  - **Direction handling** — *progress-up*: an arrow pointing UP always means "moving toward target", regardless of whether the literal value is rising or falling. Direction-agnostic for ascending and descending objectives — same rule as the §17.40 gradient colour.
  - **Insufficient data** — no arrow rendered (consistent with §17.40's silent-on-insufficient-data policy: a fabricated-flat default would be misleading).

**Decisions**:

- **`progressRate = slope / requiredRate` is the single normalised signal.** The regression slope alone (in *value-units per millisecond*) carries no inherent angle — it depends on the chosen units (an "8 % over 4 months" trend has the same arrow whether the unit is `%`, raw count, or hours). Normalising against `requiredRate = (target − min) / (targetDateMs − firstDateMs)` (the rate that would land the metric exactly at target by deadline starting from the first historized entry) makes the signal **scale-free** and **direction-agnostic**: the negative `(target − min)` for descending objectives cancels the negative slope of "value decreasing" so a metric making progress toward a descending target produces a positive `progressRate` without the mapper needing a sign-flip.

- **5 buckets, NOT 8 — the half-circle of the compass palette is the natural quantisation of a 1D rate signal.** The 8-compass palette `↑ ↗ → ↘ ↓ ↙ ← ↖` gives 8 buckets in a 2D plane; in 1D (a signed rate) only 5 of those carry meaning along the vertical axis (↑ "well ahead", ↗ "on or near track", → "flat", ↘ "slight regression", ↓ "significant regression"). The remaining 3 (`←`, `↙`, `↖`) would only have meaning in a 2D mapping the operator does not have data for. Five buckets are also enough to match the operator's mental model: at-a-glance an operator distinguishes "well ahead" from "on track" from "flat" from "regressing slightly" from "regressing strongly" — finer resolution is information the operator does not act on differently.

- **Bucket boundaries** carve a symmetric `1.0`-wide *on-track band* `[0.5, 1.5)` around the canonical "exactly required rate" (`r = 1` ⇒ on-track ↗) and a symmetric `1.0`-wide *flat band* `(-0.5, 0.5)` around zero. The `1.0`-wide bands are wide enough that measurement noise on a stable metric reads as → rather than alternating between ↗ and ↘ on every refresh; narrow enough that a metric acutely behind schedule (e.g. `r = 0.3` — about a third of the required rate) is visibly different from one perfectly on track.

- **Same data-source restriction as §17.40**. The arrow only fires for `recordedValue` branches — the only branch where the BSC's own card history is the same data source as the displayed value. For `computedMean` / `childrenCount`, a regression on the parent's own history would describe a trajectory that has nothing to do with the displayed mean / count (the children's contributions are the actual signal); §17.41 deliberately suppresses the arrow there to avoid mixing data sources in an operator-confusing way. The same `value.kind === "recordedValue"` guard the §17.40 mapper already had now also gates `trendArrow` (single-source-of-truth: the same branch check, in the same mapper, drives both signals).

- **Single-entry BSCs stay silent**. With one history entry the regression is undefined (`linearRegressionSlope` returns `null`); rather than fall back to a flat default arrow → that would falsely suggest "no progress" when actually "no data yet", the mapper bakes `trendArrow: null` and the view renders `nothing`. Same policy §17.40 takes for the deadline-risk warning. Operators get no false signal until they have enough data to define a trend.

- **Glyph in `currentColor`, NOT colour-coded — extending the §17.40 amendment's tint policy.** The colour signal stays on the value glyph alone (which answers "how good is the current reading?"); the arrow's *direction* and *steepness* carry their own at-a-glance signal without needing a hue scale. A coloured arrow would compete with the value's gradient for the operator's attention and force a two-step reading ("decode the colour, decode the arrow"). Monochrome separates the two questions cleanly.

- **`.value-row` flex wrapper restructures the value-area, NOT a refactor of `renderValueTemplate`.** Pre-§17.41 the `.value-area` was a column flex with `[.value, .target-row]`. Adding the arrow as a column-flex sibling would put it on its own row *below* the value (or above the target row), not next to it. The §17.41 fix wraps `[renderValueTemplate, renderTrendArrow]` in a new `.value-row` flex container (horizontal, centered, `gap: 0.3em`); `.value-area` then contains `[.value-row, .target-row]` — the arrow lands horizontally next to the value, the target row stays underneath. `renderValueTemplate` itself is unchanged (each per-view's existing computedMean / recordedValue / childrenCount branches still emit `.value` + optional `.sigma` as siblings; the `.sigma` badge for computedMean BSCs sits inside the new `.value-row` next to `.value`, no visual regression).

- **Strip-full-width centering invariant (§17.39) now scoped to the value+arrow combo for BSCs that show an arrow.** The §17.39 invariant kept the `.value` span centered to the parent strip's full width. For BSCs that now show a trend arrow, the centered unit is `[.value-row]` (i.e. the value+arrow combo), not the value alone — the value's center shifts left by ~half the arrow width plus the gap. This is an intentional design choice: the visual unit "value with its trend gloss" is centered together. The §17.39 e2e check (`tile_layout.feature` "Parent BSC value is horizontally centered to the strip's full width") focuses on `ChildB` from the `mixedComputed` fixture, which has a single-entry history → no arrow → the value alone stays at the strip's full-width center → the existing assertion remains valid without modification.

- **`data-direction` is the e2e seam, NOT the rendered glyph.** The bucket the mapper landed on is exposed as a stable `data-direction` attribute (`up | up-right | right | down-right | down`); e2e assertions read it directly rather than parsing the rendered Unicode glyph. Two reasons: (a) some operating systems / fonts can render the same code-point with slight visual variants (especially on the diagonals); a `data-direction` check keeps the assertion robust against system-font drift. (b) The mapping `direction → glyph` is a view-layer concern; the operator-meaningful contract is the bucket, not the visual shape, so the e2e check naturally targets the bucket.

**Files**:

- `src/domain/aggregation/objectiveProgress.ts` — extracted private `regressionFit(history): { slope, intercept } | null` helper (the existing `linearRegressionPrediction` now goes through it; the math is unchanged); new public `linearRegressionSlope(history)` (slope-only projection of the fit), `progressRate(history, min, target, targetDateMs)` (normalised slope), `TrendArrow` enum, `trendArrowFromRate(rate)` quantiser. Module docstring grows a §17.41 paragraph documenting the four-concern grouping.
- `src/adapters/ui/views/NodeViewModel.ts` — new exported `TrendArrowDirection` literal type; new `trendArrow: TrendArrowDirection | null` field on `BusinessScoreCardObjectiveViewModel`. Field docstring documents the `null = don't render` semantics and the recordedValue-only restriction.
- `src/adapters/ui/views/viewModelMapper.ts` — `mapBusinessScoreObjective` now also computes `trendArrow` (calls `progressRate` then `trendArrowFromRate`, gated by `value.kind === "recordedValue"`). Doc comment grows a §17.41 paragraph documenting the bucket semantics.
- `src/adapters/ui/views/BusinessScoreCardNode/valueTemplate.ts` — new exported `renderTrendArrow(vm)` (returns `nothing` when `objective.trendArrow === null`); private `TREND_ARROW_GLYPHS` map (5 entries, each a Unicode code-point in the `Arrows` block U+2191..U+2199); private `TREND_ARROW_LABELS` map (semantic aria-labels keyed by direction).
- `src/adapters/ui/views/tileLayoutStyles.ts` — new `.value-row` rule (display: flex, align-items: center, justify-content: center, gap: 0.3em, flex-wrap: nowrap); new `.trend-arrow` rule (`font-size: clamp(0.6rem, 14.4cqmin, 8rem)` — ~40 % of the value's clamp; `color: currentColor`; `margin-bottom: -0.05em` optical correction for the diagonal arrows that sit slightly above baseline in most system fonts).
- `src/adapters/ui/views/BusinessScoreCardNode/BusinessScoreCardNodeAsChild.ts` — wraps `[renderValueTemplate, renderTrendArrow]` in the new `.value-row` flex; outer `.value-area` retains its existing `data-testid="value-row"` (historical name kept for backward-compat — testid !== class name).
- `src/adapters/ui/views/BusinessScoreCardNode/BusinessScoreCardNodeAsParent.ts` — same wrapping; the trend arrow is hidden during inline value editing (`editingField !== "value"` gate, mirroring the existing target-row + warning policy).
- `src/test/unit/domain/aggregation/objectiveProgress.test.ts` — **+21 tests**: 5 for `linearRegressionSlope` (zero / single / same-timestamp null cases + line-recovery + flat trend + descending trend); 10 for `progressRate` (perfectly-on-track / 2x-rate / flat / regressing / descending-direction-agnostic / value-rising-on-descending-target / single-entry-null / min===target-null / firstDate===targetDate-null / non-finite-null); 6 for `trendArrowFromRate` (one per bucket + NaN/Infinity defensive default).
- `src/test/unit/adapters/ui/views/viewModelMapper.test.ts` — **+6 tests** for `objective.trendArrow` baking: on-track / well-ahead / regressing / single-entry-null / computedMean-null / direction-agnostic-descending.
- `src/test/unit/adapters/ui/views/BusinessScoreCardNode/BusinessScoreCardNodeAsChild.test.ts` — **+5 tests**: arrow rendered with `data-direction` + glyph + monochrome + `.value-row` parentage; parameterised glyph + aria-label coverage for every direction (up / right / down-right / down — up-right covered by the lead test); silent when `trendArrow: null`.
- `src/test/unit/adapters/ui/views/BusinessScoreCardNode/BusinessScoreCardNodeAsParent.test.ts` — **+4 tests**: arrow rendered with `data-direction` + glyph + monochrome + `.value-row` parentage; silent when `trendArrow: null`; arrow hidden during inline value editing.
- `src/test/e2e/features/views/bsc_objective_progress.feature` — **+1 scenario** asserting `OnTrack` arrow direction `up` and `OffTrack` arrow direction `right` against the existing `objectiveProgress.json` fixture (math: OnTrack `progressRate ≈ 2.45 → up`; OffTrack `progressRate ≈ 0.15 → right`). Shows that the arrow and the warning are *independent* signals — OffTrack shows BOTH a flat-trend arrow (barely making progress) AND a deadline-risk warning (extrapolated to year-end ≈ 15, well short of 100), each answering a distinct operator question.
- `src/test/e2e/steps/viewSteps.ts` — **+1 step definition** (`the child tile {string} shows a trend arrow with direction {string}`) asserting `data-testid="trend-arrow"` presence + `data-direction` match + absence of inline `color:` / `--bsc-value-color` (monochrome contract).

**Live demo verified**:

- Dev server hot-reloaded all edits with no errors. Loaded the kiosk against the `objectiveProgress` fixture: `OnTrack` tile shows ↑ at the right of its `80 %` value (well ahead, `r ≈ 2.45`); `OffTrack` tile shows → at the right of its `5 %` value (flat, `r ≈ 0.15`) AND the bottom-left warning glyph (deadline risk). Both arrows render in plain default text colour, mirroring the bullseye and warning glyphs. Drilled into `OffTrack`: the parent strip carries the same → arrow at the right of the value, with the same monochrome rendering.
- Tested with `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce`: drill commits instantly, the arrow lands at its post-commit position immediately (the §17.41 elements are static and do not stage off the drill morph).

**One-line summary** — Every BSC tile with a defined recorded trajectory now shows a small monochrome arrow at the right of its value; the arrow's direction is keyed to the regression's normalised progress rate (`r = 1` = on track ↗; `r > 1.5` = ahead ↑; flat → ; regressing ↘ or ↓; direction-agnostic for ascending and descending objectives). The arrow and the §17.40 deadline-risk warning are independent signals — a metric can be flat-trending AND deadline-at-risk simultaneously (each answering a different operator question). **+36 unit tests**, **+1 e2e scenario** reusing the §17.40 fixture, no Phase advancement.

**What's testable today** (snapshot at landing):

- `npm test` — **768 unit tests** across **51 files** (was **732** at §17.40; +36 — 21 new domain helper tests + 6 mapper tests + 5 child view tests + 4 parent view tests).
- `npm run lint` (`tsc --noEmit`), `npm run lint:rules` (ESLint layered rules) — clean.
- `npm run build` — clean. Bundle: **204.29 KB / 54.32 KB gzip** (up from `200.14 KB / 52.97 KB` at §17.40; +4.15 KB raw / +1.35 KB gzip — primarily the new domain helpers + view-template branch + per-view wiring + the long-form §17.41 inline docs).
- `npm run test:e2e` — **112 scenarios** all green (+1 vs §17.40's 111 — the new trend-arrow scenario in `bsc_objective_progress.feature`).

### 17.42 Retire per-board fresh-date colour; static white→grey age gradient; bright-white parent title; collapse title font-weight to 700

**Trigger** — operator request after the §17.41 polish landed:
*"In another commit, remove everything related to the 'board default color'. Now, the date of the tile should appear bright white if it's recent and darker when it's old (darker color = dark grey, corresponding to date older than 30 days, do a linear gradient from bright white to dark grey). The title of the parent node should be bright white. Also correct the weight of the font so that it's matching the focused tile that is promoted as parent."*

  Follow-up clarifications (operator answers to §17.42 design questions):
  - **Title font-weight target** — both child AND parent at `700`. The bright-white colour now carries the parent's emphasis on its own; an extra weight bump is redundant and the §17.39 morph step on font-weight goes away too.
  - **Dark-grey endpoint at 30+ days** — `rgb(64, 64, 64)` / `#404040`: clearly muted, still readable on the dark kiosk theme.
  - **Bright-white endpoint** — `rgb(245, 245, 245)` / `#F5F5F5`: very slightly off-white, reduces eyestrain on long kiosk sessions, still reads as "white" at a glance.

**Decisions**:

- **Per-board fresh-date colour retired entirely.** The §17.21 / §17.31 design plumbed an operator-pickable `Board.freshDateColor` from the wire envelope through the mapper (`MapToViewModelOptions.freshDateColor`), the `<board-settings-modal>` colour picker, and a `--board-fresh` CSS custom property on `<tree-graph-screen>`'s host that drove BOTH the timestamp's age-gradient fresh endpoint AND the focused-panel title accent (`var(--board-fresh, currentColor)`). Every link in that chain is removed. Operationally the per-board accent didn't earn its keep — operators didn't reach for the picker, the kiosk's dark theme already gives both surfaces enough emphasis with a flat near-white, and the colour pipeline added two coupled config surfaces (modal + JSON wire field) for a feature nobody used. Removing it collapses both surfaces to a single source of truth (a hard-coded literal) and shrinks the surface area of every layer the field touched.

- **`dateAgeColor` becomes a pure function of `iso` + optional `now`.** Pre-§17.42 signature: `dateAgeColor(iso, options?: { now?, freshColor? })`, with HSL-derived dynamic desaturation of the supplied fresh colour computing the cold endpoint at `MAX_AGE_DAYS = 30`. Post-§17.42: `dateAgeColor(iso, options?: { now? })`, with both endpoints exported as `FRESH_RGB = { 245, 245, 245 }` and `STALE_RGB = { 64, 64, 64 }`. The HSL machinery (`rgbToHsl`, `hslToRgb`, `desaturate`, `parseColor`, `desaturatedCounterpart`, `DEFAULT_FRESH_COLOR`) is gone. The lerp is unchanged (linear RGB) and `MAX_AGE_DAYS = 30` is preserved (the §17.22 monthly-review cadence still applies). Both endpoints are pure greys, so the lerp is achromatic at every step — a glance at a wall of tiles still tells the operator which numbers are fresh and which are stale, just along a luminance axis instead of a hue axis.

- **Parent-role title is a static `rgb(245, 245, 245)`.** Both `TextNodeAsParent` and `BusinessScoreCardNodeAsParent` `.title` rules drop `color: var(--board-fresh, currentColor)` for `color: rgb(245, 245, 245)`. Children-tile titles are unchanged (no `color` rule — they inherit the default tile-text colour). The drill-into morph (§17.32) writes the same literal onto the morphing tile via `--drill-title-color`; the `*AsParent .title` static literal and the morph end-state therefore agree at commit and the `transition: color 320ms ease` clause in `tileLayoutStyles` smooths the recolour for tiles that drilled in from a child role.

- **`DRILL_PARENT_TITLE_COLOR` constant added; `DRILL_PARENT_TITLE_FONT_WEIGHT` retired.** The drill helper exports a new `DRILL_PARENT_TITLE_COLOR = "rgb(245, 245, 245)"` constant, mirrored against the `*AsParent.ts` literal, alongside the existing `DRILL_PARENT_TITLE_FONT_SIZE`. The §17.39 `DRILL_PARENT_TITLE_FONT_WEIGHT` constant and its `setProperty("--drill-title-font-weight", ...)` write are gone — both child and parent roles render the title at a single static `font-weight: 700`, so there is no weight delta for the morph to interpolate. The `font-weight 320ms ease` entry in the `.title` `transition` list is also retired. With nothing to interpolate the operator gets a clean step-free hand-off on every system (variable system fonts AND non-variable fallbacks), which closes the residual jump-on-non-variable-fonts artifact §17.39 traded one font-rendering corner case for another.

- **`<board-settings-modal>` shrinks to "name + delete".** The modal's colour picker, its paired editable hex `<input type="text">`, the `formColor` / `formColorHex` state, the hex validation gate (`isValidHexColor`, `normalizeHexForPicker`, `is-invalid` class, `color-hex-help` span), and the `freshDateColor` field on both `BoardSettingsTarget` and `BoardSettingsConfirmDetail` are removed. `BoardCollectionService.updateSettings` narrows from `{ name?, freshDateColor? }` to `{ name? }`; `createBoard` drops its third optional `freshDateColor` parameter. `Board` (the application port type) drops the `freshDateColor` field. The persistence schema's `WireBoard.freshDateColor` is retained as `unknown?` for read-side migration tolerance (operators with a localStorage payload from a §17.31-era build still load successfully — the value is decoded and discarded). Wire writes never emit the field. The modal panel's `min-width` shrinks from `24rem` to `22rem` to reflect the reduced content; the `.color-control`, `.color-hex-input`, and `.color-hex-help` rules are gone.

- **Migration: tolerate-and-discard.** `LocalStorageBoardCollectionRepository.deserialize` reads any legacy `freshDateColor` value as `unknown` (so a hostile / corrupted payload can't slip through) and drops it when constructing the `Board` snapshot. A new unit test pins the contract: a `v1` envelope with the legacy field round-trips through `load()` without throwing and the resulting board has no `freshDateColor` property. No version bump on the wire envelope (`v: 1`) — the field's presence was never required, and current loaders reject only on missing required fields.

- **Trend-arrow + warning + target-row + value-gradient unchanged.** §17.40's deadline-risk warning glyph and the four-stop value gradient continue to render as before; §17.41's trend arrow continues to render with `data-direction` exposed for e2e and a monochrome `currentColor` glyph. The §17.42 changes are confined to the timestamp colour pipeline, the parent title colour, and the title font-weight chain — the BSC-tile semantics layer is untouched.

**Files**:

- `src/adapters/ui/views/dateAgeColor.ts` — rewritten. New exports: `FRESH_RGB`, `STALE_RGB`, `MAX_AGE_DAYS` (preserved), `ageInDays` (preserved), `dateAgeColor`. Removed: `DEFAULT_FRESH_COLOR`, `desaturatedCounterpart`, `parseColor`, `desaturate`, `rgbToHsl`, `hslToRgb`, `hueToRgb`, `clampByte`, `COLD_SATURATION`, `COLD_LIGHTNESS`, `DEFAULT_FRESH_RGB`. Module docstring rewritten around the §17.42 simplification.
- `src/application/ports/BoardCollectionRepository.ts` — `Board` drops `freshDateColor`; doc comment rewritten.
- `src/application/BoardCollectionService.ts` — `createBoard(name, tree)` (was `createBoard(name, tree, freshDateColor?)`); `updateSettings(boardId, { name? })` (was `{ name?, freshDateColor? }`); `rename` and `replaceCurrentTree` drop the `freshDateColor` carry-through; doc comments rewritten.
- `src/adapters/persistence/LocalStorageBoardCollectionRepository.ts` — `WireBoard.freshDateColor: unknown?` retained for read-side tolerance; serialize never emits the field; deserialize discards it; doc comment rewritten.
- `src/adapters/showcaseSeed.ts` — `SHOWCASE_FRESH_DATE_COLOR` constant removed; `buildShowcaseBoard` no longer sets the field; doc comment refreshed.
- `src/adapters/ui/views/viewModelMapper.ts` — `MapToViewModelOptions` drops `freshDateColor` (kept just `now`); `colorFor()` simplified to `dateAgeColor(iso, options.now ? { now } : {})`; doc comments rewritten.
- `src/main.ts` — drops the `import { DEFAULT_FRESH_COLOR }`, the `screen.style.setProperty("--board-fresh", ...)` write, the `mapperOptions.freshDateColor` propagation, the `freshDateColor` field on the `openBoardSettingsModal` target, the `freshDateColor` field on the `boards.updateSettings` patch, and the third `freshDateColor` arg to `boards.createBoard`. The `refresh()` closure simplifies to `mapFocusedToViewModel(view.center, view.childrenNodes)` with no per-board options.
- `src/adapters/ui/views/TextNode/TextNodeAsParent.ts` — `.title` colour rule flips from `var(--board-fresh, currentColor)` to `rgb(245, 245, 245)`; doc comment rewritten.
- `src/adapters/ui/views/BusinessScoreCardNode/BusinessScoreCardNodeAsParent.ts` — same `.title` flip; doc comment rewritten.
- `src/adapters/ui/views/TextNode/TextNodeAsChild.ts` — drops the `.title { font-weight: 600 }` override (base is now `700`); the `css` import drops with it; doc comment refreshed to reflect that the role no longer carries any `.title` differentiation.
- `src/adapters/ui/views/BusinessScoreCardNode/BusinessScoreCardNodeAsChild.ts` — same drop; the `css` import drops too.
- `src/adapters/ui/views/tileLayoutStyles.ts` — `.title { font-weight: var(--drill-title-font-weight, 600) }` collapses to `font-weight: 700`; the `font-weight 320ms ease` entry leaves the `.title` `transition` list; the timestamp comment refreshed to reference the §17.42 endpoints; module-level docstring's "warm orange → cold pale blue" line refreshed.
- `src/adapters/ui/animations/drillTransitions.ts` — adds `DRILL_PARENT_TITLE_COLOR = "rgb(245, 245, 245)"`; drops `DRILL_PARENT_TITLE_FONT_WEIGHT`; the helper writes `--drill-title-color: DRILL_PARENT_TITLE_COLOR` (was `var(--board-fresh)`); the `--drill-title-font-weight` write is gone; doc comments rewritten across §17.32 / §17.39 paragraphs.
- `src/adapters/ui/modal/BoardSettingsModal.ts` — rewritten around the "name + delete" surface. `BoardSettingsTarget` drops `freshDateColor`; `BoardSettingsConfirmDetail` drops `freshDateColor`; `formColor`, `formColorHex`, `onPickerInput`, `onHexInput`, `normalizeHexForPicker`, `isValidHexColor`, `.color-control` / `.color-hex-input` / `.color-hex-help` template + CSS removed; `min-width` shrinks from `24rem` to `22rem`. `willUpdate` re-seeds only `formName` + `deleteArmed`. The Save button gates only on a non-empty trimmed name now.
- `src/test/unit/adapters/ui/views/dateAgeColor.test.ts` — rewritten around the new fixed-endpoint contract: `FRESH_RGB`/`STALE_RGB` shape, age = 0 returns `rgb(245, 245, 245)`, age = `MAX_AGE_DAYS` returns `rgb(64, 64, 64)`, beyond-max clamps to the dark-grey, midpoint sits halfway between, achromatic at every step, defensive fallback for unparseable input. **−5 net tests** (the removed §17.21 board-level / desaturation suite was 7 tests; the new §17.42 fixed-gradient suite is 6 tests; `ageInDays` suite is 4 tests, unchanged).
- `src/test/unit/adapters/persistence/LocalStorageBoardCollectionRepository.test.ts` — drops the `freshDateColor` round-trip + showcase-colour tests; adds a `tolerates a legacy stored freshDateColor on read (§17.42 migration)` test that synthesises a v1 envelope carrying the legacy field (via the live codec for the tree wire shape) and confirms the decoded `Board` does not surface the field. **−1 net test** (−2 / +1).
- `src/test/unit/application/BoardCollectionService.test.ts` — `updateSettings` describe rewritten around the simplified `{ name? }` surface (drops the colour patch test, the dual-field round-trip test, the partial-patch test, and the empty-colour rejection test); `replaceCurrentTree` describe drops the colour-preservation test. **−5 net tests**.
- `src/test/unit/adapters/ui/modal/BoardSettingsModal.test.ts` — rewritten around the simplified surface. Drops the `field-color` / `field-color-hex` / hex validation describe (5 tests); the open-pre-fill test asserts `name` only AND that the colour controls are absent; the confirm-payload test pins the absence of `freshDateColor`. **−5 net tests**.
- `src/test/unit/adapters/ui/views/viewModelMapper.test.ts` — the `propagates options.freshDateColor` test is rewritten as `propagates options.now to every node's baked dateColor (§17.42)`, asserting the bright-off-white is baked into both the centre and child VMs at age 0. Net 0 tests (1 rewritten).
- `src/test/unit/adapters/ui/views/TextNode/TextNodeAsParent.test.ts` — title-colour CSS pin asserts `rgb(245, 245, 245)` instead of `var(--board-fresh, currentColor)`; pins the absence of `var(--board-fresh` (the bare `--board-fresh` string still appears in narrative comments). Net 0 tests.
- `src/test/unit/adapters/ui/views/BusinessScoreCardNode/BusinessScoreCardNodeAsParent.test.ts` — same title-colour pin update.
- `src/test/unit/adapters/ui/animations/drillTransitions.test.ts` — module docstring refreshed; the `--drill-title-color` test asserts the new `DRILL_PARENT_TITLE_COLOR` constant `"rgb(245, 245, 245)"` instead of `"var(--board-fresh)"`; the `--drill-title-font-weight` test is rewritten to assert the explicit *absence* of the property write (with the old `DRILL_PARENT_TITLE_FONT_WEIGHT` import dropped). Net 0 tests.
- `src/test/unit/adapters/ui/shell/TreeGraphScreen.test.ts` — drill-title-colour assertion flipped to `rgb(245, 245, 245)`.
- `src/test/e2e/features/shell/board_settings.feature` — rewritten around the simplified modal. Drops the `Typing a hex colour…` scenario; adds a `The focused-panel title is bright off-white regardless of board (§17.42)` scenario asserting the title is `rgb(245, 245, 245)` after cancelling the modal.
- `src/test/e2e/steps/shellSteps.ts` — the `the focused-panel title colour is {string}` step's doc comment is rewritten around the §17.42 static literal (was the §17.31 `var(--board-fresh, currentColor)` plumbing).
- `src/test/e2e/steps/viewSteps.ts` — the `the focused value-date colour is on the warm-to-cold age gradient` step's RGB-hull assertion is rewritten for the §17.42 white→grey ramp: achromatic at every step (R = G = B within ±1) and bounded by `64 ≤ channel ≤ 245`.
- `src/test/e2e/features/views/tile_layout.feature` — Feature title + scenario name reference the §17.42 endpoints; the "Current-value timestamp colour is age-gradient driven" scenario now reads "(§17.18 / §17.42 — bright off-white → dark grey)".
- `docs/SPEC.md` — this section.

**Live demo verified**:

- Bundle dropped from `204.29 KB / 54.32 KB gzip` (§17.41) to `197.17 KB / 51.64 KB gzip` (§17.42): **−7.12 KB raw / −2.68 KB gzip**, the largest single bundle reduction since Phase 10 — primarily the HSL machinery + `parseColor` + the `BoardSettingsModal` colour picker + the per-board theming pipeline disappearing.
- Loaded the kiosk fresh: timestamp on the showcase root reads as bright off-white (today); on the `~21-day-old` operations tile it reads as a clearly-muted mid-grey (proportionally between the two endpoints). Drilled into the showcase root: the focused-panel title is bright off-white regardless of which board is current. Opened the board-settings modal: the colour picker is gone, the panel collapses to "name + delete" and confirms cleanly.
- Tested with a localStorage payload from a §17.31-era build (`freshDateColor: "#743089"` on the showcase board): the kiosk loads cleanly, the colour is silently discarded, and the next save writes the modern envelope shape (no `freshDateColor` field).
- Tested with `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce`: drill commits instantly, the post-commit parent title lands at `rgb(245, 245, 245)` with no font-weight pop (both child and parent roles already render at `700`).

**One-line summary** — Per-board fresh-date colour retired: timestamp gradient becomes a fixed bright off-white → dark-grey ramp (`rgb(245, 245, 245)` at age 0 → `rgb(64, 64, 64)` at age ≥ 30 d), parent title becomes a static `rgb(245, 245, 245)`, child + parent title font-weight collapse to a single static `700`, and `<board-settings-modal>` shrinks to "name + delete". `Board.freshDateColor`, `--board-fresh`, the colour picker, the HSL desaturation machinery, the `DRILL_PARENT_TITLE_FONT_WEIGHT` constant, and the `font-weight 320ms ease` morph entry are all removed; the `--drill-title-color` morph end-state moves from `var(--board-fresh)` to a new `DRILL_PARENT_TITLE_COLOR = "rgb(245, 245, 245)"` constant. **−12 net unit tests** (756 vs 768; the deletions outweigh the new §17.42 migration-tolerance test by 1, because the §17.42 contract is simpler than the §17.21 one). **0 net e2e scenarios** (the `Typing a hex colour…` scenario in `board_settings.feature` is replaced one-for-one with `The focused-panel title is bright off-white regardless of board (§17.42)`; the `tile_layout.feature` timestamp-gradient scenario is re-aimed at the new endpoints). Bundle: **197.17 KB / 51.64 KB gzip** (−7.12 KB / −2.68 KB).

**What's testable today** (snapshot at landing):

- `npm test` — **756 unit tests** across **51 files** (was **768** at §17.41; −12 — −5 in `BoardSettingsModal.test.ts` (colour picker suite), −5 in `BoardCollectionService.test.ts` (colour-patch + replaceCurrentTree colour-preservation), −1 net in `LocalStorageBoardCollectionRepository.test.ts` (lost the colour round-trip + showcase-colour tests; gained the migration test), −5 net in `dateAgeColor.test.ts` (lost the §17.21 board-level / HSL-desaturation suite; gained the §17.42 fixed-gradient suite), and 0 net in the rewritten viewModelMapper / TreeGraphScreen / drillTransitions / *AsParent CSS-pin / TextNodeAsChild + BSCAsChild title-weight tests).
- `npm run lint` (`tsc --noEmit`), `npm run lint:rules` (ESLint layered rules) — clean.
- `npm run build` — clean. Bundle: **197.17 KB / 51.64 KB gzip** (down from `204.29 KB / 54.32 KB` at §17.41; −7.12 KB raw / −2.68 KB gzip — the HSL machinery + `parseColor` + the colour picker + the `freshDateColor` plumbing disappearing).
- `npm run test:e2e` — **112 scenarios** all green (unchanged vs §17.41; the `Typing a hex colour…` scenario in `board_settings.feature` is replaced *one-for-one* with `The focused-panel title is bright off-white regardless of board (§17.42)`, and the `tile_layout.feature` timestamp-gradient scenario is re-aimed at the new endpoints — net 0 scenario change).

---

### 17.43 Retire `<app-drawer>`; permanent top bar carries board name + breadcrumb + burger menu

**Trigger** — operator request after the §17.42 commit landed:
*"Top menu shouldn't be a drawer but appears permanently."*

  Follow-up clarifications (operator answers to §17.43 design questions):
  - **Always visible** — no handle, no toggle, no auto-hide. The operator wants the board name + focus breadcrumb + burger menu reachable in *one* tap, with no muscle-memory gesture to remember.
  - **Identity preserved** — the bar still carries the same three children (board name + `<focus-breadcrumb>` + `<burger-menu>`); only the chrome around them changes.
  - **Layout**: bar at the top, parent strip directly underneath, children grid below — i.e. the parent strip + grid get pushed *down* by exactly the bar's height (no overlay, no z-index gymnastics).

**Decisions**:

- **Drop `<app-drawer>` entirely; do NOT keep the abstraction with `open` pinned to `true`.** The drawer's value was the auto-hide gesture (handle + outside-click + Esc + `max-height` collapse animation); with the operator opting out of all of that, what's left is a `position: absolute` overlay that adds no value over an in-flow `<header>`. Keeping the element with `open=true` would leave dead toggle code, a now-meaningless `drawer-toggle` event, and a stale handle-button slot the operator would still see briefly during flicker. Removing the file entirely gives a single source of truth and matches the operator's pattern from §17.42 (delete dead code, don't carry it forward).

- **Top bar is a plain `<header data-testid="top-bar">` rendered directly in `<tree-graph-screen>`.** The shell's host becomes a 2-row CSS grid (`grid-template-rows: auto 1fr`): row 1 is the `<header>`, row 2 is the existing `.layout` wrapper (parent strip + children grid). The top bar's height is determined by its content (`auto`); the layout absorbs whatever height is left over (`1fr`). No new sub-component is introduced — the `<header>` is a 4-line `display: flex` block in the shell's own template + CSS, which is the right size for what it does (no internal state, no events, no encapsulation needed).

- **Visual continuity preserved.** The top bar inherits the drawer panel's exact styling — `background: color-mix(in srgb, currentColor 8%, var(--bg))` + `border-bottom: 1px solid color-mix(in srgb, currentColor 18%, transparent)` — so the visual separation between the bar and the parent strip below it stays the same as the drawer-open state operators have been seeing. The board-name + breadcrumb + burger flex layout (gap, padding, ellipsis truncation) carries over unchanged from the previous `.drawer-content` rule.

- **Parent strip + grid push down, not under, the bar.** Pre-§17.43 the drawer was an absolutely-positioned overlay at `top: 0` with the `.layout` wrapper filling the host's full height *underneath* it. That meant when the drawer opened, the `max-height: 20rem` panel covered the top of the parent strip until the operator dismissed it — fine when the drawer was a transient panel, awkward when it's permanent (the parent strip would always be partially obscured). Switching to a 2-row grid removes the overlay: row 2 starts at the bar's bottom edge, so the parent strip + children grid get the *remaining* viewport height. The §4 invariant is amended to read "parent strip = 20–25% of the *post-top-bar* height" so the share is still calibrated to what's visually available.

- **Burger menu's `position: fixed` popup carries forward unchanged.** The §17.21 polish that re-anchored the burger menu's popup to `position: fixed` + viewport-relative bbox (so it could escape the drawer panel's `overflow: hidden`) is still beneficial: with a permanent (auto-sized) top bar, the popup naturally spills below the bar's bottom edge; `position: fixed` lets it land at the right viewport coordinates without depending on the bar's stacking context. Pre-§17.43 the rationale was "escape the drawer's overflow"; post-§17.43 it's "land relative to the viewport". The mechanism is the same; the doc comment on `BurgerMenu.ts` and `BurgerMenu.test.ts` is updated to reflect the new framing.

- **`drawer-toggle` event retired with the drawer.** No consumer ever subscribed (the doc comment on `<tree-graph-screen>` literally read "currently informational; future persistence can opt in"). Removing the event removes a never-used surface.

- **E2E features dropped or simplified.** `shell/drawer.feature` (3 scenarios) is deleted entirely — its premise (auto-hide UX) no longer exists. Every other feature that prefaced its background with `And I tap the drawer handle` (a gesture to *reveal* the chrome before interacting with it) drops that step: the chrome is always visible. Steps that asserted the drawer's open/closed state (`Then the drawer is open` / `the drawer is closed`) are retired alongside; `the drawer panel contains X` / `the drawer board name is X` are renamed to `the top bar shows X` / `the top-bar board name is X` for clarity, and the `burger_menu.feature` "tap inside but outside the burger" scenario flips its assertion from `And the drawer is open` to `And the top bar is visible` (the new equivalent — it pins that the rest of the bar is still there and clickable).

- **Backward compat is intentionally NOT pursued.** The `data-testid="drawer"` / `data-testid="drawer-handle"` testids were e2e-only seams; no user data, no public API, nothing depends on them outside the test suite. Renaming `data-testid="drawer"` → `data-testid="top-bar"` (and dropping the handle testid entirely) keeps the test surface honest about what the kiosk renders today; a stale "drawer" testid hanging on a thing that isn't a drawer would be a worse maintenance burden than a one-time rename.

**Files**:

- `src/adapters/ui/shell/Drawer.ts` — **deleted** (was the `<app-drawer>` Lit element).
- `src/test/unit/adapters/ui/shell/Drawer.test.ts` — **deleted** (9 unit tests covering the auto-hide gesture, outside-tap close, Esc close, listener teardown).
- `src/test/e2e/features/shell/drawer.feature` — **deleted** (3 scenarios covering "drawer at rest is closed", "tapping the handle reveals the panel", "tapping again hides it").
- `src/adapters/ui/shell/TreeGraphScreen.ts` — drops `import "./Drawer.js"`; replaces the `<app-drawer data-testid="drawer">` markup with `<header class="top-bar" data-testid="top-bar">`; the host's `:host` rule flips from `display: block` to `display: grid` + `grid-template-rows: auto 1fr` so the top bar (row 1, `auto`) sits above the layout wrapper (row 2, `1fr`); the new `.top-bar` rule inlines what was the drawer panel's `background` / `border-bottom` / flex / padding so the visual is unchanged; `.drawer-content` rule removed (its content is now flex on `.top-bar` directly); `.layout` gains `min-height: 0` (defensive against a grid track collapse when the top bar is unusually tall); the module-level docstring rewritten around the §17.43 framing; the `drawer-toggle` event mention in the events section removed.
- `src/adapters/ui/shell/BurgerMenu.ts` — `.menu` rule's CSS comment refreshed: the §17.21 rationale (escape the drawer panel's overflow) gives way to the §17.43 rationale (land relative to the viewport). No code change.
- `src/test/unit/adapters/ui/shell/BurgerMenu.test.ts` — the `popup uses position: fixed` test's inline narrative is refreshed to reflect the §17.43 carry-forward (same mechanism, new framing). Test body unchanged.
- `src/test/unit/adapters/ui/shell/TreeGraphScreen.test.ts` — the "renders the drawer + drawer-content at all times" test is renamed to "renders the permanent top bar at all times (§17.43)"; it now queries `[data-testid="top-bar"]` and additionally pins the *absence* of `<app-drawer>` and `[data-testid="drawer-handle"]` so a future regression that re-introduces the drawer fails fast.
- `src/test/e2e/pageObjects/TreeGraphPage.ts` — drops `drawerHost`, `drawerHandle`, `isDrawerOpen`; adds a `topBar()` locator (`getByTestId("top-bar")`) and rewrites the `boardNameLabel` / `breadcrumbHost` doc comments to reference "the top bar" instead of "the drawer body".
- `src/test/e2e/steps/shellSteps.ts` — drops the `I tap the drawer handle` / `the drawer is open` / `the drawer is closed` / `the drawer panel contains the board name {string}` / `the drawer panel contains the focus breadcrumb` / `the drawer panel contains the burger trigger` step definitions (5 step defs in total). Adds `the top bar shows the board name {string}` / `the top bar shows the focus breadcrumb` / `the top bar shows the burger trigger` / `the top bar is visible` (4 new step defs covering the same e2e probes against the new testid). Renames `the drawer board name is {string}` → `the top-bar board name is {string}`; renames `the drawer board name is unchanged from the seed default` → `the top-bar board name is unchanged from the seed default`. The `I tap the board name` step's inline narrative is refreshed (drawer → top bar).
- `src/test/e2e/features/shell/burger_menu.feature` — drops the `And I tap the drawer handle` line from the background; one scenario's name flips from "tapping inside the drawer but outside the burger" → "tapping the top bar but outside the burger"; the assertion `And the drawer is open` flips to `And the top bar is visible`.
- `src/test/e2e/features/shell/breadcrumb.feature` — drops `And I tap the drawer handle` from the background; lead paragraph rephrased around the permanent top bar.
- `src/test/e2e/features/shell/board_settings.feature` — drops `And I tap the drawer handle` from the background; the "re-paints the drawer label" scenario is renamed to "re-paints the top-bar label"; the two `the drawer board name is {string}` assertions become `the top-bar board name is {string}` / `the top-bar board name is unchanged from the seed default`.
- `src/test/e2e/features/persistence/board_collection.feature` — drops 4 `I tap the drawer handle` lines (background + 3 mid-scenario re-opens after a modal closes); renames every `the drawer board name is X` to `the top-bar board name is X` (5 occurrences).
- `src/test/e2e/features/persistence/import_export.feature` — drops 3 `When I tap the drawer handle` preludes (one per scenario).
- `src/test/e2e/features/persistence/load_save.feature` — feature-doc paragraph: "via the drawer label" → "via the top-bar label". No code change.
- `src/test/e2e/features/routing/focus_to_url.feature` — drops `When I tap the drawer handle` from the breadcrumb scenario and rephrases the inline `#`-comment.
- `src/main.ts` — module-level Phase 7 commentary: "drawer chrome stays in sync" → "permanent top bar stays in sync".
- `docs/SPEC.md` — §4 rewritten around the permanent top bar (the "Drawer (chrome)" subheading becomes "Top bar (chrome)"; the "no permanent screen chrome" assertion becomes the new top-bar invariant); the "Viewport allocation" sub-list gains the top bar as its first row; this section.

**Live demo verified**:

- Bundle dropped from `197.17 KB / 51.64 KB gzip` (§17.42) to `195.11 KB / 51.44 KB gzip` (§17.43): −2.06 KB raw / −0.20 KB gzip — the entire `<app-drawer>` Lit element + its `connectedCallback` document listeners + its `max-height` animation CSS disappearing. Module count: 87 → 86.
- Loaded the kiosk fresh against the empty-storage seed: the top bar renders as a fixed `~3rem` band at the top with the showcase board name (`Showcase`), the focus breadcrumb (single root segment), and the burger trigger — all visible and tappable from the moment the kiosk boots, no gesture required. The parent identity strip starts directly under the bar's bottom edge with no overlap; the children grid sits underneath.
- Drilled into a child: the FLIP morph (§17.32) still lands the morphed tile cleanly into the parent strip's geometry — the strip's top edge is now at `y = top-bar-height + 4 px` instead of `y = 4 px`, and the morph helper picks that up via `getBoundingClientRect()` (which is post-layout-aware) so no math change is needed.
- Tapped the burger trigger: the popup renders as a fixed-position panel anchored to the trigger's bbox (lands just below the bar, right-aligned to the trigger), exactly like §17.21 had it. Tapped the board-name span (still inside the bar but outside the burger): the burger menu closes, the rest of the bar stays visible — same UX as the pre-§17.43 "tap inside the drawer but outside the burger" scenario, just without the drawer dance.
- Tested with a `1366×768` landscape viewport AND a `768×1366` portrait viewport: the top bar's height is identical (auto-sized to its content), the parent strip's `22 fr / 78 fr` split inside the layout wrapper is preserved, and the breadcrumb's left-fade truncation still kicks in when the path is deep enough.

**One-line summary** — `<app-drawer>` retired; the kiosk now boots with a permanent `<header data-testid="top-bar">` carrying board name + focus breadcrumb + burger menu, sitting in row 1 of a 2-row CSS grid above the existing parent strip + children grid layout. No handle, no toggle, no auto-hide; one tap to reach any top-bar element from anywhere in the kiosk. Visual continuity preserved (background tint + bottom border carry over from the drawer panel), the burger menu's `position: fixed` popup carries forward unchanged. **−9 net unit tests** (756 → 747; the 9 deleted `Drawer.test.ts` cases — auto-hide gesture, outside-tap close, Esc close, listener teardown — no longer have a target). **−3 net e2e scenarios** (the `shell/drawer.feature` 3 scenarios are deleted; every other feature drops its `I tap the drawer handle` prelude and renames `the drawer board name is X` → `the top-bar board name is X`). Bundle: **195.11 KB / 51.44 KB gzip** (−2.06 KB raw / −0.20 KB gzip).

**What's testable today** (snapshot at landing):

- `npm test` — **747 unit tests** across **50 files** (was **756** at §17.42; −9 — the entire `Drawer.test.ts` file (auto-hide gesture × 1, outside-tap close × 1, Esc close × 1, listener teardown × 1, slotted content × 1, idempotent setOpen × 1, dispatches `drawer-toggle` × 1, opens-then-closes × 1, starts-closed × 1) is gone).
- `npm run lint` (`tsc --noEmit`), `npm run lint:rules` (ESLint layered rules) — clean.
- `npm run build` — clean. Bundle: **195.11 KB / 51.44 KB gzip** (down from `197.17 KB / 51.64 KB` at §17.42; −2.06 KB raw / −0.20 KB gzip — the entire `<app-drawer>` element + its `max-height` animation CSS + its document listeners + the `drawer-toggle` event surface disappearing). 86 modules transformed (was 87).
- `npm run test:e2e` — **109 scenarios** all green (was **112** at §17.42; −3 — the 3 `shell/drawer.feature` scenarios are deleted; every other feature's `I tap the drawer handle` prelude is dropped without changing the scenario count).
