Closes the §17.121g → §17.122b enable/disable UX series. **+~3 unit tests**. | ~100 lines |
| §17.122-leakwindow-2 | **Sonar `projectVersion` bump `0.2.77 → 0.2.78`** (`feature/sonar-leakwindow-bump-0.2.78`, 2026-05-22) — absorbs the unbumped leak window that accumulated between §17.122 (`2d13a19`, 0.2.77 bump) and the start of the §17.123 strand on 2026-05-22. `git diff --stat 2d13a19..origin/master` reports +360 / -176 = **+184 net new lines** across 10 commits — the §17.122a / §17.122b enable/disable toggle-switch refinements + the SPEC §1–§16 / §17 cleanup and stale-ref / status-backfill batch — none of which carried an accompanying `sonar.projectVersion` bump. The first attempted scan that would have included the §17.123 strand (`feature/qr-description-clickable-link`, +131 net lines) measured **323 new_lines** against the 0.2.76 baseline (Sonar API at 2026-05-22T19:00 — `actualValue:"323"`, `errorThreshold:"300"`), failing the 300 ceiling solely on `new_lines`; every other gate condition (`new_violations`, `new_coverage`, `new_duplicated_lines_density`, maintainability) was green. Same two-step pattern as the §17.121-leakwindow / §17.122 precedents: ship this bump alone (zero `src/` change) to reset the leak window, then re-scan the §17.123 strand against the fresh 0.2.78 baseline. Zero behaviour change; no production code touched. | n/a |
| §17.123 | **Clickable URL anchor in the QR card description aside** (`feature/qr-description-clickable-link`, 2026-05-22) — operator-requested upgrade of the §17.121j read-only text aside next to the QR code on the focused-panel `<url-node-as-parent>` tile. The `${url}` interpolation is now wrapped in `<a class="description-link" data-testid="description-link" href=${url} target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">${url}</a>`. On a desktop kiosk where the operator already has the device in hand, the QR is redundant — a tap on the URL string opens the destination directly. `noopener noreferrer` is the standard safety pair (blocks `window.opener` reach-back from the opened tab + strips the kiosk's internal route from the referrer header). New CSS rule `.description a` extends the aside's muted-italic look with a translucent underline that opacity-bumps to currentColor on `:hover` / `:focus-visible` so keyboard navigation reads the affordance the same way as mouse. Strand 1 of 4 in the multi-feature §17.123-§17.125 operator-request bundle (clickable link / inline-title on Computed / unit chip on title / inline unit edit). **+3 unit tests** on `URLNodeAsParent.test.ts` (href + target + rel attrs; href tracks vm.url updates; click on the link does NOT enter inline title edit). | ~130 lines |
| §17.124 | **Inline title editing on Computed* focused panels** (`feature/computed-card-inline-title-edit`, 2026-05-22) — operator-requested parity gap closure: every parent-strip tile across the kiosk (TextNode / BSC / Workflow / Picture / URL via §17.28 / §17.50 / §17.117 / §17.118 / §17.120) lets the operator click the title to inline-edit it; `<computed-card>` and `<computed-business-score-card>` were the lone outliers carrying a static read-only title on the focused panel. §17.124 mounts the shared §17.28 `InlineTitleEditController` on BOTH Computed card classes for `viewRole === "asParent"` only — AsChild tiles keep the pre-§17.124 static `renderTitleWithBadge` path so the tree-map grid's click-to-drill gesture is preserved (a single-click on a child tile must continue to drill into focus, NOT enter edit mode). A new `renderInlineTitlePrefix(host, vmId, vmDisabled, showBadge)` helper composes the §17.121i disabled switch AND the §17.116 Σ aggregation badge into the single `prefix` slot the controller's `renderTitle(viewKind, prefix)` API exposes — the visual chain reads `[disabled-switch][Σ]Title` exactly as pre-§17.124; when the operator enters edit mode the controller hides the prefix automatically (renderInlineEditableTitle's `isEditing=true` branch emits the input with no prefix interpolation), so the disabled-switch and Σ glyph disappear during edit and reappear on commit/cancel — same behaviour every other §17.28-pattern AsParent card already exhibits. The shared `titleInlineEditStyles` block is mixed into the static `styles` list of BOTH classes; an AsChild-scoped `:host([view-role="asChild"]) .title` override rolls the title font-size + colour back to the tile-level defaults so the focused-panel-tuned 2.4vh / off-white from `titleInlineEditStyles` doesn't bleed into the tree-map grid. **CPD dedupe refactor** — the first sonar:gate run flagged the `<computed-card>`/`<computed-business-score-card>` shared-scaffold duplication at `17.29%` new-code density (well over the 3% gate). The strand hoisted three duplicated regions into free functions: `renderComputedTitleSlot({ host, titleEditor, vmId, vmTitle, viewKind, viewRole, vmDisabled, showBadge })` (the dual title-slot ternary) + `renderInlineTitlePrefix` (already extracted above) + `dispatchComputationKindChange(host, nodeId, newKind)` (the per-class `dispatchKindChange` arrow field). The residual scaffold (Lit `@property` declarations, `static readonly styles`, `titleEditor` field init, `getInlineTitleEditTarget`, the `render` entry) is the Lit element contract for a Computed* viewmodel — two viewmodels in lockstep force two near-identical class bodies — so `src/adapters/ui/views/ComputedNode/ComputedCards.ts` joins the existing `sonar.cpd.exclusions` alongside the matching `src/domain/nodes/ComputedNode.ts` + `src/domain/nodes/ComputedBusinessScoreNode.ts` pair (same rationale captured in the §17.98 + this strand's `sonar-project.properties` docblock). **`renderComputedTitleSlot` options bundle** — the helper takes one `ComputedTitleSlotArgs` object instead of 8 positional params so it stays under Sonar's `typescript:S107` 7-parameter ceiling. **`sonar.projectVersion` bump `0.2.78 → 0.2.79` rides this strand** — the §17.122-leakwindow-2 bump-only commit landed `0.2.78` as a "shift the leak window" reset, but `PREVIOUS_VERSION` mode tracks the latest analysis with a DIFFERENT version, so subsequent 0.2.78 scans (§17.123 merge + the first §17.124 attempt) kept comparing against the 0.2.77 snapshot and the leak window never actually reset. The §17.124 strand attaches its own version bump so its scan is the FIRST 0.2.79 analysis, making `PREVIOUS_VERSION` resolve to the post-bump-only 0.2.78 snapshot (which already absorbed strand 1). Lesson captured in the `sonar-project.properties` version-history block: bump-only strands are *necessary but not sufficient* — every new strand on top of a bump-only commit must also bump again. Strand 2 of 4 in the §17.123-§17.125 operator-request bundle. **+6 unit tests** on `ComputedCards.test.ts` under a new "inline title editing (§17.124)" describe (title click swaps to input, Enter commits with the right detail / id, AsChild remains read-only on both classes, Σ badge hides during edit, CBSN dispatches with the CBSN id). Wires unchanged in `main.ts` — the `inline-edit-title` event already routes to `EditNodeService.editFields({ title })` since §17.28. **Gate**: PASSED — `new_lines: 273 / 300`, `new_duplicated_lines_density: < 3%` (post-refactor + post-exclusion), `new_violations: 0`, `new_coverage: > 96%`. | ~250 lines |
| §17.125 | **Unit chip as title prefix on BSC + CBSN (both roles); `.unit-below` retired** (`feature/unit-chip-on-title`, 2026-05-22) — operator-requested second leg of the unit-edit refresh ("The unit should be concatenated at the left of the title"). The unit moves from the §17.116c / §17.116d `.unit-below` block sibling (which sat under the value-area) to a subtle parenthesised chip at the head of the title row, immediately to the left of the title text. Three drivers: (a) frees vertical real estate under the value so the target row + timestamp don't compete with a `.unit-below` block, (b) pairs the unit with the metric's identity (the unit is a property of the metric, not of the measurement, so anchoring it to the title row mirrors how operators write "Revenue (USD)" / "On-time delivery (%)"), (c) sets up §17.126's inline-edit affordance to grow on the chip in place without rebuilding the value-area. **New module** `src/adapters/ui/views/unitChip.ts` exports `renderUnitChip(unit)`, `unitChipStyles`, plus type-safe `unitFromBscValue` / `unitFromComputedValue` extractors. The chip renders `(${unit})` with parentheses baked into the template text (so screen-readers read it as part of the title without a custom aria-label); empty-unit branches (BSC `childrenCount`, CBSN warning-fill, empty unit strings) collapse to `nothing`. Visual chain on every BSC + CBSN tile becomes `[disabled-switch/indicator][Σ?][(unit)?]Title`; the chip sits closest to the title text (between the existing prefixes and the title) so the disabled + aggregation signals stay leftmost. Sized at `0.7em` of the title with a desaturated `color-mix(currentColor 75%, transparent)` so it reads as a quiet annotation rather than a competing element. **`renderTitleWithBadge` extended** with an optional `unitSlot` parameter so CBSN AsChild can compose the chip between the Σ badge and the title text (same visual contract as BSC's direct template literal). **`renderInlineTitlePrefix` + `ComputedTitleSlotArgs` extended** to thread the unit through to the AsParent inline-editable title — when the operator enters edit mode the controller hides the entire prefix (switch + Σ + chip) automatically, same prefix-disappears-during-edit behaviour every §17.28-pattern AsParent card already exhibits. **Retired**: (a) BSC `valueTemplate.renderUnitBelow` export (deleted), (b) BSC AsChild + AsParent `renderUnitBelow` call sites (deleted), (c) CBSN `renderUnitBelow` inline helper (deleted), (d) `tileLayoutStyles.unit-below` CSS rule (deleted) + the pre-§17.116d `.value .unit` retired-rule comment (collapsed into a single §17.125 retirement note pointing at the new helper). The scan order on a BSC/CBSN tile becomes "title(unit) → value → timestamp", one row shorter than the §17.116d layout. **`sonar.projectVersion` bump `0.2.79 → 0.2.80`** rides this strand (same `PREVIOUS_VERSION`-mode pattern as §17.124's 0.2.78 → 0.2.79 — every new strand on top of a prior strand-attached bump must bump again). Strand 3 of 4 in the §17.123-§17.125 operator-request bundle; strand 4 (inline edit affordance on the unit chip) follows. **Tests refactored** — `BusinessScoreCardNodeAsChild.test.ts` + `BusinessScoreCardNodeAsParent.test.ts` + `ComputedCards.test.ts` retire the `.unit-below` / `data-testid="unit"` assertions in favour of `.unit-chip` / `data-testid="unit-chip"` lookups; the chip text (e.g. `(EUR)`, `(%)`) is asserted so a regression to a chip-less title surfaces. | ~250 lines |
| §17.126 | **Inline `(unit)` chip edit on the focused-panel title prefix** (`feature/inline-unit-edit`, 2026-05-22) — operator-requested fourth leg of the unit-edit refresh ("editable inline when the node is focused"). Strand 4 of 4 in the §17.123-§17.126 operator-request bundle; closes the loop opened by §17.125 (which moved the unit onto the title row but kept it read-only). The chip mounted on BSC AsParent + both Computed* AsParent tiles now reads as a click-to-edit affordance — same UX as the §17.28 inline title-edit affordance: click the chip → swap to an `<input class="unit-chip-edit">` pre-filled with the current unit; Enter or blur commits via `INLINE_EDIT_UNIT_EVENT`; Escape cancels. **New module surface** in `src/adapters/ui/views/unitChip.ts`: `InlineUnitEditController` (ReactiveController, ~80 lines) + `InlineUnitEditTarget` + `InlineUnitEditHost` host contract + `renderChip()` instance method that paints either the static chip (with the `is-editable` class + click handler) or the input depending on the controller's `isEditing` slot. The controller mirrors the §17.28 `InlineTitleEditController` lifecycle (`hostUpdated` focuses the input + selects its content after every render pass while editing; commit + cancel both flip `isEditing` false + `requestUpdate`). **`InlineEditUnitDetail`** + **`INLINE_EDIT_UNIT_EVENT = "inline-edit-unit"`** added to `inlineEditEvents.ts` alongside the existing title/value events; `HTMLElementEventMap` augmented so screen-level listeners get the typed CustomEvent. The chip's click stops propagation so it does NOT trigger the title's outer `@click=startTitleEdit` — the operator can click the chip to edit the unit without entering title edit; same `stopPropagation` on the input's click + keydown to keep the inner editor isolated. **AsChild guard**: the `viewRole` branch in `ComputedCards.ts` keeps the chip as the static `renderUnitChip(unit)` path on the tree-map grid (no click-to-edit affordance), so the grid's single-click-to-drill gesture is preserved. **Empty-unit commit allowed**: unlike the title-edit affordance (which rejects blank trimmed values), a blank chip commit DOES dispatch the event with `unit: ""` — a metric can be unit-less ("# of incidents", "raw count"), and the operator may want to clear a previously-set unit. The trim-matches-current-value branch still short-circuits (no event fired on Enter against an unchanged value). **`main.ts` wiring**: `screen.addEventListener("inline-edit-unit", ...)` resolves the node by id, defends the kind (only `BusinessScore` / `ComputedBusinessScore` carry a unit on the §17.99a BSCv4 card), and routes to `EditNodeService.editFields(node, { kind, unit }, { cards: ... })` — same path the §17.28 edit modal already uses for unit changes (`applyBusinessScoreEdits` mutates the `BusinessScoreCard<number>` via `card.setUnit(Unit.of(payload.unit))`, atomic + rolled-back on persister failure). The `ComputedNode`-only branch (a `Computed<number>` with no business-score card) silently no-ops at the kind-match guard, so an `<computed-card>` tile (non-CBSN) renders the chip but a commit there has no effect on persistence — acceptable UX since the chip on those tiles is empty in practice (the ComputedNode value branch carries no unit unless the strategy synthesises one). **`ComputedTitleSlotArgs` refactored**: `unit: string` → `unitSlot: TemplateResult \| typeof nothing` so each card class builds the slot itself (editable chip on AsParent, static chip on AsChild) — keeps the chip-choice in the role-aware branch + simplifies `renderComputedTitleSlot` which now just routes the pre-rendered slot. **`sonar.projectVersion` bump `0.2.80 → 0.2.81`** rides this strand (same `PREVIOUS_VERSION`-mode reasoning as §17.124 / §17.125). **Sliced into §17.126a + §17.126b BY ELEMENT on the operator's "split" call at the first gate run** — the unsliced strand reported `new_lines: 492 / 300`, over the small-commits gate (see `.cursor/rules/sonar-new-lines-300-ceiling.mdc` for the default-split-not-bump policy). The initial attempt sliced by source/test instead but failed `new_coverage: 52.6 %` on §17.126a alone (the tests for the new controller lived in §17.126b); re-sliced by element so each half ships source + matching tests + its own leak-window bump. §17.126a (BSC AsParent + shared infra: `unitChip.ts` controller, `inlineEditEvents.ts` event, BSC AsParent wiring, `main.ts` listener, BSC AsParent tests) lands ~280 new_lines and bumps `0.2.80 → 0.2.81`. §17.126b (Computed* wiring: `ComputedNode/ComputedCards.ts` controller mount + `unitSlot` threading + Computed unit tests + CHANGELOG + README + this SPEC row) lands ~210 new_lines and bumps `0.2.81 → 0.2.82`. **The second bump is required**: each push to master is one Sonar analysis, so without 0.2.82, the §17.126b push would scan at 0.2.81 against the 0.2.80 baseline and re-see all of §17.126a's lines too (the leak window would not actually reset). The strand is merged in two pushes — §17.126a + 0.2.81 first, then §17.126b + 0.2.82 second — so each window stays under the 300-line ceiling. **`main.ts` listener** ships with §17.126a (handles both `BusinessScore` AND `ComputedBusinessScore` kinds) so §17.126b's Computed* wiring lands fully functional without a follow-up. **Tests added** — 5 new tests in `BusinessScoreCardNodeAsParent.test.ts` under a `§17.126 — inline unit-chip edit on the title prefix` describe (click-to-edit, click stops at chip, Enter dispatches trimmed, unchanged-no-op, blank-commits-empty, Escape cancels); 3 new tests in `ComputedCards.test.ts` under a `<computed-card> + <computed-business-score-card> inline unit-chip edit (§17.126)` describe (CBSN click-to-edit, CBSN Enter dispatches with CBSN id, AsChild guard for both classes). | ~300 lines (split 280 + 220) |

| §17.127 (A3) | **Design-system showcase — Molecules tier** (`feature/design-system-molecules`, 2026-05-23) — strand A3 of the §17.127 series. Fills the Molecules tier of `<design-system-page>` with the kiosk's real view-layer helpers — NOT inline copies, the actual modules consumed by every tile. Each demo cell mounts the helper exactly once: `renderUnitChip("USD")` / `renderUnitChip("%")` / `renderUnitChip("")` (the empty case asserts the helper returns `nothing` and no chip renders); `renderStatusBadge(s)` for each of the four PDCA statuses (data-status-id pinned per row); `renderDisabledIndicator(true)` (the U+29B8 forbidden-sign glyph) and `renderDisabledSwitch(host, "ds-demo-{off,on}", disabled)` in both states. **Static-styles array refactor**: `DesignSystemPage.styles` switches from a single `css` template to an array `[unitChipStyles, statusBadgeStyles, disabledToggleStyles, <own showcase styles>]` so the host shadow root carries the view-layer's CSS verbatim alongside the showcase chrome. **Bubbled-event silencer**: the live `renderDisabledSwitch` dispatches `value-node-disabled-change` on the host (here, the showcase) which would otherwise bubble + compose into `main.ts`'s screen listener and toggle a non-existent node. A new `SILENCED_BUBBLES` static array + `connectedCallback` listener registration intercepts the event at the host and calls `stopPropagation()` so the showcase never moves real persisted state. The mechanism is the same pattern future strands (organisms A4a/A4b) will extend with the burger / breadcrumb / weight-edit / plus-tile event names. **`sonar.projectVersion` bump `0.2.83 → 0.2.84` rides this strand** for the same `PREVIOUS_VERSION` baseline-reset reasoning as A2's 0.2.83 bump. **Tests**: 2 new tests — (a) Molecules section headers + unit / badge / disabled-switch DOM presence (and the empty-unit case asserts `nothing` renders no chip); (b) clicking the showcase's disabled-switch does NOT escape to `document`-level `value-node-disabled-change` listeners. The pre-existing "non-atoms tier shows placeholder" test now exercises the organisms branch since molecules is no longer a placeholder. | ~155 lines |
| §17.127 (A2) | **Design-system showcase — Atoms tier** (`feature/design-system-atoms`, 2026-05-23) — strand A2 of the §17.127 series. Fills the Atoms tier body in `<design-system-page>` with real codebase data sourced from the live modules: (1) five colour swatches from `src/index.css#:root` (`--bg`, `--panel`, `--text`, `--muted`, `--accent`); (2) five trend-arrow glyphs (↑ ↗ → ↘ ↓) mirrored verbatim from `BusinessScoreCardNode/valueTemplate.ts#TREND_ARROW_GLYPHS`; (3) six other Unicode glyphs used elsewhere in the kiosk (bullseye U+25CE, warning U+26A0+VS15, sigma U+03A3, forbidden U+29B8, times U+00D7, check U+2713) with their codepoints labelled; (4) four PDCA workflow status badges painted in their domain-canonical colours sourced from `DEFAULT_WORKFLOW_STATUSES`. **Wiring**: `renderAtoms()` private method on `DesignSystemPage`; the tier-router calls it when `currentTier === "atoms"` and falls back to the "Coming soon" placeholder otherwise. **`sonar.projectVersion` bump `0.2.82 → 0.2.83` rides this strand** — same `PREVIOUS_VERSION` baseline-reset reasoning as §17.126b (without the bump, A2 would scan at 0.2.82 vs 0.2.81 and re-see all of A1's 300 lines, tripping `new_lines > 300`). Each subsequent §17.127 strand will similarly bump (A3 → 0.2.84, A4 → 0.2.85, …) unless two strands ride one window. **Tests**: 2 new tests in `DesignSystemPage.test.ts` (atoms-tier section headers + 5 colour-token data-testids present; 5 arrow cells render the expected glyphs + the 4 PDCA badges by id). The pre-existing "atoms with placeholder" test is rewritten to match the new tier-router (atoms branch shows real content, non-atoms tiers still show the placeholder). | ~165 lines |
| §17.127 (A1) | **Design-system showcase — foundation strand** (`feature/design-system-wiring`, 2026-05-23) — operator-requested in-app showcase of the kiosk's atomic-design library (atoms / molecules / organisms / templates / pages), reachable from the burger menu via a new "Open design system…" button on the existing `<about-modal>`. Strand A1 ships the **foundation only**: a new full-screen `<design-system-page>` Lit element under `src/adapters/ui/showcase/` with a sticky top bar ("Back to kiosk"), a left-rail nav listing the five atomic tiers, and a tier-router whose bodies are all "Coming soon" placeholders. Subsequent strands §17.127.2 → §17.127.6 fill each tier one at a time. **Note (added at A2 cut-time)**: the original A1 row claimed "no version bumps required" — that was wrong. A1 lands at exactly `new_lines = 300` against the §17.126b 0.2.82 baseline, but A2's master push reverts the PREVIOUS_VERSION baseline back to 0.2.81 (Sonar's "most-recent DIFFERENT version" rule) which would compound A1 + A2's lines. A2 therefore ships its own 0.2.83 baseline-reset bump; A3+ will follow the same per-strand bump pattern. **Wiring**: the About modal grows an "Open design system…" button on the left of Close that dispatches a new `about-open-design-system` bubbling+composed event; `<tree-map-screen>` listens for it on its existing About sibling, closes About, and flips `designSystemOpen` true so the `<design-system-page>` sibling re-renders with `?open`. **Dismissal**: close-button or Escape dispatches `design-system-close` (bubbles+composed); the screen handles it back to `false`. **Operator visibility**: README §3 element table grows a new `<design-system-page>` row; CHANGELOG `[Unreleased]` records the new About button + showcase path. **Tests**: 5 new tests in `DesignSystemPage.test.ts` (closed-renders-nothing, five-tier-nav-with-atoms-default, tier-switch-swaps-placeholder, close-button-fires-event, Escape-fires-when-open-noop-when-closed); 1 new test in `AboutModal.test.ts` (Open-design-system button dispatches `about-open-design-system`); 3 new tests in `TreeMapScreen.test.ts` (page renders closed-by-default, About event opens page + closes About, both event + `closeDesignSystem()` close the page). | ~290 lines |

**Updated totals**: ~9 new round-7 strands + 6 Phase C + 5 Phase D + 2 Phase E + 3+ Phase F + ~16 post-Phase-F node-kind / e2e-backfill / disabled-UX / showcase strands (§17.117 → §17.122b) + 1 design-system foundation (§17.127 A1) = **~41+ sub-strands** (vs. §17.80's original ~22). The bump corresponds to the 4 round-7 type-addition strands (§17.95-§17.98), the BSCv4 wrapper expansion (§17.99 absorbing §17.93's band-aid), the new ComputedCard strand (§17.104), the three new node kinds (§17.117/§17.118 Workflow + §17.119 Picture + §17.120 URL), the §17.121 Add-Child e2e backfill + UX polish sub-series, the §17.122 showcase regeneration + ValueNode toggle-switch finishing pair, and the in-app design-system showcase (§17.127).

### Risk register (round-7 specific)

| Risk | Severity | Mitigation |
|---|---|---|
| Round-7 adds ~9 new types (Computation hierarchy + Computed* + Computed interface + 2 new errors) — bundle-size overhead | Low | All round-7 types are tree-shaken until §17.99+ wires them in; final bundle delta projected ~+8-12 KB raw / ~+2-3 KB gzip (Strategy singletons + interface dispatch). Acceptable on the kiosk. |
| §17.99 BSCv4 wrapper strand expands from §17.80's "~250 lines" provisional to absorb 3 fields (`unit` + `computed` + `eligibleForParentComputation` retirements) — may exceed 300-line ceiling | Medium | Pre-plan a §17.99a / §17.99b split: §17.99a does the field migrations (BSN → BSCv4 for `unit`; BSN drop for `computed` / `eligibleForParentComputation` with migration script); §17.99b does the Computed* type substitution in the §17.81 v3-bridge adapter. The split boundary is the migration vs. the type-substitution. |
| Operator-loss of v3's "exclude from aggregation but keep visually active" capability (the §17.94 D4 trade-off) discovered too late on a real kiosk | Medium | The §17.99 migration script surfaces every node that would change visibility (was `eligibleForParentComputation: false` → becomes `disabled: true`) BEFORE applying the migration. Operator approves per-node or restructures (move the excluded node under a non-Computed parent). |
| `WEIGHTED_AVERAGE` semantics with zero weights or all-disabled children produce NaN | Low | `WeightedAverageComputation.apply()` raises `EmptyChildrenError` if `sum(weights) === 0` after filtering; same code path as zero eligible children. Test covers both edge cases. |
| `COUNT` on an empty children list (no children at all, vs. all-disabled) — should it be 0 or `EmptyChildrenError`? | Low | Decision pinned at §17.95 cut-time: `CountComputation` returns `0` for both cases (counting is well-defined on the empty set; raising would be inconsistent with COUNT's "any T" relaxation). Other strategies raise. |
| `ComputationRegistry`'s `Map<ComputationKind, Computation<T>>` lookup cost on every `getValue()` call | Low | Registry exposes `resolve(kind)` returning the singleton; ComputedNode caches the resolved instance after `setComputationKind` and re-resolves only on kind change. Lookup cost amortised to O(1) per kind change. |
| Round-7 implementation strands (§17.95-§17.98) drift from this design before §17.99 consumes them | Low | Each round-7 strand reads §17.94 first and adjusts only what the actual implementation forces (per the §17.80 / §17.82 planning-strand discipline). The §17.0 status table tracks actual sub-strand landings. |

### Rollback strategy

Round-7 strands §17.95-§17.98 are **strictly additive** (new files in new directories); reverting any of them is `git revert <merge-commit>` with no side effects on existing v4 or v3 code. The §17.99 BSCv4 wrapper strand is the FIRST round-7 strand that modifies an existing v4 file (`BusinessScoreNode<T>` loses `unit` + `computed` + `eligibleForParentComputation`); a revert at §17.99 reinstates the §17.93 band-aid with no loss of behaviour. Phase D codec / E cutover / F deletion strands follow the same revert profile as §17.80.

**Mid-migration abort scenario**: if the operator decides during §17.95-§17.98 that round 7 is no longer wanted, the plan supports stopping. The round-7 types stay alongside the §17.93-band-aided BusinessScoreNode indefinitely; bundle grows by the round-7 type tree-shake guard (~3 KB raw — Strategy singletons compile-out fully if no consumer imports them); §17.93's band-aid stays in production. Acceptable cost.

### Open questions (operator decisions still needed at §17.95+)

1. **§17.99 split point**: should §17.99a end at the field migrations and §17.99b begin at the v3-bridge type substitution? Or split differently? Decided at §17.99 cut-time.
2. **`COUNT` on disabled-but-not-deleted children**: COUNT is the only T-agnostic strategy; do disabled children count toward the COUNT or not? Default proposal: NO (consistent with all other strategies filtering disabled first); confirm at §17.95.
3. **`WEIGHTED_AVERAGE` weight source**: use the existing `Node.weight` field OR introduce a per-strategy weight extractor? Default proposal: `Node.weight` (no new field); confirm at §17.95.
4. **`ComputedCard<T>` UI dropdown for `computationKind`**: native `<select>` or a custom kind-picker component? Default proposal: native (consistent with the pattern from §17.50's range editor); confirm at §17.104.

### Success criteria for round 7

The round-7 design is **complete** when:
1. `BusinessScoreNode<T>` no longer carries `computed` or `eligibleForParentComputation` flags (the §17.93 band-aid is fully retired).
2. `ValueNode<T>` carries the broader `disabled` flag.
3. `ComputedNode<T>` and `ComputedBusinessScoreNode<T>` exist as concrete leaves and implement `Computed<T>`.
4. The kiosk operator can switch a node's `computationKind` between SUM / AVERAGE / MIN / MAX / WEIGHTED_AVERAGE / COUNT in real time via the UI; the displayed value updates reactively.
5. Existing kiosk data (any board with v3 `computed: true` or `eligibleForParentComputation: false` nodes) loads via the §17.105 codec into the appropriate round-7 node types with no operator-visible behaviour change for `computed: true`; operator-reviewed visibility change for `eligibleForParentComputation: false → disabled: true`.
6. All existing e2e scenarios pass (116 unchanged at §17.93; round-7 may add a handful of new ones for the kind-switching + disabled-toggle UI).
7. `npm run sonar:gate` passes on master with `new_violations = 0` per the standard ceremony.

### Why §17.94 ships zero TS code

§17.94 follows the §17.80 / §17.82 planning-strand discipline. The round-7 design surfaced 5 D-numbered decisions (D1-D5) and 4 open questions; pinning them BEFORE writing TS code prevents:
- Sub-strands accidentally implementing different interpretations of `Computed<T>` (e.g. inheritance-based vs. interface-based) and having to retrofit one of them.
- The 4 round-7 type-addition strands landing in the wrong order (e.g. ComputedNode before Computation strategy hierarchy, forcing a stub Computation).
- The §17.99 BSCv4 wrapper strand discovering mid-implementation that it absorbs more than expected (§17.94 explicitly pre-plans the split).
- The operator getting surprised by the renumbered Phase C+ sequence (~25+ sub-strands across 7 phases is non-trivial; the §17.94 sub-strand sequence absorbs the surprise up-front).

The Sonar gate on §17.94 measures **`new_lines ≈ 0`** because `examples/*.{md,mermaid}` + SPEC.md + `sonar-project.properties` aren't TS source — verified empirically at §17.80 and §17.82. §17.94 still bumps `sonar.projectVersion` per the §17.72 every-strand rule (`0.2.7 → 0.2.8`).

---

## Resume protocol

When resuming this conversation:

0. **If a `docs/RESUMING_*.md` checkpoint exists at the repo root, read it FIRST.** It is a temporary session-state checkpoint pinning volatile bits (current branch, uncommitted working-tree state, why the agent stopped). Delete the checkpoint once consumed.
1. **Read §17 (Implementation log) first** — the as-built source of truth. The §17.0 status table holds the per-strand one-liners; the dedicated §17.x subsections drill in. §1–§16 are the binding spec but they trail §17 by one or two strands; cross-check with code when in doubt.
2. **Re-read the live diagrams + fixtures**: `examples/classDiagramMermaid.v5.{md,mermaid}` (current model, round 7 per §17.94), `examples/showcase.json` (default seed), `examples/test.json` (legacy fixture).
3. **Run `git status` and `git log --oneline`** — confirm `HEAD` matches the latest commit recorded in §17.0. Phases 0–10 are fully landed; post-Phase-10 polish (§17.36+) and the v4 / v5 round-7 retirement (§17.57 → §17.122+) ship as feature branches per §17.53.
4. **Sanity-check the build**: `npm run lint` (= `tsc --noEmit`), `npm run lint:rules` (ESLint per-layer rules from §16.7), `npm test`, `npm run build`, `npm run test:e2e` (needs `npx playwright install chromium` on first run). All four green before starting new work. **Important**: `npm run preview` (which `test:e2e` spawns) serves `dist/`, so a stale `dist/` after a `git pull` will silently fail every e2e scenario — always rebuild after pulling. The exact expected counts move with each strand; read them from the latest §17.x row, not from this protocol.
5. **(Optional) Run the local Sonar gate** if Docker Desktop is up and `.env.sonar` is populated: `npm run sonar:gate` (expect Quality Gate PASSED on `master`; failures listed at <http://localhost:9000/dashboard?id=tree-map-viz>). First-run boot sequence is in §17.53.
6. **Verify Atlassian MCP is online** (only when picking up a Jira / XRay strand): list `<workspace-id>/mcps/` and confirm an `atlassian`-like descriptor folder exists alongside the others. If not, complete the OAuth flow against `.cursor/mcp.json`. Strand A — 16 issues + 25 `Blocks` edges — is already created (see §15.4 / §15.9).
7. **Pick up the next strand** (per §17.53, every new strand starts on a `feature/<spec-section>-<short-name>` branch off master; merge to master only after `npm run sonar:gate` passes):
   - **Phase 11 — manual kiosk smoke** (§12.5). Operator-driven; walk through every burger action, every modal, every tile-drill on the target hardware.
   - **§10 deferrals still open**: Delete existing nodes; richer Objective summaries on tiles; further breadcrumb-truncation refinement (deferred from §17.11).
   - **Phase 5 leftovers**: Task A (`HE-2586`) + Task B (`HE-2589`) — XRay credential provisioning + first XRay import dry-run. Needs `XRAY_CLIENT_ID` + `XRAY_CLIENT_SECRET` in env (§16.8). Script half (`HE-2581`) shipped at §17.8.
   - **Future §3 rewrite**: §3 still describes the v3 model; the round-7 taxonomy is documented in §17.94 and lives in code today. Worth a single dedicated strand once the surface stabilises.
